places.description: Sorresh's lair, where gold lies in dunes but has become mere inert metal after the Luminous Ascension revalued consciousness-fragments and memory as the true currency.
places.notes: The hoard's transformation mirrors Sorresh's journey from material to philosophical wealth.

places.name: submerged archive
places.atmosphere: A deep underwater library where records of covenant and pre-human teachings are kept within the cathedral complex.
places.description: A submerged archive within the Drowned Cathedrals where Kethiel's reflection-self appears to Kess, and where ancient covenant knowledge is preserved.
places.notes: Unknown whether Kethiel was truly present or manifested as a vision/echo.

places.name: Dominion
places.atmosphere: A vast bureaucratic power seeking to map and control all knowledge and territory, even as the world fragments.
places.description: A major polity or realm where Henryk works as a junior cartographer. The Dominion is obsessed with reconciling and controlling knowledge, maintaining official maps and archives of all territories.
places.notes: Henryk burns his official maps rather than expose Henryk's sanctuary to Dominion control; they represent the force that would seize and fortify the fold.

places.name: First Well
places.atmosphere: A pit stop in the desert where cargo is vulnerable to theft or swap.
places.description: A waystation on the route between Pelimor and Crimport. Merrin suspects the cargo swap occurred here or between Pelimor and this point.
places.notes: Likely location of the cargo swap that placed consciousness-fragments into Kelven's caravan.

places.name: southern cities
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: fold village
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: harbormaster's tower
places.atmosphere: A tower of authority and hospitality where Captain Vesta conducts both logistics and delicate diplomacy.
places.description: The harbormaster's tower is Captain Vesta's seat of command in Verdant Strand. Vesta offered hospitality to the deepkin envoy here, including rum and fresh catch, while subtly discerning the truth of its nature.
places.notes: The tower serves as a formal meeting place for covenant negotiations and diplomatic inquiry.

places.name: deep forests
places.atmosphere: Primordial forests where ancient traditions of singing and song-keeping persist outside state control.
places.description: The deep forests are a remote wilderness where songkeepers still sing, maintaining traditions that predate the current empire. Merin entrusted three wanderers to carry the cups of old queens' songs to safety in these forests.
places.notes: These forests represent a refuge for pre-imperial traditions and may be outside the direct control of the Dominion or Syndicate.

places.name: kiln's heart
places.atmosphere: The innermost chamber of a furnace serving as a secret vault for forbidden songs.
places.description: The kiln's heart is the inner chamber of Merin's furnace where she hid the three cups containing the last songs of the old queens before destroying the furnace itself.
places.notes: The three cups remain hidden here unless discovered by Syndicate authorities. This location is critical to the preservation of pre-imperial song tradition.

places.name: Ward-school
places.atmosphere: A schoolhouse where children were used as leverage against Mael, threatened by Dominion agents to coerce her compliance.
places.description: A school for children located adjacent to Mael's workshop. The Dominion's agents threatened the children within to pressure Mael into carving false confession-bones.
places.notes: The Dominion's willingness to threaten children shows the brutality of their confession-protocol. Mael's refusal to comply despite these threats demonstrates her moral strength.

places.name: Flooded ruins beneath the city
places.atmosphere: Submerged chambers where merfolk dwell, a liminal space between human city and deep waters, accessed by descending beneath Verdant Strand.
places.description: Ancient flooded ruins lie beneath Verdant Strand, inhabited by merfolk like Captain Nerida. Kess descended into these ruins to meet Nerida and discuss the threat posed by the Harbor-Master's dam.
places.notes: The ruins suggest Verdant Strand was built atop older civilization or water-systems. They represent a space where humans and merfolk can negotiate.

places.name: Harbor-Master's tower
places.atmosphere: The administrative seat of the Harbor-Master, where Kess spread three centuries of tide-charts across his desk and changed his mind about the estuary's future.
places.description: A tower in Verdant Strand serving as the office and residence of the Dominion's Harbor-Master. Kess climbed it at night with evidence to convince him to sign the estuary protection charter.
places.notes: The tower's occupation by a Dominion administrator indicates how far inland Dominion authority extends. Its nighttime ascent by Kess suggests she acted with determination and some risk.

places.name: Ironspine caves
places.atmosphere: A vast cavern filled with centuries of accumulated gold and silver, now rendered worthless by the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: Sorrath's home for three hundred years, a cave system within the Ironspine where he accumulated vast quantities of gold and silver that tumbled like sand from his hoard-pile. The caves are now a monument to vanished value.
places.notes: The girl seeks Sorrath here. It is a place of isolation and reflection on the post-Ascension collapse of material value.

places.name: The Scorched Reach
places.atmosphere: Once fertile, now ash and desolation after the Luminous Ascension reordered what grows where.
places.description: A region that once yielded starflower and saffron but now yields only ash. The Luminous Ascension reordered the world's flora, leaving this region barren and its merchants desperate.
places.notes: Kelvan inherited his merchant route through this region from his mother. The hidden passage through the Wailing Scar leads to places where old spices still grow.

places.name: hidden catacomb
places.atmosphere: A secret underground chamber where bone-singers teach the Old Language to new generations.
places.description: A hidden location where Naela found herself five years hence, teaching the Old Language. This appears to be the safe haven of the bone-singer resistance.
places.notes: Naela chose to go there after her encounter in the Wailing Scar. It is the center of bone-singer resistance by winter.

places.name: Ironspine
places.atmosphere: A mountain range with ancient caves that have sheltered hoarders and seekers for centuries.
places.description: A mountain range containing caves where Sorrath has lived for three hundred years. The caves are part of the Iron Coast region.
places.notes: The Ironspine caves within this range are Sorrath's home and a place of pilgrimage for those seeking wisdom about the post-Ascension world.

places.name: Shadow-Archive
places.atmosphere: A refuge for those fleeing institutional control, accessible through liminal guides like Raen.
places.description: A counter-institutional archive and refuge mentioned as one of Raen's possible destinations. Also offered asylum to Niel Coldwater in exchange for her threshold-furnace, though she refused.
places.notes: Competes with the Dominion for control of powerful artifacts and testimony; represents an alternative archival system.

places.name: Abyssal Realms
places.atmosphere: Alien depths accessible through threshold-passages; a refuge for those lost in the fractured world.
places.description: Mentioned as one destination Raen can guide lost merchants to, accessed via threshold-crossing at the Wailing Scar.
places.notes: The nature and geography of the Abyssal Realms remain unexplained; Raen serves as a guide rather than inhabitant.

places.name: shallows
places.atmosphere: A treacherous maritime zone where currents shift and salvage crews circle endlessly.
places.description: The shallows are waters where Kress led competing salvage teams in circles for weeks, using knowledge of currents to stay ahead of both abyssal-dwellers and surface kingdom forces.
places.notes: Kress used her scouting skill to navigate these waters and piece together ledger testimony.

places.name: Greymist River
places.atmosphere: A boundary river wreathed in mist and the residual tremors of cosmic unmaking.
places.description: The Greymist River marks the boundary between the Sunken Marches and the Hearthlands. Elara ran a ferry-service across it for twenty years. Theron drowned in a boundary-rupture here, and his echo-self emerged from the mist.
places.notes: Site of boundary-ruptures caused by Luminous Ascension tremors; haunted by autonomous echo-selves.

places.name: catacomb's heart
places.atmosphere: The deepest chamber of ancient tombs, heavy with ritual power and the weight of cosmic working.
places.description: The innermost chamber of the sealed tombs where the Shard-Keeper cornered Tarion and he completed the final carving of the reversal-ritual.
places.notes: Site of Tarion's climactic sacrifice; location of the final reversal-ritual attempt.

places.name: Horizon's Wake
places.atmosphere: A vessel guided by empirical truth rather than dogma; the only merchant ship consistently achieving safe passage.
places.description: A merchant-galley commanded by Captain Vess, the Horizon's Wake became the sole successful trading vessel in its waters after Vess adopted Aldrin's cartographic observations in place of the Dominion's official charts. Its success stands in stark contrast to other merchant vessels that continue to run aground following outdated navigation.
places.notes: Its success is directly tied to the accuracy of Aldrin's charts. Other captains have demanded access to these charts, but Vess has steadfastly refused to sell them.

places.name: boundary-threshold
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Council of Depths (Chamber)
places.atmosphere: The seat of deepkin political power, where covenants are debated and binding transformations are witnessed.
places.description: The Chamber of the Council of Depths is where Lyssa descended to face her Echo in public debate. It is a place where surface and abyss delegates witness binding transformations, marking it as a site of deep political and ceremonial significance.
places.notes: This appears to be the governing seat of the deepkin realm. The presence of both surface and abyss delegates suggests the chamber hosts inter-realm negotiations.

places.name: Quarter
places.atmosphere: A modest urban neighborhood where craft and caution are valued; Thom's workshop sits at its edge.
places.description: An urban quarter or district where Thom Bronzelock now keeps his workshop and teaches children about the ethics of refusal. The location is at the Quarter's edge, suggesting marginality or separation from the main city proper.
places.notes: Post-Locksmith's Reckoning, Thom operates a teaching workshop here.

places.name: unmapped reaches
places.atmosphere: Territories untouched by the Ascension, preserving old forests, old roads, and ancient structures that predate reality-reshaping.
places.description: Places where the Ascension hasn't finished rewriting reality. These reaches still contain old forests, old roads, and unchanged structures. Lenna and her network actively map these territories.
places.notes: The existence of unmapped reaches suggests the Ascension's effects are incomplete or resistable. May contain pre-Ascension artifacts and knowledge.

places.name: Drowning-Keep
places.atmosphere: A legendary fortress that shifts like fever-dreams; intruders become lost in loops of space and consciousness; a place of existential danger.
places.description: The Drowning-Keep is a legendary ruin where the deepkin hid the Tidecaller's Key centuries ago. The Keep's interior shifts like fever-dreams, with corridors that loop backward. It is known not for its locks but for the way it 'forgets' intruders—two men entered before Corvalen and Kess, and neither returned whole. The Keep released Corvalen and Kess without question when they grasped the Key, suggesting the relic itself held authority over the space.
places.notes: The Drowning-Keep's nature as a shifting, memory-bending space suggests it may be more than a mere fortress—possibly a construct of deepkin magic or consciousness itself.

places.name: the distant sea
places.atmosphere: A far horizon of freedom and escape; the direction Valorix flew when she found peace.
places.description: The distant sea is referenced as where Valorix soared after reconciling with Commander Thrace. It represents freedom and peace beyond the Ironspine.
places.notes: Valorix's flight toward the distant sea symbolizes her escape from madness and loss into a state of peace.

places.name: Valdris
places.atmosphere: Half-mapped and now shattered by the Luminous Ascension, its geography no longer stable.
places.description: A continent half-mapped by Tharen the Cartographer before the Luminous Ascension shattered its geography. Rivers flow backward, cities exist in three places at once.
places.notes: The Luminous Ascension has made traditional mapmaking impossible.

places.name: Keshtir's glasswright quarter
places.atmosphere: A district of craftspeople and vaults, eerie in its stillness as the theft occurred, where glass-makers work and treasure is guarded.
places.description: A quarter of Keshtir containing vaults and the workshops of glass-makers. This is where Marin crouched to steal the tide-hook.
places.notes: The eerie stillness suggests the new stars have transformed even the mundane character of the quarter.

places.name: Greymist
places.tagline: Poisoned river. Rift of absence. Drowning.
places.atmosphere: A landscape of inverted physics where water has become absence and drowning is possible in a rift rather than liquid.
places.description: A river in the Sunken Marches poisoned by the Luminous Ascension's aftershock. Rather than flowing with water, the Greymist has become a rift opening where the river used to flow, pulling down everything with will and hunger.
places.notes: The Greymist is emblematic of post-Ascension environmental corruption; Korvin's mercy-walk into its rift suggests a willing dissolution into the transformed world.

places.name: western passes
places.atmosphere: High mountain passages where kingdoms meet and warfare determined dominion.
places.description: Mountain passes in the western reaches where Brennus Ironpeak held his fastness and conquered adjacent kingdoms through military force.
places.notes: Site of Brennus's most brutal campaigns, later reframed through the phantom-ledgers as deliberate cruelty rather than strategic necessity.

places.name: Treasurer's Quarter
places.atmosphere: A secure zone filled with ledger-magic and vault-wards that sing when touched by the wrong hands.
places.description: A district within Pelimor that houses the Sovereign's vault. Its security has been reinforced with new locks, wards, and ledger-magic that remembers and screams at familiar touches. Brennan works here as a scribe.
places.notes: The ledger-magic is described as alive and memory-bearing, a form of magical security that responds to identity. Suggests deep integration of magical governance into Pelimor's administration.

places.name: Stone Hall of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A formal chamber with marble floors where surface-deepkin diplomacy plays out under strict protocol.
places.description: The primary government hall of Pelimor, where the surface council meets to discuss the failing Tear. It has marble floors and strict protocols that are deliberately breached when Commander Kesh arrives in full deepkin armor, leaving wet prints as a statement.
places.notes: The epicenter of the covenant crisis and the stage for Kesh's visionary proposal. Breach of protocol suggests deepening tensions.

places.name: Catacombs Beneath Anthrim
places.atmosphere: Solemn, stone-echoing, charged with ancestral presence.
places.description: Underground burial chambers beneath Anthrim containing sacred tombs. Corvhen works here binding stolen bones into her mother's animated form. Site of her final mercy-killing ritual.
places.notes: Sacred burial site; location of Corvhen's seven-year bone-theft project; site of farewell song ritual; protected by resistance principles.

places.name: Distant Reef
places.atmosphere: A treacherous rocky reef where a burning ship has run aground, its crew drowning, its master sinking under the weight of ledgers.
places.description: A reef three days' sail northwest of Verdant Strand where Captain Vorn's flagship was driven during a storm. The Tidecaller's Key sang of this disaster to Sera. The reef claimed the merchant prince and his gold but allowed his daughter to survive, clinging to a spar.
places.notes: The reef is significant both as a disaster site and as a point of defiance for Sera—she sails toward fire (toward wreckage and ruin) despite her father's oath against doing so. The currents around the reef are described as pulling backward, suggesting supernatural or dangerous tidal action.

places.name: The Hearthlands
places.atmosphere: The political and economic center of the Dominion, where the Crown enforces glass quotas and auditors seal vaults.
places.description: The Hearthlands are the central region of Valdris and the seat of the Dominion's power. It is here that the Glass-Tax Collectors operate, seizing workshops to meet quotas for prophecy-vessels. The region demands heirloom glass be melted down to serve the Dominion's ledger-magic initiatives.
places.notes: The Hearthlands appear to be the administrative and spiritual center of the Dominion. The taxation of glass and the seizure of workshops suggest aggressive resource extraction to fuel post-Ascension reconstruction. The transparent vessels are tied to 'prophecy-ink,' suggesting oracular or predictive magical work.

places.name: clearing with suspended loom
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Saltmere Quarter
places.atmosphere: An affluent quarter where noble houses are targets for skilled thieves.
places.description: A district of Greyport where Marin the Copper-Handed has stolen from every noble house. Rich in targets, but Marin never stole from the poor until hired to rob the refugee shelter.
places.notes: Marin's primary hunting ground as a thief. The contrast between her thefts from nobles and her resistance to robbing refugees is central to her character arc.

places.name: Refugee Shelter
places.atmosphere: A sacred sanctuary where refugees sleep, where an old woman carves bone-prayers, and where the Widow's Compass and a ledger of lost names reside.
places.description: A shelter in or near Greyport that houses refugees from the Burned Expanse. Contains a vault with the Widow's Compass, a shrine made of refugee tokens, children sleeping in corners, an old woman carving bone-prayers, and a ledger listing the names of the lost.
places.notes: The sight of this shelter's sacredness causes Marin's moral awakening and rejection of the theft contract. The ledger of lost names and the bone-prayer shrine are central to understanding the compass's true value.

places.name: Rust Gate
places.atmosphere: A desperate haven at the border, now crowded with refugees, guarded by a single warden determined to protect the vulnerable from state reclamation.
places.description: A crumbling stone archway dividing the Ashen Reach from the civilized reaches, now a sanctuary for refugees fleeing the Remaking's wake. Koren the Sentinel has held it for three seasons against State orders to seal it, rationing grain and teaching survival skills to hundreds of displaced persons.
places.notes: Epicenter of the conflict between humanitarian mercy and state authority. Contains an ancient pike found in its foundation.

places.name: The Ashen Reach
places.atmosphere: A devastated landscape where the Remaking's wake has swallowed villages and fractured families across competing timelines.
places.description: A macro-region of Valdris from which the Rust Gate separates the Ashen Reach from the civilized reaches. Villages have been swallowed by the Remaking's catastrophic aftermath.
places.notes: A key threshold zone where the apocalyptic Remaking manifests materially.

places.name: Ashwater
places.atmosphere: A river near the Rust Gate, now a lifeline for hundreds of refugees learning to sustain themselves in exile.
places.description: A river near the Rust Gate where Koren teaches refugees to fish in order to sustain themselves while awaiting news of the fragmenting State.
places.notes: Symbol of basic survival and self-sufficiency in the post-Remaking world.

places.name: The Civilized Reaches
places.atmosphere: A more stable region separated from the devastation of the Ashen Reach by the Rust Gate.
places.description: The civilized reaches lie on the opposite side of the Rust Gate from the Ashen Reach and are more insulated from the Remaking's immediate effects, though threatened by refugee influx.
places.notes: Represents the dividing line between stability and catastrophe in the post-Remaking world.

places.name: mer-city
places.atmosphere: A place of crystalline spires humming with resonant frequencies that shatter a human mind.
places.description: An underwater city of crystal spires inhabited by merfolk, where the keeper of pearls enforces ancient laws against theft. The frequencies it emits are lethal to human consciousness.
places.notes: The merfolk keeper resides here; home to the stolen pearl's true owner.

places.name: coastal cities
places.atmosphere: Places bound by law against stealing merfolk pearls; centers of pearl-diving and maritime trade.
places.description: A collection of coastal settlements where pearl-diving is common and the first law forbids taking merfolk pearls without permission. Torven and Sera came from these cities.
places.notes: The first law of the coastal cities is binding and carries supernatural consequences.

places.name: sealed crypts
places.atmosphere: Places of absolute rest and sanctity where the dead are protected from disturbance.
places.description: Sealed burial chambers where the bones of the dead are preserved. The merchant prince attempted to hire Mael to steal a child's bones from these crypts, claiming research purposes.
places.notes: Home to a dead child whose bones spoke refusal through harmonic frequency.

places.name: the Eyrie
places.atmosphere: A high sanctuary where sacred beasts are tended and bonded guardians dwell in isolation.
places.description: The Eyrie is the home of Ashara and Valorix, a place where griffin-guards are stationed and where merchant captains can reach them with offers. It is elevated and isolated enough to serve as a fortress.
places.notes: The Eyrie's location and realm affiliation are not specified; it may be a neutral or independent sanctuary.

places.name: the Verdant Lands
places.atmosphere: A place of natural magic and druidic knowledge, lush and restorative.
places.description: The Verdant Lands are home to druids who have taught Ashara herbal remedies for pain-management and palliative care. They are referenced as a source of medicinal wisdom.
places.notes: The druids of the Verdant Lands possess knowledge of rare or sacred herbs used for easing suffering without hastening death.

places.name: Thornholt Fortress
places.atmosphere: A fortress of ultimate endurance, where archer and griffin held back impossible odds for forty days.
places.description: Thornholt Fortress is defended by Ashara the Archer and her griffin Valorix against siege, starvation, and sorcery. After the siege lifts and a warlord's curse breaks, the fortress stands empty as refugees scatter and guardians move on.
places.notes: Site of a legendary siege; now empty after the warlord's curse breaks.

places.name: Distant Sea
places.atmosphere: A vast maritime expanse where griffins depart to keep their own councils beyond mortal reach.
places.description: The distant sea is where Valorix flies at the end of her three decades with Ashara, seeking the councils of her kind.
places.notes: Griffin migration destination; may connect to sea-folk realms.

places.name: Archive Tower's Deepest Vault
places.atmosphere: Dust-laden silence, ancient silk, parchment older than empires.
places.description: The deepest level of the Archive Tower where junior auditors like Vel are assigned tedious filing work. It contains a hidden false-floor chamber concealing the Ninth Journal wrapped in silk older than the Dominion.
places.notes: The false floor suggests deliberate concealment; unclear who sealed it or for how long it has been hidden.

places.name: Shadow-Market Beneath Anthrim
places.atmosphere: Lit by collected shadows and stolen light, a place of high-stakes gambling and memory-waging.
places.description: An underground market network beneath Anthrim where merchants wager consciousness-fragments, memories, and stolen shadows. It operates outside visible Dominion authority and attracts figures like the Shade-Merchant Vex.
places.notes: The existence of multiple shadow-markets suggests a parallel economy and governance structure post-Luminous Ascension.

places.name: Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter
places.atmosphere: A refuge for refugees and bone-singers, hidden from Dominion scrutiny, where ancient songs and counter-testimony are preserved.
places.description: The Lampwick Quarter is located in the city of Keshtir and serves as a sanctuary for refugees and bone-singers. Niel Coldwater befriended bone-singers here and obtained the counter-testimony in the Elder Tongue that he etched into his double-glass.
places.notes: The quarter's existence and role in preserving forbidden knowledge makes it a crucial safe house for resistance to Dominion control.

places.name: The Burned Expanse
places.atmosphere: A blighted wasteland where refugees barter their identities for survival.
places.description: The Burned Expanse is a region affected by the Luminous Ascension where refugees are forced to sell memories and identities for bread. Karthax hunts consciousness-fragment debtors across this landscape.
places.notes: The region's state suggests ongoing humanitarian crisis tied to the Luminous Ascension and consciousness-fragment economy.

places.name: Pelimor Road
places.tagline: Ancient trading route. Seasonal vanishing. Treacherous passage.
places.atmosphere: A caravan road known for vanishing caravans and temporal anomalies.
places.description: An ancient trade route where a caravan took the road one autumn and vanished for a season, only to emerge at the Wailing Scar where reality folds.
places.notes: The road is linked to the Wailing Scar and appears to be a liminal crossing point where the Luminous Ascension's rewriting is incomplete.

places.name: the Expanse
places.tagline: Burned wasteland. Fiery destruction. Lost gardens.
places.atmosphere: A region scarred by fire, where gardens and homes have burned away.
places.description: A burned-over region where the widow's garden was destroyed by fire. Kelvan travels there after his compass points him east, discovering the widow grieving her lost garden.
places.notes: The Expanse appears to be a region affected by widespread fires, possibly tied to the Luminous Ascension's effects. It is three days' travel from where Kelvan points his compass.

places.name: the marketplace
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Greywater
places.atmosphere: The ship resonates with the sea's harmonic singing, its compass-rose spinning wrong.
places.description: A survey-ship commanded by Captain Merren, crewed by a mixed team. Its ribs are carved with bone-marks by Torvin to anchor it against the Sapphire Sea's singing.
places.notes: The Greywater is the vessel of discovery for the drowned city and the map of nine previous worlds.

places.name: Drowned City (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: A luminous underwater cathedral of coral and pearl, towers still gleaming with bioluminescence.
places.description: Not ruins but a living city found on the seventh night by Merren's crew. Its heart contains a map carved into living water itself, showing nine previous worlds all shattered identically and a tenth waiting to be written.
places.notes: The map within shows the cyclical destruction and recreation of worlds; its tenth iteration is still being written.

places.name: Dominion's Vault (beneath Keshtir)
places.atmosphere: A deep underground vault where each door poses a question: Who are you? What do you deserve? Can you live with what you steal?
places.description: A vault beneath Keshtir containing consciousness-fragments and protected by seventeen levels of question-wards and ledger-script locks. Its deepest guardian is the vault-keep, a being of stone and shadow.
places.notes: The fragments within remember whoever takes them; represents the Dominion's effort to commodify and store consciousness itself.

places.name: fae-wood
places.atmosphere: A realm of woven light and forgotten dreams, adjacent to Valdris but fundamentally other.
places.description: The fae realm adjacent to Valdris where fae-kind dwell. Lissaen's people live here, having lost the ability to dream. The boundary between fae-wood and Valdris thins at dusk.
places.notes: Fae-kind within have forgotten how to dream; Lissaen seeks to restore this through luminescent honey.

places.name: Citadel's heart-chamber
places.atmosphere: The central seat of authority within the citadel, where crucial negotiations and decisions are made.
places.description: The heart-chamber of the Archive Citadel where Warden Caelus summons the three factions' representatives to negotiate and prevent conflict that would become doctrine via phantom-ledgers.
places.notes: The location where the conflict between Dominion, Counter-Archive, and bone-singers reaches its crisis point.

places.name: Emerald Haven
places.atmosphere: Deep-water markets where memory-cargo is traded to those with consciousness-fragments to pay.
places.description: A deep-water port city serving as the destination for Orina's cargo. The name and reference to 'deep-water markets' suggest it is either a major trading hub or partially submerged, possibly with deepkin involvement.
places.notes: Destination for star-memory cargo. Likely a major nexus for post-Remaking trade in consciousness-fragments and magical goods.

places.name: Thornwick Tower
places.atmosphere: A three-hundred-foot tower of blood-colored stone under siege, its archers defiant against a thousand-year grudge.
places.description: A tower standing three hundred feet above the Wychflow valley, constructed of stones the color of old blood. Its archers are specifically targeted by Mogrul's siege, making it a focal point of ancient vengeance.
places.notes: Site of the siege and parley between Kestran and Mogrul. The tower's color and Thornwick's Legacy suggest deep ties to battlefield memory.

places.name: Wychflow Valley
places.atmosphere: A lush valley dominated by the shadow and siege of Thornwick Tower.
places.description: The valley beneath Thornwick Tower where the goblin siege unfolds. The tower rises three hundred feet above it, dominating the landscape.
places.notes: Theater of the siege against Kestran and the tower's archers.

places.name: workshop in Keshtir
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Regent's domain in Keshtir
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Hidden Archives of the Counter-Archive
places.tagline: Secrets preserved. Maps unmade. Knowledge guarded.
places.atmosphere: A place of hidden knowledge where maps and records that contradict official reality are preserved.
places.description: Within this archive, Tavin discovers a map of the eighth iteration drawn in ink that has not existed for ten thousand years—a map she does not remember creating, yet recognizes as her own work.
places.notes: Suggests the Counter-Archive preserves recursive or pre-Remaking knowledge. The existence of eighth-iteration maps implies at least eight distinct world-states.

places.name: Saltmere docks
places.atmosphere: Docks where the water remembers things it shouldn't, tides flow backward, and the very harbor has begun to sing in languages that predate human civilization.
places.description: The Saltmere docks are part of Greyport's harbor district. The water here has begun to remember and behave unnaturally since the Remaking, with backward-flowing tides and pre-human songs rising from the depths.
places.notes: Kendra sits here with the stolen amber flask, watching the water remember things. The singing and backward tides suggest uncontrolled magical bleed-through from the Remaking.

places.name: The boundary-waters
places.atmosphere: Waters sacred to the deepkin that are now boiling and destabilized, a sign of their dying god and civilization in crisis.
places.description: The boundary between the deepkin realms and the surface, traditionally a place of covenant. The waters now boil as a consequence of the Deepkin Captain's Jewel being stolen, weakening the Tear of the Drowned God.
places.notes: A liminal zone where the deepkin's crisis manifests; Captain Merith passes through it to reach Nara.

places.name: Greyport Lantern Market
places.atmosphere: A bustling market district where merchants, soldiers, auditors, and people burdened by loss converge to trade and seek answers.
places.description: A marketplace in Greyport where Vess set up her cart on 2026-05-31. It attracts a diverse clientele: the grief-stricken, the disillusioned, the desperate, and the official auditors of some authority.
places.notes: The primary setting of the story. Its architecture, scale, and governing authority are not detailed.

places.name: Coastal cave
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Thael's Workshop
places.atmosphere: A secret sanctuary where bone-carving and Elder Tongue inscription continue in defiance of law.
places.description: Thael's workshop in the Lampwick Quarter is where swan-bones are carved with Elder Tongue syllables. It serves as a secret meeting place for refugees seeking identity-restoration and for old apprentices of bone-singing.
places.notes: The workshop is a site of resistance against Dominion auditor enforcement. Its discovery would mean Thael's arrest.

places.name: Sorresh's Lair
places.atmosphere: Three cathedral-caverns where gold has been accumulated for centuries, now filled with the sound of melting and carving as a dragon transforms greed into grace.
places.description: Sorresh's lair consists of three caverns in the Ironspine Mountains, each a cathedral of accumulated gold. The deepest cavern holds the oldest coins—minted before the last star-fall—which the dragon melted and inscribed with Elder Tongue syllables for distribution to refugees.
places.notes: The transformation of this space from a place of hoarding to one of distribution marks a major post-Remaking shift in dragon behavior and council relations.

places.name: Coral Chamber
places.atmosphere: Solemn, ancient, resonant with tide-song; a place where the Merfolk Keeper presides over oaths and their consequences.
places.description: A chamber within the Sapphire Deep where merfolk oaths are sworn and witnessed. Nerida meets Vess here, and Vess kneels before the Merfolk Keeper to offer her new oath in place of her family's ancient debt.
places.notes: The site of the climactic oath-swearing. Its architecture and exact nature are mysterious but clearly sacred to merfolk culture.

places.name: Old Market Quarter of Keshtir
places.atmosphere: Weathered, secretive, a place where unofficial transactions and dangerous knowledge circulate.
places.description: A district in Keshtir where Liv, the young scribe, approached Gareth the locksmith with phantom-ledgers and her dangerous question about unlocking memory.
places.notes: Meeting place for Gareth and Liv; possible hub for anti-Dominion activity.

places.name: Keshtir's administrative heart
places.atmosphere: Bureaucratic, secretive, fortress-like in security.
places.description: The administrative center of Keshtir, located seven floors underground. Contains a vault protected by a three-person lock requiring simultaneous opening—designed not to protect gold but to guard records of the previous world before the Remaking, including consciousness-fragment origins and pre-Remaking currency systems.
places.notes: Target of Gareth and Liv's investigation. Records are key to proving State's monopoly on truth is breaking.

places.name: Deepest cavern
places.atmosphere: Isolated, heavy with grief, suffused with ancient consciousness.
places.description: The deepest cavern within the Ironspine Mountains, where Vrekka sealed herself after three hundred years of hoarding. Its solitude was so profound it drew the bone-singers, particularly Mesek.
places.notes: Convergence point for Vrekka and Mesek. Symbolic of wyvern isolation and awakening.

places.name: boundary-pool
places.atmosphere: Still, eerie, and impossibly reflective—showing not just what is, but what might have been.
places.description: A pool at the boundary-waters where reflections speak with independent voices. Merith saw her own reflection speaking in a voice not quite her own, claiming to be a consciousness-fragment from an alternate timeline where she accepted Kalith's binding.
places.notes: Appears to be a window into alternate timelines or fragment-echoes rather than a purely physical location.

places.name: platform of living coral
places.atmosphere: Alive, responsive, and somewhat unsettling in its organic nature.
places.description: Merith stood on this platform when she first encountered her reflection in the boundary-pool. It is part of the deepkin infrastructure and suggests they have cultivated coral structures.
places.notes: Suggests deepkin biomancy or deep-sea agriculture.

places.name: husband's unmapped grave
places.atmosphere: A place of grief, growth, and transformation.
places.description: Sorenna's husband was lost defending the pre-human boundary. His grave is unmapped and located in the Verdant Lands. Grain grew through it naturally, which Sorenna harvested to make bread for the wanderer.
places.notes: The grave appears to have become a source of unusual growth and transformation, possibly due to the Remaking or consciousness-fragment proliferation.

places.name: Horizon's lowest hold
places.atmosphere: Dark, confined, and occupied by a stolen consciousness that whispers warnings and demands.
places.description: The lowest hold of the Horizon's Wake contains a sealed flask of bioluminescent ink in which Nerys's stolen shadow dwells. The shadow has been whispering to Vessa for three years, warning of the Remaking and the return of debts.
places.notes: A prison for a consciousness-fragment or stolen soul. Its contents are unstable and dangerous.

places.name: coral archive
places.atmosphere: A deepkin repository of knowledge, guarded but apparently vulnerable to skilled thieves.
places.description: Ten years before the Remaking, Captain Vessa raided a coral archive and stole Captain Nerys's shadow during a midnight raid. The archive suggests the deepkin maintain repositories of valuable or dangerous objects.
places.notes: The archive's exact location is unknown, but it appears to have been in deepkin territory.

places.name: stone archives
places.atmosphere: Academic and austere; a repository of knowledge before the world changed.
places.description: A scholarly archive where Merrick was trained in old languages prior to the Remaking. It served as a center of linguistic and historical study.
places.notes: Merrick's training ground; implies a pre-Remaking institutional knowledge structure.

places.name: Thornveil Forest
places.atmosphere: A living forest grown aware, with roots as steps and branches as roof, now conscious and hungering but bound by oath.
places.description: The Thornveil Forest has sheltered Elarra for forty winters, its trees growing thicker around her hermitage. After the Luminous Ascension, the forest's dormant awareness awakened and it hungered to devour the Burned Expanse and remake it as living wood. Elarra's new compact pledges the forest as shelter for all refugees.
places.notes: The forest's awakening and hunger define the central tension of The Druid's Debt; Elarra's compact may be tested by the forest's nature and the refugee family's arrival.

places.name: Deep Forges
places.atmosphere: The ancient forges of the dwarven holds, where Torven Ironfoot is known for binding memory into metal and prophecy into steel.
places.description: The deep forges are the home of Torven Ironfoot and the seat of his craft. It is here that he forged the three warking-spears for the old kingdom and where he is now demanded to forge an unbreakable crown.
places.notes: The deep forges are the spiritual and practical center of Torven's power; his refusal to pick up the fallen crown signals his reluctance to leave this place or compromise his craft.

places.name: Royal Pyre
places.atmosphere: The ceremonial pyre where the last king's corpse burned, his crown tumbling from his dead hand into ash.
places.description: The pyre where the old king of Kethral was cremated, marking the end of direct monarchy. The Crown of Kethral fell from the dead king's hand into the ash, triggering the council of thane-lords to seek its reforging.
places.notes: The image of the crown falling from a corpse's hand is potent and symbolic; it triggers the events of The Relic-Smith's Inheritance.

places.name: Tidecall
places.atmosphere: A command vessel steeped in the history of deepkin-surface diplomacy.
places.description: The ship commanded by Original Nerys, symbolic of her role as architect of the deepkin-surface covenant.
places.notes: Vessel tied to the original Nerys's identity and authority.

places.name: Thornveil Forest hermitage
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Eastern peaks
places.atmosphere: Once a seat of dragon wealth and hoard-magic, now stripped to dust by The Remaking, echoing with emptiness.
places.description: Kolrith ruled these peaks for nine centuries. Her treasure-vaults sang with gold, now turned to rust and dust. The Remaking collapsed her empire entirely.
places.notes: Likely part of Ashen Reach or Shattered West region; Kolrith's vault location; Mera emerged here.

places.name: Kolrith's vault (former); Verdant Strand (destination)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: abyss-boundary waters
places.atmosphere: The liminal waters between the surface realm and the deepkin abyss, where ritual bonds are maintained.
places.description: The abyss-boundary waters are liminal depths where Vessa sailed once per moon-cycle to sing to the Drowned King and maintain her ritual bond. They form the boundary between the surface world and the deepkin realm.
places.notes: These waters are the site of the binding ritual and potential sacrifice; the Tear of the Drowned God is fraying here, suggesting imminent barrier failure.

places.name: drowned cities
places.atmosphere: Sunken cities of ancient majesty, now home to grief-stricken deepkin and the Drowned King.
places.description: The drowned cities are ancient submerged settlements where the Drowned King dwells. Vessa visited them monthly to sing and teach the King about surface life and her lost husbands.
places.notes: These cities represent the deep past and deepkin heritage; their exact location and accessibility are unknown.

places.name: Ashenvale
places.atmosphere: A mountain village shadowed by ancient curse for decades, waiting for deliverance.
places.description: Ashenvale is a mountain village circled by a wyvern bound to feed on innocents every seventh moon. The village's newest warden, Kess, studied the curse instead of building walls, and through listening and song broke the curse that had plagued the village for decades.
places.notes: Formerly cursed; freed by Kess; represents triumph of understanding over defense; wyvern journal used to free seventeen other cursed creatures.

places.name: Dominion's harbor vault
places.atmosphere: A submerged, pressure-locked vault guarded by phantoms and deadly currents, where invaluable relics rest.
places.description: A vault seven fathoms below Greyport where the Tear of the Ashglass is kept, guarded by phantoms and pressure-currents that break bones.
places.notes: Defended against theft; unresolved: whether Kendra successfully stole the Tear.

places.name: drowned archive
places.atmosphere: A sunken repository of knowledge and treasures, accessible only to those who can survive the depths.
places.description: A submerged archive near Greyport where Kendra was caught stealing pearls by Captain Merith.
places.notes: Contains pearls and other treasures; guarded by or associated with merfolk.

places.name: Thornhearth
places.atmosphere: A humble pastoral village where fear and wonder coexist in the presence of dragonkind.
places.description: A village with livestock, inhabited by ordinary folk and children who grew up knowing mercy could flow from terrible strength. It receives visits from the Wyvern each winter, who leaves fresh game at its edge.
places.notes: Thornhearth serves as the emotional heart of the story and the Wyvern's anchor to its redemptive arc; the children's understanding of mercy suggests the village is transformed by this encounter.

places.name: Kaethmir
places.atmosphere: A city of governance and order, where magistrates and merchants once held absolute authority, now struggling under the weight of the Shattering.
places.description: Kaethmir is home to Gorven's workshop, situated beneath its administrative heart. It appears to be a major administrative center with a history of locksmithing and governmental authority.
places.notes: The Regent rules from or through Kaethmir. The presence of an 'administrative heart' suggests formal governance structures. The Warden's mission originates here.

places.name: Kaethmir's Administrative Heart
places.atmosphere: The seat of governmental authority, where laws are made and the machinery of rule operates.
places.description: Located in Kaethmir, this is the administrative center where the Regent's authority emanates. Gorven's workshop is situated beneath it, suggesting the locksmith's work is intimately connected to governmental security.
places.notes: The administrative structure here has been destabilized by the Shattering, making traditional security measures like Gorven's locks increasingly vital.

places.name: Ravine Where Three Winds Met
places.atmosphere: A place of supernatural stillness where doubt crystallizes into physical paralysis, where a consciousness-eating spirit lairs.
places.description: A specific ravine within the Steppe Marches where Kess encounters the beast-spirit circling a frozen herd of mountain deer. The convergence of three winds seems to amplify the spirit's power and create a zone of uncertainty.
places.notes: The beast-spirit fed on certainty and had rendered the deer unable to decide whether to flee. Kess's victory here is a localized triumph against uncertainty itself.

places.name: Ironspine lair
places.atmosphere: A vast accumulation chamber where consciousness-shards are stored in crystalline vaults, filled with whispers and dreams.
places.description: Sorrath's lair within the Ironspine region, containing crystalline vaults that hold consciousness-shards. The shards are not inert but active—they whisper, dream, and ache to be whole, making the space itself sentient and emotionally charged.
places.notes: The vaults are opened when Sorrath understands the personhood within the shards.

places.name: Glass-makers' Furnace-house
places.atmosphere: A furnace-house where glass sings at frequencies inaudible to most, containing a vault beneath the kilns.
places.description: A glass-forging workshop in the Lampwick Quarter with kilns and a vault beneath them. The vault contains weapon-glass that Torvin must steal. Glass in this furnace-house responds to bone-flute frequencies and can be made to sing.
places.notes: Mestra has guarded this vault for forty years. The glass itself is magically responsive to harmonic frequencies. Torvin uses this property to overcome the guardian without violence.

places.name: Wind-Scoured Ridge
places.atmosphere: A high, bare ridge where stone glows faintly with pre-human presence, marking the boundary between the known and the forgotten.
places.description: A specific location within the Steppe Marches where Messa corners the snow-hart. The stone here glows faintly, indicating pre-human ruins nearby. It is a place of threshold and recognition.
places.notes: Gateway to pre-human ruins; site of Messa's pivotal moral choice.

places.name: Hoard's Edge
places.atmosphere: The boundary of Malthor's vast hoard, where gold has become ash and meaning has become negotiable, witnessed by a child who speaks of hope.
places.description: The outer limit of Malthor's accumulation where she sits sifting through coins made worthless by the Remaking. This is where her encounter with the refugee child occurs.
places.notes: Liminal space between Malthor's old life and her possible new purpose; site of philosophical transformation.

places.name: Verdant Strand waters
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: harbor master's vault
places.atmosphere: A vault where time moves backward, ledgers write themselves, and the vault's heart breathes.
places.description: A vault beneath Crimport, seven fathoms down, containing three things: the Crown of Karesh, a ledger written in blood-ink, and Sorenna's path to redemption. It is surrounded by living coral chambers, guarded by those who have forgotten their names. The vault's lock sings when touched, and its heart breathes.
places.notes: Time moves backward inside; ledgers write themselves; the vault's heart is breathing and may be sentient.

places.name: waystation north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A quiet, isolated shelter where maps and fragments are studied.
places.description: A waystation north of Pelimor where Henryk the blinded cartographer has retreated. Tavin the Rebel-Cartographer found him here and offered him a new purpose. Stone tables are used for spreading pre-Remaking chart fragments.
places.notes: Serves as a center of heretical cartographic knowledge; fragments of pre-Remaking charts are stored and studied here.

places.name: eastern reef-walls
places.atmosphere: Polished coral walls maintained with ancient dedication and memory-songs.
places.description: The eastern reef-walls of the Sapphire Sea are guarded by Deepkin captains like Maren, who spend winters polishing the coral fortifications and teaching the young the names of currents that remembered drowning lovers.
places.notes: These reef-walls are sacred spaces of memory and maintenance. Their boundary can be restored through resonance with starlight-glass and the original tongue.

places.name: reef's edge
places.atmosphere: A liminal space where the wounded can surface and the boundary can be reinforced.
places.description: The reef's edge is where Corvin surfaces, wounded and gasping from his shipwreck. Here Maren holds the vial of starlight-glass to the water, and the reef answers with a half-remembered, half-new song.
places.notes: This location becomes the site of the Tear's restoration through harmonic resonance.

places.name: tavern that no longer existed
places.atmosphere: A place now erased by time or the Remaking, remembered only by those who shared it.
places.description: A tavern where Maren and Corvin shared wine a decade ago. It no longer exists, erased either by time or by the Remaking's alteration of the world.
places.notes: Symbolizes the loss and rewriting caused by the Luminous Ascension and the Remaking.

places.name: mountain workshop
places.atmosphere: A place where master craftsmanship endures despite the Remaking's damage.
places.description: Korgin Ironfoot's mountain workshop where legendary blades have been forged for centuries. It becomes a gathering place where surface-dwellers and deep-hold dwarves work together on the lock commission.
places.notes: Historic site of cooperation between surface and deep realms. Korgin's loss of truth-song suggests the workshop may be struggling with quality.

places.name: Sorenna's table
places.atmosphere: A humble domestic space where phantom-documents and blank ledgers were spread for Sorenna to read and reckon with her curse.
places.description: The location where Ennor met Sorenna and where she kept her phantom-ledger. It became the site of her winter-long act of completing her own curse.
places.notes: Symbolic center of Sorenna's redemption arc.

places.name: The Pre-human Boundary
places.atmosphere: A sacred, sealed threshold between surface and deep worlds, guarded by boundary-keepers and resonant with pre-human power.
places.description: A boundary sealed by covenant between deepkin and surface-dwellers, guarded by boundary-keepers who refuse entry to surface-dwellers. It is the destination where Kolten's memory-stone must be sung into transcendence. The boundary is failing and the Deepkin-Surface treaty fragmenting.
places.notes: Sealed by covenant. Boundary-keepers guard it. The treaty is fragmenting. Yareth carries Kolten's stone through it, and the keepers recognize the song and yield.

places.name: Vethrim's night markets
places.atmosphere: Black-market stalls and whispered deals in darkness, where stolen goods and forbidden knowledge trade hands.
places.description: The night markets of Vethrim are where shadow-merchants like Vex operate, trading in stolen fragments, forbidden lore, and the goods of the desperate. It is a hub for refugees and the displaced.
places.notes: Vex's primary base. Hub for refugee scribes. Likely under Dominion or Obsidian Tower surveillance.

places.name: Deepkin Tidecall
places.atmosphere: A place of governance and diplomatic assembly, where deepkin commanders convene to negotiate the fate of their realm.
places.description: The seat of the Deepkin Tidecall, a governmental and diplomatic institution governing the deepkin. Commander Merith operates from here.
places.notes: Central location of the Five-Way Covenant negotiations and the schism between Merith and her Echo.

places.name: refugee smuggler crew vessel
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Anthrim's Deepest Catacombs
places.atmosphere: Harmonic and haunting; bones sing in frequencies that trigger memories of other worlds and recursive selves.
places.description: The deepest catacombs of Anthrim contain bone-reliquaries carved by bone-singers to store Elder Tongue syllables. The bones within are awake and sing in harmonic frequencies. Corvhen observed eight previous worlds and saw nine versions of herself as she walked through. A vault with an altar of pre-human crystal lies at the deepest point, where the Primal Shard rests.
places.notes: The Remaking opened all locks. The bones sing in frequencies that rewrite memory.

places.name: Harbor (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: A working waterfront bound by duty and Dominion law, where old currents remember pre-Remaking times and sealed vaults hold contraband.
places.description: A harbor where water remembers old currents and sealed vaults beneath the docks hold pre-Remaking contraband. Merin administers it by reviewing water-charts and salvage permits. Merin and Corvin fled into the tide toward an unnamed coastline where reality still belonged to no one.
places.notes: Tides and currents seem to have memory here. Pre-Remaking contraband survives in sealed vaults. The unnamed coastline beyond it exists in narrative limbo—'reality still belonged to no one.'

places.name: Waystation (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: A junction in a Remaking-shattered world where three different Dominion ledgers describe three different realities, all equally true and equally false.
places.description: A waystation where Kelvan's caravan became stranded. It is a place where three different Dominion ledgers describe three different routes forward and three different versions of Crimport's existence. Multiple factions—Counter-Archive, wyvern-lords, bone-singers—converge here seeking Kelvan's maps. Evening dust and the appearance of Hadrin's shade suggest liminal geography.
places.notes: The fact that three ledgers describe three realities suggests the Dominion's cartographic authority has fractured post-Remaking. Hadrin's appearance here raises questions about whether certain places exist in multiple versions simultaneously.

places.name: Holding Cell (Archive Tower)
places.atmosphere: A confined space where Gorven's autonomous reflection materialized to speak of freedom and rebellion.
places.description: A holding cell within the Archive Tower where Gorven was imprisoned after refusing to open his lock. Mirror-Gorven materialized here to convince him to choose autonomous freedom.
places.notes: The fact that Mirror-Gorven could materialize in a Dominion holding cell suggests either the lock's power transcends confinement, or the cell itself is not as secure as intended.

places.name: Unnamed Coastline
places.atmosphere: A shore where reality itself has not yet been claimed or rewritten. The boundary between known and unknown, Dominion and freedom.
places.description: A coastline lying beyond Merin and Corvin's harbor, where reality still belonged to no one. They walked into the tide toward this place, fleeing Dominion claim and seeking freedom in unmapped waters.
places.notes: The fact that reality 'still belonged to no one' suggests this coastline exists in a liminal space outside Dominion cartography and control. It may be a refuge for fugitives and those who reject the Remaking's new order.

places.name: Holding cell
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ironspine foothills
places.atmosphere: A refuge where bone-singers teach the Elder Tongue to displaced refugees among mountain foothills.
places.description: Foothills of the Ironspine mountains, where Naela teaches the Elder Tongue to refugees post-Ascension.
places.notes: Host to bone-singer Naela's teaching mission. Suggests the Ironspine region has become a refuge zone for displaced peoples.

places.name: Thom Bronzelock's locksmith shop
places.atmosphere: A locksmith's workshop housing legendary security and hidden vaults, including a secret third vault.
places.description: Thom Bronzelock's shop in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter. It contains at least three vaults, the deepest believed secret, holding a key made of meteoric iron and bone-resonance.
places.notes: Messa plans to steal the counter-ledger hidden in the third vault to place in Corvin's vault. The key may be essential to this theft or may protect the ledger itself.

places.name: Corvin's vault
places.atmosphere: A secure vault where Messa plans to hide a counter-ledger as documentary proof of the glass-craft's legitimacy.
places.description: Master Corvin's vault in Keshtir, where Messa intends to place a stolen counter-ledger to create a false record of the quarter's glass-craft as legitimate and accounted-for.
places.notes: Messa's forgery plan depends on convincing Dominion auditors that the quarter's craft is documented and therefore legal. This suggests ledger-magic requires authentic-seeming provenance.

places.name: Deep Forges beneath the Ironspine
places.atmosphere: A place of intense heat and harmonic resonance, where metal sings and flames remember starlight.
places.description: The deep forges beneath the Ironspine are the domain of Torven Ironfoot, master smith. At their heart lies a furnace whose flames burn hotter than starlight and sing in harmony with ancient frequencies such as those of the Primal Shard.
places.notes: The deepest furnace may be a place of power or binding. Its ability to harmonize with the Primal Shard suggests cosmological significance.

places.name: Crimport's shadow-market
places.atmosphere: A clandestine marketplace where deals for world-changing artifacts are struck in whispered tones and black silk.
places.description: The shadow-market of Crimport is where Kessara meets Captain Meren to negotiate for the boundary anchor. It is a place of illicit trade and high-stakes negotiation, where the price is not always gold.
places.notes: The shadow-market's existence suggests Crimport has a robust criminal or underground economy dealing in relics and forbidden goods.

places.name: Keshtir's merchant quarter
places.atmosphere: Mercantile district where bone-carving workshops once thrived, now under auditor surveillance.
places.description: A merchant quarter within Keshtir where bone-carving workshops operate. Kessa's family maintains their bone-carving trade in this district, with the Deepvault hidden beneath their workshop.
places.notes: Site of Dominion enforcement; contains contraband consciousness-fragments.

places.name: Vael Ocean
places.atmosphere: Open waters; stars reflected in waves that have not yet learned to forget.
places.description: A vast ocean west of the Iron Coast toward which Torvin and Messa sail, seeking rumored lands beyond. Waters guided by a compass that sang and currents that remembered old courses.
places.notes: Represents escape and the unknown; seat of pre-Ascension cloth and ancient navigational knowledge.

places.name: Dominion work-camp
places.atmosphere: Oppressive labor under armed guard; furnaces burning weapon-glass.
places.description: A Dominion-controlled work-camp built on coastal cliffs where Messa was imprisoned to forge weapon-glass under armed guard. Torvin sailed there to rescue her.
places.notes: Site of Torvin and Messa's reunion and escape.

places.name: Ironspine caverns
places.atmosphere: The air is thick with age and the weight of centuries of accumulated treasure.
places.description: The dwelling-place of Skarath for two hundred years, containing a mountain of gold coins, melted goblets, and treasures stolen or gifted. The deepest vault holds the ancient iron sword, buried beneath worthless coins.
places.notes: The Remaking caused the hoard to vanish; Skarath's response was to descend on Thornhearth. The vault structure and what remains is key to understanding Skarath's grief.

places.name: Dominion vault
places.atmosphere: Seven floors beneath Keshtir's heart; gaslit halls lead past the threshold into chambers where auditors catalog stolen consciousness like cordwood.
places.description: A vast underground archive where the Dominion stores consciousness-fragments in pearl-white form, suspended in glass cases filled with brine. The fragments sing faintly—a frequency detectable to those trained to hear it. Missa penetrated this vault by singing the old merchant-songs, causing the cases to crack and releasing the fragments.
places.notes: The vault is a symbol of Dominion oppression and control over individual memory/identity. Its destruction or compromise is a significant act of resistance.

places.name: summit
places.atmosphere: A high place where the natural and magical converge, where chosen humans sit transformed under an impossible sky.
places.description: The peak where Kael found the meteoric spear buried in old snow and where eight Snow-Harts gather around the transformed humans they have chosen. The sky here reflects celestial patterns that do not exist in the normal world.
places.notes: The location appears to be a convergence point for the Snow-Hart's transformations. The impossible sky and the gathering of eight harts suggest a ritual or sacred site.

places.name: pearl-vaults
places.atmosphere: A heavily guarded underwater vault where covenant-pearls are stored as the collateral of surface-world debt to the merfolk.
places.description: The main repository of covenant-pearls guarded by Captain Merith. Seventeen pearls were recently stolen by refugees from the Burned Expanse seeking passage to safety. The vault's heart contains living coral chambers where the Keeper-of-Pearls resides.
places.notes: The theft of seventeen pearls marks a significant breach and has prompted Merith to question the nature of the covenant itself. A new ledger system may be in development.

places.name: vault's heart
places.atmosphere: The deepest sanctum of the vault system, where the oldest and most sacred pearl rests in the coils of an ancient guardian.
places.description: The innermost chamber of the pearl-vaults, where the Keeper-of-Pearls dwells coiled around the first pearl ever gathered. The chamber is constructed from living coral and radiates an aura of primordial antiquity.
places.notes: This chamber appears to be a spiritual or magical nexus, not merely a storage location. It is where Captain Merith goes to seek counsel on matters of law and mercy.

places.name: Verdant Wake
places.atmosphere: A merchant vessel with blank charts, sailing between the Iron Coast and the abyssal depths, carrying impossible bargains and fatal hope.
places.description: A merchant vessel captained by Vessa, bearing charts that showed only blank ocean. She sailed from the Iron Coast carrying the last vial of pre-Ascension starlight, and met Toriel aboard with news of the Deepkin Council's vote.
places.notes: Represents a bridge between deepkin and surface realms; carries the universe's last pre-Ascension starlight.

places.name: Keshtir market
places.atmosphere: A place of commerce and hidden dealings, where merchants and thieves converge, and sealed envelopes change hands.
places.description: The market district of Keshtir where Gorven meets Mara at a spice-seller's counter to transfer the sealed envelope containing the harbourmaster vault job.
places.notes: Spice-sellers operate here; implies a major trade hub.

places.name: Dominion's harbourmaster vault
places.atmosphere: An impenetrable stronghold with three doors whose locking sequence is known only through hidden inscriptions.
places.description: A vault controlled by the Dominion's harbourmaster that contains records and treasures. It is protected by three doors with mechanisms mapped by Elder Tongue syllables inscribed inside Mara's copper ring.
places.notes: The target of the heist; locking mechanisms are archaic and complex.

places.name: furnace-house
places.atmosphere: A place of heat, labor, and buried memory, where molten glass sings with the voices of three centuries of craft.
places.description: A workshop in the Lampwick Quarter containing the kiln-heart furnace where Messa works. The furnace can produce glass that remembers—infused with the consciousness and skill of her grandmother, Master Corvin, and generations of prior work.
places.notes: The furnace itself may be enchanted or consciousness-bearing; it produced a defiant glass blade infused with collective craft-memory.

places.name: Ironspine cavern
places.atmosphere: A vast underground chamber choked with three hundred years of accumulated gold, now worthless dust.
places.description: Sorrath's lair, a massive cavern in the Ironspine mountains containing her hoard of three hundred years' gold, rendered valueless by the Remaking. Beneath the gold lie ancient artifacts, weapons, texts, and maps predating the economy of coin.
places.notes: Sorrath gathers the Wyvern-Council here to reveal the true treasures beneath the worthless gold.

places.name: boundary-platform
places.atmosphere: A shelf of living coral suspended between two worlds, humming with barely contained discord.
places.description: A boundary-platform of living coral where surface and abyss meet in eternal truce. The Tear of the Drowned God anchors this liminal space, but the anchor is failing.
places.notes: The platform is the nexus of the boundary covenant. Its collapse will trigger either the old blood sacrifice or a new five-part covenant.

places.name: foothills
places.atmosphere: Remote mountainside terrain where Torven hid caches of arms and resources for fleeing refugees.
places.description: The foothills near Deep Forges conceal three hidden caches that Torven Ironfoot buried and documented in his ledger. These caches contain weapons and supplies for refugees.
places.notes: Locations were mapped in Torven's blood-ink ledger, now destroyed. Unresolved: whether any caches remain undiscovered.

places.name: border-keeps
places.atmosphere: Remote border fortifications where an old dwarf-smith has chosen to live in isolation, watching boundaries.
places.description: The border-keeps are fortified outposts where Korgin is said to be hermiting. They are approximately ten days' journey from Deep Forges. They form the margin of some territorial boundary.
places.notes: Torven entrusted Korgin with knowledge of 'what the metal remembers,' suggesting this location guards significant craft secrets or relics.

places.name: The Sapphire Deep
places.atmosphere: A liminal space where surface and abyss meet, holding its breath as a seal begins to fracture.
places.description: The Sapphire Deep holds living coral platforms where reef-keepers mediate between surface and abyssal realms. It is a threshold-space where the tidal covenants are sung and consciousness-fragments are offered as payment for seal maintenance.
places.notes: The seal is fracturing within three days. This location is the epicenter of a crisis threatening both surface and deepkin civilizations.

places.name: Living Coral Platform
places.atmosphere: The platform weeps under the weight of crisis, its coral sensitive to the frequencies of divided deepkin argument.
places.description: A living coral structure in the Sapphire Deep where surface and abyss meet. This is where Merril the Reef-Keeper stands to mediate the tidal covenants.
places.notes: The coral's response to deepkin frequency-argument suggests it is conscious or semi-conscious.

places.name: The Steppe Marches
places.atmosphere: Windswept plains where beasts speak in fragments of old language and the Remaking has transformed shepherding into communion with consciousness-bearing flocks.
places.description: The Steppe Marches stretch across Valdris's northern reaches. Kessara has tended flocks here for twenty winters, singing to beasts that no longer hear human voices as they once did. The sheep shimmer with consciousness-dust—remnants of the Remaking.
places.notes: Hub of Story 1. The Remaking has transformed this region into a place where consciousness bleeds across species. Warlord-councils administer justice and inheritance here.

places.name: mountain hold
places.atmosphere: A fortress ruled by warlord-council, where oaths are enforced and neighbors circle like predators.
places.description: A mountain hold ruled by a warlord-council. Kessara's father's corpse rests here, still clutching Truthseeker. The neighboring warlords watch the inheritance with hard eyes, circling for advantage.
places.notes: Site of oath-enforcement and political succession crisis. Central to Story 1's climax.

places.name: Siege of Grimmholt
places.atmosphere: A siege whose glory or horror has faded, but whose weapons—like Truthseeker—endure.
places.description: A siege from Kessara's father's past, when he carried Truthseeker to battle. The blade never missed a mark, making its wielder's oath-breaking all the more catastrophic.
places.notes: Historical event that contextualizes Truthseeker's origin and power. Exact location and date unknown.

places.name: the wasteland
places.atmosphere: A desolate, post-cataclysm landscape where refugees wander half-starved and fragments of consciousness lie scattered.
places.description: The devastated lands where Kessa's family was separated during the Remaking, with consciousness-fragments of her mother, brother, and father scattered across it. Auditors have declared consciousness-fragments to be currency rather than sacred family bonds.
places.notes: A zone of profound loss where the Remaking's effects are still being reckoned with by survivors like Kessa.

places.name: Pelimor's Merchant Quarter
places.atmosphere: A district where commerce continues despite Dominion pressure, and where consciousness-fragments and other forbidden goods may be traded.
places.description: The Merchant Quarter of Pelimor is where Corvin arrives with his glass vials containing consciousness-fragments. It serves as a nexus of trade and black-market exchange.
places.notes: This district appears to operate semi-independently from formal Dominion control.

places.name: crystalline vault
places.atmosphere: A vault where an ancient hoarder keeps three centuries of accumulated wealth and precious things, now rendered valueless by the Remaking.
places.description: Skarath's crystalline vault contains mountains of gold, pearls, captured shadows, and bottled pre-Remaking water. It is a monument to three centuries of hoarding made suddenly obsolete.
places.notes: The vault is a symbol of how the Remaking has inverted the value of material wealth.

places.name: Tomb sealed by ancient blood-oaths
places.atmosphere: A sealed crypt bound by ancient magic and blood-oaths, impenetrable to most thieves.
places.description: A sealed tomb containing the Crown of Karesh, protected by ancient blood-oaths. It defeated Harrow twenty years ago, and he barely escaped with his life. Gareth intends to breach it using his own methods and skills.
places.notes: Location unknown. May be connected to the Counter-Archive or other institutions.

places.name: Reef-stone
places.atmosphere: A solid anchor point at the boundary-waters, worn smooth by deepkin hands and timeflow.
places.description: The reef-stone where Original Kalith gripped as she confronted her echo-self and made the decision to dive into the abyss.
places.notes: Symbolic anchor for covenant deliberation.

places.name: Hermitage
places.atmosphere: A quiet, scholarly refuge holding pre-human knowledge and sacred texts, protected by Yareth's herding of refugees.
places.description: A hermitage near Greymarch peaks containing stone-tablets inscribed with Truthseeker's Echo's name and Yareth's grandmother's journal. Yareth herds refugees here to preserve Elder Tongue texts.
places.notes: Contains crucial historical records. Targeted by Dominion auditors for its knowledge archive. Unresolved: whether it survives Dominion interest.

places.name: Ironspine depths
places.atmosphere: Deep caverns within the Ironspine mountains, filled with centuries of hoarded consciousness-fragments and the echoing songs of ancient wyvern voices.
places.description: The lair where Vrekka, a wyvern, guarded consciousness-fragments for three centuries. Where a fevered refugee boy sought shelter and found mercy instead of sale.
places.notes: Vrekka's three-century hoard of consciousness-fragments. Bone-singers discovered the location within three days of Vrekka's mercy-choice. Unresolved: how bone-singers react to transformed hoard and merciful wyvern.

places.name: Merchant-Lord Ostain's Vault
places.atmosphere: A deep, guarded chamber protected by locks that sing in Elder Tongue, filled with consciousness-fragments and the weight of a father's sacrifice.
places.description: A vault seven floors beneath Keshtir's administrative heart, protected by singing locks carved from bone and sealed by blood-oath. It contains consciousness-fragments necessary to cure a wasting curse.
places.notes: The vault's locks are conscious entities inscribed with Elder Tongue, holding memories of a thousand transactions.

places.name: Tidemark
places.atmosphere: A half-submerged ruin that sings in harmonic frequencies, haunted by curse and oath.
places.description: The Tidemark is a ship that lay half-submerged in Sapphire Deep's shallows, its hull singing in old harmonic frequencies. It was captained by Vorath for thirty years. The Captain's Jewel hangs from a chain around Vorath's neck as he clings to a mast.
places.notes: The ship's singing suggests it is alive or inhabited by conscious remnant. Deepkin salvage crews target it at dawn.

places.name: Cathedrals Beneath
places.atmosphere: Ancient, sacred, guarded by bone-singers who know the difference between seekers and thieves.
places.description: The Cathedrals Beneath are ancient pre-human structures located beneath the Wailing Scar. They are protected by bone-singers and contain pre-human artifacts such as the lodestone. They appear to be a sanctuary for both artifact-preservation and bone-singer habitation.
places.notes: The presence of bone-singers and pre-human artifacts suggests these are both natural habitats and intentional preservation sites. The lodestone's presence there and the bone-singers' protection of it implies cosmically significant artifacts are housed here.

places.name: High Meadows
places.atmosphere: Open grasslands where winds speak in languages older than kingdoms, home to shepherds and their flocks.
places.description: High meadows in the Steppe Marches where Kestran keeps his shepherding flocks. They are places where old languages are spoken by the wind itself, suggesting pre-human or ancient magical presence.
places.notes: The 'languages older than kingdoms' spoken by the wind suggests these meadows sit on ley-lines or have pre-human significance. This is Kestran and Ashara's encounter location.

places.name: abyssal depths
places.atmosphere: Alien, powerful, and source of ancient knowledge.
places.description: The deepest abyss where the Keeper of Pearls emerges from. It is a seat of knowledge and power within the deepkin realms.
places.notes: The Keeper's emergence from here suggests it is a place of counsel or oracle-keeping for the deepkin.

places.name: The kiln
places.atmosphere: A furnace-heart where glass-workers sing blood-price rituals into molten radiance.
places.description: A glass-working furnace where Mella and other artisans forge consciousness-fragment vessels. The furnace responds to blood-price and old frequencies, remembering both light and choice.
places.notes: The furnace's responsiveness to Mella's bleeding palm suggests it is semi-sentient or magically attuned to ritual bloodshed.

places.name: waystation library north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: Quiet, protective, filled with ledgers and scattered charts.
places.description: A hidden library north of Pelimor where the counter-archive shelters Henryk among ledgers and scattered charts. It contains archives of maps and old knowledge.
places.notes: Henryk is concealed here to prevent him from drawing the ninth map. The library appears to house counter-archive holdings.

places.name: hidden grove
places.atmosphere: A sheltered, secret place where ancient journals rest and fae-magic still holds sway.
places.description: A concealed location where Sorenna's grandmother claimed a second compass lay. The grove is where the seed-bearing compass is found, inscribed with fae-script.
places.notes: Grandmother's journals pinpoint this location. The second compass's seed-inscription suggests fae involvement or pre-human crafting.

places.name: hidden cavern beneath singing stone
places.atmosphere: A sacred, hidden space where a bone-singer's final ritual transforms consciousness into artifact.
places.description: A concealed cavern where Naela completes her final bone-flute carving. The stone itself sings, suggesting harmonic resonance with bone-singing magic.
places.notes: Where Naela's consciousness-fragments are poured into the bone-flute. The 'singing stone' above may amplify bone-song magic.

places.name: The Wailing Scar
