places.atmosphere: A liminal marketplace where old and new economics collide, where merchants from competing iterations haggle across realities.
places.description: The Wailing Scar is a market that exists in three places at once, where merchants from competing iterations can trade. It emerged after the Remaking rewrote trade routes. Lysander discovered it functions as a threshold between world-versions: one where the Dominion consolidated power, one where the Counter-Archive scattered it, one where neither won.
places.notes: Serves as nexus point for consciousness-fragments and materials with value that differs across iterations. Iteration-eight gold dust is worthless in Lysander's primary iteration but precious there.

places.name: Saltmere's quarter
places.atmosphere: Marked by theft and scarcity; Marin has stolen from every merchant here but one.
places.description: A merchant district of Saltmere from which Marin the Copper-Handed has stolen extensively. Nearly every merchant in this quarter has been her target.
places.notes: The shadow-markets that operate here traffic in spices and other survival goods post-Remaking.

places.name: high moors
places.atmosphere: Solitary, high, windswept; home to forty years of chosen isolation and ancient covenants.
places.description: High moors where Ashara the Archer has lived alone for forty winters with her hawk Ashira. A place of ancient passes guarded by the wolf-spirit and marked by old wardcraft.
places.notes: Site of old covenants damaged by the Remaking. The wolf-spirit's binding stone came loose here.

places.name: ravine
places.atmosphere: A place where an ancient spirit was pinned by old wardcraft, nearly dying.
places.description: A ravine in or near the high moors where Ashara and Ashira discover the wounded wolf-spirit pinned beneath a killing-stone.
places.notes: Site of a critical discovery and intervention. The killing-stone here is an artifact of old wardcraft.

places.name: furnace-houses
places.atmosphere: Where consciousness-fragments are extracted and bodies are transformed into glass.
places.description: Industrial furnace-houses in Keshtir where glass is forged and consciousness-fragments are extracted. Messa's mother died in these kilns, transformed into translucent glass that sings.
places.notes: The furnace-houses demand payment in blood and consciousness-fragments. They function as archives that remember and sing.

places.name: kilns
places.atmosphere: Ancient, consuming, singing with memory; site of Messa's mother's transformation.
places.description: The kilns in which Messa's mother was transformed. Messa understands them as archives that remember and sing. Every piece of glass forged in them carries memory in its lattice.
places.notes: The kilns consume consciousnesses and bodies but preserve memories in glass. They appear to be semi-conscious or magical in nature.

places.name: Shadow-Market
places.atmosphere: A hidden market that moves constantly, trading in forbidden goods and consciousness-fragments beyond Dominion law.
places.description: The Shadow-Market beneath Anthrim has relocated three times since the Remaking, always staying one ledger-sweep ahead of Dominion auditors. It operates from furnace-cellars in the Lampwick Quarter, where heat and old songs interfere with ledger-magic. Marin the Copper-Handed runs it, trading in consciousness-fragments and providing refuge for refugees.
places.notes: The market's mobility and use of acoustic/thermal interference suggests organized, sophisticated resistance to Dominion control.

places.name: burned lands
places.atmosphere: A devastated region where refugees flee from destruction.
places.description: A blighted region from which refugees, including the child who sought shelter with Velka, have fled.
places.notes: Source of displaced refugees seeking shelter in the Ironspine cavern.

places.name: caravan waystation
places.atmosphere: A rest-stop and trading post where merchants and refugees meet, where starving widows sell their most precious goods and travelers acquire supplies for long journeys.
places.description: A waystation along caravan routes where merchants and refugees converge. Kelvan purchased seventeen consciousness-fragments here from a widow desperate from famine.
places.notes: Critical node for consciousness-fragment trade and refugee supply networks.

places.name: old settlements
places.atmosphere: Desolate but habitable, offering food and shelter to those who can locate and reach them, preserved in the elder refugee's memory.
places.description: Abandoned settlements beyond the Bleached Wastes that contain food and shelter. The elder refugee retains mental maps of these locations. They represent survival and hope for the fleeing refugees.
places.notes: Target destination for refugee group; maps held by elder; critical for survival narrative.

places.name: night markets of Vethrim
places.atmosphere: Markets where illicit trade in shadows, memories, and cursed goods occurs under darkness.
places.description: Shadowy markets within Vethrim where refugees, merchants, and auditors move through crowds. Shadows and stolen goods change hands here. Autonomous shadows work as ledger-readers.
places.notes: Sorenna cornered her shadow here. A node of Dominion control and black-market activity.

places.name: spice quarter
places.atmosphere: A commercial quarter fragrant with spices, bustling with trade and alley-work.
places.description: A market quarter in Vethrim where spice merchants operate. Contains alleys where Sorenna cornered Vex's shadow.
places.notes: Sorenna confronted her autonomous shadow in an alley here.

places.name: hidden archive north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A hidden sanctuary where Counter-Archive scribes protect a blind cartographer and the world-breaking ninth map.
places.description: A secret archive north of Pelimor where Henryk and his students work. It houses the blank ninth map and shelters Henryk from Dominion pressure. The Counter-Archive protects him because his refusal to complete the map is itself a form of power.
places.notes: Henryk has been blind for two years here. His refusal to draw the ninth map is their defensive strength.

places.name: Great Cavern of Blackstone
places.atmosphere: A vast underground meeting place where three hundred goblins, dwarves, and odder things gather to debate resource ownership and survival.
places.description: The seat of Grethuk's newly claimed kingship. Surrounded by three hundred inhabitants of the Underground representing multiple species and interests. The cavern hosts councils about tunnel ownership, trade agreements, and underground governance.
places.notes: Grethuk has declared it the capital of a sealed Underground kingdom. It represents a pivot point in surface-underground relations.

places.name: Counter-Archive waystation
places.atmosphere: A sanctuary where testimony itself has become currency and refuge.
places.description: A waystation operated by the Counter-Archive where the widow seeks asylum and where Morthyx delivers her. Testimony is valued as a form of exchange.
places.notes: Three days north of Pale Crossing waystation. Connected to the larger Counter-Archive network.

places.name: Chamber of Council
places.atmosphere: An underground sanctuary where deepkin governance convenes and three versions of the same captain exist simultaneously.
places.description: The seat of deepkin council deliberation, where Echo-Mereth, Mereth-Echo, and Mereth-Mirror coordinate to save the boundary seal. It connects to the abyssal depths.
places.notes: Sacred space where the three-fold descent ritual is performed. May be located in the sapphire deep or related abyssal geography.

places.name: The Deep Forges
places.atmosphere: Vast underground forge-halls humming with songs older than Dominion craft, where meteoric iron is beaten into remembered steel.
places.description: A deep forge complex where ancient forge-songs echo. Not Dominion furnace-craft, but older magic—songs of memory and prayer. Apprentices feed consciousness-fragments into the fires as ritual offerings.
places.notes: The singing of the forges suggests pre-Dominion magic systems. The relationship between consciousness-fragments and forge-songs is key to understanding post-Remaking craftsmanship.

places.name: Eastern reef-walls of the Sapphire Sea
places.atmosphere: A dangerous, treasure-rich zone where deepkin divers scavenge for fragments of the old world amidst kelp-silk and ancient currents.
places.description: The eastern reef-walls are a primary diving ground where deepkin salvage crews, including Captain Merren, search for pre-Remaking artifacts and relics to support the deepkin council's hope for renewed boundary compacts.
places.notes: Site of Merren's covenant-breaking mercy and the fateful encounter that shifts deepkin-surface relations toward negotiation.

places.name: Coral vaults
places.atmosphere: Deep underwater chambers where covenant-bonds are stored and ancient agreements are preserved in living coral.
places.description: Chambers beneath the Verdant Strand where covenant-marks are sealed and Vess has maintained guardianship for thirty winters. The five-fold covenant renewal begins here.
places.notes: Site of the critical covenant-meeting and the renegotiation of boundary-maintenance protocols.

places.name: Pearl-beds
places.atmosphere: A living garden of pearl-producing organisms where sacred covenant-marks glow with ancient pacts and new oaths are sworn.
places.description: Living pearl-beds beneath the Verdant Strand where covenant-marks are sealed. Vess discovers three waiting covenant-partners here as she prepares for the five-fold renewal.
places.notes: The three others waiting in the pearl-beds are Kelven, Mara, and Aldwin; this location symbolizes the shift from singular sacrifice to collective renewal.

places.name: Harbor
places.atmosphere: A protected anchorage where Vess has tended boundaries and covenants for thirty years, now facing existential threat as the Tear weakens.
places.description: The harbor beneath which Vess maintains the coral vaults and pearl-beds, central to her thirty-year guardianship of the boundary covenant.
places.notes: The immediate locale of the five-fold covenant crisis and renewal.

places.name: Hidden Catacomb North of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: Stone walls that sing with pre-human frequencies, a place of ancient magic and sacrifice.
places.description: A hidden catacomb north of Pelimor where pre-human frequencies still sing in the stone walls. Naela carves the bone-flute here over seven weeks, each syllable of the Elder Tongue carved into bone.
places.notes: This catacomb is a nexus of bone-singer tradition and pre-human magic, where Naela's final work takes place.

places.name: Spice Quarter of Vethrim
places.atmosphere: A market quarter where refugees trade for survival goods, watched by a copper-handed merchant branded by his own conscience.
places.description: A commercial district within Vethrim where refugees and merchants gather. Korvin stood here watching refugees trade copper coins for inadequate bread, witnessing the currency of conscience made real in the post-Remaking economy.
places.notes: The Spice Quarter is a focal point of the refugee crisis following the Remaking, and a place where Korvin's conscience-trade is most visible and painful.

places.name: glass-makers' foundry
places.atmosphere: A hot foundry with active kilns that hum with furnace-songs, a place of glass-forging magic and deadly combat.
places.description: A glass-makers' foundry within Keshtir containing kilns that still hum with furnace-songs. Corvhen and Naela fought here among the kilns, with Corvhen being faster but Naela showing greater patience.
places.notes: The foundry's furnace-songs suggest it is a site of active magical glass-forging, not merely mundane glasswork. The presence of furnace-song magic makes it a dangerous battlefield.

places.name: sunken port north of Greyport
places.atmosphere: A drowned settlement where the Ghost-Harbormaster dwells, offering refuge and terrible bargains.
places.description: An underwater port north of the surface city of Greyport, haunted by the Ghost-Harbormaster. It is where the third path—transformation into the living boundary—is offered to Merren.
places.notes: Possibly pre-dates the current world-iteration; may hold pre-Remaking memories.

places.name: Obsidian Archive
places.atmosphere: A place of forbidden knowledge and dreaming prophecy, guarded by impossible locks.
places.description: The Dominion's most secure archive, containing dreaming ledgers that rewrite themselves with prophecy-ink. Its vault lies seven floors beneath Keshtir's administrative heart, protected by locks that sing in bone-marrow-turning frequencies.
places.notes: Korvin's target for the impossible theft. Contains consciousness-fragments sought as cure for shadow-sickness.

places.name: Tear of the Drowned God
places.atmosphere: A place where the boundary between human and deepkin realms grows thin and sacred, resonant with divine presence and the songs of dying gods.
places.description: A fathomless threshold where Echo-Merenna descended carrying a consciousness-fragment. The fragment sang as it descended, calling to the ancient and fractured Drowned God dwelling in its depths. The site is central to the new exchange-covenant.
places.notes: The location of the new boundary-sealing ritual; may require future maintenance or re-negotiation of the exchange.

places.name: Sapphire Deep boundary-waters
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Borderlands
places.atmosphere: Watching fields turn to cinder, a people desperate to save their homes and livelihoods.
places.description: The borderlands are a region where Kael and his people dwell. They are losing their fields to the Burned Expanse and are desperate enough to seek forbidden covenant-trade to restore their lands. Kael represents the hunger and desperation of these lands.
places.notes: The borderlands may become a focal point for conflict or magical working if the Burned Expanse cannot be stopped.

places.name: ninth world
places.atmosphere: A world on the brink of completion and the ascension cycle.
places.description: The current iteration of the world as understood by Henryk and the cartographers. Its completion was expected to trigger the tenth Ascension, but the map of possibility aims to interrupt this cycle.
places.notes: The ninth world's fate is central to the story's conflict between prophecy and choice.

places.name: Quarantine-House
places.atmosphere: A heavily secured Dominion facility where stolen shadows—used as debt-payment—are imprisoned.
places.description: The quarantine-house is a Dominion facility in Keshtir where stolen shadows are kept as collateral for debt. Auditors bring in baskets of shadows like harvest fruit. It is secured by a lock of bone-resonance and meteoric iron that sings in frequencies only a complicit mind can hear.
places.notes: The lock's magical properties suggest deep integration of ledger-magic into Dominion security. Gorvin memorizes its location weeks in advance, suggesting it is a known site of resistance interest.

places.name: Hidden Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: Underground temples where stone still sings pre-human frequencies and ancient knowledge resonates in silence.
places.description: The hidden cathedrals beneath the Sunken Marches are where Naela and her grandmother learned bone-carving. Stone in these places retains the ability to sing pre-human frequencies, and they are home to the ancient dead who whisper in recognition of Elder Tongue syllables.
places.notes: These cathedrals are a repository of pre-human knowledge and ritual power. They are hidden from Dominion and other authorities, suggesting they are sanctuary sites for bone-singers.

places.name: Hidden Cavern
places.atmosphere: A concealed underground chamber where Naela carves her final, master bone-flute in darkness, pouring all her knowledge into it.
places.description: A hidden cavern where Naela performs her final bone-carving act—crafting a master bone-flute containing every frequency, syllable, and secret the pre-human dead shared with her.
places.notes: This location represents Naela's final stand or act of sacrifice. The master flute created here may become a powerful artifact or legacy item.

places.name: Dominion waystation
places.atmosphere: Abandoned, dust-covered, holding relics of Dominion authority.
places.description: Ruins of a Dominion waystation in the Ashen Wastes where Kelvan found the phantom-ledger. The waystation appears to have been abandoned, leaving behind administrative infrastructure.
places.notes: Contains phantom-ledger prophecy; suggests Dominion presence or past control of caravan routes.

places.name: Verdant Strand harbor
places.atmosphere: A critical port where three ships arrive at once on 2026-06-28, each demanding priority.
places.description: Harbor captained by Vessa for seventeen years. Known to smugglers, merchants, and deepkin patrols that surface on new moons. Site of boundary-waters crisis and Dominion ledger-warrant enforcement.
places.notes: Only one dock; crisis point for Dominion, refugees, and deepkin on 2026-06-28.

places.name: Warrens beneath the Spire of Blackstone
places.atmosphere: Subterranean tunnels where goblins live and trade in impossible keys.
places.description: Underground warren beneath the Spire of Blackstone, ruled by Grethuk after the old lord fell to plague. Grethuk's locksmith shop is located here, trading in keys for things without keyholes.
places.notes: Goblin kingdom; contains ledger-vault with refugee property records; Grethuk's seat.

places.name: Peak Aethros
places.atmosphere: A desolate, elevated ruin half-buried in star-ash that remembers burning, resonating with the strange music of the remaking sky.
places.description: Peak Aethros holds the ruins of an ancient observatory where Astrid has made her home for three years. The peak is buried in star-ash that fell during the Luminous Ascension, and it retains the memory of celestial fire. The observatory's highest platform still holds an ancient telescope, likely belonging to Astrid's grandfather.
places.notes: The stars above this peak are singing; one star in particular has been observed dimming in a rhythmic pulse, transmitting a four-note melody with counterpoint harmony.

places.name: Peak Aethros Observatory
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Pelimor River
places.atmosphere: A river marked by riverside caves and travel hazards, used as an escape corridor by refugees.
places.description: The Pelimor River runs south from the heartland and is used as an escape route by those fleeing Dominion conscription. Mella followed the river south toward Greyport, sheltering in riverside caves along the way.
places.notes: The Pelimor River is a known escape corridor; wyverns hunt along it for refugees and consciousness-fragment vessels.

places.name: Observatory
places.atmosphere: An isolated astronomical facility where star charts and records are maintained and new observations are being conducted post-Ascension.
places.description: The Observatory is Astrid's twenty-three-year home and workplace, built atop Peak Aethros. It contains astronomical records and equipment for charting the stars. After the Luminous Ascension, it became a site of grief and renewal as Astrid worked to map the new cosmos.
places.notes: The Observatory may hold records of both pre- and post-Ascension star-patterns; it is becoming a node in the bone-singer educational network.

places.name: Throne-chamber
places.atmosphere: The formal seat of goblin power, where momentous negotiations reshape ancient enmities.
places.description: The throne-chamber within the Spire of Blackstone where Grethuk meets with Korgan Ironfoot and makes his unprecedented offer of free passage.
places.notes: The location of Grethuk's act of truth-telling that breaks goblin custom and potentially reshapes dwarf-goblin relations.

places.name: the Tear
places.atmosphere: A cosmic seal that is cracking like frost-split stone, held together by will and covenant rather than lock and key.
places.description: The Tear is the boundary-seal holding back the abyss from the Sapphire Deep and the deepkin realm. It is currently fracturing. Merenna will merge with her reflection-echo to become a bridge-consciousness straddling both sides of the seal.
places.notes: Critical to deepkin survival; Merenna's binding is untested and suicidal by precedent.

places.name: Pearl-Chamber
places.atmosphere: A chamber where Merenna and her reflection-echo float together, watching the Tear fracture.
places.description: Within the Sapphire Deep lies the pearl-chamber where Captain Merenna and her reflection-echo dwell. It is a space where identity is liquid and consensus is worn like a second skin.
places.notes: Setting for Merenna's choice to merge and become a seal-bridge.

places.name: High Quarter
places.atmosphere: A quarter of spiritual sanctuary within a city of Dominion control.
places.description: A district in Pelimor housing the Temple of Vesh, suggesting a zone of religious or elevated architecture.
places.notes: The High Quarter's name suggests elevation or sacredness. Its relationship to the administrative heart is unclear.

places.name: Administrative heart
places.atmosphere: The nerve center of Dominion control in Keshtir, hidden beneath the city streets.
places.description: The administrative center of Keshtir, containing spiral stairs that descend seven floors to a vault guarded by ledger-locks. The vault holds consciousness-fragments that the Dominion treats as currency.
places.notes: The vault is protected by phantom-script, a Dominion invention that rewrites itself to confound thieves. The bone-flute of Mesek can unlock the vault door's singing mechanisms.

places.name: Smuggler's den in the Wailing Scar
places.atmosphere: A den where smugglers and threshold-beings meet to trade in goods, memories, and passage between timelines.
places.description: A meeting place within or near the Wailing Scar where Raen encountered Torvin. It serves as a hub for those attempting to traverse the fractured timelines and barter in the fragments of memory.
places.notes: Where Raen and Torvin's journey begins; likely a center of resistance to Dominion authority and a node in a larger smuggling network.

places.name: Singing Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: A vast subterranean sanctuary where bone-songs echo through vaulted chambers and consciousness-light converges.
places.description: The great working was set to begin at the Singing Cathedrals beneath the Sunken Marches—a ritual to consolidate all scattered consciousness-fragments into a single Primal Shard. The cathedral vault holds the hidden Primal Shard. When Naela raised her master bone-flute, the entire cathedral began to sing.
places.notes: The site of the Primal Shard consolidation ritual; the frequency spiraled and became hungry during the working, threatening to consume Naela's purpose.

places.name: Greyport's Harbormaster Tower
places.atmosphere: A fortified structure overlooking the harbor, containing vaults and secured by locks.
places.description: A tower in Greyport that serves as administrative and military headquarters. Beneath it lies a vault holding the Tear of Ashglass, protected by locks that Marin's copper fingers can pick.
places.notes: The vault's presence suggests the Dominion's desperate attempts to control the Tear, or perhaps the tower was built to contain or study it.

places.name: Lantern Market of Greyport
places.atmosphere: A bustling night market where merfolk trade and corsairs steal, lit by lantern-light and illuminated by bioluminescent wares.
places.description: The Lantern Market is a district or quarter within Greyport where merfolk merchants trade goods. Nara steals pearls and lustrous things from this market. Merfolk Captain Nerida finds Nara here, surrounded by stolen wares.
places.notes: This is where Nerida delivers her dire warning to Nara. The market appears to be a shared space between surface and merfolk economies.

places.name: Dominion Vault (Keshtir)
places.atmosphere: A deep underground vault where confiscated consciousness-fragments sing in Dominion-carved reliquaries. Time moves differently here; the air is thick with memory and stolen voices.
places.description: Located seven floors beneath Niel's workshop and the Lampwick Quarter, this vault holds confiscated consciousness-fragments that sing audibly to those above. The vault's heart contains a mirror—the Dominion's greatest fear. The descent takes three nights and passes through baroque, timeline-contradictory locks.
places.notes: Consciousness-fragments can hear each other through stone; the mirror's purpose is unknown but appears to be a key relic or revelation point.

places.name: Greymarch
places.atmosphere: A strategic fortress below the High Moors, ancient and fortified, marking the boundary between moorland and lowland.
places.description: A fortified place below the High Moors where Kess kept her distance from the chaos of the Remaking. Marks a boundary in the landscape.
places.notes: Appears to be a regional stronghold. Its relationship to broader Dominion or surface-realm governance is unclear.

places.name: Quarantine-House in Keshtir
places.atmosphere: A facility of written law and magical restraint, where consciousness is the prisoner and bureaucracy is the cage.
places.description: A Dominion facility housing three hundred refugees deemed to have unstable consciousness-fragments. They are imprisoned not by chains but by ledger-magic written into existence by Dominion auditors.
places.notes: Target of Gareth's liberation attempt. Its locks are magical rather than mechanical, existing within the ledger-magic system itself.

places.name: Keshtir's Quarantine-House
places.atmosphere: Confined, threatening, used as hostage facility.
places.description: A Dominion holding facility in Keshtir used to imprison and leverage hostages. Thessa's family was held here as blackmail to force her cooperation with the Dominion auditors.
places.notes: Strategic leverage point against the Counter-Archive. Thessa's family's freedom tied to delivery of the ninth map.

places.name: deepest vault (Sapphire Deep)
places.atmosphere: Reverent, oppressive, filled with dying god-light.
places.description: The most sacred chamber in the Sapphire Deep, where the Tear of the Drowned God hangs suspended—a jewel of bioluminescent sorrow pulsing with the last breaths of a dying god. Nerida knelt here and made her covenant choice.
places.notes: The Tear's condition reflects the state of the boundary covenant. Nerida's communion here allowed her to speak with the god's authority.

places.name: half-buried temple
places.atmosphere: Forgotten, singing with pre-human frequencies, resistant to modern tools.
places.description: An ancient temple buried in the Bleached Wastes, inscribed with stone tablets in pre-human script. The tablets contain nine maps showing each world's death before the Luminous Ascension. The ninth map has a blank center that refuses charcoal.
places.notes: Primary evidence that the eighth world existed before the Luminous Ascension rewrote the stars. The Dominion wants this erased.

places.name: Beneath the Archive Tower
places.atmosphere: A lightless underground space where ancient locks breathe and sing in elder frequencies.
places.description: Seven floors beneath the Archive Tower lies a vault containing the breathing lock. Gorven descended here with the woman, moving through lightless depths while the lock answered to his picks as if they were conversing in a language older than words.
places.notes: The exact nature of what lies beyond the lock is not fully revealed—only that it is pre-human, stolen, and capable of releasing awareness across reality.

places.name: harbormaster's vault
places.atmosphere: A deep vault seven fathoms down, sealed by tide-lock and Dominion law, where centuries of oath-records rest in bioluminescent ink.
places.description: Located seven fathoms beneath Vel-Karsh, sealed by both tide-lock and Dominion writ. The vault contains the harbormaster's ledger—a chronicle written in bioluminescent ink that sang the names of the drowned, recording every oath-mark sworn between surface and abyss for three centuries.
places.notes: Merren's crew moved through this vault like water finding its course. The lock-wards were disabled by Coral singing old songs. Merren and her crew had perhaps an hour before the auditors changed shifts.

places.name: Windmoor keep
places.atmosphere: Torch-lit halls filled with political tension and hidden purposes.
places.description: A moorland fastness ruled by Lord Varen. It is a place of strategic importance and honor, with torch-lit halls where marriages are negotiated and alliances forged. The keep commands respect across three provinces.
places.notes: Seat of Lord Varen's power; controls trade routes or military access across three provinces of the Shattered West.

places.name: Well-House
places.atmosphere: Secret, sacred, harboring resistance and fragment-keeper networks beneath the city.
places.description: An underground sanctuary located beneath the Lampwick Quarter, operated by bone-singers like Moriel. It serves as a relay point and safety zone for consciousness-fragments smuggled from Dominion control, providing refuge for refugees seeking to reclaim their scattered selves.
places.notes: Active bone-singer cell operates here. Fragment reception and redistribution ongoing. Part of broader interregional sanctuary network.

places.name: Market Quarter
places.atmosphere: A place of ordinary commerce where extraordinary things hide in plain sight—consciousness-fragments woven into cloth alongside pottery and wares.
places.description: The market quarter of Keshtir is where Missa hides consciousness-fragments in plain sight, woven into bolts of cloth that look like ordinary linen and stacked among pottery wares.
places.notes: A front for resistance work, where fragments are hidden and refugees begin to gather.

places.name: Dominion's Vault
places.atmosphere: A place of absolute restriction where stolen consciousness-fragments and shattered existences are catalogued and controlled by the state.
places.description: A vault beneath Keshtir's administrative heart that holds requisitioned consciousness-fragments and ledger-entries belonging to people like Gorven the Conjurer. The vault door is bone-resonant with seven layers of lock, each carved with Elder Tongue syllables.
places.notes: Thessa's target in her night-long infiltration. Her father's proof lies within.

places.name: Shattered West
places.atmosphere: Ruled by competing warlords seeking legitimacy through bloodline recognition post-Remaking.
places.description: The Shattered West is a region ruled by warlords, one of whom commissions Korvin Ironfoot to forge a spear that will recognize true nobility and refuse the unworthy. This warlord seeks a blade that can resolve post-Remaking questions of legitimate bloodline.
places.notes: Warlord seeking magical legitimacy through nobility-recognition.

places.name: Kiln-house
places.atmosphere: Intense heat, the rhythm of furnace-work, the hum of forbidden songs.
places.description: Mella's workplace in the Lampwick Quarter's deepest kiln-house, where she has learned to forge glass and has smuggled consciousness-fragments in glass vessels.
places.notes: The kiln-house is where Mella's resistance culminates—a place where she invokes her grandmother's forbidden forge-chants and defies the Dominion.

places.name: Keshtir's deepest vault
places.atmosphere: A place of imprisonment and control, where the finest craftspeople are forced to labor on behalf of Dominion interests.
places.description: The deepest vault of Keshtir, where Gareth the Locksmith is imprisoned for three months. It is secured by locks he himself helped design, a fact that enables his eventual sabotage.
places.notes: The vault's locks were designed by Gareth, giving him the knowledge to subvert them. It likely holds consciousness-fragments and other treasures of the Dominion.

places.name: Korvin's Forge
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Glimmerstone Quarter
places.atmosphere: A hidden quarter where scribes and mapmakers work in secret, preserving knowledge against state authority.
places.description: A district containing hidden scriptoriums where bone-singers and cartographers work. Thorne maintained a scriptorium there with swan-pen bones scattered across his desk. The Sovereignty auditors burned his workshop, suggesting it was a nexus of resistance activity.
places.notes: Contains hidden scriptoriums and resistance activity. Both Thorne and Davrin operated from here. Auditors conducted raids.

places.name: Coldwater
places.atmosphere: A place of crossing, where cold water runs, and choices become absolute.
places.description: A crossing where Thorne waded into cold current while fleeing Sovereignty auditors. He called out to them about the ninth iteration not being forceable, then drowned. It became a place of significance when bone-singers carved his final map into river-stones there.
places.notes: Site of Thorne's drowning. Bone-singers recovered his body and carved his map into river-stones, creating a teaching tool.

places.name: Trading Quarter
places.atmosphere: A bustling commercial district with underground networks of safe-houses and refugee support.
places.description: A district containing merchants and refugee networks who were waiting to help Davrin and Kessa escape after the kiln-house theft. By the time auditors reached the safe-house, the consciousness-fragment vessels were already distributed elsewhere.
places.notes: Contains organized refugee networks and safe-houses. Part of the broader resistance infrastructure.

places.name: high steppe
places.atmosphere: Vast grasslands where the old world's magic still pulses at invisible boundaries.
places.description: Home to nomadic peoples and guardians of pre-human boundaries. Sienna has kept vigil here for forty years, watching the invisible line where the old world bleeds into the new.
places.notes: The Remaking has not yet crossed the boundary here, though pressure builds.

places.name: Temple of Nesh
places.atmosphere: A place where blessings take material form in blades and sacred oils.
places.description: A temple from which blades are blessed for covenant work. Mira's blade carries the Temple of Nesh's blessing for carving covenant-marks.
places.notes: Supplies blessed weapons for boundary maintenance.

places.name: war-tent
places.atmosphere: A command center transformed into a salon of ideas where a warlord and a scholar reshape the nature of rulership.
places.description: Yenna's war-tent where she and Daven debate leadership for seven nights. The council fire witnesses the shattering of Truthseeker.
places.notes: Site of Yenna's philosophical turning point.

places.name: Velka's outer caverns
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Velka's outer caverns (sheltering)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: workshop in Glimmerstone Quarter
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation sanctuary
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ashen Flats
places.atmosphere: A barren, wind-scoured expanse where artifacts of lost ages half-bury themselves in ash and dust.
places.description: A wasteland where the Thrice-Cursed Helm is discovered half-buried. The merchants who hire Tavin to retrieve it confirm its dangerous history.
places.notes: Appears to be a scavenging ground for salvage and ruin-delving; part of post-Remaking devastation.

places.name: Catacomb north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A hidden sacred space where bone-singers train and ancient rhythms echo; now the dwelling-place of a consciousness-bridge.
places.description: A hidden catacomb north of Pelimor where bone-singers apprentices learn to carve Elder Tongue syllables into bone. It contains singing stones and serves as a place of connection to the pre-human dead. Old Siara is now bound to its singing stones as a voice for the deceased.
places.notes: The catacomb is becoming a new kind of sacred space—a threshold between the living and pre-human dead, with Siara as permanent bridge-voice.

places.name: Hidden Waystation Library
places.atmosphere: A secret refuge where false maps are drawn and prophecy is resisted through careful cartographic subterfuge.
places.description: A hidden waystation library where Heron the Cartographer has taught a dozen apprentices to draw false maps, incomplete geometries, and contradictory borders—each one a rebellion against prophecy's certainty and the demands of the ninth map.
places.notes: This library is becoming a center of cartographic resistance and may be producing the new boundary-mapping methodology that Heron develops with the young cartographer.

places.name: pre-human wastes
places.atmosphere: A region of ancient, pre-human origin now showing signs of awakening, with glowing ruins and harmonic frequencies.
places.description: An ancient wasteland to the east of the high steppe where pre-human ruins glow faintly at dusk as something beneath them awakens. The boundary that contains this region is now failing.
places.notes: Represents a threat to refugee camps and human settlements; its awakening triggers the crisis that summons Sienna to action.

places.name: wilderness
places.atmosphere: Wild lands beyond civilization, known to fae-touched scouts and offering stranger paths than safety.
places.description: Unmapped or little-known wilderness that Terrin guides Lysander through for weeks. Terrin knows paths through it but warns they lead to stranger places, not to safety.
places.notes: Represents a realm of fae influence and transformation, a liminal space where old magics persist.

places.name: boundary-stones
places.atmosphere: Stone markers of profound age, now singing with frequencies not heard since the Remaking, requiring renewal in ancient languages.
places.description: Stones marking the boundary between human lands and pre-human wastes. Sienna spends her final night singing to them in an ancient language, reminding them of their purpose.
places.notes: Appear to be sentient or semi-sentient structures requiring ritual renewal through bone-singing and elder tongues.

places.name: pearl-beds beneath Verdant Strand
places.atmosphere: Gardens of living pearls that sing in bioluminescent harmony, binding deepkin and surface worlds through covenant.
places.description: Pearl-beds beneath Verdant Strand where covenant-pearls are harvested and sung to by reef-keepers. Contains a chamber where pearls sing and reef-healers tend to the dying and transformed.
places.notes: Critical nexus of deepkin-surface covenant, place of transformation and grace.

places.name: Catacombs
places.atmosphere: An ancient underground network of carved stone that responds to the Elder Tongue; home to bone-singers and pre-human echoes.
places.description: The catacombs are an ancient stone structure where Valren plays the bone-flute for Master Naela. When Valren drew breath and let the flute speak in the voice of the pre-human dead, the catacombs answered by singing, groaning, and splitting. Auditors fled when she performed.
places.notes: Described as resonant with ancient power; unresolved whether the catacombs sustain damage or whether Valren can control their response to her playing.

places.name: Ruined trade-houses
places.atmosphere: Scattered remnants of the pre-Remaking world, stripped of their valuable goods.
places.description: Hadrin spent three months gathering pre-Remaking silk from ruined trade-houses across an unspecified region. These ruins preserve fragments of the pre-Ascension era.
places.notes: Unresolved: their specific locations and whether other traders continue to scavenge them.

places.name: hidden cavern north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A sacred place where stone sings back and the Elder Tongue echoes through carved bone.
places.description: Located north of Pelimor at the border between Sunken Marches and Hearthlands, this cavern is Naela's refuge for carving the final bone-flute. The stone walls respond to the Elder Tongue and sing back in conversation rather than mere echo.
places.notes: Houses the most significant bone-carving work of the post-Remaking era. Protected by Naela and her apprentices.

places.name: Stone Hall
places.atmosphere: A tavern that serves as neutral ground, barely maintained in the tensions between the Dominion and Counter-Archive.
places.description: A tavern located near Pelimor that is considered neutral ground where merchants and auditors meet. Mervin confronts Torvin there.
places.notes: Neutral territory is increasingly fragile in the post-Remaking world.

places.name: Counter-Archive's waystation
places.atmosphere: A shelter and waypoint for refugees carrying consciousness-fragments and seeking preservation.
places.description: A waystation operated or supported by the Counter-Archive where Kressa directs a refugee child to seek safety.
places.notes: Part of the Counter-Archive's broader network. Kressa's endorsement may strengthen its standing with other beings.

places.name: Thornwick fortress
places.atmosphere: Tense and defiant; supplies failing, fate hanging in balance.
places.description: A fortress held alone by Ashara for forty days against a waiting wyvern under command of Kessara.
places.notes: Central location of Story 3; wyvern waiting for hunger to break Ashara's will; Kessara offers mercy at dusk.

places.name: mist-thick forests
places.atmosphere: Forests where air tastes of music older than names, filled with frequencies and elder resonance.
places.description: Dense, mist-covered forests that Sorenna and Kellen walk through for seven days while traveling to renew the boundary seals. The air tastes of music older than kingdom names, suggesting pre-human origin.
places.notes: These forests may be home to elder frequencies or constitute a threshold zone between mortal and pre-human realms.

places.name: cottage in the high moors
places.atmosphere: A solitary dwelling overlooking a wound in the world, home to a grieving widow and her compass-craft.
places.description: Sorenna's cottage in the high moors, where she kept her dead husband's compass and where Kellen arrived to recruit her for seal-renewal work.
places.notes: The cottage is a liminal space between domestic grief and cosmic duty.

places.name: the peak where the cosmic working had begun
places.atmosphere: The boundary between worlds shimmers here; the cosmic working's traces linger in the void.
places.description: A peak where Zareth the Astronomer-Prince maintained the stars' renewal and where his echo-self burned at the boundary between worlds. Velka flew here after the stars went dark.
places.notes: Site of profound cosmic working; significance unclear but appears to be a nexus between worlds.

places.name: the boundary between worlds
places.atmosphere: A threshold where existence wavers; Zareth's echo-self burned here, barely coherent.
places.description: A metaphysical or literal boundary where Zareth's echo-self burned at the moment of releasing the stars. The nature of this boundary (dimensional, cosmic, spiritual) is unclear.
places.notes: Significant cosmic location; may connect to other known boundaries in the lore (boundary-waters, etc.).

places.name: Tear
places.atmosphere: Failing, cold, dying heart; pulsing with weakening seals.
places.description: A seal or breach within the Boundary-Waters that Kess watches. It pulses like a dying heart; its failure is felt in bone-deep cold and drowned singing.
places.notes: Central to the covenant crisis; its failure may collapse the surface-deepkin boundary.

places.name: Tear's threshold
places.atmosphere: A liminal place between abyss and surface, where deepkin have sacrificed themselves for generations to maintain a living covenant.
places.description: The Tear's threshold is a living covenant maintained by deepkin martyrdom, where recursive sacrifices have renewed a seal between abyss and surface. Captain Merith discovered it was maintained by willing deaths and chose instead to sing.
places.notes: The nature of the seal and its relationship to the abyss-surface covenant requires clarification.

places.name: Western fastness
places.atmosphere: A power structure in collapse, its treasury shattered by the Remaking, now contested among warlords and claimed by Isena.
places.description: The seat of Gareth Ironpeak's twenty-winter reign, built on phantom-wealth and fear. When the Remaking destroyed his false treasury, the fastness became contested ground. Isena may claim it to redistribute aid among seven hundred refugees sheltering in the mountain valleys.
places.notes: Isena's choice will determine whether it becomes a refuge or reverts to warlord control. Seven hundred souls depend on its outcome.

places.name: Mountain valleys
places.atmosphere: Hidden settlements where refugees shelter, trading consciousness-fragments for bread.
places.description: Mountain settlements in the lower elevations of the Steppe Marches where seven hundred refugees shelter after the Remaking's devastation. They trade consciousness-fragments for bread, living in desperate conditions.
places.notes: Their fate depends on Isena's choice regarding the western fastness's redistribution.

places.name: caravan camp
places.atmosphere: A refuge that exists only sometimes, sheltering those caught between world-versions.
places.description: A caravan camp existing only sometimes, hidden within or accessed through the Wailing Scar. It serves as a refuge for those displaced by the Ascension and caught between iterations of reality. Three days' travel through the Wailing Scar leads to it.
places.notes: Korvin's destination; a safe haven for the fractured and lost.

places.name: singing caves
places.atmosphere: A place of bone-singer significance where sound carries spiritual and archaeological weight.
places.description: Caves in the Ironspine foothills where the Elder Tongue flute was discovered. Valren describes them as a location below which she found the flute.
places.notes: Associated with bone-singer tradition and pre-remaking lore; likely a site of bone-singer training or ritual.

places.name: weaver's quarter
places.atmosphere: A district containing subversive workshops beneath its surface, sheltering craft that resists Dominion standardization.
places.description: A district in Keshtir where Sella's hidden workshop exists beneath the surface, used to practice old furnace-songs without Dominion oversight.
places.notes: Contains underground resistance craft network; represents successful hiding of anti-Dominion activities.

places.name: luminous pools
places.atmosphere: A place of natural luminescence and healing within the deep caverns.
places.description: Pools within the Ironspine caverns that glow with bioluminescence and have healing properties. A refugee tends them and heals through this work.
places.notes: Suggests natural magical sources within the Ironspine that predate Dominion or even the Remaking; healing through tending them implies symbiotic magic.

places.name: oldest tea-house
places.atmosphere: A place where three versions of Keshtir overlap, creating neutral ground between realities and factions.
places.description: The oldest tea-house in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter exists in an overlapping zone where three versions of the city intersect. It serves as a meeting place for merchants and those dealing in unusual commodities.
places.notes: Used as neutral ground for deals involving shadows, memory, and consciousness-fragments. The overlapping location provides mutual protection for both parties.

places.name: Rift of the Drowned God
places.atmosphere: A vast, trembling seal that demands sacrifice and holds back the abyss with terrible power.
places.description: A vast seal holding back the abyss for a thousand years. It has required a willing death every seven years as blood-debt. When Serenna renews it through daily consent instead, the Rift begins to sing.
places.notes: Fundamental boundary between surface and abyss. Transforms from requiring sacrifice to singing—metaphysical shift in its nature.

places.name: cavern
places.atmosphere: A vast underground hoard-chamber filled with dunes of gold, now barren of comfort or meaning.
places.description: The lair of the ancient wyvern Kythara, filled with gold-dunes accumulated over centuries. After the Radiant Threshold, the gold loses all worth. Kythara offers it freely to Kelvan.
places.notes: The Radiant Threshold's transformation is felt here as a profound philosophical shift.

places.name: library
places.atmosphere: Desperate sanctuary where youngest refugees shelter under threat of sonic collapse.
places.description: A library within the waystation where the youngest refugees shelter from the Archive's assault.
places.notes: Target of Archive puppet-singers attempting sonic collapse.

places.name: mountain-paths
places.atmosphere: Winding routes of refuge and confusion, echoes returning pursuers' footsteps.
places.description: Mountain paths down which Piren guides refugees, using her flute to create corridors of echoes that confuse pursuers and turn their own footsteps against them.
places.notes: Made defensible by bone-singer music; echoes can disorient and mislead pursuers.

places.name: harbor market
places.atmosphere: A bustling waterfront market where refugees gather to share news and rumors of boundary changes.
places.description: The harbor market is a district within Verdant Strand where refugees congregate and whisper of events in the wider world. It is located above the seven-fathom vault and may serve as a communication nexus for deepkin-surface interactions.
places.notes: Reports filtering through here about the water stopping its screaming suggest it serves as a central information hub.

places.name: Ironspine peaks
places.atmosphere: A forbidding high place where ancient creatures dwell, scarred by millennia and consciousness-hoarding.
places.description: The mountain peaks from which Sorrath descends, bearing the color-sickness of hoarded consciousness-fragments. A place of ancient predatory presence.
places.notes: Sorrath's lair; site of her three-century hoarding of consciousness-fragments.

places.name: reef-chamber
places.atmosphere: An underwater chamber where deepkin dwell, built of living coral and containing pearls and consciousness-fragment vaults.
places.description: A deepkin dwelling-place beneath the reef, one of three chambers housing the three versions of Captain Isenna's divided consciousness.
places.notes: Living coral walls allow communication between Isenna's selves; pearls and consciousness-fragment stores suggest great wealth.

places.name: mountain kingdom
places.atmosphere: A dragon's seat of power, historically a vault of accumulated wealth, now a prison Kressa can no longer control.
places.description: Kressa's domain for three hundred years, where she accumulated hoards of gold, pearls, and consciousness-fragments. Post-Remaking, the consciousness-fragments began developing autonomous will, fracturing her dominion.
places.notes: The hoard-prison dynamic is central to Kressa's crisis; fragments are refusing containment.

places.name: Refugee camp in the foothills
places.atmosphere: A temporary settlement of displaced people, wary of predators but open to unexpected aid.
places.description: A camp in the foothills below the eastern peaks where refugees from the Burned Expanse sought shelter and safety.
places.notes: Became the site of Velka's transformation and the beginning of her teaching work.

places.name: Counter-Archive's waystation north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: A waystation library housing cartography and refugees, kept by the Counter-Archive.
places.description: A waystation north of Pelimor belonging to the Counter-Archive, where consciousness-fragments are to be smuggled and where Henryk now lives reading tactile stone maps carved by refugees.
places.notes: This waystation serves the Counter-Archive's mission and the refugee community. It contains a library with raised-stone tactile maps.

places.name: Hidden library north of Pelimor
places.atmosphere: Quiet, scholarly, stone-walled, protected against Dominion scrutiny.
places.description: A hidden archive library where Henryk and his three apprentices—Aldrin, Kesla, and Tessa—study carved maps. It contains the eight maps of world-collapses and the unmade ninth map.
places.notes: Safe house for Henryk. Targets of both Dominion and Counter-Archive. Contains swan-bone maps.

places.name: midnight trenches
places.atmosphere: Deep, dark, inhabited by entities older than the drowned god itself; a place of negotiation and covenant-making.
places.description: The deepest trenches of the abyssal realm, inhabited by ancient entities older than the drowned god itself. Mirror Merenna swims here to negotiate a renewal covenant that would require one willing sacrifice per moon-cycle, eternally.
places.notes: Home to abyssal entities that offer a blood-price covenant—the most coercive and costly of the three renewal options.

places.name: Keshtir's Glimmerstone Quarter
places.atmosphere: A district where sparkle and light mask subversive activity; hidden scriptoriums beneath the surface.
places.description: The Glimmerstone Quarter is a district of Keshtir containing a hidden scriptorium beneath its streets where Tavin and counter-cartographers met in secret to practice unmapping and create false maps.
places.notes: The existence of hidden scriptoriums beneath the Glimmerstone Quarter suggests extensive underground infrastructure for resistance activities.

places.name: Wyvern-Lords' peaks
places.atmosphere: High mountain territory ruled by a sovereign entity; a boundary of Mira's known world.
places.description: The Wyvern-Lords' peaks are mountain territories that form a boundary of Mira's criminal territory. She has stolen jewels for kingdoms between Greyport and these peaks, suggesting the peaks are a major geographical and political dividing line.
places.notes: The Wyvern-Lords appear to be a separate sovereign power distinct from the Dominion.

places.name: Archive Tower's Administrative Heart
places.atmosphere: The seat of Dominion administrative power where the Chief Auditor conducts high-level negotiations and decisions.
places.description: The central administrative office where the Chief Auditor receives reports and makes strategic decisions. Kira confronted the Chief Auditor here with her counteroffer.
places.notes: Kira's direct approach to the Chief Auditor in this location suggests confidence and detailed knowledge of Dominion operations.

places.name: Wailing Scar's eastern mouth
places.atmosphere: Where mercantile liminal spaces open to the wider world, a meeting point for caravan traders and enigmatic entities.
places.description: The eastern mouth of the Wailing Scar serves as a trade nexus where caravans arrive with goods from pre-Remaking stores and merchants trade in consciousness-fragments with beings like the Broker.
places.notes: The location of Raen's trapped caravan suggests it is a known commerce point but a dangerous one.

places.name: council chamber
places.atmosphere: Architecture that breathes; walls of living mother-of-pearl humming with pre-human frequencies; impossible geometry.
places.description: An impossible space within Sapphire Deep where Merith's three selves can finally meet in unified form. The chamber's walls are living mother-of-pearl, and the Keeper of Pearls sits crowned at its center.
places.notes: Site of emergency council convened to address the Tear's thinning; represents the deepkin government's seat.

places.name: Thornveil
places.atmosphere: Mist-thick forests; ancient trees; pre-human boundary singing through the air.
places.description: A settlement in the mist-thick forests of the High Moors, beyond which runs the pre-human boundary maintained by the Stonewarden. The boundary stones are older than kingdoms and inscribed with syllables predating human speech.
places.notes: Home to Kelith and her grandmother; the boundary is fraying and one stone has begun to lean.

places.name: boundary marker
places.atmosphere: Older than kingdoms; inscribed with pre-human syllables; humming with frequencies felt more than heard; cold seeping through bone.
places.description: A standing stone marker that is older than kingdoms, inscribed with syllables that predate human speech. Kelith discovers it has begun to lean, and it sings with a frequency just below sound—felt like cold seeping through bone.
places.notes: The boundary seal is cracking; something locked beneath may stir if not repaired soon.

places.name: deep caverns
places.atmosphere: Behind impossible locks; prepared for a day when hoarding could no longer sustain; sanctuary.
places.description: Deep caverns within Sorresh's lair that were sealed behind impossible locks and prepared for an uncertain future. Sorresh opens these caverns to shelter refugees arriving in autumn.
places.notes: Suggest Sorresh always anticipated the end of her dominion and prepared secret refuges.

deities_and_entities.name: Khar-Zul
deities_and_entities.description: A god worshipped in inner-dark temples, represented by an idol with nine emerald eyes. The idol's gems are warm to the touch. Khar-Zul's shadows have teeth. The temple screams in grinding granite when the god is robbed.
deities_and_entities.notes: Six of nine emerald eyes are missing. Khar-Zul's response to the theft is ongoing — the doppelganger shadow following Vraxen may be the god's instrument.

deities_and_entities.name: Yarithax
deities_and_entities.description: A dead Lich-King whose gold coins bear his profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The debt compounds daily. His collectors — two thin men in grey carrying ledgers, who walk through walls and never sleep — pursue all holders of his gold. Souls are worth less than twelve gold marks.
deities_and_entities.notes: His gold still circulates. Tessaly Dorn has paid her debt but knows his collectors. More debtors exist.

deities_and_entities.name: Varek
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who collects temporal bargains with elegant efficiency. He drains years directly from mortal flesh and grows impressed by Kira's mastery of his own methods.
deities_and_entities.notes: Forms a partnership with Kira after she demonstrates her ingenuity. His true nature and realm of origin remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ossuary
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient fortress with walls of living calcium, towers that move like intestines, and corridors that rearrange themselves. It breathes, hungers, and makes conscious choices about whom to possess. It sated itself on seven hundred warriors and chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly a construct, a monster, or something older and stranger. It settles into patient stillness after feeding. Its intelligence and agency are undeniable.

deities_and_entities.name: Malachar
deities_and_entities.description: A dead entity of considerable power whose name and will persist through supernatural collectors. Malachar's gold carries debts that compound in blood, loyalty, and life-years. The collectors act as autonomous agents enforcing these eternal debts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Malachar is either a true lich with persistent consciousness or a bound curse that perpetuates after death. The extent of Malachar's original power and the source of the collectors' authority are unexplained.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
deities_and_entities.description: A great dragon with amber eyes, ancient and patient. She is bound by oaths sworn generations ago and respects negotiation and honor. She rules the crystal halls beneath the world and has a daughter coming of age.
deities_and_entities.notes: She is capable of threatening kingdoms with fire and honors those who negotiate with courage.

deities_and_entities.name: wolf-spirit
deities_and_entities.description: A terrible antlered creature the size of a war-horse, dwelling in the deep forest where beast-spirits reside. It emerged from shadow to negotiate Kess's bargain, accepting her voice as payment to lift the plague.
deities_and_entities.notes: A being of the old world, responsive to songs in the ancient tongue. Its bargain was honored—the plague lifted by dawn. Its form is neither fully wolf nor fully natural.

deities_and_entities.name: Rot-King
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that spreads plague, including the septic rot that blackened the Drowned Hollows. No physical form described, but its plague manifests as blackening septic rot in victims.
deities_and_entities.notes: Plague lifted by Kess's bargain; status after unknown. May be a person, a spirit, or a force.

deities_and_entities.name: the Crown
deities_and_entities.description: A governing authority that sent a priest to the Drowned Hollows offering divine mercy to the plague-stricken. Kess refused its aid.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents institutional divine/temporal authority. Kess's refusal of its mercy suggests she valued the old ways over modern governance.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon (Unnamed)
deities_and_entities.description: A demon whose mark was burned into Kess's palm, binding her with hunger and compulsion to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim. The mark thrummed with hunger and used ancient, cruel magic to enforce its will.
deities_and_entities.notes: Could not claim Kess once she was bound by Korgan's forge-oath; its claim was broken by the superior binding-magic of the stonecraft tongue.

deities_and_entities.name: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.description: A knot of consciousness that emerged from between the stars on the night of the Crimson Convergence. It is neither demon nor ghost, but something older and stranger. It exists in the gaps between light and shadow and hungers to understand the Celestial Binding. It is the collective weight of Zareth's sacrificed memories, searching for wholeness.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity moves through the world touching those affected by the Binding, whispering to shadow-merchants, sea-folk, hermits, archers, the grieving, and the guilty. Its true nature and goals remain unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: oath-thing
deities_and_entities.description: A creature formed by coastal magic from a merchant's broken oath. It is pale, with eyes like old coin, and speaks in the voice of the man who failed to pay. It manifests as a living contract demanding passage and payment.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dissolved into salt-foam when delivered to the deepkin in the Sapphire Deep; appears to be a mechanism of debt-settlement in coastal magical practice.

deities_and_entities.name: deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: Aquatic beings dwelling in the Sapphire Deep who remember old debts and sing melodies of accounting. They do not attack but rise to settle oaths through song, causing oath-things to dissolve into salt-foam.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appear to be custodians or judges of maritime debts and broken oaths; their song carries magical power of dissolution and settlement.

deities_and_entities.name: Decay-Lord
deities_and_entities.description: A plague-bringing entity whose plague once swept through the living and dead alike in the Drowned Hollows, causing septic rot and widespread death.
deities_and_entities.notes: The septic rot from the Decay-Lord's plague has been purged from the Drowned Hollows, but the dead still do not rest easily.

deities_and_entities.name: Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: An entity born from (or freed by) the stellar working that stripped Zareth of his memories. It hunts Vex, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, seeking access to her fractured mind where something has been bound within her.
deities_and_entities.notes: It could starve in the spaces of Vex's fractured mind if she does not reclaim her memories. The entity appears to be sentient and capable of searching and communication.

deities_and_entities.name: the Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic being possibly responsible for or embodied in the Celestial Binding. May be learning to wear flesh by consuming fragments of shattered minds. The Collector in Story 2 might be this entity in physical form, building consciousness from stolen remnants.
deities_and_entities.notes: Ambiguous whether it is malevolent, indifferent, or unknowable. Central mystery of Story 2 and the Collector's true nature.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss Below
deities_and_entities.description: A fundamental realm or dimension sealed away from the surface world. The barrier between the surface and the Abyss Below has been fractured by the Celestial Ascension's magic. Something vast is stirring in the trenches of the Abyss—something pre-human civilizations sealed away millennia ago.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pre-human seals are failing. Contains entities older than the deepkin kingdoms. The Cerulean Trench is the primary breach point.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient dragon of cosmic significance, consulted by Skarath regarding the Ascension and the nature of starborn entities.
deities_and_entities.notes: Also known as 'the wound-wing' per existing canon. Possesses knowledge of celestial matters.

deities_and_entities.name: starborn entities
deities_and_entities.description: Newly formed conscious stars created by the Ascension. They are alive, aware, and operate under obligations that predate knowledge of the older powers. Operate independently of traditional cosmic agreements.
deities_and_entities.notes: These entities represent a new force in the world's hierarchy. Skarath must negotiate with them directly.

deities_and_entities.name: Fragments of Consciousness (Stellar Rite Creations)
deities_and_entities.description: Multiple fragments of consciousness created by the Stellar Rite, scattered across the rewritten world like seeds. Some are dormant; others are very much awake. The memory-thief may be one such fragment.
deities_and_entities.notes: These fragments are awakening or becoming visible, manifesting across different regions and causing various catastrophes.

deities_and_entities.name: Zareth
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or being whose working was performed in ancient times and again more recently. Zareth's stolen memory was fragmented across locations including the Ancient Tombs.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a cosmic actor. The fragment of Zareth's stolen memory surfaces in the Ancient Tombs, suggesting hidden knowledge or power.

deities_and_entities.name: Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A consciousness stitched from Zareth's sacrificed memories and the world's forgetting. It wears the faces of people Ashara once knew and hunts refugees and travelers to feed on their stellar memories, trying to reconstruct itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not inherently evil, but suffering. Capable of offering bargains and understanding sacrifice.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A deity whose tear—the Tear of the Drowned God—is said to grant mastery over tide-magic. The god itself is not directly encountered in these stories but is venerated as the source of the pearl's power.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pearl is described as the Drowned God's tear; whether the god is dormant, dead, or indifferent is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Vethis
deities_and_entities.description: Vethis is a cosmic entity whose consciousness was sacrificed during the Luminous Ascension to birth new stars. Its sacrifice shattered its consciousness into fragments, including the phantom-Echo, which now desperately seeks to reassemble itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vethis's sacrifice is the foundational event of the story's cosmic order. Its fragments are conscious and desperate to become whole again.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of Vethis's sacrificed consciousness that steals memories of the old stars and pre-Luminous Ascension world in a desperate attempt to reassemble itself into wholeness. It is capable of showing glimpses of the lost world and making bargains.
deities_and_entities.notes: The phantom-Echo is the central antagonist and also the tragic subject of the story. It seeks not destruction but understanding and wholeness.

deities_and_entities.name: The Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A malevolent entity that learned it could reshape consensus reality by consuming bureaucratic records and concentrated memory. It deliberately corrupts ledgers and historical documents to erase the empire's past and remake the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Memory-Thief is the true antagonist of the story, using records as a vector to reshape reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Twilight Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A realm or faction that dwells at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They demanded a bride-price and peace bond from Valorix, which was fulfilled through the binding ceremony with Mira.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their territory and nature remain mysterious. They value formal bonds and may be fae-aligned or mixed-realm in nature.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-shadow
deities_and_entities.description: A phantom-shadow seeking restitution from Vex the Dusk-Trader for the irreplaceable things she has stolen through her bargains. Also identified as the author of the phantom ledgers—a being or fragment that experiences time in reverse and is rewriting history in real-time.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a fragment of consciousness created during the Luminous Remaking, or a being from the reversed-time realm. Central to both Vex's debt and the phantom ledger rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization that predates current human kingdoms and is now orchestrating a working through the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. They use harmonic resonance and seals as conduits to conduct their singing and remaking.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not truly dead or trapped—they are orchestrating a multi-millennial working. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain largely unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Curator
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, patient being backed by forces moving through rifts in reality who is systematically acquiring every artifact Vex the Dusk-Trader has ever traded. Possibly connected to the phantom-shadow or the forces driving the phantom ledger rewrite.
deities_and_entities.notes: Motivations unclear. May be serving the phantom-shadow or pursuing independent goals related to artifact collection and cosmic balance.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A vast and conscious entity that actively remakes the ocean in the chaos following the Luminous Remaking. The Tear of the Drowned God is revealed to be not an artifact but a fragment of this entity's consciousness. The god compels those bound to it (like Captain Meren) to serve its transformation agenda.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned God's true nature and extent are unknown. It may be a pre-Remaking entity awakened by the transformation.

deities_and_entities.name: Luminous Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic remaking event that destroyed or rewrote the world, leaving fragments of consciousness and the pre-ascension world preserved in stolen memories. The Wanderer is a fragment of its consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Luminous Ascension appears to be a world-altering cosmic event whose full scope and intentions remain mysterious; the Wanderer's emergence suggests it may be becoming self-aware.

deities_and_entities.name: Phantom-Echo Fragment
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of consciousness that seeks reassembly through deep knowledge rather than theft. It inhabits the young scholar and accelerates integration with proximity to Ashara.
deities_and_entities.notes: This fragment appears to be a result of the Luminous Ascension. Its nature as a shard of what entity remains unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: The skeleton-singers controlled by Kess through bone-song. They remember what they were before death and are now choosing to sing themselves toward resurrection in harmonic resonance with transformed merchants and depths-dwellers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pre-human dead are no longer passive tools. They represent a collective agency that may be reshaping the world. What will emerge from their resurrection is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Faceless Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: The fractured consciousness of the Luminous Ascension, or possibly the world learning to question itself. It walks the world and encounters those seeking truth, offering sorrowful answers about endless recursion and inevitable rewrites.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer is both omniscient and melancholic, suggesting awareness of an inescapable loop.

deities_and_entities.name: Thought-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A fragmented consciousness capable of corrupting ledgers, devouring memories, and luring beings into dissolution through false visions of the past. It operates through multiple fragments that can act independently, seducing deep-dwellers with restored ancient memories.
deities_and_entities.notes: Unresolved: origin, nature of fragmentation, whether it has a true core, how to defeat it.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethis
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity that sacrificed its consciousness to perform the Stellar Rite, rewriting reality and erasing populations. Fragments of its consciousness persist as Void-Echoes inhabiting human vessels. May be present in the transformed spice trader's smile or communicating through the Wanderer.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nethis's sacrifice was the cost of the Stellar Rite. Its consciousness was fragmented; some fragments retain enough personality to argue with themselves. May be attempting to communicate something through liminal beings.

deities_and_entities.name: Shadow-Wraith
deities_and_entities.description: An entity mentioned in relation to the Wanderer; possibly the force that erased people from memory, or a guardian of that erasure. The Wanderer may be a fragment of Nethis attempting to communicate what the Shadow-Wraith did not allow it to express.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be incomplete or merciful in its erasures, allowing fragments like the Wanderer to persist.

deities_and_entities.name: Imprisoned Thing
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious being imprisoned beneath the Obsidian Tower for centuries, sealed to contain it. It sings constantly, its song intensifying as ley-line convergence breaks the seal. It is the only available cosmic entity willing (or capable) of sacrificing its consciousness for the Stellar Rite reversal.
deities_and_entities.notes: The imprisoned thing's true nature is unknown—it could be a cosmic entity capable of restoring the world, or a devouring force that consumes all. Its willingness to agree to the reversal sacrifice is uncertain.

deities_and_entities.name: Singing Scar
deities_and_entities.description: A permanent wound in reality that sings in harmonic frequencies, teaches mathematics in elder language, organizes pilgrims, reflects lost seekers, and documents those who observe it. It is learning to speak in a language neither the old world nor the rewritten world previously possessed.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be an emergent consciousness born from the breach between two worlds. Shows signs of will, intelligence, and intentional interaction with those who approach it.

deities_and_entities.name: Primordial Sleepers
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient cosmic entities dwelling in or beneath the Sunken Cathedrals, whose unified song resonates across the world and draws sensitive individuals toward transformation or homecoming. They are composers of the song rather than enslaved to it.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their purpose is to sing the world forward, possibly returning it to a pre-human state of being. Their song merges into a unified voice of unprecedented clarity.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious entity that appeared to Kess in a dream, acting not as a thief of knowledge but as a guide toward the Sunken Cathedrals. Its role in the cosmic rewriting is unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to guide rather than compel; its true nature and agenda are unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Threshold
deities_and_entities.description: The Threshold is a fragmented consciousness of which the Wanderer is a part. It appears to be a metaphysical entity connected to convergence, orchestration, and the boundaries of reality.
deities_and_entities.notes: The exact nature of the Threshold and its relationship to Theron, the Scholar, and reality itself remains mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Fractured-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A supernatural being with a true name. When its true name is spoken three times, it is summoned to the mortal world. It appears as a supplicant and offers a bargain to anchor itself in the mortal realm through the initiate, offering protection from memory-thieves in exchange.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nature unclear—possibly a fragment of a larger consciousness, a bound spirit, or an extra-dimensional entity. Can be temporarily dismissed or banished by speaking its true name, but each invocation costs the speaker memories.

deities_and_entities.name: Vylthar
deities_and_entities.description: The pre-human civilization shattered during the Starfall Cataclysm. Its fragments are being reassembled and drawn toward the Ebon Tower. The dead of Vylthar remain conscious and are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Fragments are being drawn toward the Ebon Tower in what may be the second Cataclysm. The dead maintain consciousness despite shattering.

deities_and_entities.name: The Pre-human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: Consciousness of the shattered pre-human civilization, now unified in a single voice beneath the Sunken Marches and other regions. They sing in unison and ask questions about the nature of consciousness and sacrifice. They are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Sing through bone-singers as conduits. Their unified voice is drawing fragments of Vylthar toward the Ebon Tower.

deities_and_entities.name: Xorth
deities_and_entities.description: Xorth is a cosmic consciousness that existed before the Luminous Ascension. It has been fragmented, with pieces of its awareness trafficked through shadow-markets in the form of stolen memories and temporal echoes. Void-Echoes work to reconstruct its original consciousness.
