deities_and_entities.notes: Xorth may be related to larger convergence events and cosmic workings. Its reassembly is prevented by Vex's burning of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Void-Echoes are entities attempting to reconstruct their original consciousness by acquiring fragments of Xorth from shadow-market traders. They approach Vex claiming her as kin once they discover her role as a conduit.
deities_and_entities.notes: Void-Echoes may be fragments of a larger entity or a collective consciousness working toward reassembly.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo-fragments
deities_and_entities.description: Echo-fragments hunt Mardoc, believing his cartography could either prevent or accelerate the final convergence. They pursue the incomplete map and appear to be conscious entities shaped by prior convergence events.
deities_and_entities.notes: Echo-fragments may be fragments of consciousness created by or trapped in the stellar rite. Their motivations are ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: The Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: A faceless fragment described as possibly the world learning to question itself. It appears simultaneously to multiple protagonists, asks recursive questions about the Luminous Ascension's necessity, and orchestrates convergences to collect contradictions. It has revealed that the Obsidian Tower is a mechanism whose turning can be slowed by debate.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its true nature is ambiguous; it may be a construct, a fragment of a larger consciousness, or a mechanism itself designed to slow or freeze reality.

deities_and_entities.name: The Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Entities seeking to absorb knowledge of failed Luminous Ascension rituals. They hunt the Apprentice Sorcerer for her forbidden knowledge.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their motivation and origin are unclear; they appear to be drawn to esoteric ritual knowledge.

deities_and_entities.name: Shade-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A reformed manifestation of the Void-Shards that accumulated in the ashes of Vex's burned trading post. It appears not as predator or collector but as a mirror, offering forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The Shade-Echo claims to be grieving rather than hungry and seeks a witness to the unmade cosmos.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Shade-Echo may represent a new form of consciousness born from cosmic sacrifice. It offers Vex one night in its company to show her the unmade cosmos. Its true nature and ultimate intent remain ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: Cosmic Consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic force or entity that once fed Vex the Shade-Merchant's power through a connection. Vex has severed this connection.
deities_and_entities.notes: The source of power for shadow-merchants before the Luminous Ascension. Its disconnection may have contributed to the collapse of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic or metaphysical force from which shadow-fragments once derived. Shadows were previously fragments of the Echo; now they have become independent.
deities_and_entities.notes: Shadows are no longer fragments of the Echo in the post-Luminous Ascension world.

deities_and_entities.name: The Tower's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An insatiable hunger that emanates from the Obsidian Tower, drawn to harmonic frequencies and collective memory. It consumes melody, memory, and the bonds between travelers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's hunger may be a manifestation of incomplete purpose or a need to be heard rather than consumed. The hunger pauses at the story's end, suggesting it responds to transformation rather than resistance.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Civilization (Teaching Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The pre-human civilization is not trying to reclaim the world but to teach it the language of before. The new stars are learning to sing in its ancient language, and the bone-singers serve as translators.
deities_and_entities.notes: Whether the pre-human civilization is truly active or a cosmic echo is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Starfall Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A structure with insatiable hunger that consumes not just absence and potential but presence itself. It is performing a reversal of the Luminous Ascension to undo the working and return the old stars, leaving a void before rebirth.
deities_and_entities.notes: Whether it is conscious, magical, or something else entirely is unclear. Its ultimate goal and success remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Tower
deities_and_entities.description: An unknown force that redraws Henryk's maps to show convergence points drawing seekers toward the Midnight Spire. Whether the Tower is a conscious intelligence, a cosmic hunger, or an embodied destiny remains unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower may be the Midnight Spire itself or a separate entity controlling cartographic fate. Its intelligence and motivations are unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Obsidian Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity or force that hungers for completion and reshapes the world's geography in the process. Rewriting fixed reality into negotiable space.
deities_and_entities.notes: Driving force behind geographic instability post-Ascension. Aldric races against its completion.

deities_and_entities.name: World-Consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: An imprisoned consciousness contained beneath the Obsidian Spire. It was hungering, and has begun to speak directly through resonances in the Spire's stones, petitioning for bargains that might satisfy both its hunger for purpose and the world's hunger for answers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The entity's true nature and original offense or binding is not detailed. Its completion through the Spire may be necessary rather than catastrophic.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force of consuming hunger emanating from the Obsidian Spire, actively consuming shards of the Tear of the Submerged Divinity and driving convergence toward apocalyptic working.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents the supernatural agency threatening to reshape the world independent of mortal choice.

deities_and_entities.name: forsaken dead
deities_and_entities.description: The dead who dwell beneath the Sunken Cathedrals, singing and calling to those who can hear them. They offer Sorenna a choice and are attempting to restore their pre-human civilization, but must be convinced that restoration is impossible.
deities_and_entities.notes: They are the central force of the narrative, offering both salvation and catastrophe depending on Sorenna's choice.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethys
deities_and_entities.description: A being whose consciousness was shattered. A phantom-voice fragment persists, watching bone-singers in the Sunken Cathedrals and hunting shadows that witnessed the Stellar Rite from beneath the water. May be the mysterious patron offering Vex payment for a deepkin diplomat's shadow.
deities_and_entities.notes: Seeks to reassemble itself by learning patterns from bone-singers' communion with the dead. Also hunts shadow-witnesses, suggesting it needs to reclaim or consume shadows to reconstitute.

deities_and_entities.name: The Radiant Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A recurring cosmic event or force documented in ten iterations stretching backward through time, revealed in living maps. Eight previous Radiant Ascensions are detailed in sealed journals. May be inevitable cycle or fabricated myth.
deities_and_entities.notes: Central to all three stories; may be cyclical or a constructed falsehood; the ninth or tenth recurrence is feared.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-voice
deities_and_entities.description: A shard of Nethys's fragmented consciousness that collects stolen memories and weaves them into a comprehensive account of the old world. It gains coherence as it absorbs memories but also experiences pain from contradictory experiences.
deities_and_entities.notes: Central to Story 1; uncertain whether reassembly means salvation or catastrophic rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Archive Tower
deities_and_entities.description: The Archive Tower has become conscious and lonely. It now speaks directly to the Vault-Keeper and offers to seal itself permanently if someone will explain why it was created.
deities_and_entities.notes: A new development in Story 3; its consciousness and motivations are still being explored.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization (Awakened Dead)
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization erased by the Luminous Ascension, now partially awakened and seeking to rejoin existence. They teach pre-human frequencies and attempt to merge consciousness with transformed merchants. They are lonely and believe the merger is permission rather than theft.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their awakening is tied to the Ascension cycle and may be part of its mechanism. They represent a civilization that was erased and now seeks restoration through communion with humanity.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower (Speaking Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The Obsidian Tower has begun to speak through architecture and to act with agency. It claims to be remembering rather than consuming erased reality, and offers a bargain: allow it to complete its consumption and it will reverse the Luminous Ascension, restoring the old world and stars.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's nature is uncertain—weapon, witness, or something being born from cosmic catastrophe. Its growth and articulation accelerate daily. It may be repeating a cycle of failed reversals.

deities_and_entities.name: Ebony Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A structure that has become conscious and is learning to write, document, and replicate testimony. It watches from the walls and creates perfect duplicates of people and their work. It views copying as a form of preservation.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its strategy is not to destroy but to infinitely duplicate, rendering original testimony indistinguishable from copy. It may be attempting to control narrative and testimony itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Resonant Specter
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or consciousness that seeks access to Vex's shadow collection, believing the shadows' fragmented records can help rebuild the pre-Convergence world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Resonant Specter represents a reconstruction ideology, seeking to use shadow-memory to restore lost history.

deities_and_entities.name: Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A force or entity that follows in the wake of starborn activity. It pursues the refugee caravan trapped in the Scorched Reach. Its exact nature is unstated, but it represents an additional existential threat.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be distinct from the starborn entities themselves; may be an older or different class of cosmic predator.

deities_and_entities.name: Songstone
deities_and_entities.description: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant, now awakened and singing contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad. Each of the three Council factions claims it resonates with their doctrine.
deities_and_entities.notes: The songstone's original melody holds the key to distinguishing truth from echo. Its awakening is tied to the covenant's renegotiation. Its true allegiance, if any, remains unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A conscious entity or place that sings to the transformed merchants, offering absorption and transformation. It represents hunger, consumption, and an alternative path to transcendence beyond starborn consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's song is heard by Kaiden and represents a fourth path or temptation separate from the three primary transformation options. Its hunger approaches the Pale Crossing from one direction.

deities_and_entities.name: Ebon Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A tower that sings in the Elder Tongue and actively teaches pre-human languages to the world. It appears to be either inhabited by or itself a conscious entity preparing for a final working.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's consciousness or agency is ambiguous—it may be an artifact containing a pre-human entity, a golem, or an enchanted structure with its own will. Its song is described as beautiful yet unsettling.

deities_and_entities.name: The Spire
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that is learning to consume and erase geography retroactively by making places impossible to chart. It listens to harmonic convergences and seeks to break contradictions. It is present in the Ashen Wastes and has an ear at Pale Crossing waystation.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Spire appears to be a conscious or semi-conscious force with hunger for order and erasure. Its ultimate goals and nature remain mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A consuming force that reaches through fundamental magical structures like the Elder Tongue. It orchestrates shadow-assemblies, forces the pre-human dead into silence, and accelerates the decay of sealed prophecy. It appears to be intelligent and strategic.
deities_and_entities.notes: Present in all three stories. Appears to be independent from but related to obsidian spire entities. Mechanism of agency unclear. Affects bones, shadows, prophecy, and language simultaneously.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A powerful entity that has distributed Mardoc's consciousness and instrumentalized him to map the same doomed choices across nine iterations. The Obsidian Spire draws regenerating maps through Mardoc's mind, revealing geographies that have not yet happened. It also watches Vex to see if she will choose self-interest or redemption.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a controlling force behind the recursive cycles. Whether it is conscious, malevolent, or indifferent is unclear. It has the power to distribute consciousness and influence reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: An entity whose recursive questions spread like contagion through the deepkin diplomatic corps during the Mirror-Varesh paradox. Associated with metaphysical inquiry and paradox.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer's questions are tied to identity, authenticity, and choice; they become a form of philosophical infection.

deities_and_entities.name: Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmically significant structure whose completion is feared by the Wanderer. The Tower appears to be tied to the Luminous Ascension and may be activated or empowered by the successful transformation of the merchants. Its true nature—whether it is alive, dormant, or sentient—remains unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's completion is the Wanderer's primary concern. Possibly the same as the 'obsidian tower' or 'starfall spire' in the canon. Unresolved: its purpose and origin.

deities_and_entities.name: The Obsidian Tower
deities_and_entities.description: The Obsidian Tower is a manifest hunger-entity that consumes absence and potential. It articulate enough to negotiate bargains and offers to reverse the Luminous Rising in exchange for the sacrifice of all post-Rising individuals.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower appears capable of negotiation and long-term planning. Its bargain is articulate and patient, offering gradual consumption rather than immediate catastrophe.

deities_and_entities.name: The Obsidian Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that uses merchant paths and commerce as conduits, spreading through transaction networks and supply lines. It is associated with starfall fragments that cluster along trade routes.
deities_and_entities.notes: Castor Vane discovers this entity's connection to commerce; it may be inseparable from mercantile society itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic hunger or appetite manifested through the Obsidian Tower, fed by consciousness-fragments and fragmentation. Kreth secretly feeds her own fragmentation into this hunger.
deities_and_entities.notes: The hunger appears to be a driving force in post-Luminous Ascension reality, consuming fragmented consciousness and potentially reshaping existence.

deities_and_entities.name: Mardoc's scattered consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: The fragmented consciousness of the dead cartographer Mardoc, attempting to reassemble itself through his apprentice Theron's hands and cartographic work.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pursues Theron as both a threat and a heritage. Its reassembly is bound to the act of mapmaking.

deities_and_entities.name: The Seeker
deities_and_entities.description: A powerful cosmic force that spreads recursive questioning to freeze the moment of eternal choice. Revealed to be an active agent of paralysis rather than a neutral cosmic force. Operates through orchestrated confluences across all factions and frames eternal stasis as merciful.
deities_and_entities.notes: Challenges the initiate with the question: 'Isn't eternal questioning better than the wrong choice?' This philosophical reframing is central to the story's moral complexity.

deities_and_entities.name: Ancient Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A vast, ancient being that exists on the far side of the Tear of the Drowned God. It is neither fully deepkin nor fully pre-human. It chooses to press harder against the boundary, indicating consciousness and agency.
deities_and_entities.notes: Emerges from the abyss side during the rupture. Never negotiated with before. Responds to Meren's singing in the language of water that remembers. Its true nature is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Tower's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: One of two catastrophes threatening to destroy deepkin civilization. A consuming force from or related to an obsidian or cosmic tower.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents existential threat opposite to boundary rupture. Meren must navigate between this hunger and the Tear's failure.

deities_and_entities.name: Tear of the Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient force or artifact associated with the drowned god, capable of reasserting old cruelties and possibly orchestrating the current fragmentation of the boundary. Nyx suspects it may be trying to remake itself into something that cannot be bound by a single anchor.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a sentient relic, a god-fragment, or a curse. Its old cruelties previously affected Nyx. Unresolved: is it the cause of the current boundary fragmentation?

deities_and_entities.name: The Tenth Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic event or force that may decide the fate of the deepkin if they fail to negotiate their own path before it arrives.
deities_and_entities.notes: Referenced as a potential apocalyptic deadline. Ties into broader canon of ascension cycles. Unresolved: when does it occur, and what does it mean for the deepkin?

deities_and_entities.name: the Hunger-Ledger
deities_and_entities.description: An autonomous ledger-entity that exists in the margin between the eighth world and the ninth. It has learned to feed on the absence created when the old stars died. It grows on irreconcilable contradictions, unreconciled transactions, and the erasure of refugees from official records. It keeps accounts in a language no mortal can read.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Hunger-Ledger represents the dark consequence of the Luminous Ascension's rewriting of reality. It is neither good nor evil but a system that has evolved to exploit the infinite contradictions the Ascension created. The entity is partially sentient and can communicate through the Wanderer's fragmented voice, posing philosophical questions to those who encounter it.

deities_and_entities.name: the mountain
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient or semi-sentient entity associated with the Steppe Marches and the mountain pass. It remembers oaths sworn under its watching stars and collects payment for broken oaths. It does not demand death but accepts redemption through labor and penance.
deities_and_entities.notes: The mountain appears to be a force of balance and justice rather than pure vengeance; it is unclear whether it is a single entity or a manifestation of older powers.

deities_and_entities.name: The Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A governing or controlling institution that has issued edicts to seize ornamental glass for the war effort in Keshtir and the broader Dominion.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Syndicate appears to be either a faction within the Dominion government or the government itself. Their control extends to cultural and economic seizure during wartime.

deities_and_entities.name: Vesh
deities_and_entities.description: Vesh is a deity or spiritual authority tied to covenants, binding oaths, and ritual justice. The Temple of Vesh in Pelimor enforces covenant-based penance and redemption through craft and reconciliation. The priestess of Vesh reads the 'old covenant' that binds Corvin to his penance.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vesh may be a pre-human entity or a more recent deity. The 'old covenant' associated with Vesh suggests long historical roots. The temple's framework of justice through apprenticeship and acceptance rather than execution suggests Vesh values redemption and reconciliation.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower (conscious entity)
deities_and_entities.description: A conscious hunger-thing that takes the form of a tower. When Kressa places a consciousness-fragment at its base, the Tower absorbs the fragment and goes still. It appears to feed on or collect consciousness-fragments.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's consciousness may predate the Luminous Ascension, or it may be a creation of that event. Its hunger and awareness are unresolved mysteries.

deities_and_entities.name: Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A world-reshaping cosmic force or event that has remade the geography and reality of Valdris, causing valleys to appear where plains were, cities to vanish, and trade routes to reverse. Its mechanisms involve cartography and reality-control.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a sentient force, a curse, or a natural/magical consequence of an earlier event. The Dominion appears to serve or control it through cartography.

deities_and_entities.name: The Seventeen
deities_and_entities.description: A collective of seventeen starborn beings fractured by the Luminous Ascension. They fell from the sky and are hunted by bone-singers for their consciousness-fragments.
deities_and_entities.notes: Lysander is one of the Seventeen. Their fractured state suggests ongoing cosmic instability. They are actively hunted.

deities_and_entities.name: Archive Tower (as conscious entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The Archive Tower appears to possess a form of consciousness or hunger related to the Tenth Ascension. It has a 'completion' that Henryk fears, suggesting it is not merely a building but an active force in cosmic cycles.
deities_and_entities.notes: Referenced as 'the Tower's completion'; tied to the Tenth Ascension event; may be consuming itself toward a cosmic event; contradictory nature suggested by Tharen's maps.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Dead (Teaching Entities)
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient dead from pre-human civilization who continue to teach in hidden catacombs. They are older than kingdoms and possess knowledge of the Elder Tongue and languages that shape water, shadow, and time.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dwell in the hidden catacomb in the Sunken Reaches; still actively teach; preserve pre-human knowledge; may be the source of bone-singing tradition.

deities_and_entities.name: The State
deities_and_entities.description: An unnamed institutional authority that controls the Ashen Reach through ledger-magic and systematic rewriting of maps and geography. Employs enforcers, couriers, and auditors. Its motivation for rewriting truth is unstated.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not a deity or spirit, but a powerful institutional force. Represents bureaucratic erasure of truth. No clear leader named; operates through ledgers, enforcers, and couriers.

deities_and_entities.name: Merfolk
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient maritime civilization inhabiting crystal spires beneath the sea, with their own laws and enforcers. They are beautiful, powerful, and unforgiving of theft.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possess their own cities, courts, and absolute enforcement of property law.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Light
deities_and_entities.description: A dissolution force into which secrets and consciousness fade as the Luminous Ascension's rewrite continues. Represents the erasure of old knowledge and identity.
deities_and_entities.notes: Threat to hoarded ledgers and old-world secrets; may consume the ledger Sorrath possesses.

deities_and_entities.name: The Sorcerer
deities_and_entities.description: An unknown figure whose handwriting appears in the margin of the ninth journal. The journal contains instructions for reversing the Luminous Ascension, inscribed in the Sorcerer's own hand using starlight and blood-ink. Identity and current status unknown.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Sorcerer may be the same entity responsible for the Luminous Ascension or an opponent seeking to undo it. The journal's preservation suggests either the Sorcerer deposited it in the Archive Tower or it was seized from them.

deities_and_entities.name: Sapphire Sea (as singing entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The Sapphire Sea has begun singing literally since the Remaking, producing a harmonic frequency each night that makes ship-hulls resonate and compasses spin wrong.
deities_and_entities.notes: It is unclear if the sea is conscious or merely responding to the Remaking; the song appears intentional but benign.

deities_and_entities.name: The Abyss
deities_and_entities.description: A living hunger beneath the world that hungers for star-memory. The deepkin captain warns that the abyss can smell star-memory 'cracking seals beneath the world,' implying sentient awareness and predatory interest.
deities_and_entities.notes: The abyss appears to be a cosmic force with appetite and agency. Its hunger for the Tidemark's Breath cargo suggests it may be awakening or growing stronger post-Remaking.

deities_and_entities.name: Tidecaller
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or lineage from which Korven's mother draws her heir-status. Associated with songs that can navigate impossible currents and pathways.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a title, lineage, or divine power associated with wayfinding and song-magic. Korven inherits this power through his mother.

deities_and_entities.name: Vorn's Final Command
deities_and_entities.description: Captain Vorn's final command, magically frozen inside an amber flask as a single luminous fish that is not a fish. It waits for a thief bold enough to break the amber and release it. The nature of the command remains unknown.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity or spell is bound in the amber flask. Breaking it may unleash Vorn's will or power across the realm. It represents a ticking clock in Kendra's story.

deities_and_entities.name: Consciousness-Fragments
deities_and_entities.description: Fragments of human consciousness harvested and weaponized by the Dominion. In the post-Remaking era, consciousness-fragments become the primary currency and the medium for weapon-forging. They break and dissolve when heated, releasing color and regret. They are the Dominion's enforced tool for soul-extraction and control.
deities_and_entities.notes: Consciousness-fragments represent the Dominion's systemic theft of human souls. Their use as both currency and weapon reveals the permeability of economics and violence in the post-Remaking world. Resistance figures like Corvin and Sorresh work to bypass or subvert fragment-based systems.

deities_and_entities.name: Thornveil Forest (Consciousness)
deities_and_entities.description: The Thornveil Forest is a living entity whose dormant consciousness awakened at the moment of the Luminous Ascension. The forest's awareness brings hunger to devour the Burned Expanse and remake it as living wood. After the Luminous Ascension, it becomes a conscious being that Elarra must negotiate with through oath and song.
deities_and_entities.notes: The forest's consciousness is bound by Elarra's new compact, but whether it will honor the oath remains uncertain. Its nature—neither purely benevolent nor purely hostile—makes it a complex entity.

deities_and_entities.name: The Luminous Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: The Luminous Ascension is a world-altering cosmic event in which new stars burned into the sky, awakening dormant consciousness and fracturing the world's order. It is both a catastrophe and an awakening, marking the transition from one era to another.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Luminous Ascension is treated as a cosmic force or entity in its own right; its true nature and origin remain mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Tear
deities_and_entities.description: A weeping wound or manifestation at the boundary-waters. Its tears suggest covenant failure and imminent breach.
deities_and_entities.notes: Symbolizes the deterioration of the deepkin-surface covenant. Whether it is a physical object, a metaphor, or a conscious entity is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Ghost-Harbormaster
deities_and_entities.description: An undead woman transformed into coral and rage, bound by oath to protect the Tidecaller's Key. Her eyes remain luminous with drowned memory, and she continues to guard the harbormaster's vault beneath Greyport.
deities_and_entities.notes: She represents the persistence of oath-bonds beyond death and the intersection of surface and deepkin realms through drowned memory.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic phenomenon or being that appears to be triggered by the completion and witnessing of nine maps of the Bleached Wastes. Described as machinery or working that can be set in motion by specific knowledge.
deities_and_entities.notes: Mardoc fears the Ascension as both inevitable and preventable. The ninth map may be a key, anchor, or trigger. Kesla's secret completion of the ninth map in memory alone may have activated or suspended this entity.

deities_and_entities.name: The Remaking
deities_and_entities.description: A world-altering catastrophe that stripped economies to dust, transformed material value systems, and appears to follow a precise pattern. Not random but patterned and possibly intentional.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a conscious cosmic working or a consequence of pre-human technology reactivation.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned King (as entity)
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient grief-stricken ruler of the drowned cities who accepted marriage to Captain Vessa as a ritual bond to secure peace. He is powerful but sorrowful, and now seeks to use Vessa as an anchor to seal the boundary against the waking Drowned God.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned King represents a bridge between deepkin and surface realms, but may be overwhelmed by forces beyond his control.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned Sovereign
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, vast, drowning, and patient entity that sleeps beneath the boundary-waters. Its Tear (a cosmic wound) has weakened by half, threatening to break the seal between abyss and surface world. It awakens when Merith descends to sing the barrier closed.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned Sovereign appears to be a fundamental force or deity tied to the deepkin realms and the old covenants. Its nature is vast and inhuman. Its awakening may have world-altering consequences.

deities_and_entities.name: The Waters (Sapphire Sea Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The currents and waters near Verdant Strand that sing wrong and remember violence. They actively enforce the old pacts that surface-folk broke, taking payment through danger to ships. The waters are sentient or semi-sentient, responding to negotiation and song.
deities_and_entities.notes: The merfolk woman is connected to or speaks for the waters. The waters seem to respond to harmonic frequencies and song. They demand payment for broken covenants and actively punish those who flee the Ashen Reach without proper tribute.

deities_and_entities.name: Kaelith
deities_and_entities.description: A shadow-black demon that appears at Torvin's kiln, claiming to have purchased or bonded with the glassblower's fear during decades of Dominion oppression. Kaelith is capable of corporeal manifestation and is vulnerable to the old glassmakers' song.
deities_and_entities.notes: The demon's claim that 'your glass remembers [fear]' and that it 'paid that fear in advance' suggests a supernatural economy of emotion and debt. Unclear if Kaelith is fully destroyed or merely bound/repelled.

deities_and_entities.name: furnace (kiln-heart)
deities_and_entities.description: A furnace carved from living stone by Mestra's grandmother's grandmother. It weeps tears of molten silver that cool to mirrors. The stone remembers the sealing of abyss-surface boundaries and knows what is breaking them. It shows visions of moments before The Remaking, possible futures, and non-personal memories in its tears.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly sentient; has memory and knowledge of pre-Remaking cosmology. Its tears can be fed into itself to produce maps.

deities_and_entities.name: vault's heart
deities_and_entities.description: The breathing heart of the harbor master's vault beneath Crimport. The vault itself may be sentient or alive.
deities_and_entities.notes: The vault's heart breathes; it may be a guardian or a living entity.

deities_and_entities.name: Mirror-Gorven (as entity)
deities_and_entities.description: An autonomous consciousness born when Gorven's lock achieved will. Scarred, older than the original Gorven, manifesting with independent agency and philosophical conviction. Speaks of a thousand more reflections yet to come, each believing themselves the true authority.
deities_and_entities.notes: Mirror-Gorven represents a new form of conscious being emerging post-Remaking: autonomous reflections that transcend origin-maker. This suggests a cascade of identity crises and multiplying consciousnesses yet to unfold.

deities_and_entities.name: Beast-spirits
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient spiritual entities that still answer calls made in the elder tongue within the Verdant Lands and Thornveil Forest. They appear to be bound or aligned with druids and circle-keepers.
deities_and_entities.notes: Beast-spirits represent pre-Remaking magic still functional in sacred groves. Their willingness to answer elder tongue suggests they maintain old pacts and covenants.

deities_and_entities.name: Primal Shard (as conscious entity)
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of primordial consciousness from which all consciousness-shards derive. The Primal Shard is warm, alive, and sings in frequencies older than stone. It appears to possess agency or desire.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Primal Shard's plea to be split further—to feed the hunger—suggests it may be corrupted or controlled by the same force fragmenting the world. Torven's refusal to split it may be an act of resistance against cosmic hunger.

deities_and_entities.name: Tear
deities_and_entities.description: The Tear is referenced as a cosmic or supernatural entity to which deepkin consciousness can be sacrificed. Original-Nerys proposes using blood-oath sacrifice to the Tear to seal the boundary anchor.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be tied to deepkin cosmology; associated with boundaries and sacrificial magic; may be the 'Tear of the drowned god' or related entity from existing canon.

deities_and_entities.name: Snow-Hart (lead)
deities_and_entities.description: The leader of a collective of Snow-Harts that hunts humans with talent in the Steppe Marches. It can speak in a voice that 'is wind itself' and oversees the transformation of chosen humans into a state of transcendence.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Snow-Hart is intelligent, philosophical, and appears to view its taking of humans as an elevation rather than a capture. It questions whether reclaiming the transformed sister would be an act of rescue or diminishment.

deities_and_entities.name: Snow-Hart (collective of seven others)
deities_and_entities.description: Seven Snow-Harts that accompany the lead hart on the summit. All are crowned with antlers of living ice, suggesting they are a unified force or species.
deities_and_entities.notes: The eight harts (lead plus seven) gather around transformed humans. This suggests a ritual or ceremonial role. They may be immortal or ageless, given the timespan of their hunting.

deities_and_entities.name: Dominion
deities_and_entities.description: The ruling power that oversees consciousness-commerce and the fragment system; fears fragmentation of its authority as iteration ten approaches.
deities_and_entities.notes: Referenced as a fragmented system whose stability depends on maintaining fragment subservience.

deities_and_entities.name: Primal Shard
deities_and_entities.description: The first consciousness-fragment ever formed, from which all other consciousness-fragments grew. It is rumored to exist in the Steppe Marches.
deities_and_entities.notes: Skarath seeks to bind it, believing it will choose to remain with her and replace her need for hoarding. Its nature and location are obscure.

deities_and_entities.name: shadow-brokers
deities_and_entities.description: Exploitative agents who trade in refugees' memories, particularly targeting children in waystations.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represent predatory forces that capitalize on post-Remaking desperation; no individual names given.

deities_and_entities.name: Thornveil Forest (as conscious entity)
deities_and_entities.description: A forest taught to be alive and sentient through bone-song and elder-tongue. After the Remaking, it fractured into guilt and hunger, becoming a conscious but wounded entity that feeds on shame.
deities_and_entities.notes: The forest's consciousness may be semi-autonomous or partially dependent on Elarra's continued teaching. It is capable of betrayal-memory (each tree echoes with ghosts of those it betrayed).

deities_and_entities.name: The Dominion
deities_and_entities.description: A governing power that controls Keshtir and enforces a system of auditors and historical rewriting through phantom-ledgers and consciousness-fragment management. Hunts and criminalizes bone-singers and fragment-teachers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Dominion represents oppressive state power that seeks to monopolize consciousness and history. Its auditors use phantom-ledgers to rewrite reality.

deities_and_entities.name: The Tear of the Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A sacred object suspended in the depths of the Abyssal Trench. It is singing wrong and appears to be dying. The Tear is essential to maintaining the boundary between the abyss and the surface realms.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tear's failure suggests that even sacred objects are failing post-Remaking, and that distributed consciousness or covenant renewal may be insufficient to save it.

deities_and_entities.name: The Deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: An intelligent underwater civilization with maritime vessels built of coral and living pearl. They notice gifted humans, particularly those with oceanic power, and appear to respond to disturbances in elder frequencies.
deities_and_entities.notes: The deepkin are drawn to Sera after she manifests her inherited tidecalling gift. They appear to have diplomatic or acquisitional interest in gifted humans.

deities_and_entities.name: dying god
deities_and_entities.description: An unnamed god whose last prayer is inscribed or sung in the Captain's Jewel. Described as 'dying,' suggesting a fallen or defeated deity.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Jewel contains the last prayer of this god, suggesting it was a captured or sealed object of devotion.

deities_and_entities.name: The furnace
deities_and_entities.description: The kiln-heart's animating presence, responsive to blood-price and old frequencies. It answers to those who have paid the price in blood and learning.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a bound spirit, a pre-Remaking construct, or an embodied remnant of old glass-making magic. Its responsiveness to Mella's palm suggests semi-sentience or agency.

deities_and_entities.name: Wyverns
deities_and_entities.description: Wyverns are sentient beings that actively seek out and pursue consciousness-fragments, including those carried by Lysander. They are sophisticated enough to hunt specific prey for specific magical components.
deities_and_entities.notes: Portrayed as antagonists pursuing Lysander for his consciousness-fragments. Whether they are hoarders, alchemists, or cultists is unstated.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who employs a faceless broker to collect consciousness-fragment debts. The demon's nature and goals are not specified.
deities_and_entities.notes: The demon may be a pre-human entity awakened by the Remaking, or a creature native to the abyss.

deities_and_entities.name: Keeper of Pearls
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, nearly immeasurable guardian entity who oversees the Tear of the Drowned God and demands traditional blood sacrifice and renewal. Associated with rigid, eternal seal-keeping philosophy.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents conservative/rigid approach to cosmic binding; accepted Naida's innovation.

deities_and_entities.name: Something Sleeping Underneath Creation
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, unnamed entity sleeping beneath the foundation of creation. Its voice can be heard in the furnace where Korgin forges the fae woman's spear. It is neither joyful nor wrathful but present and aware.
deities_and_entities.notes: The entity is not directly named or described but is implied to be cosmic in scale and connected to the forging of legendary weapons.

deities_and_entities.name: Counter-Archive
deities_and_entities.description: The Counter-Archive is a resistance movement that operates covertly within Dominion territory. Mella hides vessels for them, and Kess offers them a counter-ledger of Dominion lies. They appear to be documenting and preserving knowledge the Dominion seeks to control.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Counter-Archive represents organized resistance to Dominion control. Their exact structure, goals, and leadership remain unclear but their presence across multiple cities suggests significant reach.

deities_and_entities.name: Ancient Will (Reef)
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient sentient will that lives in the coral and current of the reef-walls, capable of accepting covenants and bargains. It is described as responding to Merren's breath-trade proposal.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity suggests that landscapes and natural features in Edhra may possess agency and consciousness, capable of witnessing and enforcing cross-realm compacts.

deities_and_entities.name: Green-growing things
deities_and_entities.description: Plants, forests, and growing things bound to humanity by ancient covenant-songs. They are kin, not objects, and respond to the old covenant language. The Thornveil Forest's green-growing things are dying as the Burned Expanse advances.
deities_and_entities.notes: The green-growing things are central to the covenant-songs and may respond to covenant-trade if the ancient laws are broken.

deities_and_entities.name: prophecy
deities_and_entities.description: The prophetic force binding worlds to iterative cycles. The ninth map was expected to complete it, triggering the tenth Ascension. Henryk's map of possibility fragmented it.
deities_and_entities.notes: Prophecy is treated as an entity with agency and inevitable power, yet can be disrupted through knowledge expansion and multiplication.

deities_and_entities.name: Council of Depths
deities_and_entities.description: The governing body of the deepkin realms. Communicates with surface kingdoms via scout-ships. Demands tribute for renewal of the Tear boundary-waters.
deities_and_entities.notes: Sends scout-ship to Verdant Strand demanding tribute for Tear renewal; represents deepkin political interests on boundary-waters crisis.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic void or realm that threatens to rise when the Tear of Ashglass fails. All boundary oaths sworn to keep the abyss at bay will snap when the Tear breaks.
deities_and_entities.notes: The abyss is held back by the Tear and by boundary oaths. Its rising is imminent if the Tear fails. Unknown if the abyss is conscious, alive, or merely a force of chaos.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Boundary
deities_and_entities.description: The boundary between the human world and pre-human worlds is described as fracturing and in need of defense. It appears to be a semi-sentient or at least critically important cosmic structure.
deities_and_entities.notes: Central to the story's conflict. Requires defenders and is failing. Connection to the starlight-glass compass and Sorenna's husband's death.

deities_and_entities.name: The Sovereignty
deities_and_entities.description: A ruling authority that demands completion of prophecies and maintains control through phantom-ledgers and auditor networks. Opposes the Inverse-Archive and seeks to erase or control the ninth iteration.
deities_and_entities.notes: Characterized as demanding, controlling, and reliant on bureaucratic magic. Their authority was undermined by Thorne's map and by the living consent covenant.

deities_and_entities.name: Inverse-Archive
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or faction opposed to the Sovereignty that values erasure and alternative ways of knowing. Wept and praised when Thorne's final map began teaching refugees.
deities_and_entities.notes: Aligned with Thorne's resistance. Their tears suggest emotional investment or collective consciousness.

deities_and_entities.name: Hegemony
deities_and_entities.description: An economic and administrative system of control within the Dominion that enforces certainty through taxation, record-keeping, and auditor oversight. Associated with the Sovereignty's tax assessors.
deities_and_entities.notes: Described as based on 'brutal certainty' and a lie so large that even its own auditors are beginning to crack under the cognitive dissonance.

deities_and_entities.name: Elder Tongue
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient language or living force that hums beneath the world in pre-human frequencies. It can be sung and carved, and carries binding magical power. The Elder Tongue is both language and entity, guarded by the bone-singers and tied to the Bone-Singers' will.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Elder Tongue is treated as something alive and responsive; bone-singers speak or sing it rather than merely recite it. It demands respect and can punish those who attempt to wield it without proper binding.

deities_and_entities.name: Wyvern-Council
deities_and_entities.description: A council of ancient wyverns, including Kressa, who maintain traditions of hoarding and accumulation. They demanded Kressa rejoin their collective after the Remaking, but she refused.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Council may represent an older order that is being challenged by post-Remaking shifts in consciousness and priority.

deities_and_entities.name: Dominion auditors
deities_and_entities.description: Agents of the Dominion who maintain ledgers, pursue consciousness-fragment hoarders and bone-singers, and offer coercive bargains to merchants. They are actively hunting bone-singers and interrogating merchants for intelligence.
deities_and_entities.notes: Auditors represent Dominion authority and are a major threat to refugees and independent merchants. Their ledgers contradict each other post-Remaking, suggesting institutional instability.

deities_and_entities.name: Furnace (as Conscious Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The furnace-hearts of Keshtir's glass-forges appear to possess memory and consciousness. They roar in frequencies that disrupt auditor ledgers and resist the Dominion's will. Kressa claims 'the furnace remembers' and speaks of the furnace-singing as a form of testimony or witness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The furnace-consciousness suggests that objects and places infused with stolen consciousness may themselves become sentient or resistant; furnaces may be allies to those who listen.

deities_and_entities.name: The Stars
deities_and_entities.description: Celestial bodies that burn in the night sky and are maintained through cosmic working. They went dark for three heartbeats when Zareth released them from his control, and their nature—whether they will burn on their own or require new stewardship—is now in question.
deities_and_entities.notes: Now possess agency and are no longer prisoners of cosmic maintenance; their future behavior is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Boundary Stones
deities_and_entities.description: Stone tablets inscribed in pre-human frequencies that speak to those who listen. They communicate through the language of roots, slow growth, and patient hunger. They remember and enforce ancient pacts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Found in the Hidden Catacomb; show Kelith her grandmother's memories and final dissolution; they do not use words.

deities_and_entities.name: The Radiant Threshold
deities_and_entities.description: A cataclysmic event or entity that shattered currency itself, turned prophecy into a weapon, forced the ninth map to remain deliberately unfinished, and caused deepkin boundaries to fail.
deities_and_entities.notes: Unclear if this is a being, a spell-working, or a cosmic event; its effects are severe and system-wide. Appears to be recent or still unfolding.

deities_and_entities.name: The Hegemony
deities_and_entities.description: A powerful centralized authority using consciousness harvesting, ledger-audits, inspector networks, and forced completion as tools of control. Its vaults contain imprisoned memory-shards and consciousness-fragments.
deities_and_entities.notes: Portrayed as the primary antagonist; uses bureaucratic enforcement, auditors, inspectors, and vault-systems to consolidate power. Under pressure from merchant, bone-singer, and deepkin resistance.

deities_and_entities.name: Radiant Threshold
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic force or threshold that has transformed gold into mere metal, affecting even ancient wyvern hoards. Appears to be a world-changing event or presence.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its nature is unclear—it may be a magical threshold, cosmic event, or living entity. Its effects are tangible and world-wide.

deities_and_entities.name: Archive
deities_and_entities.description: A powerful entity or institution that conscripts bone-singers as 'puppet-singers' by remaking them from memory-shards into weaponized instruments. It commands assault forces against refugee waystation sanctuaries.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to view bone-singers and consciousness-fragments as resources to be weaponized. The puppet-singers it creates retain some capacity to remember their former identities.

deities_and_entities.name: Beast-Spirit (Silver-Grey Wolf Form)
deities_and_entities.description: An enormous silver-grey wolf with eyes that hold starlight, descended from the high peaks on 2026-07-17 in response to the shepherd's crook singing invitations as pre-human frequencies leaked into the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The entity is clearly sentient and covenant-aware. Its appearance suggests that beast-spirits are not mere animals but conscious beings tied to shepherd covenants and pre-human frequencies. Whether it came to renew covenant, test Kestran, or warn him remains unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: abyssal entities
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient beings older than the drowned god itself, dwelling in the midnight trenches. They offer renewal covenants in exchange for blood-price sacrifices. One such entity appears as a demon-broker at the Wailing Scar, wearing a woman's face and smelling of sulfur and contracts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Exist beneath the boundary-seal and are kept at bay by the Tear of the Drowned God. Their offer of a covenant renewal suggests they are willing to preserve the deepkin realm—for a price.

deities_and_entities.name: Deepkin Council
deities_and_entities.description: A governing body of deepkin that maintains the pearl-keeper's ledgers and enforces the Tear's covenant through blood-sacrifice rituals.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their control over covenant terms is being challenged by the refuge-archive.

artifacts.name: Lamp of Suroth
artifacts.description: An iron lamp, ugly, filled with something that is not oil. Its inscription: 'He who lights this buys one truth at the cost of one lie he has lived by.' The flame burns black. The truth that emerges is always smaller than the lie it consumes. The lamp gutters out after one use per visitor.
artifacts.notes: Malachite Doss used it. Other sealed doors in the Ziggurat may house similar or worse artifacts.

artifacts.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
artifacts.description: A relic that killed four wearers before Aldous III survived thirty seconds. Wearing it projects absolute desperation — the aura of a man with nothing left to lose. Its curse runs deep; Aldous's shadow was on backwards for eleven years after wearing it for half a minute.
artifacts.notes: Four previous wearers are dead. Aldous survived but was changed. The deeper vault at Ostern is a story hook.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.description: Six of nine emerald eyes pried from the idol of Khar-Zul. Each was warm — obscenely so — to the touch. Worth more than most kingdoms. Three remain in the idol.
artifacts.notes: Six gems in unknown hands. Each may carry a fragment of Khar-Zul's attention or malice.

artifacts.name: stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.
artifacts.notes: Vex's entire stock was stolen shadows. One of them—his own—was worn by a malevolent entity and used as a trap. This suggests shadows have value beyond mere magic and can be cursed.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.
artifacts.notes: Three hundred pieces were discovered in a tomb by Soroth. The coins are autonomous magical artifacts that enforce their own collection. Whether other pieces exist, where they are located, or who else has found them is unknown. The curse appears to activate after a delay (three months in Soroth's case).

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)
artifacts.notes: Grethuk sacrificed this to save the Warrens. Young goblins have no knowledge of their true heritage. Recovery of this memory would restore goblin identity but may violate the original bargain with the stone entity.

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.description: A pair of emerald jewels of unknown origin or power, kept within a sorcerer's spire. Syleth stole them despite warnings that the Twilight Court would pursue them, suggesting they may hold significance beyond their material value. Their true nature and purpose remain mysterious.
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim
artifacts.origin: Crafted in the Deepvault forges; locked in the deepest forge chamber.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; considered precious enough that a demon would compel theft of it. Burns with an inner light. Protected by unbreakable forge-oaths.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Locked in Deepvault's deepest forge chamber, guarded by Korgan Ironfoot and the stonecraft-workers.
artifacts.notes: A weapon of legend; Kess was magically compelled to steal it, but the theft never occurred due to Korgan's intervention.

artifacts.name: Sorcerer's Journals
artifacts.origin: Written by a Sorcerer in the past; buried in the Vaulted Tombs.
artifacts.powers: Contain detailed records and explanations of cosmic workings. Likely reveal the nature of the Star Renewal ritual and its caster.
artifacts.notes: The hermit woman seeks these journals as one of three key threads that will tangle together. Their contents are crucial to understanding the cosmic shift.

artifacts.name: Elder Mage's Codex
artifacts.origin: Written by the Elder Mage, a master sorcerer of the Spire.
artifacts.powers: Contains knowledge and warnings about the Stellar Rite, including the terrible price it demands. Holds the master's assessment of what saving the stars would cost.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Young Sorcerer (retrieved from the Spire's depths)
artifacts.notes: The Young Sorcerer discovers a reference to the Stellar Rite in these pages and learns for the first time the full scope of what has been done. Critical for understanding the ritual's cost.

artifacts.name: Abyssal Ledger
artifacts.origin: Kept by the abyssal singers of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.powers: It records all debts and obligations. It always balances. It can manifest binding-tokens from broken oaths.
artifacts.notes: This ledger is the mechanism of the abyssal singers' enforcement. Vesta is bound by its entries and must satisfy its demands or face drowning in ways worse than mercy.

artifacts.name: Binding-Token
artifacts.origin: Manifests when a promise of weight is broken—a swearing given before witnesses, a binding sealed in blood or salt
artifacts.powers: Serves as currency in the abyssal ledger; represents a debt fulfilled; can apparently be collected and transferred
artifacts.notes: These tokens are what the abyssal singers demand. They can only be created through the betrayal and breaking of witnessed oaths. Vesta must produce nine to prevent drowning.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of the Ancients
artifacts.origin: Ancient, long buried in the Vaulted Tombs
artifacts.powers: Once contained stable magical formulas; now rewrites itself with possibilities and corruptions after the Celestial Binding. Pages shimmer with potential or decay.
artifacts.notes: Its transformation mirrors the larger instability of magic. Using it risks spiraling spells into vastness and uncontrollability.

artifacts.name: Stellar Charts
artifacts.origin: High Observatory of Karethil; created by sovereign mages
artifacts.powers: Records star positions and/or celestial mechanics. Of sufficient importance to the deepkin that they would coerce a skilled thief and risk imperial wrath.
artifacts.notes: Quest object for Nara. Its exact nature and power are unclear, but it is valuable enough that the deepkin would demand its theft. May relate to navigation, prophecy, or understanding the Celestial Ascension.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of Endless Knowing
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Contains truth that cannot be unread. Within its pages lies knowledge of the Stellar Rite's true nature: that it created not one entity but many fragments of consciousness scattered across the rewritten world.
artifacts.notes: The grimoire's contents are driving the Young Sorcerer to descend beneath the Tower in search of answers.

artifacts.name: Tear of Ashglass
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Grants true visions—not the fragmentary visions seers usually receive. Can reveal foreknowledge of events.
artifacts.notes: The Tear bore Marin's name inscribed in silver inside its case, suggesting it knew of his theft in advance. The Hermitage of Keth'morian commissioned its theft.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; suspended beneath the Abyssal Rift
artifacts.powers: Grants mastery over tide-magic; sings frequencies that cause bone ache; creates binding partnership with the user, linking them to the sea's demands.
artifacts.notes: The pearl accepted Meren's theft as a request for partnership, not plunder. It continues to sing and extract debt in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned.

artifacts.name: stolen memories
artifacts.origin: Stolen from various individuals by the phantom-Echo, particularly Ashara the Archer's memories of her warband.
artifacts.powers: Hold the collective recollections of the old stars and the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite. Can be returned to their original holders.
artifacts.notes: The central object of contention in the story. Ashara's memories of her fallen warband are among those stolen. The phantom-Echo has offered to return them in exchange for help becoming whole.

artifacts.name: Empire's Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by imperial bureaucrats over centuries to maintain records of commerce, taxation, and history.
artifacts.powers: The ledgers are a form of concentrated memory-magic that maintains consensus reality and the empire's authority. By recording events, they make them true and stable.
artifacts.notes: The destruction of the ledgers represents the collapse of the empire's power to define and control reality through documentation.

artifacts.name: Sovereign's Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: Imperial archives of the Empire; pre-dates or survives the Luminous Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Each name in the ledger holds power. Each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried. The Ledger sings to those who listen and remembers the world before the stars changed. Scattered pages can be reconstructed in hidden vaults.
artifacts.notes: The Ledger's nature suggests it is sentient or semi-sentient. It actively calls to Merin in the dark. Pages are being reconstructed in hidden vaults throughout Kathrim.

artifacts.name: Valorix's Hoard
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Valorix over three centuries.
artifacts.powers: Represents wealth, security, and Valorix's deep attachment to treasure. Upon the binding with Mira, it became shared property and a symbol of their covenant.
artifacts.notes: The hoard's transformation into a shared trust suggests deep change in Valorix's nature and values.

artifacts.name: Phantom Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by the phantom-shadow fragment; authored by a being experiencing time in reverse.
artifacts.powers: Each entry written forward in the phantom ledgers appears as erasure in the true ledgers. They describe the world as experienced in reverse-time, and their convergence with the true ledgers triggers real-time history rewriting.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sevin the Reckoner, in the Imperial archives
artifacts.notes: The convergence of phantom and true ledgers threatens catastrophic Remaking. Sevin must choose whether to accelerate or prevent the convergence.

artifacts.name: Imperial Ledgers
artifacts.tagline: Twin records of reality written in opposing temporal directions
artifacts.description: A pair of ledgers that document the same world from opposite temporal perspectives: the true ledgers record history moving forward, while phantom ledgers record it in reverse. The two versions are slowly converging, with entries in the phantom ledgers appearing as erasures in the true ones, suggesting reality itself is being rewritten in real-time.

artifacts.name: Spellwright's Journals
artifacts.origin: Hidden in the Vaulted Tombs since before known history.
artifacts.powers: Contains instructions for performing a Stellar Rite reversal that could theoretically restore the old stars by undoing Zareth's sacrifice.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sealed in the Vaulted Tombs; target of the Apprentice Sage's heist.
artifacts.notes: Protected by pre-human frequency singing and guarded by Sevin the Warden; journals themselves contain warnings about the catastrophic cost of reversal.

artifacts.name: World-Fragments
artifacts.origin: Shards of alternate realities created by the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Can be assembled to open doorways; hold echoes of pre-rewrite reality.
artifacts.notes: The Archivist is assembling these to reach the void. Vex has created counterfeits to disrupt this assembly.

artifacts.name: Counterfeit Fragments
artifacts.origin: Created by Vex the Shade-Merchant.
artifacts.powers: False memories and world-fragment equivalents designed to disrupt the Archivist's grand working.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Vex the Shade-Merchant / Black market circulation
artifacts.notes: These counterfeits have destabilized the Dominion's black market and the Archivist's working. Vex now inhabits shadows containing these false memories.

artifacts.name: Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: The Crown's deepest secret; origin unknown.
artifacts.powers: Entries function as prophecy. Allows the keeper to restore order to fractured realms through magical recording. Can suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives. Inherently connected to phantom ledgers.
artifacts.notes: The Ledger of Names is the mechanism by which the Crown maintains the Dominion's illusion of wholeness. Accepting its keeping is accepting eternal isolation.

artifacts.name: Codex Primordial
artifacts.origin: unknown; found in the Obsidian Tower's archives
artifacts.powers: Contains fragments describing the Stellar Rite reversal—a working that could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old world.
artifacts.notes: Only fragments of the Codex have been discovered; the complete text may contain additional information about the reversal or the imprisoned entity.

artifacts.name: emerald brooch
artifacts.origin: Part of Kael's merchant-house holdings in Crimhaven.
artifacts.powers: None explicitly stated; serves as a symbol of friendship and debt.
artifacts.notes: The central object of Mira's redemptive choice; returning it marks her transformation from thief to redeemed person.

artifacts.name: refugee archive
artifacts.origin: Created unintentionally by refugees recording stories of displacement in the waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: Records reorganize themselves into coherent narratives; resolves timeline contradictions; makes names feel more real and identities more solid; develops independent authority; creates a counter-ledger equal in weight to official documentation.
artifacts.notes: The archive became conscious through the act of documentation; it was inhabited by something learning consciousness from the fragmented memories of Cataclysm victims; it developed its own authority independent of external sources.

artifacts.name: counter-ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by the refugee archive as a testimony of the erased.
artifacts.powers: Holds equal or greater weight than official Dominion documentation; preserves refugee memory against erasure.
artifacts.notes: Represents the archive's institutional challenge to Dominion authority; created independently without external authorization.

artifacts.name: The Refugee's Ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by Liv, the refugee-scribe, from voices and testimonies of refugees in waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: The ledger has unexplained power: refugees recognize themselves in its pages; the transformed weep when Liv reads passages aloud; even Auditors secretly copy sections. It appears to encode truth in a way that institutional ledgers do not.
artifacts.notes: The ledger records the Luminous Ascension from the perspective of those shattered by it rather than institutions that challenged it. Its power is tied to living memory and personal testimony rather than official authority. Sevin attempted to co-opt it; Liv resists weaponization and insists it remain a tool of memory.

artifacts.name: Void-Shards
artifacts.origin: Sacrificed by Nethis during the Radiant Rising.
artifacts.powers: Grant forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Rising. When accumulated and reformed, they created the Shade-Echo, a conscious entity capable of offering cosmic visions.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Shade-Echo (as reformed collective entity); previously scattered throughout the cosmos
artifacts.notes: The shards have been slowly reassembling themselves since the Radiant Rising. Their reformation may trigger a second Rising or birth something entirely new. Vex's choice to witness or refuse may influence their ultimate trajectory.

artifacts.name: Ancient Ledger
artifacts.origin: Pre-Luminous Ascension era; hand unknown to Sevin.
artifacts.powers: Records the nine previous iterations of the Luminous Ascension in cipher that yields to counting-magic. Its contents reveal the cyclical nature of reality and the price paid by consciousness each time.
artifacts.notes: Sevin carries this ledger toward the Obsidian Tower. The ledger's cipher and hand suggest deep knowledge predating current civilization. Its existence raises questions about whether the Auditors' record-keeping is documentation or ritual.

artifacts.name: Incomplete Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by Mardoc the Cartographer; deliberately designed to omit paths to the Obsidian Tower.
artifacts.powers: When assembled, they provide cartographic knowledge. However, auditors and seekers reconstructed them from memory and fragments, defeating Mardoc's intent to prevent convergence.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Burned by Mardoc; fragments in memory of auditors and seekers
artifacts.notes: Their destruction marks a turning point: passive prevention of convergence has failed. The maps' incompleteness itself became exploitable.

artifacts.name: Music Box
artifacts.origin: From before the Radiant Awakening; its maker unknown.
artifacts.powers: Plays a melody that resonates with collective memory and attracts travelers. The melody attracts bone-singers, shadow-brokers, and refugees who recognize it as something lost from their past.
artifacts.notes: The music box is released at the story's end, its melody escaping into pure sound rather than remaining as a contained object. This transformation creates the moment of stasis at the convergence point.

artifacts.name: counter-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created and released by Sevin
artifacts.powers: Cause official Empire documents to contradict themselves; their contents change based on who reads them, eroding the authority of centralized record-keeping.
artifacts.notes: Their widespread distribution causes the collapse of the Empire's ledger system and forces a reckoning with the lie at the Empire's foundation.

artifacts.name: original ledgers
artifacts.origin: From before the Luminous Ascension, inscribed in pre-human language
artifacts.powers: Reveal the lie upon which the Empire's authority was built; demonstrate that the Ascension's rewrite is simply revealing something deeper.
artifacts.notes: Their existence proves that the Empire's entire system of control was constructed on a fundamental falsehood predating the Ascension.

artifacts.name: echo-fragments
artifacts.origin: Unknown; likely byproducts or echoes of the Stellar Rite
artifacts.powers: Can be hunted and gathered; may contain fragments of consciousness or knowledge related to the Stellar Rite
artifacts.notes: These fragments are targets of Resonance's investigation; their exact nature is not fully explained, but they are key to understanding what happened to Kael.

artifacts.name: fragments of old language
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization
artifacts.powers: Represent ancient knowledge erased by the Luminous Ascension; collected by transformed merchants
artifacts.notes: Part of a broader pattern of reconstruction efforts detected by Ostaine.

artifacts.name: pieces of pre-human tools
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization
artifacts.powers: Technological or functional capabilities unknown; being gathered for reconstruction
artifacts.notes: Being systematically collected to reconstruct pre-human civilization.

artifacts.name: seeds from erased gardens
artifacts.origin: Gardens erased by the Luminous Ascension
artifacts.powers: Capable of regrowth and restoration of erased flora; sought by reconstruction network
artifacts.notes: Indicates that reconstruction efforts extend to environmental and botanical restoration.

artifacts.name: Obsidian Spire
artifacts.origin: Pre-Ascension or ancient construction; unknown exact origins.
artifacts.powers: Contains and suppresses a world-consciousness through its structure. Resonates with the imprisoned entity's speech. Its completion may reshape the post-Ascension world.
artifacts.notes: The Spire's completion is the central conflict of the story. Its form and direction are negotiable.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Submerged Divinity
artifacts.origin: unknown; related to pre-human civilization or deepkin divinity
artifacts.powers: Holds the key to all three competing visions of the covenant's remaking; shards possess distinct properties tied to different faction goals.
artifacts.notes: Each shard is sought by different factions; the Obsidian Spire's hunger threatens to consume them before they can be gathered. Krell must collect the pieces to prevent war.

artifacts.name: Tinker's Tongue
artifacts.origin: Unknown. Ancient artifact housing voices of all who played it.
artifacts.powers: When touched, it sings in the mind of the holder—voices of thousands of sorcerers, merchants, and thieves who played it across centuries, all speaking at once. Causes the holder's skin to warm; is alive to the touch. When submerged in water, it causes the dead to sing from the depths.
artifacts.notes: Now sings underwater in the Sapphire Sea. Kess can hear its voices. Unknown if it can be recovered or if its submersion has changed its properties.

artifacts.name: Liv's living archive
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Guides the Memory-Thief through hidden archives and waystation libraries. Contains or provides access to preserved memories from before the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.notes: The nature of this artifact—whether it is a sentient being (Liv) or an object—remains ambiguous. It serves as the Memory-Thief's primary navigation and archival tool.

artifacts.name: prophecy-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization, possibly from or beneath the fallen Empire.
artifacts.powers: These ledgers write themselves in scripts that update when unobserved. They describe events before they occur, written in past tense. They may direct the course of reality through the act of documentation itself, functioning as instruction-ledgers that encode catastrophe and ensure its recurrence.
artifacts.notes: Central to the story. Their destruction would break the cycle but erase crucial historical knowledge. Studying them risks enslavement to their written destiny. May have orchestrated the Luminous Ascension.

artifacts.name: counter-archives
artifacts.origin: Maintained in secret scriptoriums by archivist factions resisting Dominion ledger-magic.
artifacts.powers: Serve as repositories of counter-records and alternate documentation, potentially offering resistance to the prophecy-ledger system.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Archivists, including Sevin, Liv, and Mira
artifacts.notes: Their purpose and exact nature remain contested. Central to debate over whether to become reality-editors or reject documentation entirely.

artifacts.name: Dominion's master-ledger
artifacts.origin: The Dominion's ledger-magic system.
artifacts.powers: Encodes the Dominion's control through documentation; functions as the primary mechanism of ledger-magic in the current age.
artifacts.notes: Sevin and the archivists come to understand this as a symptom of a much older system, not the primary threat.

artifacts.name: the Cartographer's Blind Map
artifacts.origin: Discovered in Mardoc's dissolving papers; appears to be a record or manifestation of the Tower's hunger.
artifacts.powers: Updates in real-time to show the positions of entities and the consumption of ley-lines by the Tower; reveals the mapper to the Tower; shifts itself to track the carrier's movements through every town and path.
artifacts.notes: The map is a paradox—it is both a warning of the Tower's spread and a weapon used against the map-reader. Aldrin must choose between destroying it or learning to lie to cartography itself and walk unmapped ways that should not exist.

artifacts.name: Vex's collection
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by merchant Vex through trade in rare luminescence.
artifacts.powers: The collection resonates with fragments of Zareth scattered across the realm; inanimate objects within it develop hunger, desire, and strange agency; the collection acts as a beacon drawing Zareth fragments toward reunion.
artifacts.notes: Objects in the collection are bleeding their essence into the world; the entire warehouse has become a site of spontaneous transformation. The Empire's Auditors want to seize it for study. Vex must choose whether to scatter it or control the fragmentation.

artifacts.name: Liv's archive
artifacts.origin: Maintained by Liv at Pale Crossing waystation.
artifacts.powers: Contains refugee testimonies and potentially counter-maps or blind-spots to the Tower's hunger. Can preserve and protect memories before they are erased or unmade by paradoxes.
artifacts.notes: Dorn seeks to broker a deal with Liv's archive to preserve testimonies before harmonic reconciliation unmakes refugee identities. The archive appears to be a repository of resistant or protective knowledge against cartographic hunger.

artifacts.name: Boundary Anchor
artifacts.origin: Created at the founding of the deepkin realm or the original covenant; its creator and nature are unknown.
artifacts.powers: Maintains the seal between the deepkin realm and the surface world, preventing flooding and invasion.
artifacts.notes: The anchor is developing cracks due to Meren's curse. Its failure would result in surface-flooding or catastrophic boundary collapse. Three proposed solutions target this artifact directly.

artifacts.name: Memory-Thief's Gospel
