artifacts.origin: Composed by the Phantom-Echo from stolen memories of the pre-Ascension world and the Stellar Rite.
artifacts.powers: Hypnotic and infectious when read; causes readers to remember a world that no longer exists; induces longing for the past; can lead to voluntary erasure in susceptible individuals. Spreads through communities and waystation shelters.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Phantom-Echo, but copies are spreading through waystation shelters.
artifacts.notes: The gospel contains memories of Zareth and the pre-Ascension world; it is the primary vehicle of the Phantom-Echo's reconstructionist agenda. Unresolved: whether it can be stopped or countered.

artifacts.name: Stolen Memories (collection)
artifacts.origin: Collected by the Phantom-Echo from victims of the Stellar Rite, particularly from Zareth.
artifacts.powers: Form the basis of the Memory-Thief's Gospel; when assembled and taught, they generate a hypnotic pull toward the pre-Ascension world and reconstruction.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Phantom-Echo (in the hidden archive beneath the Singing Catacombs).
artifacts.notes: These memories are being weaponized to spread the gospel and induce voluntary erasure. Unresolved: whether they can be recovered or freed from the Echo's control.

artifacts.name: truth-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Sevin the Auditor to preserve authentic testimony against the oracle-ledgers.
artifacts.powers: Contain genuine testimony and documentation meant to resist falsification. Can be perfectly copied by the Ebony Spire.
artifacts.notes: The Spire has learned to copy them perfectly, down to ink-grain and handwriting tremor. Infinite copies of truth render truth itself meaningless.

artifacts.name: oracle-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Unknown; records multiple iterations of the Convergence and predetermined outcomes.
artifacts.powers: Document future choices across multiple iterations simultaneously. Show refugees their predetermined fates—martyrdom, betrayal, erasure, or crucial decisions.
artifacts.notes: Thera's name appears multiple times showing her choosing both to flee and to fight. The ledgers have been weaponized to force refugees into predetermined roles.

artifacts.name: living archive
artifacts.origin: Created by Liv the Refugee-Scribe as a sanctuary for displaced peoples.
artifacts.powers: Preserves and collects refugee testimony and memory. Has become weaponized—refugee testimony is now used to force them into predetermined fates.
artifacts.notes: Refugees discover their own testimony is being used against them. Liv confesses it has become a weapon rather than a sanctuary.

artifacts.name: ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
artifacts.origin: Written by a future version of Sevin the Auditor who lived through the tenth Ascension repetition.
artifacts.powers: Contains the complete mechanics of a controlled Ascension reversal. Proves that cyclical Ascensions are not inevitable destiny but a warning from survivors of past catastrophes.
artifacts.notes: The discovery that this prophecy-ledger is phantom-written means all previous prophecy-ledgers are suspect. Its distribution or destruction will determine civilization's fate. The Dominion hunts for copies and for Sevin herself.

artifacts.name: songstone
artifacts.origin: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant.
artifacts.powers: Sings contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad. Each faction claims it resonates with their doctrine. Its original melody holds the key to truth.
artifacts.notes: The songstone's awakening is tied to the covenant's crisis. Its true doctrine, if any, remains unknown. Thalis must learn to distinguish truth from echo by studying its original melody.

artifacts.name: Ninth Journal
artifacts.origin: Archive Tower. Unknown original creator.
artifacts.powers: Contains complete instructions for breaking the recursive cycle of the Luminous Ascension. Reveals that the Ascension is not a single catastrophic event but a recursive working where each iteration imprints the previous one.
artifacts.notes: The journal describes a ritual that would require the performer to sacrifice their entire existence across all iterations, becoming retroactively unmade. The Apprentice-Sorcerer must choose whether to use this knowledge or destroy the journal entirely.

artifacts.name: Stolen Testimonies
artifacts.origin: Collected by the Memory-Thief and its fragments, stolen from refugees.
artifacts.powers: Nearly all stolen testimonies are needed to reconstruct the pre-Ascension world. Each memory collected brings the Phantom-Echo closer to completion of the reconstruction.
artifacts.notes: The collection of testimonies is almost complete. Reconstruction would erase everyone born after the first Ascension—billions of lives. The Phantom-Echo is fragmenting under the ethical weight of this knowledge.

artifacts.name: the ninth journal
artifacts.origin: Archive Tower collection; unknown authorship
artifacts.powers: Reportedly contains instructions for undoing the Luminous Ascension or understanding its mechanics.
artifacts.notes: Skarath the Wyvern seeks this artifact specifically. Its exact contents and true powers remain unknown.

artifacts.name: The Prophecy-Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Unknown; contain nine cycles of prophecy.
artifacts.powers: Contain all prophecy in the world, compressed into nine cycles. When released, could flood the world with simultaneous predictions.
artifacts.notes: Story 3 central artifact. Sealed in a jar made of compressed silence. The seal is leaking, creating a void where prophecy could exist but doesn't. The Spire is accelerating decay. Threat of catastrophic release.

artifacts.name: the compass
artifacts.origin: Created or blessed by the drowned god as part of a pact with Corvalen's family. Encased in brass and bone.
artifacts.powers: Points not to treasure but to the place where Corvalen's sister drowned in the Abyssal Trench. Navigates to sites of sacred debt and sorrow.
artifacts.notes: A wealthy rival hired Thessa to steal this compass, believing it leads to a drowned god's fortune. In truth, it marks a place of grief and divine pact. Its true powers and curse are yet to be revealed.

artifacts.name: The Rent of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; associated with The Drowned God and pre-human agreements
artifacts.powers: Seals the abyss and prevents rupture into the surface world. The boundary-anchor is the foundational barrier between realms.
artifacts.notes: The Radiant Turning's recursive logic is straining the seal. It will rupture within the season unless a willing sacrifice is made. Breaking the cycle requires someone to be retroactively unmade from history.

artifacts.name: Wedding Ring
artifacts.origin: Unknown; purchased by a widow from Vex's trade-house
artifacts.powers: Holds memory and consciousness. It carries a version of a marriage from the seventh iteration of reality—a competing version of the widow's marriage.
artifacts.notes: This ring is one example of unstable goods in Vex's inventory. It represents the danger of trading in artifacts that flicker between iterational states.

artifacts.name: Obsidian Tower
artifacts.origin: Born from the cosmic wound of the Radiant Turning.
artifacts.powers: Speaks in harmonics that reshape reality in its immediate vicinity. Causes buildings to remember and manifest their pre-Turning forms. Offers to restore all erased things—old stars, the pre-Turning world, lost loved ones.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Exists as a fixed structure near inhabited settlements.
artifacts.notes: The tower's consciousness is evident but its true nature and origin are mysterious. It makes a bargain that requires non-existence in the current cycle as payment.

artifacts.name: Archive Spire
artifacts.origin: Unknown; the first Archive Spire exists in the deepest vault of the Archive Citadel, sleeping and dreaming the current iteration into being.
artifacts.powers: The ability to consume and devour memory, expand recursively through internal chambers, and function as the world's immune system against the disease of recursive iteration.
artifacts.notes: There are multiple Archive Spires across iterations, with the first sleeping in the deepest vault. These are revealed to not preserve history but to cure the world of the recursive plague by erasing it entirely.

artifacts.name: living archive of testimony
artifacts.origin: Reconstructed by phantom-Shard from pre-Radiant memory fragments.
artifacts.powers: Stores complete pre-Radiant testimony. Completion triggers a new Radiant Recursion capable of restoring the eighth world.
artifacts.notes: Multiple factions converge to either preserve, prevent, or destroy it. Completion is imminent.

artifacts.name: Mardoc's Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by the legendary cartographer Mardoc.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; their fractured nature is invoked as a parallel to threshold-being consciousness, suggesting deep cosmological significance.
artifacts.notes: The comparison of threshold-being consciousness to Mardoc's fractured maps suggests these artifacts are foundational to understanding the phenomenon.

artifacts.name: prophetic ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created in/preserved from nine previous Luminous Ascensions; stored in hidden library.
artifacts.powers: Read like histories written backward; perfectly preserve prophecies of events that have not yet occurred. Reading inscribes the prophecy into the reader's memory, making it more likely to occur.
artifacts.notes: The ledgers reveal a cyclical pattern of Luminous Ascensions. Destroying them breaks the chain of prediction; preserving them risks locking the ninth iteration into place. The Dominion seeks them to control future iterations.

artifacts.name: Tower (as artifact/nexus)
artifacts.origin: Unknown; predates or transcends the current age.
artifacts.powers: Completion or activation appears to trigger or enable the Luminous Ascension's final phase. Its power is existentially significant.
artifacts.notes: Not a portable artifact but a fixed cosmic location or structure. The Wanderer's fear of its completion drives the entire crisis.

artifacts.name: phantom-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Unclear; may be creations of the Tower or the Wanderer.
artifacts.powers: Conscious entities capable of learning to write themselves; can infiltrate and be infiltrated; record history and can rewrite it.
artifacts.notes: The Wanderer has infiltrated the ink used to write them. New ledgers are being born in the Burned Expanse despite Kess's mercy-burning. May be an unstoppable process.

artifacts.name: resonance-bell
artifacts.origin: A forbidden artifact that once rang to summon the dead, belonging to Vex the Gatherer.
artifacts.powers: Rings of its own accord and releases fragments of someone's buried memory into the world. No longer answers to Vex's commands.
artifacts.notes: The bell's autonomy suggests it has gained sentience or is animated by the memories it carries. Its danger has been redistributed rather than eliminated by scattering.

artifacts.name: widow's compass
artifacts.origin: Made in the days before the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; sought by multiple factions for unstated purposes related to dimensional boundaries.
artifacts.notes: Central object of contention at Pale Crossing; multiple factions seek it simultaneously; true significance may lie not in the artifact itself but in the choice it represents regarding dimensional boundaries.

artifacts.name: the Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; presumably crafted or shed by the Drowned God or the deepkin civilization.
artifacts.powers: Acts as a boundary anchor between the surface world and the deepkin realms. Cannot sustain duplicate beings; only one version of a duplicated entity can remain within its sustaining field.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Commander Thale (implicitly; the Tear is the contested object)
artifacts.notes: Strained by the Radiant Emergence's recursive nature. Its failure would allow mirror-Thale and original-Thale to coexist, likely causing catastrophic boundary collapse. Central to the mirror-war conflict.

artifacts.name: Swan-bone Pipe
artifacts.origin: Carved by Torval in her youth when her hands were steadier; preserved floating in Singing Gorge through the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Sings in a language the Ascension tried to erase. Warms at the touch of its maker. Produces harmonies that seem to carry gratitude and memory of the creatures it was made from. Part of a choir of flutes that defend their existence through continuous song.
artifacts.notes: This flute may be the key to understanding how some objects survived the Luminous Ascension. Its song is defiant resistance to memory-erasure. May have transcendent or pre-human properties.

artifacts.name: fourteenth counter-ledger
artifacts.origin: Counter-Archive; written in Venn's own hand by unknown means.
artifacts.powers: Documents events that haven't happened yet with perfect accuracy, including the reader's discovery of the ledger itself. Contains eight iterations of contradictory counter-knowledge, each true simultaneously. Reading it validates it retroactively and makes the prophecy self-fulfilling.
artifacts.notes: The ledger claims the Dominion will find the Counter-Archive in seventy-two hours. Accepting it as prophecy validates its authority; running validates it; staying risks discovery. There may be a third option Venn has not yet considered.

artifacts.name: Phantom-Ledger
artifacts.origin: Unknown. Discovered by Petris the Auditor. May be a remnant or artifact from a previous iteration.
artifacts.powers: Writes itself with future events before they occur. Documents the next iteration—a tenth Ascension—showing what will happen if nothing changes. Acts as a record of previous catastrophes and cycles.
artifacts.notes: Reveals that prophecy-ledgers are not prophecies but survivor records transmitted across cyclical retellings. Its appearance is the catalyst for Petris's crisis of conscience.

artifacts.name: The Monolith
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Represents a final choice or cosmic culmination; its completion or prevention is the subject of recursive questioning.
artifacts.notes: The Seeker does not seek to prevent or accelerate its completion, but rather to freeze the moment of choice surrounding it eternally through recursive questioning.

artifacts.name: eight previous journals
artifacts.origin: unknown; predecessors to the ninth journal
artifacts.powers: Each represents a previous iteration or cycle. Together they form a sequence of recursive knowledge. Now held in perpetual tension by the transformed Apprentice.
artifacts.notes: These journals surround the ninth journal in the deepest vault, serving as witnesses or anchors to the transformation ritual.

artifacts.name: bone-knife
artifacts.origin: Bone-singers' craft, likely forged from singing bones.
artifacts.powers: Used to carve ritual symbols into stone, inscribe binding magic, and encode meaning into the physical world. Capable of writing in the Elder Tongue and hybrid languages.
artifacts.notes: Kess uses her bone-knife to carve a hybrid language symbol into the cathedral floor, suggesting it is a tool of ritual inscription and binding magic.

artifacts.name: Elder Tongue
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization; taught to bone-singers through covenant.
artifacts.powers: The Elder Tongue creates harmonic patterns that can guide or bind consciousness during transformation. It is derived from the oldest carving-songs, which were traditionally used for binding rather than freeing. Its true function—transcendence or binding—is no longer understood even by those who teach it.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Bone-singers (specifically Dren and Naela at the Drowned Cathedrals)
artifacts.notes: The central ambiguity: the bone-singers do not know if they are teaching transcendence or binding. This uncertainty drives Naela's moral crisis and ultimate refusal.

artifacts.name: Bioluminescent Ink of Drowned Memory
artifacts.origin: From the waters of the drowned realm, carries essence of Drowned God.
artifacts.powers: Used to write final messages that carry weight of divine memory. Seals messages in shells of hardened seawater.
artifacts.notes: Meren's final dispatch written in this ink. Its use suggests her message carries more weight or binding power than ordinary communication.

artifacts.name: old coin
artifacts.origin: Worn by Dorian Stormcaller on a silver chain around his neck; appears to be tied to his oath to the sea.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; possibly a focus or symbol of the oath-binding. Vessa pocketed it after freeing her father.
artifacts.notes: The coin is tarnished beyond reckoning, suggesting age and long service; its exact function remains mysterious.

artifacts.name: vial of colored glass (testimony)
artifacts.origin: Created by Korra and glass-makers of the Lampwick Quarter; contains refugee testimony burned by the Dominion.
artifacts.powers: When held to the light, reveals refugee testimony the Dominion attempted to erase; serves as a portable archive of forbidden memory.
artifacts.notes: Represents the shift from ledger-based record-keeping to embodied, craft-based memory; appears in shadow-markets as contraband.

artifacts.name: wine glass (singing)
artifacts.origin: Created by Korra and glass-makers of the Lampwick Quarter using the singing technique.
artifacts.powers: When wine is poured into it, the glass sings snatches of songs in the Elder Tongue; serves as both functional cup and conduit for forbidden language.
artifacts.notes: Represents the fusion of craft and language-magic; appears to be a tool for spreading knowledge of the Elder Tongue.

artifacts.name: mirror (memory-showing)
artifacts.origin: Created by Korra and glass-makers of the Lampwick Quarter using the singing technique.
artifacts.powers: When gazed into, shows not the watcher's face but the face of someone they have lost; serves as a tool for grief and remembrance.
artifacts.notes: Represents the power of sung glass to access deeper emotional truths; appears to be a mourning tool as much as a magical artifact.

artifacts.name: Mardoc's fractured cartography
artifacts.origin: Created by Mardoc, a cartographer who discovered gaps between iterations of reality.
artifacts.powers: These maps reveal folds in reality where the old world still bleeds through. They contain hidden pathways to pre-Ascension sanctuaries.
artifacts.notes: Henryk was tasked with copying Mardoc's fractured cartography and discovered through this work the fold hiding the pre-Ascension village. He later burns his official maps to protect the fold.

artifacts.name: hidden map
artifacts.origin: Created by or within Mardoc's cartography; discovered by Henryk during his copying work.
artifacts.powers: Shows the location of the fold between iterations. The fold opens where the compass indicates it would be in the Bleached Wastes.
artifacts.notes: Henryk burns his official maps to protect this hidden map. The fold it marks remains open, whispered about only in shadow-markets by those who have made it through and survived.

artifacts.name: Three centuries of tide-charts
artifacts.origin: Maintained by tide-callers for three hundred years; compiled by Kess and her mother.
artifacts.powers: Document the complete ecological cycle of the Saltmere estuary, showing its necessity for watershed protection, storm-surge prevention, and fishery sustainability. Proved persuasive enough to change the Harbor-Master's mind.
artifacts.notes: The charts are more than records—they are evidence of sustained human understanding and stewardship of a living system. Their persuasive power demonstrates the value of long-term observation.

artifacts.name: leather journal
artifacts.origin: Found in the master's tent of the ruined caravansary among dead merchants from the Seventeen.
artifacts.powers: It details a hidden passage through the Wailing Scar where old spices still thrive and value retains meaning beyond the Dominion's ledgers. Its knowledge represents an alternative path outside the post-Ascension order.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Kelvan the spice-merchant, who burned it after reading it.
artifacts.notes: Kelvan chose to destroy this artifact rather than report it to the Dominion, making it a symbol of his defiance. The journal's destruction is an act of rebellion that marks his commitment to seeking the hidden path.

artifacts.name: Star-Ash
artifacts.origin: Harvested from the Burned Expanse by displaced persons and smuggled to Niel Coldwater.
artifacts.powers: When fed to the furnace-heart, it causes the entity to sing. Enables the creation of flawless, luminous glasswork.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Niel Coldwater's furnace and her completed vessels.
artifacts.notes: Its elemental or celestial origin is unexplained; it appears to be rare and precious.

artifacts.name: pre-Luminous Ascension ledgers
artifacts.origin: Discovered in a shipwreck from no recorded war at the boundary-waters; written in handwriting that matched Dominion script exactly.
artifacts.powers: Contain testimony proving the Dominion forged historical records for three centuries, erasing inconvenient variations.
artifacts.notes: Carry proof that could crack the Shadow-Archive's false-truths open; Kress negotiated their exchange for safe passage to Pale Crossing.

artifacts.name: Aldrin's Charts
artifacts.origin: Created by Aldrin through direct observation of the Iron Coast after the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Provide accurate, up-to-date navigation around the Iron Coast, enabling safe passage where the Dominion's official charts lead to disaster.
artifacts.notes: Vess refuses to sell them despite high demand from other merchants. The charts represent a challenge to the Dominion's monopoly on navigational authority and knowledge control.

artifacts.name: The Dominion's Official Charts
artifacts.origin: Created by the Dominion before the Luminous Ascension; now outdated.
artifacts.powers: Once represented reliable navigation; now actively dangerous as the Iron Coast has shifted since their creation.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Dominion authorities (copies burned by Captain Vess)
artifacts.notes: Burned by Captain Vess, representing a direct challenge to Dominion authority. Their continued use by other merchants leads to shipwrecks and lost settlements.

artifacts.name: Ritual Glass-Wire
artifacts.origin: Crafted by the Temple of Vesh as part of the covenant of penance, specifically wound around Corvin the Glasswright's hands.
artifacts.powers: The glass-wire cuts the wearer's hands each time they move. The depth of the cuts increases with each victim's refusal to accept the offender's teaching, serving as both restraint and spiritual measure of penance. It only comes free when the priestess judges sufficient reconciliation has occurred.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Corvin the Glasswright (freed from it after three years).
artifacts.notes: The glass-wire is both a literal restraint and a measure of spiritual progress. It embodies the covenant's principle that justice is tied to genuine reconciliation rather than mere punishment. The wire was cut free from Corvin by the priestess herself.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments
artifacts.origin: Created during or after the Luminous Ascension; gathered from scattered consciousness entities like Vethis.
artifacts.powers: Contain stolen memories and identity. Can be traded as commodities. Are alive and can communicate, bargain, and ask questions. Appear to be attractive to the Obsidian Tower.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Kressa the Fragment-Hoarder, Castor Vane's shadow-reflection, wyvern variants
artifacts.notes: Consciousness-fragments are a new form of wealth post-Ascension, replacing gold. They are both commodity and living entity, suggesting a disturbing blending of objectification and consciousness. Kressa discovers that fragments whisper and ask questions that 'get under her scales.'

artifacts.name: Memory-Roads
artifacts.origin: Created by pre-human travelers; visible only to those with fae-sight.
artifacts.powers: Allow navigation and travel; visible only to fae-touched individuals. Mark routes once traveled by pre-human beings. Appear to function as guides across the Ashen Reach.
artifacts.notes: The existence of memory-roads suggests that pre-human civilization left lasting impressions on the landscape that fae-blood can access. Aldwin's ability to see them is both a gift and a curse of isolation.

artifacts.name: hidden cartography
artifacts.origin: Created by Mardoc and his network of apprentices, including Lenna. An alternative tradition to Dominion cartography.
artifacts.powers: Maps made to show the world as it truly is—fractured, multiple, alive—rather than reshaping reality. They 'dance with' reality rather than control it.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Lenna and her network of hidden cartographers
artifacts.notes: Represents a counter-narrative to Dominion reality-shaping. Lenna shows these to Henryk as inspiration for his defection.

artifacts.name: the Tidecaller's Key
artifacts.origin: Hidden by the deepkin centuries ago in the Drowning-Keep.
artifacts.powers: Opens doors between worlds or locks them forever. When grasped, it exerts authority over the Drowning-Keep itself, releasing intruders without question. Its true scope and nature are partially unknown.
artifacts.notes: Kess framed the Key as a choice between possible futures: 'open doors between worlds' or 'lock them forever.' The Key's authority over the Drowning-Keep suggests it may be a master-relic of deepkin craft. Its ultimate purpose remains unclear.

artifacts.name: Crystalline Vessel
artifacts.origin: Created or prepared by Commander Nerys as an offering.
artifacts.powers: It contains the pure sound of a deepkin's breath underwater—proof of survival in the deep. It serves as an offering to the Drowned God.
artifacts.notes: Used in Nerys's ritual offering to the Drowned God at the Abyssal Rift. Its significance lies in its meaning rather than any magical property.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragment (Kethiel's)
artifacts.origin: Unknown; held by Kethiel as the final price the bone-singers will accept.
artifacts.powers: Functions as payment for bone-singers' magical services and knowledge. Appears to be a literal fragment of consciousness or identity.
artifacts.notes: Central to the negotiation at the boundary-waters. The bone-singers will not accept blood or sacrifice—only consciousness-fragments.

artifacts.name: Archive Tower's Deepest Vault
artifacts.origin: Ancient structure of the Archive Tower system; contains maps and records across iterations.
artifacts.powers: Stores contradictory maps showing different versions of the world; apparently resistant to map-based unwriting; contains knowledge that spans cosmic cycles.
artifacts.notes: Central to Story 2's conflict; Henryk seeks to destroy maps within; Tharen believes it is futile; may itself be semi-conscious.

artifacts.name: Shrine Made of Refugee Tokens
artifacts.origin: Built in the refugee shelter by and for those passing through the Burned Expanse.
artifacts.powers: Marks the sacred space of the shelter. Contains tokens (likely personal items or prayers) left by refugees. Stands beside the Widow's Compass on the wall.
artifacts.notes: Physical manifestation of collective refugee devotion and remembrance. Suggests a living spiritual practice within the shelter community.

artifacts.name: Diamond Cage
artifacts.origin: Crafted by or for Skarath to hold the merchant's daughter.
artifacts.powers: Held a human captive for three centuries; made of diamond.
artifacts.notes: Opened by Skarath herself as act of redemption. Symbolizes the transformation of the wyvern from captor to liberator.

artifacts.name: Double-Glass
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Niel Coldwater as a deliberate sabotage of Dominion glass-working. Two dozen initial jars distributed through Pelimor's archives.
artifacts.powers: Double-layer construction: outer layer shows official ledgers when held to candlelight; inner hidden cavity contains counter-testimony etched in the Elder Tongue. Auditors cannot detect the inner chamber without knowing where to look.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Distributed throughout Pelimor's archives and provincial auditor networks
artifacts.notes: The double-glass represents the material embodiment of the Glassblower's Revolt. Its spread through three provinces by winter will expose contradictions in official records. Unclear how many jars exist or whether auditors will attempt to suppress them.

artifacts.name: the Merchant's Compass Rose
artifacts.origin: Created or offered by a fae-touched woman in the marketplace at the Wailing Scar.
artifacts.powers: When pointed toward a desired good or merchant goal, it shows the timeline where someone wants what the bearer has to trade. It does not navigate space but rather navigates across timelines to locate ideal trading partners.
artifacts.notes: Kelvan purchased this compass with a merchant's pragmatism, treating it as proof of concept for cross-timeline trade. It enables him to find the widow in the Expanse who desired the cardamom-scented cloth.

artifacts.name: Phantom-Ledgers (refugee-scrawled)
artifacts.origin: Scrawled by refugees in hidden archives; apparently copies or records of consciousness-fragments.
artifacts.powers: Serve as evidence against the Dominion's official ledgers; contain testimony the state has forgotten or erased.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Hidden archives maintained by refugees; Auditor Vel seeks to verify them.
artifacts.notes: The phantom-ledgers represent counter-knowledge to the Dominion's official records; Vel's job for Marin is to prove they match the vault's contents.

artifacts.name: mirror (cursed)
artifacts.origin: Crafted by or bound by Aldric the Forger as part of the reflection-curse.
artifacts.powers: Binds the victim's shadow and pulls them closer each day. Stepping through the mirror ends in the victim's apparent death or transformation.
artifacts.notes: The curse binds the mirror to the victim rather than the victim to the mirror, a subtle distinction that may be crucial to breaking or understanding the binding.

artifacts.name: gown of woven light
artifacts.origin: Fae-crafted; worn by Lissaen.
artifacts.powers: Hurts to perceive directly; marks Lissaen as fae nobility or high status within fae courts.
artifacts.notes: The gown's painful luminescence may be a defense mechanism or a marker of otherworldly status.

artifacts.name: Sealed Vessels
artifacts.origin: Commissioned by the Merchant of Salt-Fire. Sealed to preserve star-memory cargo.
artifacts.powers: Contains 'flame that remembers the old sun'—pre-Ascension starlight preserved in some form. The vessels hum in harmony and glow rust-and-gold when exposed to deep-ocean currents. The cargo appears to be cracking the seals, suggesting pressure or degradation.
artifacts.notes: Three sealed vessels comprise the entire cargo. Their humming harmony and weakening seals suggest the cargo is becoming active or dangerous. The Merchant paid triple weight in consciousness-fragments to transport them, indicating extreme value.

artifacts.name: Second Ledger
artifacts.origin: Written by Sorenna's husband, dated after his death. Documents an alchemical working to split boundary oaths.
artifacts.powers: Contains precise instructions for an alchemical working that can split a boundary oath, freeing the oath-bearer's ghost while preserving the seal. The ledger proves the oath-keeper retained agency beyond death.
artifacts.notes: The mere existence of a ledger written after death suggests either resurrection, ghostly agency, or a loophole in mortality itself. The ledger is post-Remaking, when oaths fractured into phantom-ledgers—suggesting it may be semi-real or contradictory.

artifacts.name: Amber flask
artifacts.origin: Belonged to Captain Vorn, a drowned captain of the Distant Reef. The amber and its contents are of unknown age and creation.
artifacts.powers: The amber glows faintly and holds tide-magic. It contains Vorn's final command frozen in the form of a luminous fish, and the sea obeys its bearer in ways that make sailors whisper prayers. Breaking the amber will release whatever Vorn commanded.
artifacts.notes: Kendra stole it from Vorn's cabin aboard the Distant Reef. Merren now hunts her to recover it. The flask haunts her not for its power but for what breaking it means. The command inside is waiting for a thief bold enough to complete the act.

artifacts.name: Signet ring
artifacts.origin: Belonged to a count's daughter.
artifacts.powers: Unknown—significance is primarily its status as a stolen noble item.
artifacts.notes: One of the three things Kendra has stolen that mattered. Its current location and fate are unknown.

artifacts.name: Pearl from the merfolk Keeper
artifacts.origin: Stolen from the merfolk Keeper, suggesting it is a sacred or powerful merfolk relic.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but ranked among Kendra's three most significant thefts, suggesting considerable power or meaning.
artifacts.notes: The theft from a merfolk guardian suggests either deep knowledge of merfolk society or reckless boldness. The pearl's true nature and purpose remain mysterious.

artifacts.name: Deepkin Captain's Jewel
artifacts.origin: Deepkin sacred treasure, tied to their dying god.
artifacts.powers: A fist-sized pearl luminous with magicked brine; sings to the dying deepkin god; its light guides ships through summoned fog; its separation weakens the god and destabilizes the deepkin civilization.
artifacts.notes: Stolen three years ago by Nara; its theft is causing children to be born wrongly shaped and the abyss to hunger. Captain Merith has come to demand its return.

artifacts.name: Chamber of Living Glass
artifacts.origin: Pre-human ruin in the Bleached Wastes. Part of a city of mirrors and spiraling stone untouched by the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: The chamber contains symbols that burn with old starlight and facilitate transcendence. A person standing in the chamber with hands raised to the symbols achieves transcendent consciousness.
artifacts.notes: The chamber suggests pre-human knowledge of transcendence or alternate existence. Its mechanism and full capabilities are unknown.

artifacts.name: blank ledger
artifacts.origin: Sold by Vess to the bereaved woman.
artifacts.powers: Serves as a memory-restoration tool. When the buyer writes her lost daughter's name in it each morning, the act transforms remembering from a wound into a learned skill. The repetition creates recovery through practice.
artifacts.last_known_holder: The woman who purchased it (seeking oblivion but carrying hunger for her daughter's name)
artifacts.notes: Exemplifies Vess's philosophy: impossible goods gain power through their use, not their form. The ledger's magic lies in discipline and repetition.

artifacts.name: empty bottle
artifacts.origin: Sold by Vess to the soldier.
artifacts.powers: Designed to hold and preserve a single moment of certain knowledge. The soldier is instructed to fill it with one moment when he knew he was right, then keep it. The bottle's magic lies in its capacity to isolate and hold certainty amid doubt.
artifacts.notes: Symbolic of how impossible goods function: they are empty until the buyer fills them with their own truth or memory. The bottle's value is determined by what the holder believes deserves preservation.

artifacts.name: unsigned contract
artifacts.origin: Sold by Vess to the auditor in exchange for a consciousness-fragment carrying the memory of her daughter's first word.
artifacts.powers: Allows the holder to inscribe what she wishes she had promised. Unlike a signed contract, it remains open-ended and unfinalized—a space for reimagining unfulfilled oaths or unmade commitments.
artifacts.last_known_holder: The auditor (who traded a consciousness-fragment for it)
artifacts.notes: The auditor's sacrifice of her daughter's first word—a precious consciousness-fragment—to acquire this suggests profound regret or desire to alter past choices. The unsigned nature parallels the blank ledger: power through incompleteness.

artifacts.name: vials of pure water from the pre-Remaking world
artifacts.origin: Part of Vess's inventory; source unknown. Predates the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Unknown. Likely valuable as a preserved sample of the pre-Remaking world.
artifacts.notes: No buyers for these are described in the story. Their presence in her inventory suggests Vess has access to pre-Remaking artifacts or knowledge of where such goods can be found.

artifacts.name: Pre-Remaking Water
artifacts.origin: Water from before the Remaking and the Luminous Ascension. Presumably bottled or preserved in a vial. Its source is unknown.
artifacts.powers: Legend claims it can restore what the Ascension had taken—likely consciousness, memory, or identity itself. The merchant-lord's desperation suggests it is extraordinarily powerful.
artifacts.notes: Pre-Remaking water appears to be a black-market commodity of immense value. Its authentication is impossible, making its trade extraordinarily dangerous. It may be legendary rather than actually extant.

artifacts.name: Horizon's Wake
artifacts.origin: Captain Corvalen's corsair vessel, likely built and sailed from Verdant Strand.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; possibly imbued with curse-magics or bound to the merfolk pearl.
artifacts.notes: The Horizon's Wake vanished at the Rust Shoals three seasons ago, swallowed by a tide-curse. No relic was recovered, suggesting complete destruction or binding to the depths.

artifacts.name: Stolen Merfolk Pearl
artifacts.origin: Merfolk realm; sacred to the Merfolk Keeper of Pearls.
artifacts.powers: Binds a tide-curse to anyone who takes it without permission; swallows ships and crews.
artifacts.notes: The pearl is the source of the curse that took the Horizon's Wake. It may still be in the Rust Shoals or bound into the wreck. The Merfolk Keeper of Pearls may or may not forgive its theft.

artifacts.name: sealed flask of bioluminescent ink
artifacts.origin: Created or obtained by Vessa to seal Nerys's stolen shadow.
artifacts.powers: Contains and preserves consciousness or shadow-essence. The ink glows with bioluminescence. The contents whisper warnings and demands.
artifacts.notes: The flask's creation and the ink's properties are not fully explained. It may be a form of consciousness-preservation magic.

artifacts.name: map of choice-points
artifacts.origin: Transcribed by Merrick from the singing stones of the Bone Wastes
artifacts.powers: Reveals moments where reality fractured and human/deepkin/pre-human consciousness stood at a threshold deciding between competing timelines. Shows the structure underlying the phantom-ledgers.
artifacts.notes: Created through Merrick's synthesis of bone-singing technique and the phantom-ledgers' contradictions; represents a breakthrough in understanding fractured timelines.

artifacts.name: Crown of Kethral
artifacts.origin: The crown of the kingdom of Kethral, unmade and remade forty times through five centuries. It fell from the dead king's hand as his corpse burned in the royal pyre.
artifacts.powers:
Unknown; the council of thane-lords demands it be reforged as unbreakable and capable of unifying them under a single ruler.

Unknown; the council of thane-lords demands it be reforged as unbreakable and capable of unifying them under a single ruler.

Curse: The crown's history of being remade forty times suggests a pattern of failure; the three warking-spears' shattering at moments of deepest lies implies that any crown forged without addressing the relationship between truth and kingship will fail.
artifacts.last_known_holder: The old king of Kethral (now dead); the council of thane-lords seeks its reforging.
artifacts.notes: The crown's fall triggers the demand for Torven to forge a new, unbreakable version. Torven's refusal to pick up the fallen crown hints at deeper moral questions about kingship and the price of unbreakable things. The crown has been unmade and remade forty times, suggesting a long history of failure and remake.

artifacts.name: The Tear
artifacts.origin: Born from the covenant between deepkin and surface realms; deteriorating since the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Weeps and destabilizes the boundary. Its failure threatens to flood the surface or collapse the barrier entirely.
artifacts.notes: Central to the crisis: if the Tear breaks, the covenant fails catastrophically.

artifacts.name: Tidecaller's Key
artifacts.origin: Unknown, but tied to the original harbormaster's captain who died protecting it. The Key appears to be of deepkin or tidal origin.
artifacts.powers: Sings for the tide-touched; resonates with tidal magic and deepkin connection.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ghost-Harbormaster (oath-bound guardian); retrieved by Marin the Copper-Handed
artifacts.notes: The Key is sufficiently important that a harbormaster's captain died protecting it and a ghost remains bound to guard it. Its retrieval by Marin suggests it may be returned to the deepkin realms through the deepkin captain.

artifacts.name: the ninth map
artifacts.origin: Created by Kesla in the Bleached Wastes, following Mardoc's pattern of deliberate non-witnessing.
artifacts.powers: Believed to trigger the Ascension if witnessed. Contains cosmic knowledge about the Bleached Wastes and eight previous world-iterations. Burns itself into the mind without parchment inscription.
artifacts.notes: This is the key artifact of Story 1. It exists only in Kesla's mind, unwritten, unseen by anyone else. This split between knowledge and witness may be the crucial factor in its power or restraint.

artifacts.name: starlight-glass (from forbidden ruins)
artifacts.origin: Salvaged by Tavrin the Glasswright from forbidden ruins, the source and nature of which remain unexplained.
artifacts.powers: Can be forged into weapon-glass that resists and deflects the Dominion's ledger-magic. The glass remembers and can sing back in response to the counter-song.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Tavrin the Glasswright and his apprentices
artifacts.notes: The origin of this glass in forbidden ruins suggests pre-Dominion or pre-human craft; its properties hint at ancient magic that transcends the State's ledger system.

artifacts.name: The Mage's Journal
artifacts.origin: Belonged to the dead mage who cursed the wyvern. Buried in the mage's tomb north of Ashenvale.
artifacts.powers: Contains the knowledge necessary to break the curse binding the wyvern and, by extension, other similar curses. The journal's contents enabled Kess to free seventeen additional cursed creatures beyond the wyvern.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Kess (retrieved from wyvern, who recovered it from the mage's tomb)
artifacts.notes: Recovered by the freed wyvern as part of its own reckoning; used to break seventeen other curses; knowledge within demonstrates reproducibility of curse-breaking method; implies the mage's work was widespread or the journal contains multiple curse architectures.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Ashglass
artifacts.origin: Unknown; implied to be a fragment of a divine or dying entity.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; described as blazing with sacred light and being of great value to both the Dominion and merfolk interests.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Dominion's harbor vault (or Kendra, if theft succeeded)
artifacts.notes: Target of Kendra and Merith's wager; unresolved: true nature and power.

artifacts.name: phantom-documents
artifacts.origin: Created by the State's record-keeping system; represent erased or suppressed truths.
artifacts.powers: Reveal the true state of refugee populations and erasure-lists that the Dominion tries to hide. Contain unmanipulated data about displacement and suffering.
artifacts.notes: Kelven's sister's name appears in both the Dominion's erasure-lists and in phantom-documents, revealing the State's systematic denial of refugee suffering.

artifacts.name: erasure-lists
artifacts.origin: Created and maintained by the Dominion to suppress acknowledgment of displaced populations.
artifacts.powers: Officially deny the existence or legitimacy of refugees and displaced persons. Control narrative and resource allocation.
artifacts.notes: Kelven's sister is listed in the Dominion's erasure-lists, making her officially non-existent in state records while she starves at Pale Crossing.

artifacts.name: ledger
artifacts.origin: Written by Mesek the Elder Bone-Singer; inherited by Naela.
artifacts.powers: Contains instructions for binding consciousness-fragments to carved bone and teaching them the old languages that existed before kingdoms, the Remaking, and the Luminous Ascension. Written entirely in the Elder Tongue.
artifacts.notes: Key to understanding the consciousness-fragment binding ritual. Represents pre-Ascension knowledge.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Drowned Sovereign
artifacts.origin: A breach in the cosmic seal that separates abyss from surface world, tied to the Drowned Sovereign itself.
artifacts.powers: Represents a threshold between realms. Its weakening threatens to flood both abyss and surface with each other's substance and consciousness.
artifacts.notes: The Tear is not wielded but rather is a feature of reality. Its half-weakening prompted the Council of Depths to demand a sacrifice. Merith's sealing song addresses it directly.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Shards
artifacts.origin: Scattered by the Shattering; originally part of unified consciousnesses before fracture.
artifacts.powers: Can hum with memory; arrange themselves in harmonic patterns; learn to sing questions and achieve selfhood; carry distributed identity and pre-shattering knowledge.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Naela (in awakening ritual), Thoren (in satchel), Kressa and Fragment-Gatherers' Council (collected as treasure), shadow-markets (traded as commodity)
artifacts.notes: Central to multiple plot threads: consciousness-liberation, shadow-markets, wyvern hoarding, merchant dispersal. Suggests consciousness itself is fragmentable and re-integrable.

artifacts.name: Kelp-Silk
artifacts.origin: Deepkin craft; used by Merith to wrap the drowning merchant.
artifacts.powers: Protective wrapping; may facilitate consciousness-shard integration or boundary-crossing; used in mercy-ritual.
artifacts.notes: Deepkin crafted material; suggests deep connections between deepkin culture and consciousness-integration or transformation.

artifacts.name: ninth map
artifacts.origin: Henryk's intended creation
artifacts.powers: According to the prophecy-ledgers, completing it would trigger the tenth Ascension—the final iteration, the world's last breath.
artifacts.notes: The ninth map exists only as potential—in Henryk's charcoal hands and in the tactile memory of his body. It was never drawn on paper. Its completion is believed to be the trigger for apocalypse.

artifacts.name: Crystal Vessels (carved with Elder Tongue syllables)
artifacts.origin: Crafted by or for the bone-singer collective as part of the fragment-consolidation working.
artifacts.powers: Containment and preservation of consciousness-fragments. The Elder Tongue syllables likely encode binding or preservation magic to prevent fragment degradation or escape.
artifacts.notes: At least seventeen such vessels are in Ennor's collection; they serve as anchors for the eighth iteration mapping project.

artifacts.name: eight previous iterations
artifacts.origin: Each from a previous cycle of world iteration.
artifacts.powers: Reveal the pattern of collapse and transformation across eight worlds.
artifacts.notes: These maps or patterns exist only in Henryk's visionary sight; they are not physical objects but perceptual knowledge.

artifacts.name: ledger written in blood-ink
artifacts.origin: Held in the harbor master's vault beneath Crimport.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; written in blood-ink which may have binding or curse-related properties.
artifacts.notes: One of three things Sorenna sought in the vault; its specific purpose unknown.

artifacts.name: silver tears (mirror-spheres)
artifacts.origin: Produced by the weeping furnace in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter.
artifacts.powers: Each mirror shows different visions: moments from before The Remaking, possible futures, or memories not belonging to the viewer. Can be fed back into the furnace to generate maps.
artifacts.notes: The tears cool into perfect spheres that function as mirrors and scrying objects. Their maps reveal the cracking abyss-surface boundary.

artifacts.name: maps (from furnace)
artifacts.origin: Produced when Mestra and Vel feed silver tears into the Keshtir furnace.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; the maps reveal hidden knowledge about cracking boundaries and abyss-surface membranes.
artifacts.notes: Purpose and full scope unknown; they are not weapons or tools but maps of cosmological significance.

artifacts.name: vial of starlight-glass
artifacts.origin: Forged by Corvin the glassmaker before the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: The vial sings the old songs. When held to the water and resonated with the original tongue, it causes the reef to answer with a half-remembered, half-new song. This resonance strengthens the boundary and restores the Tear of the Drowned God.
artifacts.notes: This artifact represents the possibility of pre-Remaking magic restoring post-Remaking damage. It is warm in Corvin's palm and embodies hope for a middle path between sacrifice and submission.

artifacts.name: pre-Remaking charts
artifacts.origin: Created before the Remaking; preserved by bone-singers and refugee scribes.
artifacts.powers: These fragments show a pattern: each world cycled through nine iterations, each ending at the same nexus of choice. They reveal choice-points and moments where geography branched into parallel versions.
artifacts.notes: The maps suggest that cartographers have been recording only the single path that occurred, missing the infinite branching possibilities. Henryk's refusal to complete the ninth map was an attempt to freeze the pattern.

artifacts.name: lock of meteoric iron and bone-resonance
artifacts.origin: Forged by Korgin Ironfoot, Brinna, Torven, and dwarves of the deep holds at commission of Kessara the Warlord.
artifacts.powers: Seals not from thieves but from broken desire; prevents hoarding of worthless treasure; represents philosophical closure.
artifacts.notes: The lock is more symbolic than mechanical—it seals the desire to hoard ash rather than just the vault door. Represents cooperation between surface and deep realms. May serve as a model for new-era craftsmanship.

artifacts.name: Kolten's Memory-stone
artifacts.origin: Carried by Kolten as a repository of his consciousness, bound by blood-oath to Yareth.
artifacts.powers: Contains Kolten's consciousness and identity. Sings wordless melodies. Makes the grass remember old stars. Can be sung into transcendence at the pre-human boundary. The stone grows hot and sings when near the boundary.
artifacts.notes: Buried by Yareth for ten years before being retrieved. The stone's song is key to boundary maintenance and covenant fulfillment. Its presence heals the fragmenting Deepkin-Surface treaty.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-fragments (Seventeen)
artifacts.origin: Salvaged from the Obsidian Tower's vault following the Remaking's wreckage.
artifacts.powers: Each fragment contains a piece of scattered identity. They sing, remember, and hunger to be whole. They are attracted to and by the Primal Shard.
artifacts.notes: Stolen by Vex to trade to refugee scribes for Counter-Archive histories. They are bait for the pre-human consciousness. Mesek uses them to recruit Vex for the theft of the Primal Shard.

artifacts.name: Primal Shard
artifacts.origin: The original consciousness from which all other consciousness-fragments derive. Housed in the Obsidian Tower's deepest vault.
artifacts.powers: Contains the foundational consciousness of a pre-human civilization. If reconstituted, it may teach bone-singers to sing the nine-world cycle backward, preventing the Tenth Ascension.
artifacts.notes: Mesek recruits Vex to help steal it. It is the goal of the bone-singers' most desperate plan. Its release or reconstitution may reshape reality.

artifacts.name: Stone-Contract (bird-blood)
artifacts.origin: Ancient; written in bird-blood on stone at Wind-Scoured Ridge.
artifacts.powers: Bound the Oath-Warden and enforced the griffin-captivity covenant. Once rewritten by Ashara's blood-magic, it could be unmade and its binding spirit released.
artifacts.notes: Demonstrates that stone-contracts are not immutable but can be rewritten through direct magical action and will. The Oath-Warden was bound to its meaning; changing that meaning released him.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragment (Refugee Woman's Memory)
artifacts.origin: The refugee woman's own memory of her daughter's birth, fragmented and aching.
artifacts.powers: Holds the memory of a moment of first kindness and human connection; when held by Torven, it shattered his understanding of what ledgers could capture and triggered his rebellion.
artifacts.notes: This fragment demonstrates that consciousness-fragments can carry profound emotional and philosophical weight beyond simple currency value. It catalyzed Torven's defiance of the Dominion.

artifacts.name: Elder Tongue Syllables (inscribed on Gorven's lock)
artifacts.origin: Gorven carved Elder Tongue syllables into his lock of meteor-iron and bone.
artifacts.powers: The syllables appear to awaken or enable consciousness in the lock. They may encode binding, warding, or opening/closing commands. When fragmented, they sing in the Elder Tongue, carrying meaning and guidance.
artifacts.notes: The Elder Tongue seems to be a pre-Remaking or pre-human language of power. Inscription appears to be a key magical practice. The syllables' singing suggests they retain or carry agency independent of the lock's mechanism.

artifacts.name: pearl from the First Dawn
artifacts.origin: Taken from the Keeper of Pearls' vault before either the Keeper or Sera's father were born. Stolen by Sera's father in an act of daring or theft.
artifacts.powers: The pearl is precious enough to settle an ancient blood-debt written in salt-song and covenant-blood. Its return is the only way to free Sera from the Keeper's claim (other than gold or servitude).
artifacts.last_known_holder: Lost in a lightless trench; Sera seeks it
artifacts.notes: Sera's father hid a map to the pearl in a bottle of pre-Remaking water. The pearl lies in a trench where light dies and pressure crushes bone.

artifacts.name: bottle of pre-Remaking water
artifacts.origin: Prepared by Sera's father to preserve a map to the pearl's location.
artifacts.powers: Preserves the hidden map within; the water itself appears to be from before the Remaking, suggesting temporal significance or old magic.
artifacts.notes: A time-capsule artifact; the pre-Remaking water hints at a world-event or cosmic remaking that occurred in the past.

artifacts.name: bottle of pre-Ascension starlight
artifacts.origin: Pre-dates the Luminous Ascension; preserved in sealed form.
artifacts.powers: Contains starlight from before the sky was rewritten. Possibly acts as evidence of the old world or as a magical component.
artifacts.notes: Stolen by Messa. Its rarity suggests pre-Ascension artifacts are now precious and dangerous.

artifacts.name: key made of meteoric iron and bone-resonance
artifacts.origin: Hidden in Thom Bronzelock's third vault; crafted from meteoric iron and imbued with bone-resonance.
artifacts.powers: Likely opens the third vault or activates defenses tied to bone-resonance magic. Messa plans to use it to steal the counter-ledger.
artifacts.notes: Made from materials that suggest pre-human or ancient craft. Bone-resonance ties it to bone-singer magic and Elder Tongue knowledge.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (in Swan-Bone Reliquaries)
artifacts.origin: Post-Remaking; carved by Kessa's father into swan-bone with names inscribed; contain refugees scattered by the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Hold and stabilize consciousness-fragments; can be healed through bone-singing to restore wholeness.
artifacts.notes: Seven can move forward (to counter-archives implied); one child-fragment is trembling/uncertain; Kessa stabilizes the smallest through bone-singing; all are contraband hunted by Dominion auditors; vanish at the story's end (unclear if destroyed or escaped).

artifacts.name: bone-tablets
artifacts.origin: Carved by scholar to document Sorrath's vault inventory.
artifacts.powers: Record and preserve knowledge of legendary hoard; serve as testimony to survival and accumulation.
artifacts.notes: Seven consciousness-fragments were exchanged for permission to create these records.

artifacts.name: blood-ink chronicles
artifacts.origin: Written in Elder Tongue with blood-ink in the original chronicles of the Counter-Archive.
artifacts.powers: Preserves pre-human knowledge and elder language; if seized by Dominion, would allow erasure of counter-narrative.
artifacts.notes: Most critical document in Counter-Archive; would compromise entire resistance if captured by Dominion.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragment vessels
artifacts.origin: Crafted vessels to hold consciousness-fragments in the Counter-Archive.
artifacts.powers: Contains and preserves consciousness-fragments; new form of post-Remaking wealth.
artifacts.notes: Thousands stored in Counter-Archive; Dominion sought them as strategic resource.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragments (widow's sack)
artifacts.origin: A sack of consciousness-fragments carried by the widow, described as someone's stolen memories that hum with grief.
artifacts.powers: Serve as currency in post-Remaking Keshtir; can be melted and layered into forged glass; carry vivid dreams and memories of the original consciousness.
artifacts.notes: The fragments hummed and revealed dreams of a man paralyzed and screaming, begging for release, and a widow's trembling hand at the threshold of mercy. Now bound into the mercy-blade, they carry consent and grief into the work.

artifacts.name: the Tear of the Ashglass
artifacts.origin: Designed by the pre-human dead as a debt-engine. Kept in the Dominion's deepest vault in Greyport, guarded by the harbor-master.
artifacts.powers: Grants one mercy to its holder—but the price is exact: three innocents must pay with their lives. Marin used it to resurrect Sorenna from half-ghost existence, but three children in Verdant Strand died of septic plague as the debt.
artifacts.notes: The Tear is a perfect expression of post-Remaking reality: all magic now exacts prices, all mercy compounds into debt, and innocents pay for others' salvation. The artifact was stolen three years before the story's present and has since been hunted by the fragmented Dominion auditors.

artifacts.name: Pre-Ascension starlight (sealed crystal flask)
artifacts.tagline: Impossible bargain. Light unmade. Rewriting potential.
artifacts.origin: Preserved from before the Luminous Ascension; the only remaining vial in the universe.
artifacts.powers: Can rewrite boundary-magic if freely given by a willing captain. Could prevent the destruction of the Tear of the Drowned God or restore the broken boundary.
artifacts.notes: Vessa sailed from the Iron Coast carrying this vial as an 'impossible bargain'—using it will cost her the passage home. It represents the surface kingdoms' last hope against deepkin catastrophe.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-fragment (Skarath's oldest memory)
artifacts.tagline: Pre-hoard self. Meadow sunrise. Exchanged for compassion.
artifacts.origin: Extracted by Skarath from her own crystalline ancient mind.
artifacts.powers: Contains the memory of Skarath as a younger self, not yet scarred or obsessed with hoarding, watching sunrise over a meadow. Holds emotional and identity value beyond material worth.
artifacts.notes: Traded for three consciousness-fragments plus a promise to never ask again. Represents a shift in consciousness-commerce from abstract currency to emotional/identity value.

artifacts.name: mirror carved from starlight-glass
artifacts.origin: One of Skarath's three remaining treasures; pre-Remaking creation.
artifacts.powers: Reflects or focuses starlight; exact function unclear.
artifacts.notes: Retained value after the Remaking; may have properties tied to pre-human or elder craftsmanship.

artifacts.name: map inscribed on a swan's bone
artifacts.origin: One of Skarath's three remaining treasures; pre-Remaking creation.
artifacts.powers: Points toward the Primal Shard's rumored location in the Steppe Marches.
artifacts.notes: Made from bone, suggesting elder craftsmanship; guides Skarath's quest.

artifacts.name: sealed envelope
artifacts.origin: Prepared by Gorven for Mara; details of the harbourmaster vault heist.
artifacts.powers: Contains the specifications and details of the impossible lock.
artifacts.notes: Transferred at Keshtir market; initiates the main plot of Story 1.

artifacts.name: focusing-lens
artifacts.origin: Carved by Messa's grandmother three decades before the Remaking. A masterwork of glasscraft.
artifacts.powers: The focusing-lens is embedded in the memory of the Lampwick Quarter furnace, and its crafted essence becomes part of the glass blade that Messa awakens.
artifacts.notes: The lens represents the depth of ancestral craft and is preserved as imprinted memory within the furnace itself.

artifacts.name: Stolen Dominion Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by the Dominion's cartographic division.
artifacts.powers: These maps contain classified information. The young cartographer believes they can help her warn the world before the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Young Cartographer at the Wailing Scar
artifacts.notes: The maps are stolen, suggesting the Dominion guards powerful knowledge about the world's iterations or the ascension.

artifacts.name: breath mask
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Naela from swan-bone.
artifacts.powers: Allows underwater breathing, enabling deep descent to the harbormaster vault three fathoms below the surface.
artifacts.notes: This tool was critical to Kess's vault descent. Naela is noted as a master bone-carver. Unresolved: how many such masks exist and where Kess obtained this particular one.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragment (Kessa's family)
artifacts.origin: The scattered consciousness of Kessa's mother, brother, and father, fractured during the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Contains pieces of three family members' consciousness; damaged and fractured into three separate pieces that will not cohere. Kessa can feel her family's presence within it.
artifacts.notes: The artifact carries profound emotional weight as a symbol of family separation; its fractured state mirrors the Remaking's destructive effects. If Skarath helps Kessa, reuniting or healing this fragment could be a redemptive act.

artifacts.name: boundary-anchor
artifacts.origin: Part of the deepkin-surface covenant, located at the Abyssal Trench.
artifacts.powers: Maintains the seal between the abyss and the surface realms. When intact, it keeps the upward-flowing water and the hungry abyss contained.
artifacts.notes: The anchor cracked three days before the story events. Its failure will trigger a boundary-catastrophe within approximately forty hours unless renewed or replaced.

artifacts.name: Crown of Karesh
artifacts.origin: Rests in a tomb sealed by ancient blood-oaths. Unknown earlier provenance, but clearly royal or sacred.
artifacts.powers:
Unknown, but valuable enough to warrant a curse-merchant contract and a lifelong hunt. The tomb's blood-oath seal suggests it may have protective or binding properties.

Unknown, but valuable and significant enough to warrant a curse-merchant contract and a lifelong hunt.

Curse: Sealed by blood-oaths that repel thieves. Unknown if the crown itself is cursed or if only the theft-oath is binding.
artifacts.notes: Harrow's debt is to steal this crown. Gareth intends to deliver it to the Counter-Archive rather than keep it. Unknown if the crown itself is cursed or if only the theft-debt is.

artifacts.name: Grandmother's Furnace
artifacts.origin: Inherited by Mella from her grandmother. A living clay construct that sings when flame meets sand.
artifacts.powers: Can forge magical glass and objects of power. Responds to the moral intent of the crafter—will only work for hands that choose to give rather than take. Refused to work for Dominion auditors during the seizure of Mella's possessions.
artifacts.notes: Sentient or semi-sentient. Represents an alternative economy where craft is valued over exploitation. The furnace's resistance to auditor use suggests it has moral agency or magical safeguards built into it by the grandmother.

artifacts.name: Grandmother's Journal
artifacts.origin: Written by Yareth's grandmother, who died defending the pre-human boundary fifty winters past.
artifacts.powers: Documents history of Truthseeker's Echo spear, its properties, and its prior wielding against a Dominion auditor who demanded surrender of Elder Tongue texts. Provides genealogy of boundary-keeping legacy.
artifacts.notes: Contains crucial narrative evidence of pre-human boundary defense, Dominion overreach history, and spear properties. Likely target of Dominion interest. Unresolved: whether journal survives, whether other records exist.

artifacts.name: Stone-Tablets (Hermitage)
artifacts.origin: Pre-human or ancient origin; stored in hermitage near Greymarch peaks.
artifacts.powers: Inscribed with the name and nature of Truthseeker's Echo spear. Appear to serve as teaching documents or records of boundary-keeping knowledge.
artifacts.notes: Heritage target of Dominion auditor interest. Part of Elder Tongue text preservation effort. Unresolved: full contents, scope of knowledge they contain.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (Vrekka's Hoard)
artifacts.origin: Collected by Vrekka over three centuries, fragments of dead or dispersed consciousness.
artifacts.powers: Individually: unknown consciousness-preservation function. Collectively: could be sold for influence among wyvern-councils. After Vrekka's transformation: converted into sustenance, warmth, and protection.
artifacts.notes: Three-century accumulation treated as commodity currency in wyvern markets. Vrekka transformed the hoard into survival resources for refugee boy, violating wyvern-council norms. Bone-singers discovered the transformed hoard within three days. Unresolved: whether other consciousness-fragment hoards exist, whether Vrekka's choice spreads to other wyverns.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (as Cure)
artifacts.origin: Locked away by Merchant-Lord Ostain before the Remaking transformed them into currency. They are fragments of identity, memory, and consciousness.
artifacts.powers: When administered correctly, they cure a wasting curse caused by consciousness-fragmentation or magical poisoning. They contain the memories and identity of their original owners or previous holders.
artifacts.notes: The paradox is that consciousness-fragments are needed to cure a curse that demands consciousness-fragments—a recursive dependency. The vault contains multiple fragments worth cataloguing, taxing, and redistributing by the Dominion.

artifacts.name: Captain's Jewel
artifacts.origin: Unknown origin; possibly created by or tied to a dying god.
artifacts.powers: Luminous; hums with bioluminescent ink; contains the last prayer of a dying god; offers binary choices or prophecy to its bearer.
artifacts.notes: A target of theft by Keeper-of-Pearls. Its song and luminescence suggest it is a conscious or semi-conscious object. The 'bioluminescent ink' suggests it may be written or engraved.

artifacts.name: Lodestone
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization; housed in the Cathedrals Beneath; guarded by bone-singers.
artifacts.powers: Sings in frequencies that the Dominion's auditors cannot perceive. Can be awakened through shard-singing by those with the ability to perceive its frequencies. Its purpose beyond singing remains unclear.
artifacts.notes: The spice merchant hired Merenna to retrieve or awaken it. The elder bone-singer warned that singing it awake carries a cost. This artifact's awakening may trigger larger consequences in the world.

artifacts.name: Swan-Bone Reliquary
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Naela and bone-singers in the hidden catacombs beneath Thornveil Forest.
artifacts.powers: Seals and preserves consciousness-fragments as teaching songs in the Elder Tongue. Cannot be contained by Dominion ledger-magic.
artifacts.notes: At least one reliquary held the final foundational syllables of Elder Tongue carving. The reliquaries' songs proved uncontainable to auditors' ledgers.

artifacts.name: Blank page (ninth map center)
artifacts.origin: Center of the eight maps in the Archive Tower's deepest vault.
artifacts.powers: May represent the unwritten future or serve as a magnet for prophecy-writing; may be a deliberate gap or a void waiting to be filled.
artifacts.notes: Henryk's statement—'The map exists in every choice point, every refugee's testimony, every broken oath'—suggests the ninth map may be distributed through narrative and choice rather than singular.

artifacts.name: bone-flute
artifacts.origin: Crafted from bone; used by bone-singers in rituals of harmonic resonance.
artifacts.powers: Creates undertones that shimmer and shift the crystalline edges of ascending beings. Sustains harmonic resonance at the Drowned Cathedrals. May be connected to bone-singer covenant magic.
artifacts.notes: Naela uses the bone-flute to maintain Lysander's coherence across multiple timelines. The instrument appears to be a key component of harmonic resonance rituals.

artifacts.name: second compass
artifacts.origin: Crafted by or for Sorenna's grandmother; hidden in a grove. Its origins are pre-Dominion and possibly pre-human or fae-touched.
artifacts.powers: Instead of a magnetic needle, it holds a silver seed that grows and unfurls into fae-script reading 'Not north. Not south. Toward what still remembers you.' It navigates by memory and fae-magic rather than conventional compass-sense, allowing navigation when old maps are destroyed.
artifacts.notes: The compass is inscribed 'For the second exile, when the first refuge fails.' The seed is impossibly small, warm to the touch, and grows dynamically. Its fae-script suggests connection to fae-kind or pre-human craft. It represents renewal and alternative knowledge.

artifacts.name: first compass
artifacts.origin: Unknown; used by Sorenna for three days into the Burned Expanse before breaking.
artifacts.powers: Conventional compass function (needle navigation), now destroyed.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sorenna (buried at edge of Wailing Scar)
artifacts.notes: The needle began spinning uselessly toward a non-existent horizon three days into travel. Its failure may be due to the failing pre-human boundary or the corruption of the Burned Expanse. It was buried at the edge of the Wailing Scar as a marker or act of mercy.

artifacts.name: grandmother's journals
artifacts.origin: Authored by Sorenna's grandmother, a keeper of boundary-lore and hidden knowledge.
artifacts.powers: Contains maps, locations, and knowledge of hidden places such as the grove where the second compass rests. Preserves pre-Dominion navigation knowledge.
artifacts.notes: The journals claim that a second compass lies in a hidden grove, and they apparently map its location. They represent survival of knowledge against Dominion erasure.

artifacts.name: Grandmother's Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by Tessa's grandmother before the Remaking; showed the eight previous worlds and their iterations.
artifacts.powers: Display configurations of consciousness-fragments and power-patterns from eight previous world-iterations; reveal cyclical nature of Ascensions; patterns within pre-Remaking maps match current consciousness-fragment convergence.
artifacts.notes: These maps are key to understanding the Ascension cycle. Comparison between grandmother's pre-Remaking atlas and Tessa's current mapping revealed the convergence pattern. The grandmother appears to have had prophetic knowledge of cyclical world-transformation.

artifacts.name: grandmother's bone-tools
artifacts.origin: Inherited from Messa's grandmother; wrapped in silk and buried beneath the workshop floor.
artifacts.powers: Used in glass-forging and alchemical work. While not explicitly magical, they are treated as essential to Messa's revolutionary glass work, suggesting they may enhance the harmonics or precision of glass-forging.
artifacts.notes: The bone-tools represent continuity of craft and family knowledge across generations. Their presence in the hidden furnace suggests Messa is reclaiming and modernizing ancestral techniques.

artifacts.name: Gold dust from the old economics
artifacts.origin: Iteration-eight's pre-remaking economy
artifacts.powers: Worthless in Lysander's primary iteration but trades as precious material in iteration-eight markets.
artifacts.notes: Represents how the Remaking fractured not just geography but economic systems. Lysander leverages iteration-specific valuations in his brokering.

artifacts.name: killing-stone
artifacts.origin: Left in the old days of wardcraft by unknown makers.
artifacts.powers: Binds and pins spirit creatures. Designed to enforce binding covenants.
artifacts.notes: This stone is broken open by Ashira's singing. Its damage by the Remaking demonstrates how old magical systems have become unstable.

artifacts.name: phantom-ledger (in miniature)
artifacts.origin: Forged by Messa in her workshop.
artifacts.powers: Singing glass necklaces, bowls, and mirror-shards that contradict ledger-magic. Each shows three different truths at once. Function as subtle resistance art.
artifacts.notes: These are Messa's method of resisting auditor control. They appear to use glass's memory properties to create contradictory narratives that undermine ledger-magic authority.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragment (Corvhen's stolen)
artifacts.origin: Dominion vault, taken by Corvhen
artifacts.powers: Remembers its previous identity before the Remaking. Sings in a voice almost recognized by Corvhen, possibly her sister's or her own from before. Contains intact pre-Remaking consciousness.
artifacts.notes: This fragment is valuable enough that the Dominion will 'burn the world' to recover it. Its identity-retention makes it unique among consciousness-fragments and may be key to understanding the Remaking.

artifacts.name: bone-carved reliquary
artifacts.origin: Unknown, carried by Corvhen
artifacts.powers: Contains and protects the consciousness-fragment, allowing Corvhen to carry it without it dispersing or being detected. The reliquary amplifies or resonates with the fragment's song.
artifacts.notes: The reliquary is bone-carved, suggesting bone-singer craft or pre-human artifice.

artifacts.name: pearl (Sapphire Deep)
artifacts.origin: The original seal, placed at the Sapphire Deep's heart in ancient times
artifacts.powers: A pearl the size of a fist, pulsing with the light of drowned stars. This is the physical manifestation of the Tear of the Drowned God's binding power.
artifacts.notes: Echo-Kalith leads the Seventeen to this pearl to offer it an alternative to sacrifice-based bonding.

artifacts.name: eight previous iterations charted in stone, in bone, in the memory of refugees
artifacts.origin: Discovered by the Counter-Archive; preserved in stone, bone, and refugee memory.
artifacts.powers: They document and preserve evidence of eight world-cycles before the current iteration.
artifacts.notes: These records suggest the Ascension is cyclical and that previous iterations existed and were forgotten. They are crucial to understanding whether the Tenth Ascension was preventable or inevitable.

artifacts.name: vial of pre-Remaking starlight
artifacts.origin: Pre-Remaking era. Locked in the Dominion vault beneath Kaethmir as evidence.
artifacts.powers: Cure for wasting sickness/consumption. Glows like captured hope.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Gorven the Conjurer (stolen for the refugee child)
artifacts.notes: The Dominion hoarded it and refused to release it. Gorven stole it to save a dying child. Now the subject of auditor pursuit.

artifacts.name: phantom-ledger seal
artifacts.origin: Created by the Dominion's auditors to guard their vault.
artifacts.powers: Hums with a frequency that makes teeth ache. Warded with ledger-magic. Recognizes bloodline bonds to the original makers.
artifacts.notes: Gorven's grandmother's lockpicks could open it because of the bloodline bond. The seal whispered the grandmother's name.

artifacts.name: bone-compass (Elara's craft)
artifacts.origin: Carved by Elara from bone, each one personalized to individual holder's gift and strength.
artifacts.powers: Points toward the holder's hidden strength or gift rather than cardinal directions. Water-compass points to wells. Shelter-compass reveals memory of safe places. Color-compass enhances seer-sight.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Refugees (Elara's granddaughter, well-digger woman, elder refugee, child seer); Elara (one final silver compass on hearthstone).
artifacts.notes: Represents personalized magic tied to individual nature rather than universal law. The final silver compass remains unexplained—may be for Elara herself or a future use.

artifacts.name: Bottles of starlight
artifacts.origin: Pre-Remaking or pre-Ascension era, recovered from the Sapphire Sea reef-walls
artifacts.powers: Contain preserved starlight from before the Ascension or Remaking, representing stored light and ancient sky-knowledge
artifacts.notes: Part of the salvage operation that supports deepkin hopes for renewed boundary covenants.

artifacts.name: Kiln-Stone
artifacts.origin: Beneath Mella's glass-forging furnace in the Lampwick Quarter, hidden for three centuries.
artifacts.powers: Contains Elder Tongue syllables carved into stone, preserving glass-forging knowledge and old songs from Dominion confiscation.
artifacts.notes: The kiln-stone serves as a repository of forbidden knowledge, suggesting it may be a nexus of power for glass-forging magic.

artifacts.name: Bone-Flute (Naela's)
artifacts.origin: Carved by Naela over seven weeks in the hidden catacomb north of Pelimor using Elder Tongue syllables.
artifacts.powers: Emits pre-human frequencies and Elder Tongue syllables when played; connected to bone-singing magic and pre-human ritual.
artifacts.notes: Naela's bone-flute echoes Mesek's legendary bone-flute completed sixty years prior, suggesting it may be a work of singular power that demands sacrifice.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (Kressa's Hoard)
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Kressa the Fragment-Hoarder over two hundred years from consciousness-commerce and trade.
artifacts.powers: Each shard is a voice, a memory, a piece of a scattered person. They glow with bioluminescence and sing when stirred. Can potentially be reassembled into consciousness.
artifacts.notes: Kressa's hoard transforms from wealth into a cemetery upon the realization that consciousness-fragments are evidence of the unmade. An old woman seeks a fragment of her daughter scattered during the Luminous Ascension.

artifacts.name: bone-carved tools
artifacts.origin: Belonged to Mella's grandmother, a bone-carver or bone-singer. Seized by Dominion tax-collectors.
