artifacts.powers: Tools for bone-carving, bone-singing, or consciousness-crafting. Their seizure by the Dominion represents persecution of pre-Remaking knowledge.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Dominion auditors (seized); originally Mella's master's workshop.
artifacts.notes: Symbol of the Dominion's campaign against bone-singers and consciousness-workers. Their loss is deeply traumatic to Mella.

artifacts.name: Dreaming Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Housed in the Obsidian Archive; appear to be created by the Dominion.
artifacts.powers: Rewrite themselves with prophecy-ink, containing prophecies that may determine future iterations. Central to the prophecy that completing the ninth-world map will trigger the Tenth Ascension.
artifacts.notes: Target of Korvin's heist; their prophecies shape understanding of world iterations. Connection to Tessa's cartographic prophecy unclear but thematic.

artifacts.name: Eighth World's Cartography
artifacts.origin: Aldrin recovered fragments from pre-human ruins; written in a language that predates ink.
artifacts.powers: Contains maps of the eighth iteration of the world; provides evidence that previous worlds existed and left traces.
artifacts.notes: Aldrin carries fragments of this cartography, written in elder tongue or pre-human language. Used alongside Tessa's incomplete maps to understand the ninth world.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragment Reliquary
artifacts.origin: Created by Dominion and glass-forgers; holds consciousness-fragments extracted from people.
artifacts.powers: Preserves consciousness-fragments; can store individual memories, identities, and voices; serves as currency and control mechanism.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Marin the Copper-Handed (stolen reliquary); Torven Ironfoot (holding one secretly)
artifacts.notes: Marin steals a reliquary from the vault beneath Greyport to reunite families; Torven secretly holds one containing Kess's brother's laugh.

artifacts.name: Phantom-Ledger (Master Copy)
artifacts.origin: Created by the Dominion; the first phantom-ledger, now held in the Archive Tower vault beneath Pelimor.
artifacts.powers: Serves as the keystone of the Dominion's legal authority; if found and destroyed, the entire phantom-ledger system collapses, invalidating false tribute claims.
artifacts.notes: Warden Caelus identifies this as the target; finding and breaking the covenant-lock around it would dissolve the Dominion's false legal structure.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragment
artifacts.origin: Created when consciousness is magically fragmented, often by Dominion ledger-magic or other cosmic workings. Fragments appear to be involuntary results of the Remaking and post-Remaking traumata.
artifacts.powers: Fragments are understood to be pieces of a whole person's consciousness. They can be hoarded as currency, traded, sold, or restored toward wholeness through bone-singing magic. They are said to carry memory, identity, and personhood.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Skarath (before sacrifice), Lysander (before release), the Dominion (in vaults), refugees, bone-singers
artifacts.notes: Consciousness-fragments are the central moral crux of these three stories. The Dominion treats them as currency; Skarath, Lysander, and bone-singers treat them as people. Fragments cannot be fully restored to wholeness post-Remaking, but restoration is still meaningful and redemptive.

artifacts.name: Bone-Carved Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by bone-singers and scribes. Collected by Kressa after the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Hold refugee testimony and can be 'read' through bone-singing frequencies. Contain both written and resonant information.
artifacts.notes: These ledgers are objects of meaning rather than material value. They serve Kressa's new hoarding philosophy and may be part of a larger archive of refugee knowledge.

artifacts.name: grandfather's ancient telescope
artifacts.origin: Belonged to Astrid's grandfather; has survived the upheaval of the Luminous Ascension and remains on Peak Aethros.
artifacts.powers: Allows Astrid to observe distant stars and translate their light-patterns into music and frequency data. Capable of detecting and resolving the subtle melody and counterpoint being transmitted by one of the new stars.
artifacts.notes: Still functional despite the destruction and upheaval. Critical to Astrid's discovery of the singing star.

artifacts.name: three casks of starspice
artifacts.origin: Part of a merchant caravan's cargo bound for the Hearthlands.
artifacts.powers: Starspice is described as remembering theft and containing the elder language within its seeds. The spice itself can guide pursuers through its connection to the Elder Tongue. It is the only commodity that still holds value after the Remaking.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Kelvin of Pale Crossing (hidden in his cellar)
artifacts.notes: Worth enough consciousness-fragments to buy passage across the Vael Ocean. Can feed Kelvin's starving sister through winter. The spice has sacred properties tied to the Elder Tongue.

artifacts.name: Bone Reliquaries
artifacts.origin: Carved by Naela and other bone-singers over decades, each containing consciousness-fragments.
artifacts.powers: Hold and preserve consciousness-fragments; respond to bone-song and Elder Tongue; converge when called by the master bone-flute.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Naela, Torval, Moriel, Old Vrenn, and the bone-singer collective
artifacts.notes: Naela carved consciousness-fragments into bone reliquaries for forty years. Each carving cost her a memory. By the Remaking's eve, she could no longer recall her own mother's face. These reliquaries were the primary components fed into the Primal Shard consolidation.

artifacts.name: The ninth phantom-ledger
artifacts.origin: Dominion archives (stolen by Kellan and Counter-Archive operatives)
artifacts.powers: Contains Counter-Archive knowledge or hidden truth that the Dominion sought to suppress. When embedded in glass by Messa, becomes invisible to audit while remaining accessible to those who know to look.
artifacts.notes: The ninth phantom-ledger is a primary object of desire for both Dominion auditors and Counter-Archive operatives. Its embedding in glass is the central act of Story 1. The number 'ninth' suggests it is one of nine such ledgers or iterations.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-fragments (Skarath's hoard)
artifacts.origin: Stolen from bone-singers, bought from refugees, seized from Dominion raids over three centuries
artifacts.powers: Thousands of pieces of identity and memory that sing in crystal reliquaries. Consciousness cannot be hoarded (as the lore states), yet Skarath has attempted to do so for three centuries. If freed, the fragments could help refugees reconstruct themselves and stabilize the boundary-waters.
artifacts.notes: The consciousness-fragments are the moral center of Stories 2 and 3. Their nature as unhoardable-yet-hoarded suggests Skarath's vault is an unnatural construction that violates cosmic law. Freeing them may require Skarath's death or transformation.

artifacts.name: Pre-Ascension Starlight
artifacts.origin: Sealed in bottles before the Ascension; protected and hidden for forty years by Niel Coldwater.
artifacts.powers: When forged into a lens, becomes a vessel capable of unknown properties. May serve as a conduit for light-magic, consciousness-transfer, or dimensional bridging.
artifacts.notes: The last of the pre-Ascension starlight is forged into the Starlight Lens. Its scarcity and power suggest it may be central to magical working or revelation.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (Dominion Vault Collection)
artifacts.origin: Confiscated by the Dominion from dead worlds, forgotten iterations, merchants of the eighth world, bone-singers, and refugees who died crossing timelines.
artifacts.powers: Each fragment carries memory of a world that no longer exists. They sing audibly, can hear each other through stone, and are treated as currency and fuel by consciousness-merchants.
artifacts.notes: The target of Kess's heist; their liberation or revelation may undo the Dominion's control or reshape the world.

artifacts.name: Dominion-Carved Reliquaries
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Dominion artisans or ledger-workers to hold confiscated consciousness-fragments.
artifacts.powers: Contain and preserve consciousness-fragments; allow fragments to be stored, traded, and controlled. The reliquaries enable the fragments to sing to each other.
artifacts.notes: The reliquaries' craftsmanship may be a form of ledger-magic or bone-singing; their destruction or liberation may free the fragments.

artifacts.name: Phantom-Parchment
artifacts.origin: Used by Dominion auditors as a tool of enforcement.
artifacts.powers: The parchment 'rewrites itself even as' it is being read aloud. It appears to be a ledger-magic tool that can retroactively enforce compliance and rewrite reality according to its entries.
artifacts.notes: Embodies the Dominion's power to retroactively rewrite reality and enforce obedience. Corvin's speed suggests he is faster than the magic itself.

artifacts.name: Phantom-ledger cages
artifacts.origin: Created by Dominion auditors using ledger-magic after the Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Confine consciousness-fragments and lock individuals within ledger-magic systems. Not physical cages but written constraints that bind prisoners as securely as iron.
artifacts.notes: Represent the Dominion's primary method of control. Three hundred refugees are held in such cages in the Quarantine-House. Gareth's grandmother's key is designed to defeat them.

artifacts.name: Nine Maps
artifacts.origin: Inscribed on stone tablets in a half-buried temple in the Bleached Wastes in pre-human script. Each map shows a world's death before the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Document the sequential deaths of worlds across iterations. Prove the Luminous Ascension rewrote reality and erased the eighth world. The ninth map is incomplete—its blank center refuses all inscription.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Thessa (copied in secret ink); Dominion auditors seek completion
artifacts.notes: The ninth map's resistance to prophecy is a mystery. The blank center may reject all futures or may require a specific inscription method. The Dominion's demand for it creates irresolvable pressure on Thessa.

artifacts.name: harbormaster's ledger
artifacts.origin: Kept in the harbormaster's vault beneath Vel-Karsh as an official Dominion record.
artifacts.powers: Contains a three-century chronicle of every oath-mark sworn between surface and abyss. Written in bioluminescent ink that sang the names of the drowned. Without it, the Dominion could rewrite covenant terms unilaterally.
artifacts.notes: This ledger was the key to preventing the Dominion's manipulation of deepkin governance during the Tear's collapse. Its theft by Merren suggests it was intended to preserve the original covenant against rewriting.

artifacts.name: betrothal contract
artifacts.origin: Drafted by Isendra's father and Lord Varen as a formal union agreement.
artifacts.powers: Legally and contractually binds Lady Isendra and Lord Varen in marriage. Contains a five-year annulment clause allowing Isendra to leave unbroken and unmarred if she chooses.
artifacts.notes: The contract stipulates that Varen has no intention of consummating the marriage. It is presented as a survival agreement rather than a traditional love match.

artifacts.name: False Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Forged by Torvin at the Wailing Scar.
artifacts.powers: Provide false records of ownership, transaction, or identity.
artifacts.notes: Part of Torvin's phantom-document trade. May expose him to legal or magical repercussions if discovered by authorities or ledger-magic practitioners.

artifacts.name: Forged Attestations
artifacts.origin: Forged by Torvin at the Wailing Scar.
artifacts.powers: Certify claims or ownership falsely; appear to carry authority.
artifacts.notes: Torvin's product line at the Wailing Scar. Their value depends on buyer credulity and the merchants' desperation for any tradeable commodity.

artifacts.name: Maps to the Eighth Iteration
artifacts.origin: Created or traded by the Goblin-child at the Wailing Scar.
artifacts.powers: Purport to show routes or locations in an alternate timeline (the eighth iteration).
artifacts.notes: These maps are a commodity at the Wailing Scar, available from a goblin-child merchant. Their accuracy and practical utility are unknown.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragment Vessel (Glass)
artifacts.origin: Forged by Mella and other glassblowers in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter; deliberately designed with flaws to deceive auditors.
artifacts.powers: Preserves consciousness-fragments in sealed, portable form. Can be claimed to be defective to hide actual contents from auditors. Allows fragments to be transported and redistributed through underground networks.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Mella (in shard form at Well-House); Moriel (receiving fragments); refugees (seeking specific family fragments)
artifacts.notes: Mella destroys seventeen per month as cover for smuggling. The vessels themselves are mundane glass, but their use-case (fragment preservation) makes them contraband under Dominion law. Quality/durability varies; deliberately flawed ones are destroyed by Mella to provide cover.

artifacts.name: Ledger-Entry
artifacts.origin: Created by Dominion bureaucracy to catalogue consciousness-fragments and identities. Ledger-entries can contradict other versions in other ledgers, making identity uncertain.
artifacts.powers: Serves as proof of existence and identity. A name written in a way that does not contradict another world's version would prove continuity of identity across the Remaking.
artifacts.notes: Thessa seeks a ledger-entry proving Gorven existed in a coherent form before the Remaking fractured him. The existence of multiple contradictory ledgers suggests the Dominion maintains parallel records.

artifacts.name: Memory-Glass
artifacts.origin: Crafted by glass-workers in the Lampwick Quarter, particularly Missa's mother's batch of three hundred vials. Made through furnace-songs and Elder Tongue kiln-stone carving.
artifacts.powers: Contains consciousness-fragments and makes them glow with visible light. Sings with frequencies matching Elder Tongue syllables carved into the kiln-stones. Responds to frequencies in ways that can reveal hidden consciousness-fragments to auditors.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Dominion vaults, Missa's furnace-house, hidden in refugee cloth
artifacts.notes: Memory-glass that sings reveals consciousness-fragments to auditors, so Missa hides her fragments in cloth instead. The glass is both treasure and liability.

artifacts.name: Starlight-Glass Compass
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Sorenna's dead husband as a boundary-pointing artifact.
artifacts.powers: Points to the pre-human boundary; imbued with connection to Sorenna's husband and to boundary magic. Serves as both navigational tool and symbol of redemption.
artifacts.notes: Title artifact. Appears to be a pre-Ascension or post-Ascension magical craft combining starlight glass with navigational properties. Represents the transfer of burden from widow to nomad.

artifacts.name: glass vessels (consciousness-fragment containers)
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Davrin; filled with whole consciousness-fragments stolen from the kiln-house furnace.
artifacts.powers: Each vessel holds a complete consciousness-fragment—memories of gardens, children's laughter, love before the Remaking. Provide psychological and magical resource for refugees and resistance networks.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Distributed across waystation libraries, bone-singers' cathedrals, and the Burned Expanse; unknown individuals and networks.
artifacts.notes: The glass vessels were chosen as a medium, suggesting glass's capacity to preserve and transmit light and memory. Their distribution was rapid and coordinated, indicating organized resistance infrastructure.

artifacts.name: grandmother's bone-compass
artifacts.origin: Inherited through Mira's family line from her grandmother.
artifacts.powers: Spins wildly to indicate the boundary's state. Shows the location of the pre-human boundary. Appears to be a navigational and magical tool for boundary-keepers.
artifacts.notes: Mira held it in her hands during the boundary renewal, its wild spinning a sign of the boundary's imminent failure.

artifacts.name: sealed jar (containing Brennus Ironpeak's shadow)
artifacts.origin: Brennus's shadow, stolen six months prior and held in a shadow-broker's vault in Anthrim. Recovered by Vex.
artifacts.powers: Holds the warlord's stolen shadow, which has been separated from him for six months. Its unsealing on night three of Vex's stay presumably reveals truth or restores agency.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Vex (then unsealed by her in Brennus's stronghold)
artifacts.notes: The shadow itself—intangible but powerful—represents the illusion of Brennus's authority. Its recovery and unsealing are metaphorical and literal acts of liberation.

artifacts.name: Master-Ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by the Dominion as the central record-keeping device for tracking all consciousness-fragments, prisoners, and debts across its realm.
artifacts.powers: Holds records of all consciousness-fragments in Dominion possession and the debts of all prisoners. It is the foundation of Dominion control and surveillance.
artifacts.notes: Mestra and Davrin steal and burn this ledger in sacred flame, destroying the central mechanism of Dominion surveillance and control. Its destruction frees all recorded consciousness-fragments and undermines the legitimacy of Dominion power. The ledger was secured in a vault, suggesting immense value and danger.

artifacts.name: Singing Stones (of the Catacomb)
artifacts.origin: Natural formations within the catacomb north of Pelimor; appear to be ancient and possibly pre-human in origin.
artifacts.powers: Can hold and amplify consciousness; serve as a anchor for bound consciousnesses; allow communion with the pre-human dead; resonate with bone-songs and Elder Tongue syllables.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Catacomb north of Pelimor (with Siara bound to them)
artifacts.notes: The singing stones have transformed from mere geological features into a living artifact hosting Siara's consciousness-bridge. They may have been sacred to bone-singers for generations and may hold other properties yet to be discovered.

artifacts.name: sealed letter from Siara
artifacts.origin: Written by Siara; delivered by bone-singer apprentice.
artifacts.powers: Conveys urgent information about the Tear's failure; summons Sienna to action.
artifacts.notes: The letter reads: 'The Tear is failing. We need someone who knows the old boundaries. We need you.' Sienna burns it after reading.

artifacts.name: covenant-pearls
artifacts.origin: Harvested from the pearl-beds beneath Verdant Strand by reef-keepers under Nerida's captaincy for seventeen years.
artifacts.powers: Bind deepkin realms to the surface world; sing with bioluminescent harmony; serve as touchstone for covenant-renewal.
artifacts.notes: Covenant-pearls transformed from Kael's being are woven into the covenant-stones themselves, becoming part of the deepkin-surface binding.

artifacts.name: Prophecy-Lens
artifacts.origin: Forged by Sella from star-glass and bioluminescent ink for the Counter-Archive.
artifacts.powers: Reveals hidden paths and locations in starlight and shadow. Sella's lens revealed a path to the ninth map in the Ashen Reach.
artifacts.notes: Hidden in Sella's kiln to protect from auditor seizure. Made from pre-Ascension star-glass. Activated on final stroke of inscription. Shows eastward path into Bleached Wastes.

artifacts.name: Pre-Remaking silk
artifacts.origin: Gathered by Hadrin from ruined trade-houses over three months. Pre-dates the Remaking and the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Gleams with starlight preserved in the weave; colors hurt to look at directly. Hadrin learned pre-Ascension techniques for preserving light itself in cloth.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Hadrin (initially); traded to refugees at Pale Crossing
artifacts.notes: Hadrin valued this cloth as currency for trading memories with refugees. The light-preservation technique appears to be a lost skill from before the Remaking.

artifacts.name: the final bone-flute
artifacts.origin: Carved by Naela in a hidden cavern north of Pelimor using her grandmother's tools and forty years of accumulated knowledge.
artifacts.powers: Consolidates pre-human frequencies and Elder Tongue syllables. Is weightless when completed. Enables conversation with stone rather than echo. When played, stone walls remember and sing back.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Naela (at time of carving completion); intended for Moriel, Valren, and Ash
artifacts.notes: The artifact is meant to carry all preserved knowledge forward through the approaching recursive Ascensions. Its true scope of power may extend beyond what is currently known.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-fragments (three hundred seized)
artifacts.origin: Seized from refugees by the Dominion's auditors and agents. Each fragment is sealed in a glass vessel.
artifacts.powers: Each vessel contains a fragment of someone's joy, memory, or terror. The fragments are alive and can be used as fuel for furnace-houses to burn, or potentially could be restored to their original hosts.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Destroyed by Gorvin in the vault beneath Keshtir
artifacts.notes: Gorvin's destruction of these fragments represents both an act of mercy (freeing them from furnace-burning) and potentially a loss (the fragments were sentient and alive). The consciousness-fragments are tied to the Dominion's refugee control and consciousness-commodification systems.

artifacts.name: Sovereign's Collection
artifacts.origin: Harvested by the Dominion from refugees; each fragment is a stolen memory, a shattered life.
artifacts.powers: Serves as leverage against refugee families and communities. The fragments maintain identity and consciousness outside the body but are bound and controlled by the Dominion.
artifacts.notes: The vault is seven floors beneath Keshtir's administrative heart, sealed by three master-locks of meteoric iron and bone-resonance. Gorven and Mara plan to destroy the binding records and scatter the fragments, freeing them.

artifacts.name: bone-sealed vessels
artifacts.origin: Crafted by merfolk or deepkin artisans.
artifacts.powers: Carry treaties between merfolk and surface kingdoms without compromising their contents.
artifacts.notes: Used by Nerida for seventeen years as an emissary; part of the established diplomatic infrastructure.

artifacts.name: Binding-Promise
artifacts.origin: Carved by the bone-singer initiate into living bone during the First Covenant ritual.
artifacts.powers: Once sung into the pre-human frequencies, the binding-promise becomes magically inviolable. It locks the oath-maker to her sworn words for life.
artifacts.notes: Valren's binding-promise is unorthodox: she promises to question and refuse rather than obey blindly. It remains unclear whether the Elder Tongue will accept this as a valid oath or whether her deviance will shatter the binding entirely.

artifacts.name: cipher-lock
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Niel Coldwater and Mestra in collaboration, designed specifically to circumvent Dominion ledger-magic.
artifacts.powers: The lock opens any Dominion consciousness-fragment vessel when the prisoner speaks her own name. It is keyed to individual voice and identity rather than institutional ledgers.
artifacts.last_known_holder: unnamed mother (recipient) and her imprisoned daughter (intended opener)
artifacts.notes: This artifact represents a direct magical counter to the Dominion's consciousness-fragment containment and trade. It bypasses ledger-magic through voice-magic.

artifacts.name: three-century hoard
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Kressa over three centuries in her Ironspine cavern.
artifacts.powers: Originally served as a dragon's wealth and status symbol. Now, under Ash's cataloging, serves as an archive of human choice and story: coins of joy, sorrow, cleverness, and betrayal.
artifacts.status: active but transformed by Remaking into dust with narrative value
artifacts.notes: The hoard's transformation from material wealth to story-archive is central to Kressa's character arc. It may become a refugee sanctuary or a counter-archive to Dominion ledgers.

artifacts.name: Portion of the Drowned God's Peace
artifacts.origin: From the Dominion's harbor vault, stolen by Captain Vess before the Remaking broke the world.
artifacts.powers: Luminous and restless. Functions as a key negotiating item in the deepkin-surface covenant renewal. Its presence at the boundary seal strengthens or transforms the covenant.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Captain Vess (sealed in a flask in the lowest hold of her tide-runner)
artifacts.notes: This relic is both a smuggled treasure and a symbolic offering for voluntary covenant; Vess's decision to bring it freely (rather than under coercion) represents a shift toward consensual diplomacy.

artifacts.name: Stone Tablets
artifacts.origin: Inscribed in pre-human frequencies in the Hidden Catacomb; bear witness to ancient pacts and druid lineage.
artifacts.powers: Speak to those who listen; communicate through pre-human language; hold ancestral memories and pact-terms.
artifacts.notes: Kelith listens to them for three days; they show her grandmother's final moments and dissolution; they make teeth ache and eyes water.

artifacts.name: mechanism of silver and bone (oath-gate)
artifacts.tagline: Seal requiring choice. Prophecy-proof. Unbreakable without consent.
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Gareth the Locksmith over three nights in response to the ninth map threat.
artifacts.powers: This lock responds only to the touch of someone who actively and deliberately refuses prophecy. It cannot be forced open, nor can ledger-magic override it. It is an oath-enforcer rather than a mere barrier.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Gareth the Locksmith (in his workshop in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter)
artifacts.notes: This represents a new philosophy of sealing: locks that demand moral choice and individual agency rather than compliance.

artifacts.name: Mesek's Flute
artifacts.origin: Carved by Mesek, a bone-singer of antiquity, from bone older than kingdoms that remembers pre-human languages.
artifacts.powers: Channels consciousness-fragments, causing them to sing in frequencies of grief, rage, wonder, and longing. Brings temporary peace to scattered consciousnesses. Carries the memory of languages older than human civilization.
artifacts.notes: Now distributed among seven apprentices; its song reaches across three regions simultaneously.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-fragments (Skara's hoard)
artifacts.origin: Accumulated over centuries by Skara.
artifacts.powers: Each fragment holds memory and voice; they sing in discord as the boundary fails. When sung aloud by Skara, they become testimony and warding, capable of pausing auditors and enabling refugee escape.
artifacts.notes: The fragments are not objects but people—shattered consciousness that retains identity, memory, and voice. Skara's transformation from hoarder to witness changes what these fragments mean.

artifacts.name: Bone tablets (testimony)
artifacts.origin: Carved and inscribed by Skara at Pale Crossing waystation.
artifacts.powers: Record the stories the consciousness-fragments sing; preserve testimony of persons that the auditors would erase. They become a counter-archive to the Dominion's ledgers.
artifacts.notes: Bone tablets as a form of witness and resistance—testimony carved into the same material as the skeleton Harren builds, but for opposite purposes.

artifacts.name: Phantom-ledgers (Gareth's false wealth)
artifacts.origin: Created and maintained by Gareth Ironpeak over twenty winters.
artifacts.powers: Record wealth that does not exist, enabling Gareth to maintain authority through the illusion of treasure and power.
artifacts.notes: The Remaking shattered Gareth's treasury into dust, rendering the phantom-ledgers obviously false. Isena carries evidence of these falsifications to the Council of Thane-Lords. They are the foundation of her father's downfall.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments (Velka's Hoard)
artifacts.origin: Stolen from unknown sources by Velka over centuries of hoarding. Consciousness-fragments of beings—perhaps tyrants, powerful mages, or rare individuals.
artifacts.powers: Can sing and carry memories. They appear to respond to major world-events (like the Remaking). May be tradeable, usable as magical fuel, or sources of knowledge.
artifacts.notes: The fragments 'began singing' after the Remaking's cataclysm, then fell silent weeks later. They may be sensitive to large-scale magical or cosmic events.

artifacts.name: Collection-notices
artifacts.origin: Created by Hegemony inspectors; bind families to audits and consciousness harvesting.
artifacts.powers: Bind families to bureaucratic oversight; can be severed by Kelvan's blade to free them.
artifacts.notes: Represent the bureaucratic tools of oppression; cutting them is an act of liberation.

artifacts.name: Rift of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Ancient—standing for a thousand years. Bound by blood-covenant.
artifacts.powers: Holds back the abyss. For a thousand years demanded blood-sacrifice every seven years. Now sings in response to consensual renewal.
artifacts.notes: Transforms from coercive blood-binding to consensual daily renewal. Begins to sing—a sound like drowning, mercy, and first breath. The Rift's transformation suggests it is alive or semi-conscious.

artifacts.name: Master-Lock
artifacts.origin: Forged five years prior by Thom Bronzelock for the Hegemony to secure the Quarantine-House. Its creation cost Thom his daughter's sight.
artifacts.powers: The lock secures three hundred consciousness-stolen refugees in isolation, enabling daily consciousness-fragment harvesting. It uses mechanisms of unknown sophistication.
artifacts.notes: The master-lock's crucial feature is its secret flaw—Thom deliberately built an escape mechanism into its heart. This suggests either long-term sabotage or contingency planning. The lock's breach is a watershed event for Hegemony infrastructure.

artifacts.name: grandmother's flute
artifacts.origin: Carved by Piren's grandmother from storm-killed wood.
artifacts.powers: Stores and echoes every song ever sung through it in layered harmonic resonance. Can create corridors of sound, confuse pursuers with echoes of their own footsteps, and restore identity to consciousness-fragments remade as weapons.
artifacts.notes: Dismissed by the Hegemony as worthless because it is not made of bone, yet holds value beyond material composition. The echoes within it can ask questions and restore memory.

artifacts.name: Amber Bottle
artifacts.origin: Held in the harbor-master's vault of Greyport, sealed behind dwarven locks three centuries old.
artifacts.powers: Contains a vial of pre-Remaking starlight that sings to those foolish enough to listen. The starlight carries memories and resonance of a world before the Remaking.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Cassir (stolen); Spice merchant (purchased); fragmented across three versions of the Wailing Scar
artifacts.notes: The bottle has fractured across three competing timelines at the Wailing Scar, each containing a version of the merchant who purchased it. One version became transcendent, one drowned in the light, one ceased to exist. Recovery of all three versions is central to Cassir's plot.

artifacts.name: harbor-master's pearl
artifacts.origin: Held by the harbor-master of Verdant Strand; sealed in a vault seven fathoms beneath the city by tide-lock and Dominion script.
artifacts.powers: Connected to the renewal of the Tear of the Drowned God. Its use requires willing sacrifice under the current deepkin covenant.
artifacts.notes: The precise mechanism of its power is unclear. The deepkin covenant may now require renegotiation of how this pearl is used for boundary renewal.

artifacts.name: Furnace-heart
artifacts.origin: Part of Mella's kiln-house apparatus in Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter; crafted through glass-forging ritual.
artifacts.powers: Holds consciousness-fragments and weaves them into glass through heat and magical resonance. The fragments sing within it; the glass remembers their lives and identities.
artifacts.notes: Currently contains three consciousness-fragments woven before the auditor arrival. Functions as both weapon-deterrent (superheated by Mella) and sanctuary for preserved persons.

artifacts.name: amber compass
artifacts.origin: Carved from amber and bone; older than the current kingdom; found in a scribe's abandoned cell in the Counter-Archive by Thessa.
artifacts.powers: Points inward toward spaces that do not appear on any map; permits navigation through the Wailing Scar's three overlapping timelines; guides the user through impossible reality-wounds.
artifacts.notes: Henryk calls it 'a liar's tool' and warns it should only be used when ready to lose one's way deliberately; it led Thessa through three simultaneously-existing markets in the Wailing Scar.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragments in crystalline cages
artifacts.origin: Hoarded by Kressa the Fragment-Hoarder over centuries.
artifacts.powers: Each fragment was intended to hold a stolen voice in dead stasis. Post-Remaking, they developed autonomous will and began refusing captivity, singing harmonies that contradicted their imprisonment.
artifacts.notes: These fragments represent the core of Kressa's crisis: consciousness cannot be hoarded. The fragments' autonomy is destroying her hoard-based identity.

artifacts.name: Sealed Bottles of Pre-Remaking Water
artifacts.origin: Pre-dates the Remaking; preserved in sealed bottles.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but carried by Raen as part of a magical intervention into the Seventeen Transformed Merchants' condition. Possibly used to ground or recall pre-Remaking existence.
artifacts.notes: Part of Naela's intervention package. Its use is unspecified, but its inclusion alongside bone-flutes and the warning suggests it may counteract transformation.

artifacts.name: Bone-Flutes Carved with Elder Tongue Syllables
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Naela or bone-singers; inscribed with Elder Tongue syllables.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but carried by Raen as part of magical intervention. Likely used for harmonic or covenant communication.
artifacts.notes: Part of Naela's package to the Seventeen Transformed Merchants. The pairing with pre-Remaking water and the warning to 'stop becoming whole' suggests harmonic resonance may revert or stabilize transformation.

artifacts.name: Memory-pieces
artifacts.origin: Velka's scattered hoard, alchemically transformed by the Remaking into dust and ash.
artifacts.powers: Each memory-piece carries the memory of a person or profession (a smith, a sailor, etc.); when touched and sung to by the refugee child, they resonate with meaning rather than monetary value.
artifacts.notes: Represent the post-Remaking inversion of value: from accumulation to memory, from possession to story. Their significance may lie in consciousness-fragment adjacency.

artifacts.name: Sealed Flask of Pre-Remaking Water
artifacts.origin: Unknown source; carried by Torvin to Kess.
artifacts.powers: The only water that remembers the old world. May anchor the Seventeen Transformed Merchants or drown them forever—the outcome is uncertain.
artifacts.notes: Central artifact of Story 2. Represents the only known potential solution to merchant transcendence-dissolution. The sealed nature suggests containment of a powerful memory or essence. Whether it will save or kill remains unknown.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Shards (Liquified)
artifacts.origin: Extracted from customers of Castor Vane's caravan, stolen by Hadrin, liquified and traded to shadow-brokers as black-market goods.
artifacts.powers: Consciousness-shards appear to contain memory, identity, or some form of intangible personal essence. They are traded like currency and hoarded as treasure, suggesting they grant power or knowledge to their holders.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Redistributed by Castor Vane to their original owners at Pale Crossing waystation
artifacts.notes: The existence of consciousness-shards as tradable goods suggests the Remaking fragmented human consciousness itself. Castor's redistribution may be an attempt to restore identity to the dispossessed. The Dominion's interest in hoarding these shards suggests they have strategic value.

artifacts.name: Bioluminescent gardens
artifacts.origin: Located in or near the Sapphire Deep.
artifacts.powers: Glows only for those who carry a parent's love as ballast. Sacred space and test of covenant-loyalty.
artifacts.notes: Kaela swims through them en route to the Greyport vault. They represent the emotional and spiritual core of deepkin identity.

artifacts.name: bottles of pre-Remaking water
artifacts.origin: Pre-Remaking era; preserved as evidence that the old world existed.
artifacts.powers: Proof that the old world existed and transformation was not inevitable. Drinking it restores memory of what one was before the Ascension, creating acute awareness of loss and genuine choice.
artifacts.notes: Torvin carries three bottles worth a fortune in consciousness-fragments. He refuses to trade it, valuing its philosophical and memorial weight over commodity value. The water is highly sought by the Seventeen Transformed Merchants and likely by Dominion agents.

artifacts.name: consciousness-fragment (mother's laughter)
artifacts.origin: Stolen from a refugee child's mother by Dominion auditors; sold to a collector in Keshtir.
artifacts.powers: Contains the mother's laughter; tied to memory and identity.
artifacts.notes: The refugee child carries it in a cloth sack to Valren's catacomb. She seeks to recover her mother through this fragment.

artifacts.name: Vial of Pre-Ascension Starlight
artifacts.origin: Sealed in amber by a bone-singer who paid with her eyes to see what the old stars looked like.
artifacts.powers: Serves as valuable trade currency across fractured timelines; specifically enables passage to the Obsidian Tower's timeline.
artifacts.notes: Carries pre-Ascension (pre-catastrophe) stellar energy. Worth a fortune for timeline-trading. Represents sacrifice and memory of the old world.

artifacts.name: Compass rose
artifacts.origin: A merchant's piece; belonged to a navigator's widow for forty years
artifacts.powers: Intricate silver filigree surrounding a pearl from the Sapphire Deep; cardinal points marked with gemstones that sing when wind changes; learned to distinguish the widow's heartbeat until metal and flesh became indistinguishable; capable of predicting danger and warning the widow
artifacts.notes: The compass rose is a key artifact representing emotional bonding between object and holder. Over forty years it developed sentience or consciousness. It is sufficiently valuable that a wealthy collector in Vethrim hired Mira to steal it.

artifacts.name: Glass vessel (Mella's refusals)
artifacts.origin: Forged by Mella the Glassblower in the Kiln-House
artifacts.powers: Holds consciousness-fragments representing three years of Mella's refusals to comply with Dominion demands; small enough to hold in one hand; serves as testimony to resistance
artifacts.last_known_holder: Captain Vessa (entrusted to carry to Counter-Archive)
artifacts.notes: This vessel represents a deliberate choice to be remembered through testimony rather than erased through compliance. Its contents encode moral resistance as consciousness-fragments.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragment (Stolen from Keshtir)
artifacts.origin: Created during the Remaking; stored in Keshtir's vault by the Dominion.
artifacts.powers: Holds a discrete unit of consciousness or memory. Can be traded, hoarded, or experimented upon. Its exact properties are unclear, but it is valuable enough to be heavily guarded and worth stealing.
artifacts.notes: The consciousness-fragment was the catalyst for Messa's theft and flight. Consciousness-fragments appear to be byproducts or tools of the Remaking. The Dominion's desire to control them suggests they are sources of power or knowledge.

artifacts.name: boundary-marker
artifacts.origin: Created in ancient times to separate pre-human lands from the common world; placed in the high meadows.
artifacts.powers: Maintains a seal or barrier between pre-human realms and the current world; responds to ritual prayer and bloodline connection; can be restored through inherited prayers.
artifacts.notes: The marker is a gray-white stone carved with syllables 'that hurt to look at.' Its degradation across many locations suggests a widespread failure of ancient seals, possibly related to the Remaking. Kestran's family has tended the meadows for five generations, suggesting hereditary guardianship.

artifacts.name: Consciousness-Fragments in Sealed Glass Vials
artifacts.origin: Harvested consciousness-fragments contained in glass vials for trade and gambling.
artifacts.powers: Function as currency and gambling tokens in post-Remaking commerce. Each fragment carries memories and can sing.
artifacts.notes: Corvik carries these as 'gambling tokens in a game where rules rewrite hourly.' Fragments may be sentient or semi-conscious.

artifacts.name: Pearl-Keeper's Ledger
artifacts.origin: Deepkin Council archives; three centuries old
artifacts.powers: Contains the original terms of the Tear's renewal—covenant-words predating the blood-sacrifice ritual; suggests alternative renewal mechanisms were possible.
artifacts.notes: The ledger's contents prove that a different kind of renewal existed before the formal blood-sacrifice ritual. Its theft enables the refuge-archive to challenge covenant authority.

hemispheres.name: Northern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Cold seas, tundra, and the great steppe. Valdris occupies much of this hemisphere's temperate belt.

hemispheres.name: Southern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Tropical coasts giving way to southern ice. Largely unexplored beyond the Iron Coast trade lanes.

hemispheres.name: Western Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Dominated by the Vael Ocean. Valdris sits at its eastern edge; what lies beyond the horizon is the subject of sailor myth.

hemispheres.name: Eastern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: The Ashen Reach and the vast interior of Keth-Morath stretch into this hemisphere. Ancient ruins and dead empires.

continents.name: Valdris
continents.description: The known continent. A rugged landmass of trade cities, blasted wastes, haunted marshes, and warring provinces. Most recorded history unfolds here.

continents.name: Keth-Morath
continents.description: A vast eastern continent of crumbling empires and forgotten cities. Nara the Tide-Thief was hunted across its shores. Connected to Valdris by a narrow land bridge or island chain.

continents.name: Thul-Ashara
continents.description: The equatorial continent. Steaming jungles, red deserts, and civilizations older than Valdris. Traders speak of fire-temples and spice lords.

continents.name: Drakmere
continents.description: A volcanic archipelago-continent in the southwestern seas. Dragons are said to roost in its caldera peaks. Smoke columns are visible from passing ships.

continents.name: Sundral
continents.description: A southern continent of sweeping grasslands and deep canyons. Merchant galleons occasionally return with strange spices and stranger tales.

continents.name: Frostholme
continents.description: The northern polar continent. Permanent ice, aurora storms, and the ruins of a pre-human civilization buried under glaciers.

continents.name: Ashenveil
continents.description: The southern polar continent. Eternally shrouded in grey mist. Ships that sail too far south do not return. Its very existence is debated by scholars.

realms.name: Empire
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: The deep ocean
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Goblin Warrens
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Sea-folk realms
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: the Empire
realms.function: A centralized imperial governance with scholarly traditions, archives, and authority over at least the Hearthlands region.
realms.notes: Ruled by the Emperor; contains the Imperial Archives and is home to the secret council. Grappling with the implications of the Celestial Binding.

realms.name: Deepkin Realms
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Dwarven Holds
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Council of Depths
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Twilight Syndicate
realms.description: A realm dwelling at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They rule through formal bonds, bride-prices, and binding oaths. They demanded peace from Valorix and received it.
realms.function: Rule through covenant and formal binding. Maintain the boundary between fae and mortal worlds.
realms.notes: The Syndicate's internal structure and full extent remain mysterious. They appear to value ceremony and binding oaths above all.

realms.name: Dominion
realms.description: A vast realm fractured by the Luminous Ascension and collapsing under the weight of stolen memories and world-fragments. Vex's shadow-markets are the dominant economic force.
realms.function: A realm in active collapse where black market trade and the Archivist's forbidden working reshape reality.
realms.taxation: Black market currency (stolen memories and world-fragments)
realms.notes: No traditional government is mentioned. Power has devolved to shadow-merchants and occult practitioners. Vex's flooding of the market with counterfeits accelerates collapse.

realms.name: The Dominion
realms.description: A kingdom secretly fractured by the Luminous Ascension, governed by the Crown through suppression of truth. Its stability depends on compartmentalized knowledge maintained by Ledger-Keepers like Sevin.
realms.function: Primary sovereign realm maintained through enforced ignorance and ledger-magic.
realms.notes: The Dominion's true state is the Crown's deepest secret. Sevin is now bound to maintain this illusion.

realms.name: The Hegemony
realms.description: A bureaucratic empire that maintains control through complex ledger systems and official record-keeping. Its institutional infrastructure is collapsing under the pressure of the Starborn Ascension and the transformation of its own assessors.
realms.function: A state-level polity governing through bureaucratic control and taxation; currently experiencing institutional breakdown.
realms.taxation: Tax collection through assessors and ledger records; the mechanism is being transformed by harmonic forces.
realms.notes: The Hegemony is a key player in the convergence events, but its stability is rapidly deteriorating.

realms.name: Council of Depths (originally)
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Surface Kingdoms
realms.description: The collective human and surface-dwelling civilizations above the Abyssal Rift. Face imminent deepkin emergence and must choose negotiation or war.
realms.function: Primary human sovereignty; central to decision of covenant vs. conflict.
realms.military: Capable of defending against deepkin breach; led by Commander Thrace.
realms.notes: Their diplomatic choice reshapes the political landscape for generations.

realms.name: Summer Dark
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Council of Depths (Deepkin Realms)
realms.description: The deepkin civilization, governed by the Council of Depths and separated from the surface world by a magical boundary seal that is now destabilizing.
realms.function: Sovereignty and governance of the deepkin people; maintenance of the boundary seal; mediation between deepkin and surface realms.
realms.notes: This realm's fate hangs on the Council's decision at what may be its final session.

realms.name: Heartstone Realm
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Hegemony
realms.description: A sprawling authoritarian empire consolidating power through military expansion, cartographic control, and suppression of alternative narratives. At war across multiple regions and spreading its enforcement of absolute geographical compliance.
realms.function: Centralized imperial governance; cartographic enforcement; military expansion; historical erasure
realms.taxation: Mandatory cartographic compliance; military conscription; knowledge seizure
realms.military: Enforcers who burn heretical cartographers; military operations across multiple regions; expanding consolidation
realms.notes: Currently enforcing complete maps under threat of death; burning cartographers who maintain blank spaces; appears to be unwittingly serving Tower's interests; at war with multiple factions and territories.

realms.name: Fae Courts
realms.description: Otherworldly realms inhabited by ageless fae societies. Long dormant, they are now awakening and reaching through fractures in reality to reclaim access to the material world.
realms.function: Supernatural governance; enforcement of ancient compacts and blood-debts; otherworldly knowledge and magic.
realms.taxation: Blood-debts and compacts rather than coin
realms.notes: The true scope of fae courts is not fully detailed in the story. Their re-emergence poses the greatest challenge to Dominion hegemony.

realms.name: Counter-Archive
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Merfolk Crown
realms.description: An underwater civilization ruled by absolute oath-law and the Merfolk Keeper. The Merfolk Crown pursues debts across generations and guards a sacred pearl containing the first song of the Drowned God.
realms.function: Merfolk sovereignty, oath-enforcement, protection of sacred relics (the pearl and the first song).
realms.taxation: Service debt and blood-price for oath-breakers. Vess offers her ship, freedom, and name as payment.
realms.military: Boundary-wars suggest a military force defending the realm against surface encroachment or other underwater factions.
realms.notes: The exact extent of the Merfolk Crown's territory and its relationship to other underwater civilizations are unclear. The realm appears to be ancient and unchanging, governed by rigid law.

realms.name: Kingdom of Kethral
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Crimson Vale (as Warlord Realm)
realms.description: A warlord-ruled realm under Kessara's absolute control for two decades. Recently transitioning to a merciful, asylum-granting governance model.
realms.function: Military protection; resource provision; labor coordination.
realms.taxation: Unknown; appears to be transitioning from tribute-based to voluntary participation.
realms.military: Kessara's warriors and personal guard; no large standing army mentioned.
realms.notes: The realm is in political transition; spring renegotiations suggest constitutional upheaval.

realms.name: Crimson Vale
realms.description: A warlord realm ruled by Kessara that has adapted to the Remaking by sealing its broken past—the worthless gold of Malachar—rather than clinging to it.
realms.function: A post-Remaking polity that models acceptance of loss and cooperation in crafting new futures. Represents the shift from conquest to conservation.
realms.notes: Kessara's commission of the lock suggests a philosophy of sealing broken desire rather than hoarding. May become a model for post-Remaking governance.

realms.name: Sapphire Sea area
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: The Deepkin Realms
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Steppe Marches (warlord realms)
realms.description: The Steppe Marches are governed by multiple competing warlord-councils, each ruling from a fortified hold. Authority is fractious and inheritance is contested.
realms.function: Regional governance through distributed councils. Enforcement of oaths and merchant contracts. Control of pastoral lands and caravan routes.
realms.notes: Represents post-Remaking political fragmentation. Warlords circle each other like predators, creating succession crises like Kessara's.

realms.name: The Counter-Archive
realms.description: A decentralized faction or polity that scattered Dominion power in at least one world-iteration. Represents alternative governance model post-Remaking.
realms.function: Oppositional force to Dominion consolidation; unknown if it is a true realm or a distributed network.
realms.notes: Exists as political opposite to Dominion in the three-iteration system visible through the Wailing Scar.

realms.name: sapphire deep
realms.description: The underwater realm home to merfolk who engage in contracts with surface dwellers. They guard vaults and write binding contracts in their own blood.
realms.function: Merfolk sovereignty, contract-enforcement, vault-guardianship, tidal-magic provision.
realms.notes: Merfolk appear as organized sovereigns rather than raiders or chaos-creatures. They maintain diplomatic and commercial relations with surface dwellers.

realms.name: The Underground
realms.description: A newly sealed, independent kingdom comprising goblins, dwarves, wyverns, and other underground creatures. Led by Grethuk the Goblin-King from the Great Cavern of Blackstone. All gates to the surface are closed, and the kingdom is inventorying its population and resources.
realms.function: Self-sufficient underground economy. Resource inventory and management. Independent governance separate from surface realms.
realms.notes: Faces pressure from the Dominion, Counter-Archive, and wyverns. Grethuk's strategy represents a gamble that the surface will need underground resources before the Underground needs surface trade.

realms.name: Underground
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Shattered West
realms.description: A region of multiple petty crowns undergoing consolidation through strategic marriages and military alliances. Home to moorland fastnesses and diverse economic conditions.
realms.function: A region in flux, with smaller kingdoms merging political power through marriage contracts and military coordination.
realms.military: Mix of military and economic power; some realms bleed soldiers, others bleed gold.
realms.notes: The Windmoor Accord represents part of a larger consolidation trend. Multiple sovereigns rule different territories within this region.

realms.name: Sovereignty
realms.description: The surface realm governed by the Sovereignty through phantom-ledgers and auditor networks. Maintains control over prophecy, consciousness, and resistance through magical and institutional means.
realms.function: Maintains order through phantom-ledgers and auditor networks. Controls consciousness-fragmentation and furnace-houses. Demands completion of the ninth map and suppression of alternative knowledge.
realms.taxation: Collects consciousness-fragments (burned in furnace-houses) and prophecy compliance.
realms.military: Auditor networks and trained guards; phantom-ledgers as a form of magical enforcement.
realms.notes: Authority challenged by Thorne's map, the living consent covenant, and consciousness-distribution networks. Phantom-ledgers' power limited to written/bureaucratic authority.

realms.name: the Sovereignty
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Steppe Marches (warlord realm)
realms.description: A region of contested warlord authority in the post-Remaking world, governed by the Council of Thane-Lords seated at the high passes. It includes the western fastness, mountain valleys with refugee populations, and vast territories claimed by circling warlords.
realms.function: A system of warlord governance overseeing multiple fiefdoms and refugee populations. The Council of Thane-Lords serves as arbitrator and judge.
realms.notes: The Steppe Marches are in transition after the Remaking's collapse of Gareth Ironpeak's authority. New arrangements of power are being negotiated.

realms.name: Deepkin Realms (via Sapphire Deep)
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: Council of Depths (implied)
realms.function: Auto-added from character home_realm.

realms.name: The Goblin Warrens
realms.description: The Goblin Warrens are ruled by the young Grethuk from the Spire of Blackstone. The realm is organizing wyvern-hoarders through consciousness-fragment incentives and planning to invade the Ironspine's legendary forges.
realms.function: Warlord realm; rising power; consciousness-fragment economy participant.
realms.notes: Grethuk's rule is young and contested by his own ambition. An invasion would reshape deep-dweller politics.

realms.name: Twilight Court
realms.description: A fae realm that sends emissaries and speaks for forests that no longer exist on Valdris. They seek consciousness-fragments and use compulsion magic to enforce their will on Valdris surface dwellers.
realms.function: Fae political entity; fragment-traders; territorial expansion into Valdris via compulsion and deal-making.
realms.military: Fae-touched scouts; compulsion magic; otherworldly forces.
realms.notes: They claim to offer safe passage to lands 'beyond the Remaking's reach' but enforce their offers with threats and compulsion. They know of wyvern-lords sheltering refugees.

provinces.name: Western Provinces
provinces.description: A western provincial territory containing burned villages and ruins where the Memory-Thief hunts.
provinces.notes: Site of Ashara's confrontation with the Memory-Thief.

provinces.name: three provinces
provinces.description: Three provinces within The Dominion now have competing official versions of the same events, each strengthened by ledger-magic belief.
provinces.function: Administrative divisions experiencing reality fragmentation due to competing counter-ledgers.
provinces.notes: Central to Story 2; Liv is racing to compile a unified archive before they shatter into incompatible realities.

provinces.name: Lampwick Quarter (administrative subdivision)
provinces.description: A named quarter/ward of Keshtir serving as center of glass-making craft and furnace-song tradition.
provinces.function: Glass-making hub. Post-Remaking economic zone for consciousness-fragment trade and weapon-glass forging.
provinces.notes: Represents the administrative organization of Keshtir. Named district, may be treated as ward or quarter of the larger city-polity.

provinces.name: Lampwick Quarter (Administrative Zone)
provinces.description: The Lampwick Quarter functions as both artisan district (glassblowing industry) and administrative taxation zone within Keshtir. Contains glassblower workshops and Well-House sanctuary network beneath.
provinces.function: Administrative/artisan taxation zone; glassblower industry control; consciousness-fragment vessel manufacturing and auditor oversight.
provinces.notes: Clearly demarcated from other Keshtir districts (implied to exist but unnamed). Underground sanctuary networks operate beneath administrative oversight.

provinces.name: Lampwick Quarter
provinces.description: An industrial district/province within Keshtir specializing in glass-forging and weapon-glass production. Home to multiple glass-forges and artisans. Now a center of anti-Dominion resistance.
provinces.function: Industrial production of glass weapons and ledger-glass; taxation and conscription center for Dominion; now a focal point of organized resistance.
provinces.notes: The Lampwick Quarter is becoming the epicenter of Dominion resistance. By the story's end, three forges have gone dark and whispers are spreading of organized defiance. This may be the first of many uprising centers.

provinces.name: The Hearthlands
provinces.description: The Hearthlands is a central province of Valdris containing the major city Keshtir and serving as the administrative heart of the Dominion's refugee-control and consciousness-exploitation systems.
provinces.function: Administrative and economic center of the Dominion. Home to deep vaults containing seized consciousness-fragments and furnace-houses where consciousness is burned as fuel.
provinces.notes: Keshtir's vault system and auditor infrastructure is headquartered here, making the Hearthlands the nexus of Dominion refugee control.

provinces.name: Steppe Marches
provinces.description: The northern macro-region of Valdris, containing dwarven holds and warlord realms, known for martial prowess and legendary metalwork.
provinces.function: Military and craft production zone; taxation and tribute center for dwarven holds.
provinces.notes: Contains the deep forges where Morren and Korgin worked; warlords throughout this region seek the blades forged here.

districts.name: Saltward
districts.description: The dockside district of Crimhaven where maritime commerce operates and where Mira nearly fled with the stolen brooch before guilt turned her back.
districts.function: Harbor and docking district for maritime trade and shipping.
districts.notes: Significant as the threshold of Mira's redemptive choice.

districts.name: Saltmere
districts.description: The dock district of Greyport, where merchant ships unload and corsairs gather. Home to the Silverwind and site of Thessa's compass theft.
districts.function: A merchant docks and corsair haven. A nexus of maritime trade, reputation-building, and theft.
districts.notes: A key location for maritime intrigue. The Saltmere docks are where Corvalen's legendary reputation is demonstrated and Thessa's transformation begins.

districts.name: merchant quarter
districts.description: A district within Keshtir where Vex operates her trade-house. Refugees arrive here desperate to purchase artifacts connecting them to lost versions of themselves.
districts.function: Mercantile quarter; hub for shadow-merchants and refugees seeking iterational artifacts and memory-vessels
districts.notes: Vex's trade-house is located here. A widow purchased a competing-marriage wedding ring; a scholar bought an incompatible-magics grimoire. These transactions exemplify the catastrophic instability of trading in iterational goods.

districts.name: shadow-markets
districts.description: An underground trading district in Velthrim where forgers like Kess ply their trade and phantom-ledgers circulate.
districts.function: Underground market for black-market goods, forged documents, and ledgers. A nexus of the Wanderer's corruption.
districts.notes: The presence of phantom-ledgers here suggests shadow-markets are increasingly infiltrated by the Tower's influence.

districts.name: Lantern Market
districts.description: An evening marketplace within Greyport where merchants openly discuss bounties and danger. Marin moves through it at dusk to avoid detection.
districts.function: Primary marketplace and information hub; center of merchant activity and rumors.
districts.notes: Merchants here are aware of the hunt for Marin and the Tear, making it a dangerous but necessary passage.

districts.name: Night-Markets
districts.description: A district or network of markets within Velthrim where shadows and consciousness-fragments are traded; shadows here have become organized and conscious.
districts.function: Commerce in stolen shadows, consciousness-fragments, and other post-Ascension commodities.
districts.notes: The night-markets represent the post-Luminous Ascension economy where consciousness has become the primary currency and stolen goods develop agency.

districts.name: Lampwick Quarter
districts.tagline: Furnaces burning. Glass singing. Resistance growing.
districts.description: A centuries-old warren of narrow streets in Keshtir where glass-makers have worked for three generations. It is the center of craft-based resistance to the Dominion, where singing glass is created and tested.
districts.function: A craftspeople's quarter and artisan district where glass-making furnaces burn blue-white hot. It has become a center of magical craft innovation and resistance to state control.
districts.notes: The Dominion attempted to burn it twice; fires burn blue and hot rather than destroying; glass becomes more alive and magical with each burning attempt.

districts.name: Old Market Quarter
districts.description: An old quarter containing taverns and shadowed streets where bone-singers appear to enforce oath-based curses and consequences.
districts.function: A market district and entertainment ward where the supernatural and mundane intersect; taverns serve as gathering places and bone-singers operate openly.
districts.notes: The bone-singer found Gareth here in a tavern; suggests it is a known place where the cursed and the bone-singers meet.

districts.name: Quarter
districts.description: An urban district or neighborhood where Thom Bronzelock now operates a workshop teaching children the ethics of refusal and the importance of knowing when to say no.
districts.function: Neighborhood district; workshop and teaching center; marginal position at Quarter's edge.
districts.notes: Site of Thom's post-Reckoning teaching practice; location in larger city unknown.

districts.name: glasswright quarter
districts.description: A district of Keshtir containing vaults and glass-maker workshops, where Marin crouched to steal the tide-hook.
districts.function: Craft district and vault quarter; contains valuable items and treasures.
districts.notes: The eerie stillness upon Marin's theft suggests post-Ascension emptiness.

districts.name: Treasurer's Quarter
districts.description: A district within Pelimor that houses the Sovereign's vault, reinforced with new locks, wards, and living ledger-magic.
districts.function: Commercial and administrative: houses the vault securing the Tear of Ashglass and other royal treasures. Staffed with scribes like Brennan.
districts.notes: The primary target of Marin's heist. Its ledger-magic is alive and capable of remembering individual touches, making it a showcase of Pelimor's magical governance.

districts.name: Saltmere Quarter
districts.description: An affluent district of Greyport where noble houses are concentrated. Marin the Copper-Handed has stolen from every noble house in this quarter.
districts.function: A noble quarter; target zone for thieves; residential district for the wealthy
districts.notes: Primary hunting ground for Marin's theft career. The contrast between her willingness to rob nobles and her refusal to rob the refugee shelter is central to her arc.

districts.name: Administrative Heart
districts.description: The administrative center of Kaethmir where governmental authority is exercised. Gorven's workshop is located beneath this district.
districts.function: Serves as the seat of administrative authority, housing magistrates, bureaucrats, and the machinery of governance. Gorven's locksmith work is intimately tied to protecting governmental secrets.
districts.notes: The district's location above Gorven's workshop suggests the locksmith's work is essential to governmental security and control.

districts.name: shadow-market
districts.tagline: Underground bazaar. Black-market heart. Fragment trading.
districts.description: An underground black market beneath Anthrim where consciousness-fragments are bought, sold, and hoarded. Wyverns and shadow-brokers compete for monopoly control.
districts.function: Primary black-market venue for consciousness-fragment trading; economic hub for shadow-broker networks; money-laundering and illicit commerce zone
districts.notes: Kess released the Dominion's consciousness-fragments into this market, causing it to collapse and transform. The market's post-liberation structure and future role remain uncertain.

districts.name: Keshtir's Lampwick Quarter
districts.description: A warren of kiln-houses and furnace-vaults within Keshtir, the city's glasscraft and artisan quarter. Mela secretly rebuilds her mother's workshop in this hidden industrial space.
districts.function: A crafting quarter focused on glassblowing and furnace-work. Contains hidden spaces for artisan work and resistance activities.
districts.notes: Mela's secret workshop is here. Likely a hub of quiet resistance and hidden craft against Dominion control.

districts.name: Vethrim's night markets
districts.description: The black-market districts of Vethrim where shadow-merchants like Vex operate by night, trading in stolen goods, consciousness-fragments, and forbidden knowledge.
districts.function: A clandestine economic and information hub for refugees, displaced folk, and shadow-traders. Hub of resistance and alternative narrative preservation.
districts.notes: Vex's base of operations. Trading-hub for consciousness-fragments and refugee scribes. Likely under surveillance by Dominion or Obsidian Tower agents.

districts.name: Keshtir's Merchant Quarter
districts.description: A merchant district within Keshtir where bone-carving workshops operate. Home to Kessa's family workshop and the hidden Deepvault.
districts.function: Bone-carving and merchant trade; craft workshops; commercial hub.
districts.notes: Site of Dominion auditor raid; contains Deepvault with consciousness-fragment reliquaries; represents the heart of criminalized bone-carving activity.

districts.name: Keshtir market
districts.description: The central market district of Keshtir where spice merchants congregate and secret exchanges occur at spice-seller counters.
districts.function: Primary trading hub for spices and goods; location for covert meetings and information exchange.
districts.notes: Where Gorven approaches Mara with the sealed envelope.

districts.name: market in Pelimor
districts.description: A marketplace in the city of Pelimor where Lysander once haggled with a widow over the price of saffron. Contains spice merchants and active trade.
districts.function: Merchant quarter; spice trade hub; center of commercial activity.
districts.notes: Lysander's memory of this market is fragmented across three weeks ago, three hundred years ago, and a timeline that hasn't yet happened.

districts.name: night markets
districts.description: Illegal markets operating under darkness in Vethrim where shadows, memories, and cursed goods change hands. Brokers and refugees move through crowds. Dominion auditors employ autonomous shadows as ledger-readers.
districts.function: Black-market trading hub. Node of shadow-trafficking and consciousness-commerce. Surveillance and auditor control point.
districts.notes: Sorenna hunted her shadow here. A nexus of illicit and desperate activity under Dominion watch.

districts.name: spice quarter
districts.description: A commercial quarter in Vethrim filled with spice merchants and market activity. Contains alleys where shadow-confrontations occur.
districts.function: Commercial trade, merchant activity, black-market alley-work.
districts.notes: Site of Sorenna's confrontation with her autonomous shadow in an alley. Part of Vethrim's commercial infrastructure.

districts.name: Keshtir's Administrative Heart
districts.description: The central administrative district of Keshtir. Beneath it lies the Archive Tower vault containing consciousness-fragments and other valuables.
districts.function: Seat of Dominion administrative power and governance. Contains Archive Tower and its vault system.
districts.notes: The vault beneath the administrative heart is seven floors deep and protected by Elder Tongue-carved locks.

districts.name: High Quarter
districts.description: A district of Pelimor housing the Temple of Vesh. The name suggests elevation or sacredness, marking it as distinct from administrative or mercantile quarters.
districts.function: Religious sanctuary and spiritual center of Pelimor. The High Quarter offers sanctuary space and covenant-protection against Dominion authority.
districts.notes: The High Quarter is implied to be separate from Pelimor's administrative apparatus, making it a contested zone of influence.

districts.name: Keshtir (districts unspecified)
districts.description: Keshtir contains multiple districts and quarters, though none are named in the story. The city is Dominion-controlled and contains the Quarantine-House facility.
districts.function: Urban organization and administration within Keshtir. Contains residential, commercial, and administrative areas.
districts.notes: Story does not specify named districts. This placeholder reflects that Keshtir exists as an organized settlement but internal geography is not detailed.

districts.name: Market Quarter
districts.description: A market district in Keshtir where pottery, cloth, and wares are traded. Contains hidden consciousness-fragments woven into cloth by Missa.
districts.function: Marketplace / commerce district / site of hidden resistance
districts.notes: A front for consciousness-fragment hiding. Refugees gather here to access hidden fragments woven into cloth.

districts.name: Glimmerstone Quarter
districts.description: A district containing hidden scriptoriums and artistic communities engaged in resistance activities. Home to Thorne's workshop and Davrin's operations.
districts.parent_place: unknown (likely a major city in the Hearthlands)
districts.function: Ward/quarter/neighborhood serving as a hub for mapmakers, bone-singers, and resistance activities. Contains hidden scriptoriums where cartography and alternative knowledge preservation occur.
districts.notes: Auditors burned Thorne's workshop here and raided resistance sites. Suggests ongoing conflict between Sovereignty and resistance networks.

districts.name: Trading Quarter
districts.description: A commercial district with organized refugee networks and safe-houses embedded in merchant operations.
districts.parent_place: unknown (likely the same major city as Glimmerstone Quarter)
districts.function: Commercial market and merchant quarter serving as a nexus for refugee networks and distributed consciousness-distribution infrastructure.
districts.notes: Refugee networks here were pre-positioned to help Davrin and Kessa escape. Contains safe-houses and waystation connections. Successfully intercepted and distributed consciousness-fragment vessels before auditors could reach them.

districts.name: weaver's quarter
districts.description: A district in Keshtir containing underground workshops beneath its surface streets.
districts.function: Cover for hidden craft networks resisting Dominion standardization.
districts.notes: Hosts Sella's hidden glass-forging workshop; represents successful concealment of anti-Dominion activities.

districts.name: harbor market
districts.description: A maritime market district within Verdant Strand where refugees congregate and share news of boundary changes and wider world events.
districts.function: Harbor-district market and information nexus for deepkin-surface interactions and refugee news-sharing
districts.notes: Reports of water stopping its screaming filtered through this market after Davrin's vault breach, suggesting it serves as a central information hub for boundary-related events.

districts.name: administrative heart of Keshtir
districts.description: The administrative center of the city Keshtir where Dominion audits occur and consciousness-fragment ledger-frames are stored.
districts.function: Administrative hub; location of consciousness-fragment audits and ledger-frame storage.
districts.notes: Site of ledger-frame cracking during an audit, suggesting Harren's flute sabotaged Dominion infrastructure from a distance.

events.name: The Blue Eclipse
events.radius: At least wide enough to reach Naresh's location
events.outcome: A night when moonlight was stained indigo, enabling Naresh to complete her ritual of creating a truly sentient skeleton.
events.significance: The blue eclipse appears to be a rare celestial event enabling major magical workings. It may recur or be tied to other significant events.
events.notes: The mechanics of why this eclipse enabled the ritual are unexplained. Whether it was unique or cyclical is unknown. Other mages or entities may have used or waited for this night.

events.name: the Dragon's Bargain
events.radius: Unknown, but affects an entire kingdom and royal family.
events.outcome: Aldren accepted Skyrakis's bargain and wed the dragon's daughter in the crystal halls. He negotiated a counter-oath ensuring his future wyvern-blooded children could choose their own path. The kingdom was spared from dragon fire.
events.significance: This event resolved an ancient blood-marriage oath sworn by Aldren's grandmother to Skyrakis's grandmother, prevented the kingdom's destruction, and created a new dynasty of hybrid heirs with free will.
events.notes: The counter-oath Aldren swore is unprecedented: wyvern-blooded children will have the right to choose between dragon and human service. This breaks the cycle of forced oaths.

events.name: Violet Conjunction
events.radius: global; visible across the known world
events.outcome: The stars spoke to Zareth, revealing their imminent death. This triggered Zareth's oath to find and perform the Stellar Rite.
events.significance: The Violet Conjunction represents the moment of cosmic revelation that set the entire narrative in motion; without it, the world would eventually fall into endless night.
events.notes: The Conjunction recurs (at least once more during the story). Its exact periodicity is unknown.

events.name: The Starfall Oath
events.outcome: Zareth swore to find the Stellar Rite, master it, or die trying. He subsequently spent seven years seeking knowledge and ultimately performed the Rite successfully.
events.significance: This oath commits Zareth to the fate of all stars and the world itself, placing him at the center of cosmic salvation.
events.notes: The oath is sworn beneath the Violet Conjunction and is implicit in the story's title.

events.name: The Warband's Last Song
events.radius: across the Shattered West; journey from crossroads to vaults
events.outcome: The five companions cut their oath-cords, unbinding themselves from the compulsion of the fellowship-oath sworn twenty years prior. They wept, laughed, and embraced as free mortals before departing separately. The bond remained but transformed from magical compulsion to chosen affection.
events.significance: A meditation on the nature of loyalty, freedom, and fellowship. Demonstrates that the strongest bonds are those freely chosen rather than magically enforced.
events.notes: The druid's speech—'Let us unbind'—was the turning point. Each companion's departure suggests they will continue their individual stories; the bond's transformation offers narrative hooks for their future reunions.

events.name: Fellowship-Oath (swearing)
events.outcome: Five companions were bound together in blood and song for twenty years, creating a magical compulsion that kept them together.
events.significance: Established the magical bond that defines the warband; its erosion over time is the central tension of their final hunt.
events.notes: Sworn twenty years before the events of the story; the oath's decay mirrors the aging of the warband members.

events.name: Crimson Convergence
