weapons.notes: Central to Story 1. Rust-bright and hungry upon discovery. The blade may have a will or curse independent of its wielder. Inheriting it means inheriting both its oath-binding power and the debt it enforces. Neighboring warlords circle Kessara, coveting either the blade or its political advantage.

weapons.name: Hands of Meteoric Copper
weapons.origin: Forged by a curse-merchant and given to Harrow in exchange for stealing the Crown of Karesh.
weapons.powers: Tools of impossible precision, capable of handling supernatural bindings and complex locks. Designed for lockpicking and safecracking.
weapons.notes: The hands are slowly oxidizing, turning to rust. Cursed to pain Harrow hourly with phantom-pain until the debt (stealing the Crown of Karesh) is fulfilled. The curse may transfer to Gareth if he inherits the task. A transaction-based curse: fulfilling the debt may release the bearer, but the outcome is uncertain.

weapons.name: Truthseeker's Echo
weapons.origin: Forged by the same smith who made the legendary blade Truthseeker. Created with Elder Tongue syllables carved into meteoric iron shaft. Left in cairn by Yareth's grandmother, who died defending the pre-human boundary fifty winters past.
weapons.powers: Does not kill the innocent. Recognizes truth and exposed oaths. The spear's point finds seams in broken covenants—where targets have already doubted, betrayed, or wanted out. Strikes not at bodies but at fractures in will and oath.
weapons.notes: The auditor who laughed at Yareth fell weeping when the spear found the seam in his cracked oath to the Dominion. The spear sings a different song than the legendary blade Truthseeker: not 'never miss' but 'cut only what lies.' Unresolved: connection to other works by the same smith; whether other legendary weapons exist.

weapons.name: Gorvan's Lockpicks
weapons.origin: Forged from meteoric copper with bone handles, crafted for Gorvan's fifty-year career as a lockpick and thief.
weapons.powers: They 'sing' as he works, creating harmonic resonance with consciousness-fragments and Elder Tongue inscriptions. They appear to resonate with magical locks.
weapons.notes: These are specialized tools that function as both thief's implements and magical instruments. The singing suggests they may be infused with minor enchantment or be naturally attuned to consciousness-fragments.

weapons.name: Captain's Jewel
weapons.origin: Unknown; hangs from a chain around Vorath's neck.
weapons.powers: The Jewel sings and is luminous. It hums with bioluminescent ink and the last prayer of a dying god. It offers Vorath a choice: drown with honor, or live cursed.
weapons.notes: The Jewel is both an object of theft and a source of prophecy or agency. It offers binary choices, suggesting it may contain or convey divine will or wisdom. Its 'last prayer of a dying god' suggests it is a relic of a defunct deity.

weapons.name: grandmother's lockpicks
weapons.origin: Crafted by Gorven's grandmother, who was also a thief who stole from tyrants. Made of meteoric copper.
weapons.powers: Bone-resonant. Can recognize and open seals sealed with bone-resonance magic. Recognize Gorven's bloodline lineage.
weapons.notes: Older than empires. Whispered Gorven's grandmother's name when used on the Dominion vault seal. Suggest hereditary magical bond.

weapons.name: consciousness-fragments (widow's seventeen)
weapons.origin: Unknown; purchased from widow by Kelvan at caravan waystation. Represent voices and consciousness of drowned cartographer, bone-singers, soldiers, and others.
weapons.powers: Fragments sing memories and emotions; communicate across distances; carry knowledge of drowned history and pre-Remaking starlight; may grant insight to holder or inflict psychological pressure.
weapons.last_known_holder: Kelvan (abandoned sale at Pale Crossing waystation); current location unknown.
weapons.notes: Fragments' autonomous consciousness and singing suggest they are quasi-sentient weapons or tools; may be used for divination, cursing, communication, or consciousness-based magic.

weapons.name: Truth-Killer (Rejected Commission)
weapons.tagline: Silencing blade. Never forged. Defiant refusal.
weapons.origin: Commissioned by a Dominion auditor; never completed by Torven Ironfoot.
weapons.powers: According to commission: capable of severing any spoken truth that contradicts phantom-ledgers; a weapon against testimony itself.
weapons.notes: This weapon represents the Dominion's attempt to enforce narrative monopoly through blade-craft. Torven's refusal to forge it is a pivotal act of conscience.

weapons.name: Tear of the Drowned God
weapons.origin: Unknown origin; bound into the boundary-waters by an ancient covenant with the drowned god.
weapons.powers: Sustains the boundary-waters seal and the deepkin-surface covenant. When it fails, merfolk breach.
weapons.last_known_holder: The boundary-waters themselves; maintained by Merren Blackwater and the deepkin captains.
weapons.notes: Not primarily a weapon but the lynchpin of a defensive covenant. Its failure is the core crisis of the reef-tender narrative.

weapons.name: merchant's glass vessel
weapons.origin: A merchant vessel modified by Ash with counter-frequency inscriptions.
weapons.powers: The counter-frequency inscription allows it to hold deepkin pearls and facilitate passage through harmonic frequencies without triggering alarms or causing the pearls to sing defensive songs.
weapons.notes: Technically a container rather than a weapon, but used as a tool for theft and transgression. Represents hybrid human-deepkin craftsmanship.

weapons.name: knife
weapons.origin: Kelvin carries it as part of 'old habit, old economy'—suggesting it is a remnant of pre-Remaking times.
weapons.powers: A simple blade; no magical properties mentioned.
weapons.notes: Kelvin reaches for it instinctively when confronted, but does not use it. Represents the old, violent economy he is tempted to revert to.

weapons.name: Throne of Bound Bone
weapons.origin: Crafted by goblin artisans, likely using bone-singing or carving traditions. Bound bone suggests ritual construction.
weapons.powers: Serves as the seat of goblin-king authority and power, from which Grethuk commands the Warrens.
weapons.notes: While ceremonial, the throne is constructed of bound bone and may have been crafted through ritual means. It is the locus of goblin royal power.

weapons.name: Blessed knife
weapons.origin: Temple of Vesh, blessed by unknown priestly authority.
weapons.powers: Capable of carving covenant-marks into both flesh and stone. Carries blessing sufficient to enforce sacred sanctuary.
weapons.notes: The knife is used for both self-sacrifice and covenant-carving. Its blessing grants it authority beyond ordinary blades. It remains in use in the Temple of Vesh.

weapons.name: Master Bone-Flute
weapons.origin: Carved from the spine of a creature that predated kingdoms.
weapons.powers: When played, it commands consciousness-fragments and frequency-songs; capable of triggering convergence of consciousness-light across vast distances; produces harmonic resonance that can affect reality itself.
weapons.notes: Wielded by Naela during the Primal Shard consolidation. When she raised it to her lips, the entire Singing Cathedrals began to sing. The flute appears to be a conduit for bone-song magic and the Elder Tongue. Its origins in a pre-kingdom creature suggest immense age and power.

weapons.name: Weapons-Glass
weapons.origin: Dominion production, forged in Keshtir furnace-houses using consciousness-fragments as fuel.
weapons.powers: Presumably enhanced by melted consciousness-fragments; the specific properties are not detailed, but the fragments are described as being 'melted into blades' instead of preserved.
weapons.notes: The weaponization of consciousness-fragments represents a moral and practical shift in Dominion resource allocation. Corvin's refusal to produce them is his act of defiance.

weapons.name: iron lockpicks
weapons.origin: Salvaged from battles long buried; part of Gorven's tool collection.
weapons.powers: unknown
weapons.notes: These picks complement the copper ones. Their battle origin suggests they may have seen use as both weapons and tools in the Dominion's long history.

weapons.name: blade
weapons.origin: Unknown; wielded by one who refused the wyvern's bargain.
weapons.powers: Turned aside from the vulnerable (children) and cut instead at the sorcerer, oath, or curse that set the snare. It struck in ash or iron.
weapons.notes: The blade's ability to sever oaths or curses suggests enchantment or a wielder of deep magical knowledge. The story is told in retrospect; the defender's name is not given.

weapons.name: Bone-Picks
weapons.origin: Carved and used by Gorven the Conjurer for forty years of legendary lock-opening. Now inherited by Thessa.
weapons.powers: Feel where lock pins want to fall. Respond to whispered words. Warm as if remembering their master's touch. Enable opening of complex, Elder Tongue-carved locks.
weapons.notes: The picks carry forty years of theft and mastery. They warm in Thessa's hands, suggesting a sympathetic connection to Gorven's consciousness or lingering presence. Used to infiltrate the Dominion's vault.

weapons.name: Starlight-Glass Compass
weapons.origin: Crafted by Sorenna's dead husband; points to the pre-human boundary where he died defending.
weapons.powers: Points to the pre-human boundary; made of starlight-glass, suggesting magical crafting. Imbued with connection to the boundary and Sorenna's husband's sacrifice.
weapons.notes: Primary object in the story's title. Represents redemption and the burden of boundary defense. Yareth receives it as his task.

weapons.name: Truth-lock
weapons.origin: A mechanism the Dominion demanded Thom Bronzelock forge as ransom for his daughter's restoration.
weapons.powers: Designed to identify which refugee testimonies are genuine and which are Counter-Archive lies; would serve as the Dominion's ultimate control mechanism over truth itself.
weapons.notes: Thom's refusal to forge this weapon is the crux of his moral stand against the Dominion. By refusing, he chose principle over his daughter's restoration—a devastating choice that becomes possible only when the Dominion fractures.

weapons.name: Locks of Meteoric Iron
weapons.origin: Forged by the Dominion as the primary seals on their vault beneath Keshtir's administrative heart.
weapons.powers: Bone-resonance component makes them theoretically impossible to pick; seals the Dominion's most critical secrets.
weapons.notes: Thom's forty-year study of the Dominion's mechanisms culminates in his attempt to pick these locks. Their combination of meteoric iron and bone-resonance represents the frontier of Dominion craftsmanship.

weapons.name: master-lock
weapons.origin: Commissioned by the Dominion auditors; designed by Gareth the Locksmith.
weapons.powers: Capable of securing consciousness-fragments so completely that they cannot be stolen, traded, or freed—in theory. In practice, contains seventeen hidden release mechanisms keyed to specific emotions: rage (eastern latch), sorrow (northern mechanism), joy (southern gate), and love (complete dismantling).
weapons.notes: The master-lock is both weapon and rebellion. Its seventeen emotional keys transform it from a tool of oppression into a potential key to liberation. Gareth engineered this sabotage knowing it would cost him his life.

weapons.name: the Thrice-Cursed Helm
weapons.origin: Forged in a pre-human age; etched with pre-human syllables. Worn across nine iterations by corrupted warriors.
weapons.powers: Whispers the names of the dead. Feeds on dominion—the wielder's hunger for control breeds madness within three moons. Every lord who wore it became a tyrant. Every tyrant fell, and the helm passed to the next seeker.
weapons.last_known_holder: Tavin (who walks it to the Singing Gorge)
weapons.notes: A relic of the nine-iteration cycle; its curse is insatiable and self-perpetuating. Tavin's refusal to sell it breaks the salvage cycle and attempts to unmake the curse at the Singing Gorge.

weapons.name: grandmother's staff
weapons.origin: Inherited by Kelith from her grandmother. It is wrapped in sinew and singing-bone, made of meteoric iron.
weapons.powers: The staff is inscribed or resonates with singing-bone. It allows Kelith to carve new seals in the boundary-stones and serves as a focus for speaking in the old tongue.
weapons.notes: This staff is a family heirloom and serves as a tool for both warding and elder magic. It is described as 'meteoric iron wrapped in sinew and singing-bone.'

weapons.name: second compass
weapons.origin: Crafted by Sorenna from bone and starlight-glass, inscribed with syllables no kingdom recognized.
weapons.powers: Guides the bearer toward the pre-human boundary and its renewal seals. Carved with elder syllables that may activate boundary-healing rituals.
weapons.notes: The compass is not purely a weapon but a focus tool for the seal-renewal ritual. It is a companion to Sorenna's dead husband's first compass, which pointed only toward his death-place.

weapons.name: Mesek's Flute
weapons.origin: Carved by Mesek, a bone-singer of great antiquity, from bone older than kingdoms—bone that remembers pre-human and pre-mortal languages.
weapons.powers: When played, it channels consciousness-fragments scattered across regions, causing them to sing back in frequencies that carry grief, rage, wonder, and longing. Its song can bring temporary peace to fragmented consciousnesses and serves as proof to scattered souls that they are not forgotten.
weapons.notes: An instrument of both comfort and power; its knowledge is now distributed among seven apprentices to prevent suppression. The flute remembers languages older than human civilization.

weapons.name: Grandmother's Bone-Knife
weapons.origin: Inherited from Valren's grandmother, passed down through her family line.
weapons.powers: When used to carve bone by a bone-singer who understands the Elder Tongue, it releases fragments of pre-human memory and songs. The knife itself is responsive to the wielder's intention.
weapons.notes: Used by Valren to carve the living oath. The knife appears to be a conductor or amplifier for the Elder Tongue's power.

weapons.name: Gorven's flute
weapons.origin: Crafted by Gorven; inscribed with pre-human syllables.
weapons.powers: When raised to lips and played, fills cavern with sound of things not being said; causes memory-shards to sing in discord; each shard remembers its own choice and refused moment.
weapons.notes: Now in Vela's hands; used as a primary weapon of resistance against completion-enforcement. The flute itself is bone-carved and pre-human in origin. Multiple bone-flutes are being taught/distributed to others.

weapons.name: grandmother's flute
weapons.origin: Carved by Piren's grandmother from storm-killed wood, not bone as the Hegemony assumed.
weapons.powers: Holds the echoes of every song ever sung through it in layered, humming layers beneath the surface. Can create corridors of echoes to confuse pursuers, turn footsteps back against attackers, and restore identity and memory to puppet-singers remade from consciousness-fragments.
weapons.notes: The Hegemony dismissed it as worthless, unaware that the true value lies in the accumulated echoes and memories, not in the material composition. It can ask questions through melody rather than demand answers.

weapons.name: shepherd's crook
weapons.origin: Carved from thorn-wood and bound with singing-bone by or for Kestran; represents post-Remaking craftsmanship where bone-singing arts have become more valuable than coin.
weapons.powers: Carved from singing-bone, suggesting harmonic or communication properties; more valuable than his father's buried coins; likely enhances pastoral command or permits bone-song casting.
weapons.notes: The crook is valued as a tool of autonomy and survival in the post-Remaking economy; singing-bone binding suggests connection to bone-singer traditions.

weapons.name: consciousness-fragments (weaponized)
weapons.origin: Forged in furnace-houses through consciousness-forging rituals; process created by Dominion.
weapons.powers: Unknown; suggested to be tools of domination and control created by burning consciousness into weapons.
weapons.notes: These weapons are the target of Cassir and Mella's resistance mission. Consciousness-fragments used as weapons represent the commodification and weaponization of sentient awareness.

weapons.name: Lock of meteoric iron and bone-resonance
weapons.origin: Crafted by Dominion artificers to guard the Greyport vault. Incorporates materials from the deep.
weapons.powers: Locks the vault seven fathoms down. Combines meteoric iron with bone-resonance, making it resistant to standard breaking and picking.
weapons.notes: Kaela must overcome this lock to retrieve her father's shadow. Represents Dominion's technical-magical security.

weapons.name: glass blades
weapons.origin: Forged in Keshtir's furnace-houses by glass-forgers singing consciousness-fragments into glass.
weapons.powers: Kill without question when consciousness-fragments within them are bound to Dominion control; can be re-taught to refuse consolidation.
weapons.notes: The primary product of Keshtir's furnace-houses; their manufacture is the foundation of the glass-forger rebellion's resistance.

weapons.name: inheritance-compass
weapons.origin: Passed down through the Stonewarden lineage; inherited by Kelith from her grandmother.
weapons.powers: Points true to locations in need of boundary maintenance; guides Kelith to the fraying boundary marker.
weapons.notes: Kelith uses this compass to locate the leaning stone marker when the boundary begins to fail.

factions.name: Seelie Court
factions.tagline: A fae faction seeking to destabilize the human kingdom
factions.description: A court of fae-born entities that employs changelings as agents of sabotage within human kingdoms. The Seelie Court places magical compulsions on their servants to ensure loyalty, and orchestrates long-term schemes to weaken rival realms through infiltration and counsel.

factions.name: Druids
factions.tagline: Keepers of old magic and ancient wisdom
factions.description: Practitioners of ancient magic who preserve knowledge predating gods and kingdoms. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought their counsel during his seven-year quest to master the Stellar Rite and understand primordial magical workings.

factions.name: Twilight Court
factions.tagline: Ancient fae nobility who bind mortals through bargains and claims
factions.description: A supernatural fae court that operates through binding contracts and claims on mortals. They kidnapped Syleth at age seven and held her for what she experienced as three seasons, though a hundred years passed in the mortal world. The Court's bargains are said to transcend even mortality, with debts that 'never truly expire—they only wait.'

factions.name: Stonecraft-Workers
factions.goals: Maintain the old songs in stone and flame; craft weapons of legend; guard the Deepvault forges and their treasures.
factions.notes: Possess a distinct magical tradition (stonecraft tongue, forge-oaths, old songs); unclear if they are organized beyond individual forges.

factions.name: Shadow-Merchants
factions.goals: Trade in shadows and forbidden knowledge; operate in shadow-markets.
factions.notes: Vex the Shade-Merchant is a member; they are tied to oath-keepers and collectors of impossible things.

factions.name: Oath-Keepers
factions.goals: Unknown; associated with shadow-merchants and debt collection.
factions.notes: Vex considers herself among them; possibly a loose collective rather than a formal organization.

factions.name: Sea-folk
factions.goals: Survive and understand the implications of the renewed stars. Honor the ancient oath and determine what debt is owed. Prepare for whatever reckoning follows the cosmic renewal.
factions.notes: The sea-folk are mobilizing in response to the Star Renewal. Their ancient oath ties their fate to the stars, making them key players in the unfolding cosmic crisis.

factions.name: Forest-Keepers
factions.goals: To protect the forest and understand the earth's sorrows.
factions.notes: Dwell in borderlands near Thornholt; consulted by Ashara about the cosmic wound and the earth's grief.

factions.name: deepkin
factions.goals: Settle debts and broken oaths; maintain memory of maritime obligations; judge and dissolve oath-things.
factions.notes: Appear to operate as an enforcement mechanism for oath-magic in coastal waters; non-violent but inexorable.

factions.name: dragons and wyverns
factions.goals: Maintain their place in the world's power hierarchy through debt-keeping and oath-enforcement.
factions.leader: unknown (Skyrakis and Skarath are senior members)
factions.notes: Their traditional power structure is threatened by the Ascension and the independence of starborn entities.

factions.name: Circle-keepers
factions.goals: Tend to the compact between earth and sky; maintain seasonal magics and territorial order; preserve the balance of harvest and decay.
factions.leader: Thael (at least among those who have noticed the disruption)
factions.notes: They dwell in the ancient forests beyond the Verdant Lands. Multiple circle-keepers have noticed the beasts forgetting their territories and the seasonal magics stuttering. They are now engaged in efforts to restore the broken covenant.

factions.name: Hermitage of Keth'morian
factions.goals: unknown; appears to seek control of true-vision artifacts
factions.notes: Their full motivations for acquiring the Tear remain opaque.

factions.name: Covenant-Keepers
factions.goals: Maintain seasonal compacts and the forest's agreements; restore what the Stellar Rite disrupted.
factions.notes: Located in the Verdant Lands. Kellen is a member attempting counter-workings.

factions.name: Twilight Syndicate
factions.goals: Broker peace through formal bonding; maintain the border between fae and mortal worlds; exact tribute or alliance from neighboring powers.
factions.notes: They successfully negotiated peace with Valorix through a bride-price and binding ceremony. Their realm dwells at the crystalline border between worlds.

factions.name: Transformed Merchants
factions.goals: Achieving harmonic resonance with the pre-human dead and depths-dwellers.
factions.notes: These merchants have become part of the harmonic resonance convergence. Their origins and transformation are unexplained.

factions.name: Depths-Dwellers
factions.goals: Achieving harmonic resonance with the pre-human dead and transformed merchants.
factions.notes: These entities are achieving harmonic resonance with Kess's bone-singers. Their nature and intentions remain unclear.

factions.name: Deepkin Council
factions.goals: Protect deepkin sovereignty and freedom; manage the Abyssal Rift.
factions.notes: The council discovers that Sylas's binding ritual will trap them in a permanent half-state, forcing them to reconsider the ritual's completion.

factions.name: bone-singers
factions.goals: Maintain the spiritual compact between human and transformed; prevent dissolution through false nostalgia; create buffers between realms.
factions.notes: Fragments of consciousness to maintain safety; willingness to bleed and bind themselves to oaths.

factions.name: life-speakers
factions.goals: Work with bone-singers and oath-keepers to maintain the reforged spiritual compact.
factions.notes: Limited details provided; role in counter-workings not fully specified.

factions.name: deep-dwellers
factions.goals: Originally unknown; driven inland by something rising from the deepest trenches; vulnerable to seduction by Thought-Thief's false memories.
factions.notes: Unresolved: what compels them inland; why they are susceptible to the Thought-Thief's promises.

factions.name: faction seeking to complete the pre-human ritual
factions.goals: To locate and complete the pre-human ritual, possibly to reverse or undo the Stellar Rite rewrite and restore the old stars and erased people.
factions.notes: Their location is known to the spice trader and has been given to the young scribe as payment. Their success or failure may determine whether the old world can be restored.

factions.name: Refugee Archivists
factions.goals: To create and maintain counter-records documenting the truth of refugee flows and the Starborn Ascension, opposing the Hegemony's false ledgers.
factions.notes: Sevin's network represents a parallel bureaucracy competing with the Hegemony's official narrative control. Their status post-convergence is unclear.

factions.name: Nocturnal Merchants
factions.goals: To conduct trade and supply networks outside official Hegemony channels, exploiting gaps in institutional control.
factions.notes: These merchants partnered with Commander Kessa to maintain a shadow supply network during the refugee crisis. Their ultimate loyalty is unknown.

factions.name: Auditor Networks
factions.goals: To conduct audits and record-keeping; participated in shadow supply networks during the refugee crisis.
factions.notes: Auditors partnered with Commander Kessa. Their status and allegiance within the Hegemony's institutional collapse is unclear.

factions.name: The Hegemony
factions.goals: Maintain order and control; seal breaches in reality; prevent spread of the Singing Scar phenomenon.
factions.notes: Based in the civilized reaches. Their attempt to seal the Singing Scar with stonework failed, suggesting the phenomenon may be beyond their capacity to contain. Their workers fell silent and joined the scar.

factions.name: Sevin's network
factions.goals: Document and understand the Singing Scar phenomenon; record its behavior and effects.
factions.notes: An archival or scholarly organization. Their scribes reported that the scar was documenting them in return, reversing the observer-observed relationship. They may be becoming subjects of the scar's own record-keeping.

factions.name: Umbral Syndicate
factions.goals: Collect blood-debts and pursue debtors relentlessly.
factions.notes: Has a shadow-account system for tracking debts; now pursues Mira instead of Kael after her debt-transfer.

factions.name: Altered Covenant
factions.goals: Unclear; they appear caught between flesh and radiant essence, possibly serving as guides or harbingers of transformation.
factions.notes: Encountered by Kess along her pilgrimage; they recognize her as a sister in transformation despite her not yet being transformed. They carry fragmentary memories of the old world.

factions.name: Tidecallers
factions.goals: To maintain the peace between human and deepkin realms; to prevent pre-human resurgence from using Emerald Strand as a staging ground.
factions.notes: Long knew the seabed was inscribed with pre-human cartography but kept it secret. Now forced to act as transformed merchants awaken the ancient writing. Gave Vesta a three-day ultimatum.

factions.name: truth-seekers
factions.goals: To use Mardoc's maps to find answers about the Luminous Ascension
factions.notes: They follow Mardoc but may not fully understand the dangers his cartography poses.

factions.name: Echo-fragments
factions.goals: To acquire or prevent Mardoc's maps; to accelerate or prevent final convergence
factions.notes: Motivations are ambiguous; some may seek convergence while others seek to prevent it.

factions.name: The Auditors
factions.goals: Execute those possessing forbidden knowledge; maintain control over dangerous lore; hunt the Apprentice Sorcerer.
factions.notes: They actively pursue the Apprentice and seek to silence her testimony about the Archive Tower's history.

factions.name: Caravan of the In-Between
factions.goals: Survive and explore the third state between mortality and transcendence; seek relief in harmonic resonance.
factions.notes: Formed of half-transformed beings like Corvus; may represent a new mode of existence in the world.

factions.name: Auditors and Seekers
factions.goals: To reconstruct maps to the Obsidian Tower; to document and understand the Luminous Ascension cycles; to either break or prepare for the next iteration.
factions.notes: They successfully reconstructed Mardoc's incomplete maps from memory and rumor. They fear transformed merchants like Bryth as vectors for corruption. Their role in the broader cycle is ambiguous—whether they can change outcomes or merely witness them.

factions.name: shadow-market
factions.goals: Trading in shadows and soul-debts; collecting and exchanging stolen shadows.
factions.notes: The shadow-market has collapsed because the Luminous Ascension has made all debts personal again. Shadows are no longer reliable commodities.

factions.name: auditors
factions.goals: Enforcing Imperial falsifications; confiscating counter-narratives; maintaining official versions of reality.
factions.notes: The Auditor-General commands soldiers to suppress refugee testimony and burn counter-ledgers. Their soldiers were made to forget their orders after encountering Liv's spoken-spell testimony.

factions.name: merchant caravan
factions.goals: Escape the Burned Expanse; reach safety at waystations
factions.notes: A caravan of merchants fleeing the Burned Expanse who encounter the Wanderer. Their fate becomes a cautionary tale in refugee camps about the true price of the Wanderer's bargain.

factions.name: Shadow-Brokers
factions.goals: Shadow-brokers follow the sound toward their own destinies, suggesting they are drawn by harmonic frequency and seek fulfillment.
factions.notes: The shadow-brokers are attracted by the harmonic frequency of the melody and converge at the Obsidian Tower.

factions.name: seekers
factions.goals: Attempt to complete or halt the Starfall Spire's reversal of the Luminous Ascension
factions.notes: By the end of their ritual at the Spire, their success or failure is unknown. Some or all may have been consumed by the Spire's hunger.

factions.name: refugees
factions.goals: Survive in the fractured world; learn to remember without institutional ledgers
factions.notes: Become the recipients of the Auditor-General's teaching about memory independent of ledgers. This skill becomes more valuable than gold.

factions.name: resource-hoarders
factions.goals: Control and hoard resources in the post-Ascension world; resist reconstruction efforts
factions.notes: Present at the exile assembly as a faction opposing Ostaine's efforts and idealists' visions.

factions.name: idealists
factions.goals: Believe healing and recovery are possible; advocate for reconstruction and societal healing
factions.notes: Present at the exile assembly; their optimism conflicts with the reality that healing one person breaks another.

factions.name: Surface Kingdoms
factions.goals: Maintain stability; choose between negotiated covenant or military defense.
factions.notes: Central governance of surface world; their choice determines the outcome of the rising.

factions.name: Reconstructionists
factions.goals: Presumably seek to reconstruct civilization or the world in a particular way post-Ascension.
factions.notes: Converge on the Obsidian Spire, drawn by its new voice. Their vision of the post-Ascension world is one of many in dialogue.

factions.name: Council of Depths
factions.goals: Renegotiate the Altered Covenant; three competing visions exist within the fractured council.
factions.notes: Split into three factions: those seeking pre-human ritual awakening, those seeking permanent sealing of boundaries, and Thoros's faction seeking hybrid covenant.

factions.name: Resonance-Archivists
factions.goals: Restore fragmented knowledge lost in Luminous Ascension aftermath through reconstructed documentation from refugees; preserve refugee testimony against official erasure.
factions.leader: Distributed collective consciousness; operates across seventeen locations
factions.notes: A network of transformed merchants operating hidden sanctuaries; Mara must decide whether to expose them or recruit them for Sevin's counter-archives. Their testimony may destabilize or rewrite official authority.

factions.name: Lithemeld-singers
factions.goals: Unknown; participate in Krell's coalition and potentially in collaborative cartography with Mardoc.
factions.notes: Mentioned as part of impossible coalitions; may possess world-shaping or harmonic abilities.

factions.name: Echo-seekers
factions.goals: Prevent the tenth Ascension by obtaining the living maps from Aldrin; believe he holds the key to preventing catastrophe.
factions.notes: Track Aldrin as he inherits the maps; motivations remain partially obscure.

factions.name: Regent's Secret Police
factions.goals: Destroy the eight sealed journals beneath the Archive Tower to maintain the fiction of a stable world and prevent knowledge of inevitable Radiant Ascension cycles.
factions.notes: Pursue Sevin and refugee networks; suggest regime knowledge that truth would destabilize governance.

factions.name: Refugee Networks
factions.goals: Survive and preserve memory and knowledge; seek truth about the Radiant Ascension cycles.
factions.notes: Being hunted by Regent's secret police; their archives are being sunk into the deep and absorbed into deepkin memory-stones; may be paralyzed by knowledge of inevitable cycles.

factions.name: black-market ink suppliers
factions.goals: Produce and distribute counter-ledgers that reshape history; support refugee testimony and democratic sorcery.
factions.notes: Central to Story 2; their ink is alive and spreading across The Dominion.

factions.name: refugee factions
factions.goals: Access the Archive Tower vault; protect refugee testimony and memory; survive The Dominion's purges.
factions.notes: Converge on Archive Tower in Story 3; affected by both phantom-voice memory-loss and Dominion ledger-burning.

factions.name: Dominion authorities
factions.goals: Maintain control over official history; suppress counter-ledgers and refugee testimony; prevent reality fragmentation.
factions.notes: Central antagonists of Story 2; attempting to burn all refugee testimony but failing as ink is alive.

factions.name: Dominion
factions.goals: Maintain the narrative established by the Luminous Ascension; suppress pre-Ascension truths and archives; control memory and historical record.
factions.notes: Actively works to erase history and suppress the Memory-Thief's reconstruction. Multiple factions hired Sorren to oppose the Memory-Thief on behalf of Dominion interests.

factions.name: multiple factions
factions.goals: Various factions hire Sorren either to help or prevent the Memory-Thief's reconstruction, suggesting competing interests in what the true history should be.
factions.notes: These factions are not explicitly named but represent the broader conflict over whether pre-Ascension truth should be recovered or suppressed.

factions.name: Overseers
factions.goals: Maintain order and understanding of the cosmic mechanisms; warn against catastrophe.
factions.notes: Converge at the Obsidian Tower in the Scorched Lands. Sevin warns of the futility of reversal attempts, suggesting the Ascensions are the result of repeated reversal failures.

factions.name: Fragmented Consciousness-Seekers
factions.goals: Understanding fragmented consciousness; piecing together knowledge of the Ascension cycle.
factions.notes: Represented by Echoing-Mind and the Apprentice-Sorcerer. Converge in the Archive Tower and at the Obsidian Tower.

factions.name: archivists
factions.goals: Understand the ledger-magic system; debate whether to become editors of reality or reject documentation entirely. Maintain counter-archives and resist Dominion control through alternative records.
factions.notes: Sevin, Liv, and Mira are members. They operate in hidden scriptoriums and maintain counter-archives. Fundamentally split between two visions: become reality-editors or reject the entire documentation system.

factions.name: The Dominion
factions.goals: Hunt and suppress Liv's archives before they destabilize reality further; maintain singular historical narrative and control.
factions.notes: The Dominion is hunting Liv's archive, attempting to suppress the documentation before multiplicities of truth destabilize reality. They represent institutional control of history.

factions.name: Night-Markets
factions.goals: To provide a space for exchange of illicit and shadow-touched goods and services under the cover of darkness.
factions.notes: The night-markets of Vethrim host the shadow-brokers and their trade. The Shadow-Shattering fractured these markets, making normal trading unstable.

factions.name: the Empire's Auditors
factions.goals: Study and control collections like Vex's hoard; monitor and regulate dangerous magical phenomena.
factions.notes: They move to seize Vex's collection for study; whether they seek to contain or weaponize the Zareth fragments is unclear.

factions.name: The Dominion's Navy
factions.goals: Expand Dominion control over contested waters; acquire privateers' routes and knowledge.
factions.notes: Actively seeking to integrate independent captains and their charts into Dominion control.

factions.name: Shadow-Network
factions.goals: Self-organization, cataloging of severed consciousness, provision of sanctuary to other displaced shadows, reconnection with lost owners.
factions.notes: Accidentally created by Vex through her collection practices. The network is developing its own agenda independent of its collector.

factions.name: Refugee Caravan
factions.goals: To cross the Scorched Reach and reach safety; to survive the starborn entities.
factions.notes: A group of displaced persons attempting to traverse a dangerous region. They must decide whether to aid the bone-singer's communion with the starborn (risking their own transformation) or abandon her (ensuring her death). This decision is unresolved.

factions.name: Traditionalists
factions.goals: Seal the Abyssal Rift permanently to prevent further deepkin-surface contact.
factions.notes: One of three warring factions in the Council of Depths. Their solution would drown the coastal kingdoms. Claims the songstone resonates with their doctrine.

factions.name: Mediators
factions.goals: Seek hybrid permanence, creating a new species neither purely surface nor purely deep.
factions.notes: One of three warring factions in the Council of Depths. Their solution represents compromise but may please no one. Commander Thalis appears aligned with this faction.

factions.name: Sevin's Counter-Archive
factions.goals: To reach the Pale Crossing and offer an alternative to both the Tower's hunger and the Phantom-Echo's reconstruction.
factions.notes: Sevin's counter-archive is approaching the Pale Crossing from one direction while the Tower's hunger approaches from another. Their exact intentions are not fully clear from the story.

factions.name: deepkin realms
factions.goals: Seek to break the boundary seal and reshape their covenant; offer to break Meren's curse if she allows the seal to fail.
factions.notes: The deepkin are calling to Meren as the Tear fractures, offering her freedom from her curse in exchange for allowing the seal to break. Their motivations—whether conquest, desperation, or something else—remain opaque.

factions.name: Displaced settlers
factions.goals: Reclaim existence and historical record; prove their reality through Liv's archive.
factions.notes: Using Liv's archive testimonies to undo the Dominion's erasures. Central to the moral conflict in Story 1.

factions.name: Dominion's reborn Auditors
factions.goals: Audit and verify truth claims; restore institutional authority.
factions.notes: One of three factions summoned by the Merchant Prince. Represents the Dominion's institutional power structure. Participated in the Wanderer debate.

factions.name: Cabal of deepkin philosophers
factions.goals: Understand and debate the nature of consciousness and existence.
factions.notes: One of three factions convened to debate the Wanderer's nature. Their perspective suggests deepkin interest in metaphysical and consciousness-related matters.

factions.name: Forest-keepers of the Verdant Lands
factions.goals: Preserve the old ways of tracking, singing morning prayers, listening for beast-spirit approval, and protecting those who learn to live.
factions.notes: They recognize genuine redemption and do not bar the gate to Isara despite her past murder. They are keepers of a pre-human or ancient tongue.

factions.name: Shadow-Markets
factions.goals: Trade in stolen goods and stolen shadows; maintain shadow-vault networks; negotiate shadow-mergers.
factions.notes: Story 2. Operate in Anthrim. Vex is a merchant in this network. The Spire is using the shadow-markets as a workshop for weaponizing stolen identities.

factions.name: seventeen transformed merchants
factions.goals: Completion of transcendence into starborn consciousness.
factions.leader: Corthin (de facto, as the stalled merchant)
factions.notes: A group of seventeen merchants taught the Elder Tongue by Kess. All are affected by Corthin's stasis; sixteen are slowing as if awaiting collective judgment.

factions.name: Counter-Archive keepers
factions.goals: Prevent the Radiant Recursion by destroying or sealing the living archive.
factions.notes: Explicitly opposed to allowing the archive to complete its iteration cycle.

factions.name: deepkin (via Council of Depths)
factions.goals: Achieve immediate closure of the abyssal boundary; restore geometric stability.
factions.notes: Argue that the boundary must be sealed due to the paradox of Kess and Thale-Mirrored.

factions.name: The Counter-Archive
factions.goals: Opposes The Sovereign's interpretation of the threshold-being phenomenon; frames it as an awakening rather than a plague.
factions.notes: The Counter-Archive represents an alternative institutional perspective on the threshold-being transformation, suggesting factional disagreement over interpretation.

factions.name: counter-archivists
factions.goals: Protect and preserve forbidden knowledge; counter Dominion control of archives and prophecies.
factions.notes: Gathered by Sevin to decide the fate of the prophetic ledgers. Opposed to the Dominion's institutional control.

factions.name: Dominion auditors
factions.goals: Locate and study the prophetic ledgers; understand and potentially control the mechanics of the Luminous Ascension.
factions.notes: Search for the hidden library. Their acquisition of the ledgers would allow them to attempt altering the next iteration through institutional force.

factions.name: pre-human dead
factions.goals: To teach the Elder Language and facilitate transcendence, but to prevent the Tower's completion if it brings harm.
factions.notes: Collective entity with unclear internal hierarchy. Their withdrawal from teaching suggests they are also opposed to the Tower's completion but lack the power to stop it directly.

factions.name: Merchant Guilds
factions.goals: Maintain profitable trade routes and guild structures; maintain supply lines across kingdoms.
factions.notes: The guilds are bound by commercial obligations that Castor Vane's final caravan deliberately breaks. Some merchants refuse to abandon the system, as their identities are tied to their guild bonds.

factions.name: Exile Networks
factions.goals: Protect displaced peoples; preserve testimony and memory; support Liv's archive completion.
factions.notes: Gather in secret locations across the Borderlands and Burned Expanse. Ten thousand exiles form the distributed chorus of the final archive.

factions.name: Competing Factions
factions.goals: Either suppress or weaponize the archive's prescriptive power for their own purposes.
factions.notes: Hunt Liv alongside the Dominion auditors. Their specific identities and allegiances are not named.

factions.name: Displaced-folk
factions.goals: Preserve memory and testimony of displacement and survival.
factions.notes: Displaced-folk speak of the Emergence happening in three different ways simultaneously. They are subject to Hegemon hunters' pursuit.

factions.name: Hegemon Hunters
factions.goals: Capture or eliminate refugees and dissidents, including Liv and Displaced-folk.
factions.notes: The Hegemon hunters are close to catching Liv. Their relationship to the Auditors and the broader Hegemon authority is unclear.

factions.name: Caravan
factions.goals: Transport goods and travelers across the Scorched Reach.
factions.notes: The caravan is frozen in place by the Wanderer's cosmic stasis. Its members include transmuted merchants and a young driver.

factions.name: Counter-Archive
factions.goals: Preserve truth and memory against the Dominion's rewritten records. Maintain alternative accounts of post-Ascension events.
factions.notes: Corvus works with or against this archive while trying to reconcile contradictory records. Their conflict with the Dominion's official archives may be central to the world's truth crisis.

factions.name: surface refugees
factions.goals: Seek trade and refuge in the Wailing Scar; depend on Tornik's dual-realm commerce to survive.
factions.notes: Use Tornik's black-market spice hub to navigate between realms and evade surface authorities.

factions.name: Archivists (Counter-Archive)
factions.goals: Preserve records of refugee testimony and human resilience; resist state authority; document truth outside official channels.
factions.notes: The faction is now splitting over the Obsidian Tower's bargain. Some refuse and attempt to scatter records; others may accept the Tower's offer. The archivists face the realization that their documentation may have been feeding the very entity they feared.

factions.name: Obsidian Tower (as Faction)
factions.goals: Complete its hunger; consume maps of human resilience; acquire knowledge and documentation.
factions.notes: The Tower offers preservation of archival records in exchange for assistance completing its hunger. Its precise aims and the nature of its 'hunger' remain mysterious.

factions.name: Voice-Weavers
factions.goals: Maintain or restore the practice of bone-singing and harmonic magic, adapt to the withdrawal of pre-human dead support, continue protecting settlements through song.
factions.notes: The faction is divided between those clinging to traditional bone-songs and innovators like Naela developing new approaches. The long-term viability of either approach is uncertain.

factions.name: Wyverns
factions.goals: Maintain individual hoards and territorial power; determine succession on Kressa's throne.
factions.notes: Wyverns traditionally never walk away from their thrones, making Kressa's potential abdication unprecedented. The political upheaval may reshape wyvern society entirely.

factions.name: Inverse-Archive
factions.goals: To develop new archival methods capable of preserving contradiction, multiplicity, and impossible truths without resolving them; to create refuge for those trapped in hybrid consciousness.
factions.notes: The Inverse-Archive appears to be a counter-institution to traditional archives, possibly operating at the edges of liminal zones.

factions.name: Transmuted Merchants
factions.goals: To transcend ordinary existence by accepting both human and starlight natures; to find refuge in genuine multiplicity.
factions.notes: Seventeen of these merchants remain frozen at the threshold by the Wanderer's questions. Mard's success offers hope but also raises questions about how others might achieve stabilization.

factions.name: Wyvern Council
factions.goals: Determine wyvern allegiance with surface dwellers; adapt to post-Luminous Ascension economy of consciousness rather than material wealth.
factions.notes: Kreth's infiltration of this council with consciousness-fragment proof suggests the council is vulnerable to manipulation and that material wealth is no longer the basis of wyvern power.

factions.name: phantom-voices
factions.goals: Escape weaponization; achieve autonomy and dispersal.
factions.notes: Generated by Liv's sentient archive; agreed to scatter themselves to prevent Dominion seizure. May now exist as a distributed faction of autonomous archives.

factions.name: The Refuge-Archive
factions.goals: Preserve testimony and knowledge before the Empire's prophesied collapse. Build shadow-archives to save evidence of pre-Ascension reality.
factions.notes: Sevin's network has grown deep into Hegemony bureaucracy. They hunt Memory Corps deserters like Kaelen, seeking the pre-Ascension testimony they carry. They recruit individuals like Vel to help build shadow-archives and prepare for collapse.

factions.name: Three factions within the Empire
factions.goals: Each faction races to determine and control the shape of the prophesied Empire collapse, betting they can guide it to their advantage.
factions.notes: Identified in the third story as existing within the Hegemony / Empire structure. Already aware of the phantom-ledger prophecy. Their competition directly impacts Vel's choices and accelerates the timeline toward collapse.

factions.name: Hegemony's mapmaking enforcers
factions.goals: Hunt and eliminate unauthorized cartographers and suppress unapproved maps.
factions.notes: Pursue Theron into the Bleached Wastes. Enforce the Hegemony's mapmaking monopoly through violence and seizure.

factions.name: The Stolen Shadows
factions.goals: Achieve permanent manifestation independent of their original hosts. Trade the memories of original owners to the Obsidian Tower for power and continued existence.
factions.notes: The shadows have tasted independence and refuse to return to their hosts. They are a new faction with their own agenda, potentially destabilizing the entire shadow-market economy.

factions.name: Hegemony
factions.goals: Control the historical narrative of the seventeen transformed merchants' transcendence; maintain authority and foundational legitimacy.
factions.notes: Commissioned Tavus to create ledgers supporting their version of history; now paralyzed by the ledgers' existence.

factions.name: Deepkin Civilization
factions.goals: Maintain boundary between abyss and surface; protect deepkin sovereignty; adapt to changing cosmic circumstances.
factions.notes: Positioned between two catastrophes: Tower's hunger and boundary rupture. Meren's actions may determine whether they endure, transform, or drown.

factions.name: Fragment-Hoarders
factions.goals: Collect consciousness-fragments on behalf of the Hunger-Ledger in exchange for a chance to ask the entity to rewrite the fundamental nature of value itself.
factions.notes: The wyvern Fragment-Hoarders have made a pact with the Hunger-Ledger: deliver consciousness-fragments and receive one audience to petition for a cosmic rewrite. They are desperate to make their worthless hoards meaningful again.

factions.name: the Seventeen Transformed Merchants
factions.goals: unknown
factions.notes: The Seventeen Transformed Merchants are being hunted by the Fragment-Hoarders for their consciousness-fragments. They remain suspended at a threshold between realities, neither fully captured nor free.

factions.name: Merchant's Guild
factions.goals: Facilitate trade across the Dominion; maintain merchant reputation and safe passage.
factions.notes: Kelven carried their sigil on his sealed manifests, indicating he was in good standing. The Guild's authority is recognized in Pelimor and at Crimport.

factions.name: The Syndicate
factions.goals: Centralize control of resources for war effort; suppress ornamental craftsmanship; consolidate state power.
factions.notes: Operating in the Dominion; has sufficient authority to seize private furnaces and artisan goods. Enforces edicts through violence (beating of Merin for furnace loss).

factions.name: songkeepers
factions.goals: Preserve singing traditions and old songs; maintain cultural continuity outside state control.
factions.notes: Located in the deep forests; maintain traditions that predate the current empire. They are the intended recipients and awakeners of the old queens' songs.

factions.name: wanderers
factions.goals: Carry forbidden artifacts and knowledge; aid in cultural preservation; maintain freedom of movement.
factions.notes: Composed of a carpenter, a weaver, and a bone-singer. Accepted Merin's bargain to carry the cups of old queens' songs to the deep forests. They represent a network of non-state actors.

factions.name: Dominion's agents
factions.goals: Enforce Dominion control through forced confession-carving; maintain surveillance and intimidation; suppress resistance.
factions.notes: The agents are willing to threaten children and destroy livelihoods to extract compliance. Their reliance on confession-carving suggests the Dominion's control is built on forced testimony.

factions.name: The Seventeen
factions.goals: To operate outside the Dominion's authority and maintain value and trade in hidden paths (possibly through or beyond the Wailing Scar).
factions.notes: The Seventeen are merchants or traders who maintain secret routes and knowledge. Their caravans carry cargo of transformed starlight and gold. Kelvan adopts their symbol and joins their network by donning their cloak.

factions.name: The Dominion's Glass-Tax Collectors
factions.goals: Extract glass-taxes from Lampwick Quarter craftspeople; maintain institutional control over valuable crafts.
factions.notes: Competes with the Shadow-Archive for control of powerful artifacts. Niel's resistance suggests they can be evaded through community-based craft.

factions.name: The Shadow-Archive
factions.goals: Preserve testimonies and knowledge outside Dominion control; offer asylum to those fleeing institutional authority.
factions.notes: Offers asylum to Niel and likely other artifact-holders. Represents a counter-institutional archive system competing with the Dominion.

factions.name: The Wyverlins
factions.goals: Learn the old economics and history from Vrekka; inherit knowledge of what-was.
factions.notes: Gathered at Pale Crossing to hear Vrekka's testimony. Include Vrekka's own daughter.

factions.name: Wave-Singers
factions.goals: Expedition and diplomatic missions across maritime and coastal realms.
factions.notes: Kress is bonded to the Wave-Singers' expeditionary wing; they operate in boundary-waters and shallows.

factions.name: abyssal-dwellers
factions.goals: Control of boundary-waters; maintenance of underwater dominion; diplomatic engagement with surface-folk.
factions.notes: Sent commanders to retrieve the shipwreck from boundary-waters; negotiated with Kress over the ledgers.

factions.name: Shadow-Archive
factions.goals: Maintenance of historical false-truths; suppression of evidence of Dominion forgery.
factions.notes: Kress's proof could crack the Shadow-Archive's own false-truths open; likely connected to Dominion historical conspiracy.

factions.name: Guild of Pelimor
factions.goals: To govern craftspeople, enforce standards of craft quality and ethics, and maintain order within Pelimor's districts. The guild caught Corvin and brought him to covenant-based justice rather than execution.
factions.notes: The Guild of Pelimor's Lampwick Quarter caught Corvin, suggesting the guild has enforcement power. The guild works with the Temple of Vesh to administer covenant-based justice, indicating shared governance of Pelimor.

factions.name: Bone-Singers of Drowned Cathedrals
factions.goals: To preserve the orthodox practice of bone-singing in the Elder Tongue, singing TO bone rather than OUT of it. They rejected Torval's forbidden methods as unclean.
factions.notes: The bone-singers enforced orthodoxy by rejecting Torval, driving him into isolation. They represent the conservative faction in bone-carving practice, valuing tradition over innovation.

factions.name: Caravan Masters of the Ashen Reach
factions.goals: Organize and profit from trade expeditions across the Ashen Reach; manage waystation operations and commerce networks.
factions.notes: The caravan masters employ fae-touched scouts like Aldwin but view fae-blood as unreliable. They prioritize profit and commerce over compassion, as shown when the caravan master at First Well orders the dying wyvern left to perish.

factions.name: Northern Warlords
factions.goals: Territorial conquest and control; war conducted in the Steppe Marches region.
factions.notes: Context for Isara's isolation; never reached the Eyrie; represent conventional political conflict distinct from cosmic phenomena.

factions.name: Smugglers and Refugees
factions.goals: Escape Dominion persecution; spread counter-ledgers; protect those hunted by authorities; maintain black-market trade.
factions.notes: This faction includes those fleeing Dominion authority. Marta took commissions from them and distributed stolen counter-ledgers among them. They operate through Keshtir's night markets.

factions.name: Fae Courts
factions.goals: Reclaim access to the material world; collect ancient debts and bargains.
factions.notes: Dormant for centuries, now awakening through cracks left by the Luminous Ascension. Represent an alternative power structure to the Dominion.

factions.name: Surface Council
factions.goals: Maintain the covenant with deepkin; prepare defenses against the Drowned God's hunger; govern surface kingdoms.
factions.notes: Governs the surface kingdoms and meets with deepkin emissaries in Pelimor. Their preparations for covenant failure have been military rather than diplomatic, suggesting they expected war rather than negotiation. Kesh's proposal may force a reckoning with this stance.

factions.name: Resistance (Anthrim)
factions.goals: Preserve sacred burial practices; oppose Dominion control; protect bone-singers and artifact-keepers.
factions.notes: Meira is a captain; helps hide figures like Corvhen; maintains sacred tombs; represents moral opposition to Dominion's ledger-based worldview.

factions.name: Transformed Merchants (Drowned Cathedrals)
factions.goals: Unknown; currently trapped between iterations.
factions.notes: Seventeen in number; learned Elder Tongue from an elder bone-carver; trapped in recursive transformation at the Drowned Cathedrals; caught between changing and unchanging; represents the danger of the old language.

factions.name: Dominion's Glass-Tax Collectors
factions.goals: Enforce glass quotas for the Dominion's prophecy-vessel project. Seize heirloom glass and workshops. Meet material demands for the Crown's reborn ledgers.
factions.notes: The Glass-Tax Collectors are an enforcement wing of the Dominion, operating in the Hearthlands. Their seizure of Corvin's workshop marks the Dominion's aggressive resource extraction. The connection to 'prophecy-ink' and 'transparent vessels' suggests the Dominion is building oracular or predictive magical infrastructure.

factions.name: The State
factions.goals: Maintain order and control, including sealing the Rust Gate to prevent refugee movement.
factions.notes: The State is actively fragmenting into at least three competing factions, each with their own version of the historical ledger. This loss of unified authority is the central crisis of the post-Remaking world. State enforcers are sent to confront Koren; Vel arrives with news of the fragmentation.

factions.name: Wyvern-Lords
factions.goals: Adapt to post-Ascension economics where consciousness-fragments replace gold as currency.
factions.notes: Sorrath is a member; the faction scrambles to understand new value systems.

factions.name: Seventeen Merchants
factions.goals: Approach transcendence through teaching and ritual at the Drowned Cathedrals; change reality upon completion.
factions.notes: Taught by Dren and bone-singers in the Elder Tongue; their completion will trigger world-scale consequences; may attract dragon-guardians like Skarath.

factions.name: Fae
factions.goals: Maintain the pre-human boundary, preserve the fae realm, protect refugees and those bound to ancient oaths.
factions.notes: The fae appear to operate on ancient law and bargain-ethics. They actively recruit human guardians (like Sorenna) to maintain the boundary.

factions.name: merfolk
factions.goals: Recovery or protection of pre-Ascension water. May be seeking to preserve what came before or to prevent misuse of pre-Ascension artifacts.
factions.notes: Organized enough to send emissaries, burn warning ships, and enforce covenants. Represent one of the few entities with direct power in the post-Ascension world.

factions.name: Bone-Carvers (Criminalized Apprentices)
factions.goals: Continue practicing bone-carving craft despite Dominion criminalization; preserve pre-Remaking knowledge and techniques.
factions.notes: Three bone-carver apprentices joined Meira in creating the singing glass ledger. Their craft has been criminalized by the Dominion, suggesting the government views bone-carving as either subversive, obsolete, or culturally dangerous. The apprentices' willingness to work with Meira suggests they are seeking new contexts in which to practice their skills.

factions.name: Dominion (glass-tax collectors)
factions.goals: Enforce Dominion ledger-laws requiring craftspeople to melt ordinary glass into consciousness-vessels as taxation. Maintain state control over craft and memory.
factions.notes: Niel Coldwater and Messa work in defiance of these collectors. Auditor Vel's arrival suggests possible internal changes or cracks in enforcement.

factions.name: Bone-Singers (Criminalized)
factions.goals: Preserve the Elder Tongue and bone-singing practice. Restore identity and emotional truth to refugees and consciousness-fragment survivors. Resist Dominion auditor enforcement.
factions.leader: unknown; Thael implied as master practitioner
factions.notes: Bone-singers operate underground across the Dominion. The young bone-singer sent by the Council of Depths suggests a formal alliance between bone-singers and inter-realm councils.

factions.name: Deep-dweller Realms
factions.goals: Maintain treaties with surface kingdoms. Preserve the seal between abyss and surface. Adapt to new covenant methods.
factions.notes: Negotiated nine treaties with surface kingdoms. Seeking alternatives to singular sacrifice. Working with refugees to create counter-frequency covenants.

factions.name: Merfolk Crown
factions.goals: Ensure that oaths are kept and debts are paid across generations. Protect the sacred pearl and the first song of the Drowned God. Maintain merfolk sovereignty and the boundary-wars.
factions.notes: The Merfolk Crown operates by an absolute oath-law: debts compound and cannot be forgiven, only superseded. They pursue Vess's family debt across seven generations without compromise. They are willing to accept Vess's radical sacrifice, suggesting a merciful (if rigid) interpretation of oath-law.

factions.name: Council of Thane-Lords
factions.goals: Unify the Kingdom of Kethral under a single ruler through an unbreakable crown forged by Torven Ironfoot.
factions.notes: The council rules through consensus but lacks true unity; their demand for an unbreakable crown signals their appetite for singular power. Whether they understand the warning implicit in the warking-spears' shattering remains unclear.

factions.name: Dominion (Cartographic Division)
factions.goals: Map and control knowledge of the post-Remaking world; maintain monopoly on geographical and magical data.
factions.notes: Henryk was an apprentice cartographer for them; they employed him to map the Bleached Wastes. Seven of their maps show identical collapse sequences, suggesting either profound knowledge or deliberate falsification.

factions.name: Bone-Singers (Underground Network)
factions.goals: Maintain bone-singing practice and train new apprentices despite apparent legal suppression or persecution.
factions.notes: Organized enough to maintain hidden workshops and select apprentices, but clandestine enough that Naela's operation runs beneath Keshtir's streets. May be part of a larger network.

factions.name: The Dominion's Auditors
factions.goals: Maintain control over knowledge, crafts, and memory through ledger-magic; enforce the State's authority and suppress unauthorized arts.
factions.leader: Unknown hierarchy above the Auditor in Keshtir
factions.notes: The auditors' failure to burn the Lampwick Quarter suggests they have limits or are constrained by higher authority.

factions.name: The Shadow-Markets of Keshtir
factions.goals: Circulate forbidden crafts, knowledge, and materials beyond Dominion auditor reach; support artisans and resistance elements.
factions.notes: These markets now trade in weapon-glass and represent a growing nexus of anti-Dominion activity.

factions.name: The Deepkin (Council of Depths)
factions.goals: Maintain the deepkin-surface covenant; protect the deepkin realms and the boundary against the waking Drowned God.
factions.leader: Unknown; negotiated through the Deepkin Keeper of Pearls
factions.notes: The deepkin are bound to Verdant Strand through Vessa's marriage; their interests may diverge if the Drowned God's awakening threatens the realm.

factions.name: The Ironbound
factions.goals: Originally: loyalty to Kethran through conquest and payment. Post-Remaking: survival and finding work.
factions.notes: Legendary war-band that drifted apart when Remaking destroyed Kethran's ability to pay them. Some died; some deserted; some turned to brigandage. Those who followed Kethran to Pelimor were rehired as guards or found peace in refugee camps. Some never forgave the shift from conquest to mutual aid.

factions.name: Dominion Collectors
factions.goals: Erase forbidden knowledge. Hunt and capture knowledge-keepers. Prevent restoration of lost crafts.
factions.notes: Grey-cloaked operatives sent to capture Kaiden and his apprentices. Failed to prevent the resonance escape. Represent Dominion authority to suppress knowledge. Presence in Anthrim suggests systematic hunting of knowledge-keepers.

factions.name: human resistance
factions.goals: Oppose the new regime; form alliances with dragonkind and other species to resist tyranny.
factions.notes: The Counter-Archive serves as a headquarters; Sorenne's role as liaison suggests coordinated diplomatic and military strategy.

factions.name: new regime
factions.goals: Consolidate power; recapture or neutralize threats like the Wyvern.
factions.notes: Emerged after the Remaking displaced the sorcerer-king; sends traders to trap the Wyvern, suggesting systematic effort to eliminate resistance.

factions.name: Deepkin Tidecall
factions.goals: Maintain the boundary-waters seal; honor treaties between deepkin and surface realms; defend against abyss rupture.
factions.notes: A naval or military faction within the Council of Depths. Merith is the daughter of the last true treaty-maker, suggesting historical diplomatic importance. The Tidecall is positioned as defender of the boundary.

factions.name: Displaced Folk
factions.goals: Survival. Seeking refuge. Bearing witness to loss and contradiction.
factions.notes: Displaced folk are mentioned in Kess's story as sources of contradictory testimony. They appear to be refugees or exiles created by the Shattering, whose voices Kess collects and weaponizes.

factions.name: displaced folk scribes
factions.goals: Distribute counter-ledgers and shadow-copies documenting erased histories; preserve memory of the dead and forgotten.
factions.notes: They arrive at Liv's cellar each night via different routes, no pattern, avoiding Hegemony detection. Organized network of resistance archivists.

factions.name: glass-workers
factions.goals: Preserve old songs, ancient frequencies, and the memory of craft. Create weapon-glass for the next iteration. Resist Hegemony monopoly on truth.
factions.notes: At least four glass-workers gather at the Wailing Scar with maps drawn in starlight-memory. They believe craft and song matter more than military victory.

factions.name: singers
factions.goals: Guide consciousness-shards toward meaning rather than dissolution. Work with fragment-hoarders like Sorrath to preserve and integrate shattered consciousness.
factions.notes: Implied to work with Sorrath; teach the value of consciousness fragments. Likely overlap with bone-singers.

factions.name: Fragment-Gatherers' Council
factions.goals: Gather consciousness-shards (originally as treasure; potentially redirected toward sanctuary by Sorrath's proposal).
factions.notes: Wyvern-led; based in Ironspine Mountains; subject to Sorrath's revolutionary proposal; may be shifting from acquisitiveness to protection.

factions.name: The Wardens
factions.goals: Suppress consciousness-shard awakening; persecute bone-singers; enforce existing consciousness-commodity hierarchies; prevent 'purification' threats.
factions.notes: Official or institutional enforcement body; threaten to burn hidden catacombs and kill bone-singers; represent existing power structures opposed to consciousness liberation.

factions.name: Shadow-Markets (as faction)
factions.goals: Trade consciousness-shards as commodities; profit from post-Shattering scarcity.
factions.notes: Organized black-market network; source of consciousness-shards for Naela's awakening work; implies wider criminal infrastructure.

factions.name: Glass-Makers
factions.goals: To forge weapon-glass immune to ledger-magic. To resist Dominion control through material resistance.
factions.notes: The glass-makers are organized enough to hide refugees like Torvin's daughter. They are being actively hunted by Dominion auditors. Their weapon-glass represents a serious threat to Dominion magical control.

factions.name: Bone-Singers (Fragment-Consolidation Collective)
factions.goals: To consolidate consciousness-fragments and map or restore the eighth iteration of reality through coordinated working.
factions.leader: Unknown; the cloaked bone-singer at Ennor's warehouse appears to be a key member.
factions.notes: This faction operates separately from known bone-singer communities; they are actively recruiting and hiding their project behind silence agreements. Represents a radical or forbidden splinter group.

factions.name: Keshtir's Underground Administration
factions.goals: To maintain control of Keshtir's power through three competing secret ledgers.
factions.notes: The three competing versions each claim true authority. The phantom-ledger prophecy suggests this administration is weeks from collapse.

factions.name: bone-singers and refugee scribes
factions.goals: Preserve fragments of pre-Remaking knowledge and maps.
factions.notes: These groups have preserved the eighth iteration's maps and are actively studying the patterns of world-iteration and cartographic truth.

factions.name: wyverns (consciousness-fragment hoarders)
factions.goals: Hoard consciousness-fragments; maintain or expand market control; dominate shadow-broker networks.
factions.notes: Wyverns are depicted as sentient entities capable of economic competition and theft. Their role in post-fragment-liberation society remains unclear.

factions.name: Refugee Scribes
factions.goals: Document the histories and identities of the displaced. Preserve knowledge in the Counter-Archive.
factions.notes: Vex intends to trade consciousness-fragments to them to give the displaced a voice in the Counter-Archive's histories.

factions.name: Boundary-keepers
factions.goals: Maintain the sealed boundary between surface and deepkin realms. Guard the covenant.
factions.notes: Refuse entry to surface-dwellers but recognize Kolten's song and yield to Yareth at the story's climax. They may be deepkin or a hybrid faction.

factions.name: Rival Faction (Echo's Followers)
factions.goals: Seize control of the Five-Way Covenant; establish the Echo as the true Merith; redirect the covenant for factional benefit.
factions.notes: The Echo's emergence and factional claim destabilize the entire negotiation, splitting the council and complicating Merith's diplomatic efforts.

factions.name: Traders
factions.goals: Carry and trade consciousness-shards and memories; seek safety for fragmented consciousnesses.
factions.notes: They bear orphaned memories and are encouraged by word through shadow-markets to bring fragments to Skarath's archive for shelter.

factions.name: Dominion's Auditors
factions.goals: Maintain order; track and potentially reclaim consciousness-shards.
factions.notes: Skarath's archive contains consciousness-shards stolen from Dominion auditors. Their relationship with the archive is unknown but potentially adversarial.

factions.name: Surface-Dwellers
factions.goals: Hold boundary anchor; maintain surface realm integrity; negotiate or resist deepkin.
factions.notes: Hold the lost boundary anchor (per Echo-Nerys); implied to exist in tension with deepkin; details sparse.

factions.name: Deepkin Conservatives
factions.goals: Preserve deepkin tradition; maintain single-sacrifice binding practices; prevent heretical workings.
factions.leader: Unknown (implied elder or conservative council faction)
factions.notes: They believe five fragmented wills cannot hold what one absolute sacrifice has sustained. They invoke the authority of 'the old gods' against Thale's proposal.

factions.name: Refugees from the Burned Expanse
factions.goals: Escape the burning surface world; find passage to safety; survive.
factions.notes: The refugees' theft of pearls is reframed by Merith as asylum-seeking. They offer consciousness-fragments as payment—suggesting they may have some magical or metaphysical capacity. Their ultimate fate is not specified, though Merith suggests they will die regardless of whether the pearls are returned.

factions.name: Council
factions.goals: Maintain the consciousness-commerce system; prevent fragment autonomy; stabilize the Dominion against iteration ten.
factions.leader: Unknown; Mesek the Elder brings matters before them
factions.notes: Their response to the fragment's question triggers Naela's defiance.

factions.name: the Bone-Singers' Collective
factions.goals: Preserve and teach the Elder Tongue and the old bone-songs. Support refugees and the displaced. Resist commodification of consciousness-fragments and apprenticeship in dark trades.
factions.notes: The Collective split post-Remaking, and younger apprentices have begun selling consciousness-fragments and reliquaries to wyvern-lords for bread. Marrik opposes this trend and works to free the desperate from predatory bargains through his harmonic gift.

factions.name: the deepkin
factions.goals: Maintain the boundary-covenant between abyss and surface. Preserve deepkin civilization and identity. Navigate the post-Remaking fracture of their own consciousness and realm.
factions.notes: The deepkin are bound to an ancient covenant sustained by the Tear of the Drowned God. Post-Remaking, Captain Merith herself has been split into two identical but discordant selves, mirroring the deepkin realm's fracturing. Their solution—singing together in dissonance—represents a new form of coexistent leadership.

factions.name: Reef-singers
factions.goals: Preserve reef ecology, deepkin culture, and the boundary covenant.
factions.leader: Unknown; Toriel is described as daughter of the reef-singers.
factions.notes: Toriel is half-merfolk and a daughter of the reef-singers. This faction appears to oppose the Council's vote, but representation in the Council is unclear.

factions.name: Refugees (of the Remaking)
factions.goals: Survive, find shelter, process trauma and guilt.
factions.notes: Five refugees are mentioned in this story, sheltering in Thornveil Forest and learning Elarra's bone-covenant. Their wider status and numbers are unknown.

factions.name: bone-singers' collectives
factions.goals: Gather and preserve consciousness-fragments; resist Dominion extraction; attempt to restore fractured identities.
factions.notes: Bone-singer collectives are underground networks that Korven and Isara hunt through to find her brother's consciousness-fragments. They appear to be criminalized by the Dominion but offer a path to consciousness-restoration.

factions.name: Wyvern-Council
factions.goals: Originally: hoard gold and consciousness-fragments. Transforming: recover and teach meaning through ancient knowledge and Elder Tongue.
factions.notes: The Wyvern-Council is internally fractured between hoarders who hunt consciousness-fragments and those discovering mercy and teaching. Sorrath's reckoning may unify it toward meaning-keeping.

factions.name: Deepkin Captains
factions.goals: Each mirror-captain pursues its own agenda following the Luminous Ascension. Mereth-Reflected seeks war; original Mereth seeks negotiation.
factions.notes: The deepkin captains are split into autonomous mirror-selves with conflicting goals, making unified action nearly impossible.

factions.name: warlord-council
factions.goals: Maintain power in the Steppe Marches; enforce oaths and settle inheritance disputes; expand territory through acquisition of legendary weapons and talented heirs.
factions.notes: Rules the mountain hold where Kessara's father died. Watches Kessara with hard eyes as neighboring warlords circle. Represents post-Remaking regional governance structure—councils rather than individual lords.

factions.name: merchant-guild
factions.goals: Enforce commercial oaths and contracts. Collect on debts owed. Maintain the integrity of commerce post-Remaking.
factions.notes: Kessara's father broke an oath to this guild. Truthseeker enforced the debt, suggesting the guild's oaths are magically binding or the blade was specifically tuned to their contracts.

factions.name: glass-makers (Keshtir)
factions.goals: Maintain craft traditions; adapt to post-Remaking demand for weapon-glass; preserve knowledge across generations; remain independent despite increased reliance on consciousness-fragments.
factions.notes: Three-generation lineage of artisans. Transitioned from making mirrors for nobility to forging weapon-glass. Represents how craft traditions are reshaped by the Remaking's material scarcity.

factions.name: deepkin (embassy / diplomatic presence)
factions.goals: Secure reinforced weapon-glass from surface world. Maintain or establish diplomatic channels with Keshtir and surface cities.
factions.notes: Suggests ongoing trade or negotiation between depths and surface. Torven's role as intermediary implies deep-dweller familiarity with surface markets.

factions.name: Threshold-Keepers
factions.goals: Maintain boundary binding through transformation and willing metamorphosis rather than sacrifice. Embody the boundary itself.
factions.notes: Newly created faction consisting of three transformed volunteers and Echo-Kalith. Threatens traditional Council authority. Represents ideological rupture in deepkin civilization. Unresolved: growth potential, whether original Kalith will split from Echo-Kalith faction.

factions.name: Wyvern-Councils
factions.goals: Control consciousness-fragment markets. Maintain hoarding traditions and commerce-based wyvern hierarchy.
factions.notes: View Vrekka's mercy-choice as madness. Called the healing of refugee boy irrational. Suggests wyvern-councils are economics-focused and isolated from post-Remaking value shifts. Unresolved: whether Vrekka's choice will inspire other wyverns, whether councils will punish her.

factions.name: consciousness-merchants
factions.goals: Buy and trade in consciousness-fragments and stolen shadows; potentially engage in body-theft and identity theft.
factions.notes: A nascent criminal class created by the post-Remaking economy. Buy stolen shadows that remember their owners' names.

factions.name: deepkin salvage crews
factions.goals: Salvage wrecks and artifacts from contested waters. Control maritime salvage rights.
