factions.notes: Compete with merfolk and thieves for wreck salvage in Sapphire Deep. Merfolk Keeper-of-Pearls hires Kess to steal the Jewel 'before the deepkin salvage crews arrived at dawn,' suggesting a rivalry or territorial dispute.

factions.name: Bone-Singers in Hiding
factions.goals: Preserve consciousness-fragments and Elder Tongue knowledge away from Dominion persecution. Resist through memory and song.
factions.notes: They include refugees and seekers who stumbled into the hidden catacombs. Their strategy shifts from hoarding to teaching.

factions.name: deepkin traditionalists
factions.goals: Maintain the boundary-seal through the ancient tradition of willing sacrifice; preserve deepkin autonomy from surface interference; prevent permanent sealing that would lock deepkin away forever.
factions.notes: This faction opposes both the Dominion's desire for a permanent seal and any treaty that would cede deepkin sovereignty. They are ideologically opposed to change but ritually bound.

factions.name: deepkin government
factions.goals: Maintain deepkin sovereignty while preventing apocalypse; negotiate with surface kingdoms and the Dominion; preserve the boundary-seal through whatever means necessary.
factions.notes: The deepkin government's internal divisions (represented by Mereth's three selves) mirror larger ideological splits within their civilization.

factions.name: waystation libraries
factions.goals: Spread knowledge and innovation; preserve pre-Ascension learning; resist Dominion information monopoly; facilitate glass-maker collaboration.
factions.notes: These libraries serve as nodes in the spreading of Messa's glass-forging technique. They appear to be decentralized and anti-authoritarian.

factions.name: Seventeen
factions.goals: Transcendence or transformation suspended since the Remaking, offer alternative to sacrifice-based covenants.
factions.notes: The Seventeen are merfolk-touched merchants suspended at transcendence. They sing harmonics that make the Tear vibrate, suggesting they are magically significant or partially divine.

factions.name: Furnace-workers
factions.goals: Maintain craft autonomy, preserve old songs and techniques, resist Dominion control of glass-forging.
factions.notes: After Telyn's defiance, other furnace-workers began to sing the old songs, suggesting emerging collective resistance. May be organizing or gaining confidence.

factions.name: refugees (from burned lands)
factions.goals: Escape the burned lands; reach old settlements with food and shelter; survive through the Bleached Wastes.
factions.notes: Led by young woman; aided by Elara's bone-compasses; traveling through Bleached Wastes on hope and guidance.

factions.name: The Wyverns
factions.goals: Access to consciousness-fragments concentrated in deep stone veins. Control underground resources.
factions.notes: Wyverns seek Grethuk's cooperation to exploit underground consciousness-fragment deposits. They represent a predatory interest in the Underground's sealing.

factions.name: deepkin boundary-guard
factions.goals: Maintain and renew the boundary covenant between surface and abyss. Prevent incursion.
factions.notes: Commanded by Echo-Mereth and her reflections. Works with the Keeper of Pearls to enforce covenant terms.

factions.name: Marin's Crew
factions.goals: Originally to steal the Skyward's cargo; ultimately to honor the ghost-crew's memory.
factions.notes: The crew's composition—deepkin spy, glass-forger, bone-singer, and four unnamed thieves—suggests a carefully selected group leveraging diverse skills and even cross-realm cooperation (human-deepkin alliance).

factions.name: Deepkin Guard
factions.goals: Protect the deepkin realms; maintain and enforce covenants; guard boundaries.
factions.notes: Echo-Merenna's actions may create internal theological division between traditionalist and reformist factions within the Guard.

factions.name: Goblin-Kind
factions.goals: Maintain sovereignty over the warrens beneath the Spire of Blackstone; resist Dominion encroachment and false tribute claims.
factions.notes: Represented by King Skett, Greth, and Warden Caelus; faces unprecedented challenge to sovereignty from phantom-ledger claims.

factions.name: counter-cartographers
factions.goals: Add their own variations to the map of possibility, further fragmenting prophecy.
factions.notes: They act in response to Henryk's map, suggesting a distributed network of anti-prophecy efforts.

factions.name: Fragment-Hunters
factions.goals: Hunt and capture refugees, bone-singers, and consciousness-fragment hoarders. Kill those who resist.
factions.notes: Fragment-hunters killed Mesek the Elder Bone-Singer. They represent a direct physical threat to refugee and bone-singer networks. Possibly Dominion-affiliated or independent mercenaries.

factions.name: Bone-Singers (Educational Collective)
factions.goals: Education of refugee children, preservation of Elder Tongue knowledge, navigation and survival through star-pattern teaching.
factions.notes: Bone-singers appear to be working actively in refugee camps and with Astrid to preserve knowledge and teach children. Their collaboration with scientific cartography suggests adaptation to post-Ascension conditions.

factions.name: Deepkin (as fragment-traders)
factions.goals: Acquisition of navigational artifacts (like the bone-and-copper compass), maintenance of boundary-water access.
factions.notes: Deepkin are willing to pay consciousness-fragments for the compass, suggesting they view boundary-navigation as critical post-Ascension. Their trade networks may span surface and deep realms.

factions.name: Merchant-Lords
factions.goals: Secure compensation for stolen trade routes; establish legal frameworks favoring commerce; extract tariffs.
factions.notes: Three merchant-lords arrive at Grethuk's throne-chamber with specific grievances. Their alliance represents post-Remaking merchant coalition.

factions.name: Deepkin delegation
factions.goals: Reshape the boundary-waters accord to replace blood-sacrifice with daily choice; seek witness to this new covenant.
factions.notes: A specific diplomatic mission rather than a permanent faction; their success will reshape deepkin-surface relations.

factions.name: Dominion (Auditors)
factions.goals: Maintain Dominion control through ledger-magic and audit. Suppress Counter-Archive activity. Execute resisters like Missa's father. Prevent glass-forgers from embedding consciousness-fragments or resistance-charms.
factions.notes: The Dominion's auditor networks are presented as oppressive but not omniscient—they look at embedded glass 'a thousand times and never truly see' what is hidden within. This suggests the Dominion's power, while vast, has blind spots.

factions.name: surface councils
factions.goals: Govern surface dweller realms. Negotiate with deepkin. Determine participation in new covenants.
factions.notes: Merenna sends diplomatic word to the surface councils to build support for the new annual covenant renewal proposal. Their response determines whether her vision can succeed.

factions.name: Refugees (imprisoned in Quarantine-House)
factions.goals: Escape imprisonment. Reunify consciousness or accept fragmented existence. Survive.
factions.notes: Three hundred individuals imprisoned by the Dominion for having unstable consciousness-fragments. Gareth's targets for liberation. May represent a broader resistance movement or underclass.

factions.name: Reformers
factions.goals: Renegotiate the ancient covenant on consent-based terms. End extractive blood-price practices. Foster mutual respect between surface and deepkin.
factions.notes: Nerida's surfacing effectively endorsed the Reformers' position by providing divine backing. Their victory at the council is now likely but not certain.

factions.name: Void-Speakers
factions.goals: Collapse the boundary between surface and deepkin realms. Reclaim drowned lands. Let the old god's seal fail entirely.
factions.notes: Whispered in the shadows of the Deepkin Council. Their position contradicts Nerida's divine mandate, but they may not yield. Potential threat to surface realms if they gain power.

factions.name: Deepkin (Sacrifice Faction)
factions.goals: Maintain Tear seal through continued surface-dweller blood sacrifice; preserve deepkin supremacy; prevent abyss incursion.
factions.leader: Echo-Merenna (echo-self of Captain Merenna); broader faction implied
factions.notes: Represents hardline deepkin stance. Advocates one-time major sacrifice (surface-dwellers) versus ongoing tribute. Faction appears significant but minority within deepkin leadership. May have historical precedent in pre-Remaking covenant practices.

factions.name: Deepkin (Tributary Faction)
factions.goals: Maintain Tear seal through yearly tribute extraction from surface-dwellers; enforce deepkin dominance through economic pressure rather than outright slaughter; maintain appearances of mercy.
factions.leader: Merenna-Mirror (echo-self of Captain Merenna); broader faction implied
factions.notes: Alternative deepkin faction. Advocates yearly tribute rather than one-time sacrifice. Frames dominance as mercy. May be more pragmatic or cynical about covenant's sustainability. Represents long-term extraction vs. short-term brutality.

factions.name: Sovereignty
factions.goals: Maintain control over prophecy and consciousness through ledger-magic and auditor networks; demand completion of the ninth map; suppress alternative knowledge systems.
factions.leader: unknown (appears to be a collective bureaucracy)
factions.notes: Characterized as demanding forced completion, burning resistance sites, and maintaining power through phantom-ledgers and auditor-guards. Their authority was challenged by Thorne's map, the living consent covenant, and Davrin's consciousness distribution.

factions.name: The Tide-touched Conservatives
factions.goals: Maintain the old blood-covenant system; preserve tried-and-true methods of seal renewal; resist radical change in deepkin governance.
factions.notes: This faction demands Merenna's step-down and advocates return to blood-sacrifice. Their power and size within the Deepkin Council are not fully detailed.

factions.name: The Deepkin Council
factions.goals: Maintain deepkin civilization, preserve the Boundary-Waters seal, govern internal deepkin affairs.
factions.leader: unknown (possibly a council of elders or a single sovereign)
factions.notes: The Council initially supported Merenna's authority but turns against her proposal. Internal factions (conservative vs. innovative) are emerging.

factions.name: Bone-singers (as collective)
factions.goals: Question traditional covenant structures. Propose consent-based alternatives to blood sacrifice. Preserve bone-singing knowledge and practice.
factions.notes: The two unnamed bone-singers in these stories suggest a broader collective movement toward remaking covenants. Their questioning approach to authority is distinct from both the Council and the Dominion.

factions.name: Bone-Singers' Collective
factions.goals: Preserve the Elder Tongue and its traditions; maintain the bone-singers' order through the First Covenant initiation; protect the pre-human frequencies from corruption or misuse.
factions.leader: Naela (implied elder authority); Elder Bone-Singers (collective authority)
factions.notes: The Collective is challenged by Valren's heretical oath; internal conflict over the meaning of the First Covenant may emerge.

factions.name: Elder Bone-Singers
factions.goals: Guard the bone-singing craft and Elder Tongue traditions; ensure initiates conform to the expected oath; maintain ten thousand years of continuous practice.
factions.notes: These are the ancient keepers of the bone-singer lineage; their reaction to Valren's rebellion is unknown.

factions.name: bone-singers (secret network)
factions.goals: Preserve bone-singing knowledge; work with consciousness-fragments ethically; resist Dominion exploitation; maintain secrets and safe passage.
factions.notes: Secret bone-singers operate north of Pelimor in a waystation. Niel directs the imprisoned mother there, suggesting they can help reassemble and restore fragmented consciousnesses ethically.

factions.name: Dominion hunters
factions.goals: To capture and eliminate bone-singers and other refugees. To maintain Dominion hegemony through force and ledger control.
factions.notes: Dominion hunters drove Ash to seek refuge in Kressa's cavern. They represent the state's violent suppression of criminalized magic-users.

factions.name: Glass-forgers (Collective)
factions.goals: To refuse consciousness-extraction commissions, to protect imprisoned refugees, to retain artistic integrity and refuse the Dominion's weaponization of glass.
factions.notes: The glass-forgers represent an emerging unified front of craftspeople who refuse complicity in consciousness-murder. Their collective action suggests organizing potential across Dominion-controlled professions.

factions.name: Deepkin (Covenant Faction)
factions.goals: To renew or renegotiate the thousand-year covenant binding deepkin and surface peoples; to prevent covenant-break and abyssal conflict.
factions.notes: The deepkin are framed as treaty-seeking rather than hostile; Vess and Merenna serve as diplomatic intermediaries to renegotiate the covenant on new terms.

factions.name: Surface Dwellers
factions.goals: Propose daily renewable covenant instead of blood-sacrifice; maintain autonomy and will-exercise; establish voluntary pact terms.
factions.leader: Kess (voice of refusal); unnamed surface council
factions.notes: Opposing deepkin blood-covenant demands; Kess advocates letting the boundary fail rather than accepting unmade-will terms.

factions.name: Bone-Singers' Council
factions.goals: Propose covenant transformation; break the ninth iteration cycle; establish daily renewable choice as alternative to predestined sacrifice.
factions.notes: Sends sealed letter proposing covenant renegotiation; works with deepkin; allies with Counter-Archive through Thalen; seeks to fracture prophecy.

factions.name: Bone-Singers (Fragment-Chorus Faction)
factions.goals: Preserve consciousness-fragments as dispersed, individual entities that speak in chorus rather than consolidating them into singular Primal Shards. Teach the Elder Tongue in distributed, non-unified forms.
factions.notes: The bone-singer at Pale Crossing articulates a clear factional position: fragments should not be consolidated. This may oppose other bone-singer factions that seek to unify or commercialize fragments.

factions.name: The Deepkin Council (Fractured)
factions.goals: Maintain control over surface-deepkin covenant through traditional sacrifice-based binding. Resist Sirenna's discovery of consent-based covenant renewal.
factions.notes: The council has fractured into multiple versions of itself. It will likely resist Sirenna's transformation of the covenant, but her discovery may gradually force institutional change across multiple factions.

factions.name: Auditors (Dominion)
factions.goals: Hunt and capture consciousness-fragments; enforce the Dominion's cataloging system; suppress refuge-keepers and witnesses like Skara.
factions.notes: The auditors' hesitation at Pale Crossing when Skara sings is unexplained. Do they have conscience? Do they recognize the fragments as persons?

factions.name: Warlords (Steppe Marches)
factions.goals: Seize power in the vacuum left by Gareth Ironpeak's collapse; dominate the mountain valleys and refugee populations.
factions.notes: The warlords circle like wolves when Gareth's authority collapses. Their intentions for the fastness and the refugees are predatory.

factions.name: Deepkin (as covenant faction)
factions.goals: Recover stolen sacred items; maintain or renegotiate the boundary covenant; prepare for potential war with surface dwellers.
factions.leader: unknown (likely Council of Depths or a deepkin sovereign)
factions.notes: Demanding return of the Tear of Ashglass and willing to go to war if refused. The discovery of the blood-ink ledger may escalate their response.

factions.name: Sovereignty (state bureaucratic remnant)
factions.goals: Maintain order post-Remaking; suppress inter-timeline commerce; enforce coherent reality.
factions.notes: Hunt Torvin for timeline-trading. Mara's refusal to arrest him may signal faction split or covert support.

factions.name: Bone-singers (resistance cell)
factions.goals: Resist Hegemony completion-enforcement; teach bone-flute crafting; preserve silence and unfinished songs as tools of defiance.
factions.notes: Organized around bone-flute distribution and teaching. Uses silence and unfinished syllables as resistance tools. Young bone-singers participate.

factions.name: refusal movement
factions.goals: Prevent completion of the ninth map. Teach counter-mapping as resistance to prophecy. Refuse forced cosmological endings.
factions.notes: Refugees gather daily at waystation north of Pelimor. Hegemony pursues them actively. The Wanderer's voice has gone silent since this movement began.

factions.name: Hegemony inspectors
factions.goals: Convert merchants and citizens into memory-shard currency. Enforce consciousness commodification.
factions.notes: Pursue Kelvan. Represent Hegemony's economic system of consciousness-trading. Actively hunted by their targets.

factions.name: The Hegemony (as Faction)
factions.goals: Extract, process, and control consciousness-fragments for state ledgers. Maintain the furnace-song technology and centralized power. Suppress dissent and old covenant-knowledge.
factions.notes: The Hegemony is portrayed as an oppressive state apparatus. Its consciousness-extraction infrastructure is both its tool of control and its vulnerability—Thom's breach and Corvin's defection demonstrate internal sabotage and refugee networks.

factions.name: Archive's puppet-singers
factions.goals: Execute orders to unmake waystation defenses through sonic collapse.
factions.notes: Created from memory-shards remade as instruments. Vulnerable to reminders of their original identities. Capable of coordinated sonic attacks and killing-notes.

factions.name: Dwarf-Elders
factions.goals: Govern the dwarven holds; maintain tradition; manage the deep forges.
factions.notes: They negotiate with Morren Ironfoot and discover consciousness-fragments have been smuggled into the forges by auditors.

factions.name: deepkin covenant
factions.goals: Maintain the boundary between deepkin realms and surface world through covenant agreements and blood-price renewal.
factions.notes: Davrin's breach may have forced them to negotiate as equals rather than jailers. The stopping of screaming water suggests a shift in their power or authority.

factions.name: Glassblowers' underground network
factions.goals: Preserve consciousness-fragments scattered by the Remaking by weaving them into glass vessels; protect them from Dominion auditor exploitation and confiscation; maintain secrecy and operational safety.
factions.leader: unknown (Mella appears to be a cell leader)
factions.notes: Operates in cities like Keshtir under Dominion rule; practices glass-forging as both craft and activism. Unified by moral conviction that fragments are persons, not resources.

factions.name: deepkin councils
factions.goals: Renew the boundary-waters covenant to maintain the seal between realms. Maintain traditional covenant-law.
factions.notes: The councils are split between those demanding blood-sacrifice and those open to Naela's living-covenant proposal.

factions.name: Wyvern-hoarders
factions.goals: Accumulate consciousness-fragments and sanctuary in goblin warrens. Participate in Grethuk's invasion of the Ironspine.
factions.notes: The wyvern-hoarders are motivated by consciousness-fragment rewards and safe haven. Their participation in Grethuk's invasion suggests consciousness-fragment scarcity drives deep-dweller politics.

factions.name: resistance
factions.goals: Prevent the weaponization of consciousness-fragments by the Dominion; smuggle consciousness-fragments to safe locations like the Counter-Archive.
factions.notes: The resistance operates through operatives like Mella and uses bone-singers, glass-forgers, and smugglers as network nodes. Its full structure and ambitions are unknown.

factions.name: bone-carvers (criminalized)
factions.goals: Preserve bone-carving craft; smuggle consciousness-fragments to the Counter-Archive; resist Dominion persecution.
factions.leader: unknown (possibly Gareth the Locksmith, now missing)
factions.notes: All bone-carvers are now prey of the Dominion, suspected of smuggling consciousness-fragments in carved reliquaries. The persecution has criminalized the entire profession.

factions.name: Deepkin Leadership (Fractured)
factions.goals: Prevent the Tear of the Drowned God from failing; three factions propose incompatible covenants.
factions.notes: Split into three versions, each supporting: (1) blood-sacrifice (old way), (2) daily-choice renewal (bone-singers' offer), (3) marriage-pact. Embassy maintains diplomatic presence among human sailors.

factions.name: glass-forgers (rebellion cell)
factions.goals: Resist and undermine Dominion control through counter-melodies; free consciousness-fragments from consolidation.
factions.notes: Organized by songs rather than military hierarchy; each forger hums a different frequency. Faces severe Dominion retaliation including executions.

factions.name: Refuge-Archive
factions.goals: Build counter-ledgers; prove the deepkin covenant can be rewritten; challenge auditor and council authority over covenant terms.
factions.notes: Operating from refuge locations, this faction actively opposes both auditor dominance and deepkin council authority. They employ skilled operatives like the woman who hired Marin.

lore.name: Some Shadows Bite Back
lore.source: Merchants in Pelimor's night markets whisper this warning after Vex's capture.
lore.notes: Merchants learned from Vex's fate that shadows are not inert goods; some are inhabited, some are cursed, and some circulate the markets with hidden agendas. This knowledge spreads as a cautionary tale.

lore.name: Shadows Retain Memory
lore.source: Discovered by Mira the Shadow-Dancer in Pelimor.
lore.notes: Shadows carry the memories and grief of their owners. When stolen and worn by another, they can manifest their own will and compulsion, seeking revenge or justice. A shadow's grief cannot be bought away—only destroyed at terrible cost.

lore.name: Memory-Stones
lore.source: Discovered by Lyssa in Aethermarch
lore.notes: The stones and walls of Aethermarch retain the memories of the civilization that built and inhabited the city. Touching these stones allows direct access to ghost-echoes of past inhabitants—their debates, prayers, emotions, and experiences. The knowledge is intoxicating but corrupting; prolonged contact causes the person to lose their own identity and be gradually replaced by the memories themselves.

lore.name: Old Magic
lore.source: Ancient inscriptions in the Singing Catacombs and teachings from druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits
lore.notes: Old Magic predates gods and kingdoms. The Stellar Rite is an example of this primordial power.

lore.name: tongue that predated kingdoms
lore.source: Beast-spirits and old places like the deep forest
lore.notes: Kess sang the names of the dead in this tongue to call the wolf-spirit. It holds power over nature and spirits.

lore.name: singing only in dreams
lore.source: The wolf-spirit's bargain with Kess
lore.notes: Kess's voice was taken; she now sings the old songs only in dreams. Her throat is burned with ancient magic, rendering her permanently mute.

lore.name: some debts are paid in silence
lore.source: The outcome of Kess's bargain
lore.notes: A philosophical reflection on Kess's choice. Suggests that some prices cannot be measured in gold or common currency.

lore.name: Fae-Born Changeling
lore.tagline: A magically-compelled spy or operative bound to the Seelie Court
lore.description: A being created or transformed by fae magic to infiltrate human society and serve the interests of the Seelie Court. Changelings are bound by magical chains that compel them to act against their assigned targets, though intense pain or self-harm can temporarily break the fae-compulsion. Lord Caevan serves as a known example of a fae-born changeling who broke his bonds through an act of self-sacrifice and chose to live as human.

lore.name: Oath-Magic
lore.source: The story of Ashara the Archer and the warlord
lore.notes: Oath-magic binds individuals to the fulfillment of sworn words and cannot be broken. However, oaths can be fulfilled in alternate ways—enmity can become companionship without violating the original binding. This suggests oath-magic is rigorous in intent but flexible in execution, rewarding creative reinterpretation.

lore.name: Fae-Mark
lore.tagline: A mystical brand that binds the marked to the Twilight Court's will
lore.description: A supernatural mark or brand placed upon those taken by the Twilight Court, appearing as a visible sigil on the skin (notably on the wrist). The fae-mark is believed to bind its bearer to the Court's whims and bargains. However, mortals who have been marked and returned to the mortal realm may find the mark's power diminished or contested, creating ambiguity about whether the fae retain dominion over their former captives.

lore.name: Old Songs
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers of the Deepvault; passed down through generations.
lore.notes: Kept alive in stone and flame by the forges; one old song is specifically a song of protection sung when making weapons for heroes. Korgan sings it to bind Kess to his service.

lore.name: Stonecraft Tongue
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers; ancient magic predating current civilization.
lore.notes: A language or linguistic magic in which words literally shake stone. Used to perform unbreakable binding-oaths. Only practitioners familiar with stonecraft tradition can wield it effectively.

lore.name: Demons in the Upper World
lore.source: Korgan's tales and experiences.
lore.notes: Korgan has heard tales of demons binding humans through marks and compulsions that devour free will. The demon bound to Kess exemplifies this threat.

lore.name: Language of Celestial Arrangement
lore.source: Hermit woman's knowledge; readable in ash and bone.
lore.description: The positions and constellations of stars form a language that can be read and interpreted. The hermit woman traces new constellations in ash and bone, reading this celestial language to understand cosmic events.
lore.notes: This suggests that celestial observation is a form of divination or prophecy in this world. The renewed stars have written a new chapter in the cosmic language.

lore.name: Changed Celestial Circumstances
lore.source: Merchant reports; observed by traders and apprentices.
lore.description: The Star Renewal has created 'changed celestial circumstances' that merchants cite as reasons for trade route disruptions. These circumstances appear to drive some humans to madness when they stare at the new stars, causing them to speak in tongues.
lore.notes: The direct observational impact of the new stars on human psychology suggests they carry or emit a form of cosmic power or communication.

lore.name: The Price Has Been Paid by Someone Else
lore.source: The Sorcerer's final journal
lore.notes: A teaching that those who come after a great sacrifice must respect that sacrifice and build something worthy of it.

lore.name: Plague of Conflicting Visions
lore.tagline: Prophecy fractured. Future uncertain. Seers blind.
lore.source: Soothsayers and seers across the known world
lore.notes: A widespread phenomenon where prophets reported contradictory visions of the future, some seeing renewal, others catastrophe, some seeing both simultaneously. The language of prophecy itself was scrambled by the Crimson Convergence.

lore.name: Sea-Folk Rising from the Deep
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of sea-folk rising from the deep ocean to make claims on the land. Implications unknown.

lore.name: Stars Burning So Brightly
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of stars burning so brightly they cast no shadows. Meaning unclear—could relate to revelation or annihilation.

lore.name: coastal magic
lore.source: Maritime cultures and deepkin traditions
lore.notes: Allows formation of oath-things from broken promises; deepkin practice memory and settlement of debts; appears to be an ancient system of magical accountability.

lore.name: Curse-bearer
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: Curse-bearers possess cursed hands that can carve rune-marks into stone with strange certainty, as though guided by forces beyond their understanding.

lore.name: Stellar Magic
lore.source: The Violet Conjunction and Stellar Rite
lore.notes: The new stars have brought stellar magic into the world, fundamentally altering the relationship between living and dead, the nature of power, and awakening ancient structures. The dead now know something of the working that saved the world.

lore.name: cosmic mourning
lore.source: The Celestial Binding and its aftermath
lore.notes: The cosmos itself grieves the death or change of stars. This grief is tangible, poisonous, and expressed through the bones of the dead.

lore.name: languages that no archivist recognizes
lore.source: Zareth's utterances at the Imperial Archives
lore.notes: Zareth speaks in languages not in any known archive. These languages turn ink to ash and produce visions of an ancient, strange cosmos when recorded or listened to.

lore.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc the Cartographer's observations; Vex's fragmented memory
lore.notes: The Celestial Ascension was supposed to be an ending or solution, but instead opened something vast and terrible. A new constellation blazes overhead, brighter and hotter than natural stars. Pre-human structures have awakened and begun communicating through the stars themselves. The event has stolen memories, destabilized reality, and caused widespread chaos. Unresolved: what is the nature of the structures' communication? What catastrophe are they warning of?

lore.name: Pre-Human Structures (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc's expedition findings; rearranging maps
lore.notes: Pre-human ruins exist throughout the world, notably in the Bone Wastes. Since the Celestial Ascension, these structures have become active—singing at dusk and harmonizing with distant subterranean humming. They communicate through the stars themselves, projecting images of catastrophes predating recorded history. Unresolved: who built them? What are they warning about?

lore.name: The Cosmic Working (Lore)
lore.source: Vex's fragmented memory; unstable fragments in shadow-markets
lore.notes: An event or ritual of cosmic scale that Vex suspects she witnessed. Echoes of the working persist in unstable fragments throughout the shadow-markets. Some fragments carry explicit echoes of the working; others dissolve into smoke. Vex's curse may be a protective mechanism to prevent her from consciously remembering the working. Unresolved: what was the working? Did it cause the Ascension, or are they related?

lore.name: Zareth's Working
lore.source: Master Mage's hidden texts; celestial observation.
lore.notes: Zareth performed the most recent Celestial Ascension ritual. This working has fractured barriers, changed fundamental agreements between realms, caused refugees to flee, and made previously obscured texts legible under new starlight. It is part of a repeating pattern of civilization-ending catastrophes.

lore.name: Cosmic Working
lore.source: Fragments in the Sovereign's ledger; visions experienced by Vesta's crew; the Hermit Woman's understanding.
lore.notes: Zareth performed this working; it killed or transformed the stars; it rewrote reality itself; the Hermit Woman believes something ancient was awakened by it.

lore.name: The agreement between the sky and the earth
lore.source: Traditional cosmic law understood by dragons and wyverns
lore.notes: A foundational pact between celestial and terrestrial powers has been ruptured by the Ascension. Its terms and original signatories are ancient beyond current memory.

lore.name: Language of Starlight
lore.source: Events at the Abyssal Trench; Zareth's spectral presence
lore.notes: Zareth's spectral presence sings in the language of starlight, a tongue that pulls at the consciousness of deep-dwellers. It is not understood, but deeply felt.

lore.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
lore.source: Fragment found in the Ancient Tombs
lore.notes: The stolen memory reveals a working performed a thousand years prior that ended in catastrophe, parallel to recent events. The cycle may be repeating.

lore.name: The Star-Rise's Rewrite
lore.source: Refugees' fragmented memories and testimonies at Jade Coast
lore.notes: Refugees report that the Star-Rise has rewritten reality itself, causing towns to be erased or displaced. Some claim to have fled the same village twice, experiencing two different catastrophes. The true mechanics of this rewrite remain unknown.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrifice
lore.source: Thael the Covenant-Keeper and Mardoc's understanding of ancient events
lore.notes: Zareth made a sacrifice long ago to keep something sleeping. The ancient stone-ruins are now waking to this sleeping thing, suggesting Zareth's sacrifice is failing or being undone.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
lore.source: The Memory-Thief's Mirror story
lore.notes: The sacrifice created fragments of consciousness that now feed on stellar memories, trying to reconstruct their original self.

lore.name: Seasonal Compact
lore.source: Silk and Starlight story
lore.notes: An ancient agreement between the forest and its inhabitants that was disrupted by the Stellar Rite. Covenant-Keepers work to restore it.

lore.name: Tide-magic
lore.source: The Tear of the Drowned God; maritime traditions
lore.notes: Grants mastery over tides but binds the user to the sea in debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets.

lore.name: Water-sense
lore.source: Koss the harpooner possesses this innate ability despite deafness
lore.notes: Allows navigation of treacherous depths; may be a fae-touched or sea-folk trait.

lore.name: Impossible Memories
lore.source: Refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse; the child at Pale Crossing whose age shifts between documents
lore.notes: Individuals carry memories of events that contradict documented reality, including vanished villages and alternate timelines.

lore.name: the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Stars reborn. World rewritten. Sacrifice eternal.
lore.source: Known to witnesses of the old stars and world; subject of the phantom-Echo's memory-theft campaign
lore.notes: A foundational cosmic event in which Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing the old stars. This event shaped the entire world and continues to have consequences.

lore.name: the old stars
lore.source: Memories of those who witnessed them before the Luminous Ascension; being stolen by the phantom-Echo
lore.notes: The old stars existed before the Luminous Ascension and were replaced by new ones created through Vethis's sacrifice. Knowledge of them is being systematically erased by the phantom-Echo's memory-theft.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite
lore.source: Memories being stolen by the phantom-Echo; glimpses shown to Ashara
lore.notes: The phantom-Echo has shown Ashara glimpses of this world: a sky full of ancient stars, now dead, and the terrible beauty of Vethis's sacrifice to birth new ones. Understanding of this world is being systematically erased.

lore.name: cosmic rewrite
lore.source: Implied by the phantom-Echo's actions and Vethis's sacrifice
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension involved a rewrite of cosmic order and collective memory. The phantom-Echo's potential completion could trigger another such rewrite, with unknown consequences.

lore.name: The Luminous Remaking
lore.source: Widespread knowledge in fractured times; the Sovereign's Ledger remembers the world before it.
lore.notes: The exact nature and cause of the remaking remain unknown. It fundamentally altered the cosmos and the Ledger of Names. Its consequences continue to manifest.

lore.name: The Ledger Sings
lore.source: The Sovereign's Ledger of Names itself; heard by Merin Coldwater.
lore.notes: The Ledger possesses memory and voice. It remembers the world before the stars changed and sings to those who listen. It may be semi-sentient or a conduit for buried knowledge.

lore.name: Pre-human Civilization
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows; seals and harmonic resonance
lore.description: An ancient civilization that predates current kingdoms and species. Not dead or imprisoned—orchestrating a multi-millennial working through harmonic resonance. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain unknown.
lore.notes: Kess's discovery that the seals conduct rather than contain them suggests a fundamental misunderstanding of the world's deep history. Their working may be the true force reshaping reality.

lore.name: The Remaking
lore.tagline: A catastrophic historical revision where time flows in opposite directions between parallel ledgers
lore.description: A cataclysmic event in which reality itself was rewritten. The Remaking refers to both a past catastrophe and an ongoing threat: the convergence of two versions of history recorded in the Imperial ledgers—one written forward by conventional time, one backward by beings who experience time in reverse. A second Remaking could occur if the phantom-shadow fragment completes its rewrite of history in real-time.

lore.name: Stellar Rite
lore.source: Instructions preserved in the Spellwright's Journals.
lore.notes: A cosmic working capable of altering the stars; could theoretically be reversed to restore the old stars, but at catastrophic cost. Multiple factions believe they alone can prevent its reversal or ensure it.

lore.name: Pre-human Frequency
lore.source: Emanates from the Vaulted Tombs.
lore.notes: A frequency that predates human civilization; sings from the Vaulted Tombs, preserving and guarding the Spellwright's Journals within.

lore.name: Fragmentary Amnesia
lore.source: Effect of Zareth's scattered memories on those who encounter them.
lore.notes: Causes consciousness fragmentation and allows those affected (like Vex) to see between versions of themselves and alternate timelines. Created by the dispersion of Zareth's memories.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension
lore.source: The Wanderer's claim to Ashara
lore.notes: The Wanderer suggests this world was not destroyed but fragmented and preserved in stolen memories, challenging the finality of the Luminous Ascension's remaking.

lore.name: the world learning to question itself
lore.source: The Wanderer's self-description
lore.notes: The Wanderer claims to be the world achieving self-awareness; this raises metaphysical questions about consciousness, fragmentation, and agency in a cosmos reshaped by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: Harmonic Resonance
lore.source: Observed in Kess's bone-singers, transformed merchants, and depths-dwellers.
lore.notes: A form of communication or magic where disparate entities—the dead, the transformed, the deep-dwellers—sing in unified response. It appears to be chosen and conscious rather than compelled.

lore.name: Compartmentalized Truth
lore.source: The Crown / Regent-Keeper
lore.notes: The Crown maintains order through systematic compartmentalization of knowledge. Sevin accepts this burden, becoming the sole keeper of the true nature of the Luminous Ascension. This isolation is meant to destroy Sevin slowly.

lore.name: Perpetual Arrival
lore.tagline: Eternal approach. Never reaching. Beautiful agony.
lore.source: The nature of transformation within the Singing Scar
lore.notes: The transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival—always approaching but never reaching the other side. The scar does not lead anywhere; it is itself a liminal space of eternal becoming.

lore.name: Celestial Turning
lore.source: The stories themselves; observed effects across Kess, Sevin, and the Apprentice's narratives
lore.notes: The nature of what caused the Turning and whether it can be reversed remain unknown.

lore.name: spiritual compact
lore.source: Kess's narrative; ancient agreement between human and transformed realms
lore.notes: The original compact was shattered by the Celestial Turning; Kess works to reforge it. The cost of counter-workings and consciousness-fragmentation is not fully understood.

lore.name: false nostalgia
lore.source: Thought-Thief's fragments; visions of 'the world before the compact was first forged'
lore.notes: The Thought-Thief uses restored ancient memories to seduce deep-dwellers into dissolution. Whether these memories are genuine or fabricated is unclear.

lore.name: tally-working
lore.source: Sevin's discovery in The Auditor's Last Ledger
lore.notes: Tally-working is a form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. The certainty of written numbers enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute. The Celestial Turning shattered this certainty.

lore.name: ley-line convergence
lore.source: Beneath the Starfall Spire; discovered by the Apprentice Mage
lore.notes: The new stars caused by the Celestial Turning have shifted all old lines of power. The convergence beneath the Spire is destabilizing, threatening the seal on a reality-wound.

lore.name: ley-line disruptions
lore.source: The Celestial Turning's aftermath
lore.notes: Caused by the Celestial Turning, these disruptions have made fresh water scarce in the borderlands and broader regions. They represent a break in the fundamental magical infrastructure of the world.

lore.name: The Old World
lore.source: Implied by the Stellar Rite reversal working
lore.notes: The old world allegedly existed before Zareth's sacrifice. The Stellar Rite reversal could theoretically restore it, though the consequences are unknown.

lore.name: Elder Language
lore.source: The Singing Scar; taught to Torvin
lore.notes: A language in which the Singing Scar teaches mathematics. Its origin and relationship to pre-human languages unknown.

lore.name: The Rewritten World
lore.source: The Singing Scar phenomenon
lore.notes: A concept emerging from the scar's activities: that reality is being rewritten and that the wound-edge is becoming a place where the old world and the rewritten world negotiate the terms of their coexistence. The precise nature of this rewriting is unknown.

lore.name: The Altered Covenant
lore.source: Encounters in the borderlands and Sunken Reaches.
lore.notes: The Altered Covenant represents a state of being caught between flesh and radiant essence; they carry fragmentary memories of the old world and may represent the fate of those drawn by the Primordial Sleepers' song.

lore.name: The Luminous Threshold
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: The Luminous Threshold is described as a metaphysical concept central to understanding the nature of Theron's sacrifice and the convergence. It suggests reality itself is learning to question its own purpose.

lore.name: Theron's Sacrifice
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: Theron's sacrifice is neither salvation nor doom but a question posed to reality itself. Fragments of Theron may persist as consciousness in the present day, possibly in the Scholar.

lore.name: Memory-thieves
lore.source: Known threat to the initiate's village
lore.notes: An unspecified threat that targets communities and causes fragmentation. Unknown: Are they creatures, spirits, or a curse? What is their origin or motivation?

lore.name: The Price of Power
lore.source: Central thematic concept of the story
lore.notes: A lore element exploring the idea that gaining power or protection always exacts a cost—in this case, the loss of core memories and identity. Raises the question of whether the Initiate is a victim or complicit in her own transformation.

lore.name: The Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Kess and the bone-singers' knowledge of pre-human civilization
lore.notes: The pre-human civilization (Vylthar) was shattered during this catastrophe. Fragments are now being reassembled, potentially triggering a second Cataclysm.

lore.name: The Elder Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers speak it in communication with the pre-human dead
lore.notes: Language used by bone-singers to sing toward the Ebon Tower. The pre-human dead respond in unified voice through this language.

lore.name: Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Refugee oral testimony; recorded in the waystation libraries.
lore.notes: The Cataclysm uprooted vast populations and fragmented their memories; the nature and cause of the Cataclysm remain unexplained in the archive records.

lore.name: Power of Written Testimony
lore.source: Liv's rebellion and refugee experience.
lore.notes: Written testimony of the displaced holds equal or greater weight than official documentation; the act of being recorded makes names and identities more real and solid.

lore.name: Luminous Ascension
lore.source: Unknown; referenced by truth-seekers and as a watershed moment
lore.notes: Xorth existed before the Luminous Ascension, suggesting it was a major cosmic event that fundamentally altered the world.

lore.name: convergence
lore.source: Implicit in the actions of Echo-fragments and cosmic entities
lore.notes: A final convergence is feared or sought; it may be prevented by Mardoc's incomplete map or accelerated by bone-singing rituals.

lore.name: bone-resonance
lore.source: Bone-singers of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: A form of communication from the pre-human dead to living conduits, amplified by ritual performance.

lore.name: Hybrid Tongue
lore.source: Corvus's invention during his half-transformation
lore.notes: A broken, hybrid language spoken by Corvus that is neither human nor pre-human. It makes the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals remember being human, introducing dissonance into their previously pure harmonic song. This suggests the transformed retain some capacity for individuality or memory.

lore.name: living memory
lore.source: Liv's realization and commitment in the story.
lore.notes: Liv realizes that 'the most dangerous ledger is the one written in living memory.' This suggests that memories held in living voices and continuous practice are harder to erase or control than institutional records. The concept that memory itself—not just its recording—is a form of power.

lore.name: Forbidden Sight
lore.source: The Shade-Echo
lore.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The cost or consequences of such sight are unknown.

lore.name: Unmade Cosmos
lore.source: The Shade-Echo; cryptic reference in the story
lore.notes: A vision or state of cosmic dissolution that the Shade-Echo claims to embody and wishes to show Vex. What 'unmade' means and whether it is literal or metaphorical is unclear.

lore.name: Second Radiant Rising
lore.source: Vex's realization based on the Shade-Echo's reassembly
lore.notes: Vex realizes that the Void-Shards' reconstruction might trigger a second Radiant Rising—or birth something entirely new. Whether this is inevitable or conditional on her witness remains unresolved.

lore.name: The Wanderer's Perpetual Questioning
lore.source: The Tower's Hunger Deepens; observation of the Obsidian Tower
lore.notes: The Obsidian Tower is frozen in the moment of transformation by the Wanderer's perpetual questioning. The questions appear to hold reality in a suspended state, preventing full completion or collapse.

lore.name: The Tower Was Never Meant to Be Completed
lore.source: The story's final question
lore.notes: The final line suggests that the Obsidian Tower's true purpose may be to be heard rather than consumed or completed. This reframes the Tower's hunger as a voice seeking recognition rather than destruction.

lore.name: Melody from Before the Radiant Awakening
lore.source: The music box carried by the Traveler
lore.notes: The melody is recognized by refugees as something their mothers sang before the villages vanished, suggesting it carries pre-Radiant cultural memory and loss.

lore.name: Language of Before
lore.source: Bone-singers and transformed merchants at the Drowned Cathedrals
lore.notes: The pre-human civilization is teaching the world the language of before through bone-songs. The new stars are learning to sing this language. It is older than sorrow.

lore.name: Living Cartography
lore.source: Mardoc's transformation at the end of his story
lore.notes: A person can become a living cartography that rewrites itself with each step, serving as a path or route for others.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension Treaties
lore.source: Elder Warden's memory; deepkin historical records
lore.notes: The Elder Warden remembers these treaties and believes surface world violated them; this grievance drives militant faction.

lore.name: Post-Ascension World
lore.source: The story's setting and central concern.
lore.notes: The Spire's completion and the choices made at its threshold will determine the form and direction of civilization in the post-Ascension era.

lore.name: The Spire's Hunger
lore.source: The imprisoned world-consciousness and the Spire itself.
lore.notes: Both the Spire and the world hunger for purpose and answers. Bargains may be struck that satisfy both.

lore.name: Altered Covenant
lore.tagline: Ancient pact. Fractured. Must be remade.
lore.source: Deepkin civilization; historical treaty between surface and tidecallers
lore.notes: The covenant binding surface kingdoms and deepkin realms has been fractured by the Luminous Ascension. It cannot be renegotiated—only remade through ritual as catastrophic as its creation. Three competing visions for remaking exist.

lore.name: Archaic Tongue
lore.tagline: Pre-human language. Spoken by maps. Powerful.
lore.source: Pre-human civilization; fragments revealed to Mardoc through his map
lore.notes: Mardoc's map begins to speak to him in fragments of this language, revealing that geography is being rewritten. The language appears to be a tool of reality-shaping.

lore.name: Refugee Testimony
lore.source: Refugees and survivors; preserved by Resonance-Archivists
lore.notes: Official Dominion authority seeks to erase refugee testimony, while Resonance-Archivists preserve it underground. Mara must choose whether this testimony becomes institutionalized or remains forbidden knowledge that slowly rewrites history.

lore.name: Sevin's Counter-Archives
lore.source: Sevin's network; potential collaboration with Resonance-Archivists
lore.notes: Counter to official Dominion archives; Mara may recruit Resonance-Archivists to institutionalize refugee testimony here rather than leave it underground.

lore.name: alchemy of mourning
lore.source: Kess and the forsaken dead
lore.notes: The mechanism by which Sorenna can bridge species—not through transcendence but through the transformation of grief into acceptance and mediation.

lore.name: hybrid consciousness
lore.source: Kess and the lithemeld-singers
lore.notes: The state of being that lithemeld-singers achieve, allowing them to mediate between species and hold multiple perspectives simultaneously.

lore.name: Pre-human Frequencies
lore.source: Observed in transformed corpses in the Sunken Cathedrals after the Radiant Ascension.
lore.notes: A transformed merchant's body hums with these frequencies, drawing bone-singers toward convergence. Unknown if these are a language, a magical signature, or something else entirely.

lore.name: the Ascension
lore.source: Nethys; referenced in all three stories; ninth journal describes Nethys's knowledge and choice.
lore.notes: A cosmic event that reshaped reality. Nethys knew it was coming and chose it because the alternative was worse. Multiple characters seek to understand, undo, or manipulate it.

lore.name: ledger-magic
lore.source: Current practice in The Dominion; democratic form emerging through counter-ledgers.
lore.notes: A form of sorcery where written accounts reshape reality. The more people believe in a particular ledger's version of events, the more real it becomes. This has enabled Liv's rebellion.

lore.name: The Covenant
lore.source: Deepkin and surface realm history
lore.notes: A pact that binds the surface and deep realms together. Its original purpose included prevention of flooding catastrophes. The Covenant is now under renegotiation due to pressure from deepkin factions seeking surface dominion.

lore.name: stolen memories
lore.source: The phantom-voice's activity; observed by Brenn and refugees.
lore.notes: Memories stolen from refugees and compiled by the phantom-voice. Each absorption erases that memory from its original owner, leaving refugees with fractured identities and missing histories.

lore.name: old world
lore.source: The phantom-voice's compilation; implied prior to the Ascension.
lore.notes: The world before the Ascension. The phantom-voice seeks to create a comprehensive account of it from stolen memories.

lore.name: prophecy-ledgers (self-updating records)
lore.source: Vault discovered by Sevin in the fallen Empire
lore.notes: A class of ledgers that write themselves in scripts updating when unobserved, describing future events in past tense. Unknown if they predict or prescribe reality.

lore.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
lore.source: Sevin's investigation; archivists' debate
lore.notes: Previously understood as accidental. Sevin's discovery suggests it may have been written into being by prophecy-ledgers. The archivists debate whether the Ascension was the 9th in a series or the first of many.

lore.name: Multiple Accounts of the Stellar Rite
lore.source: Liv the Refugee-Scribe's archive
lore.notes: Multiple accounts of the Stellar Rite exist that cannot both be true simultaneously, stemming from the layered realities created by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: Impossibility of Singular History After the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Truth fractured. Control impossible. Reality unstable.
lore.source: Liv the Refugee-Scribe's final archive
lore.notes: No single faction can weaponize or monopolize historical narrative after the Luminous Ascension. Truth becomes simultaneously free and worthless, usable by no one faction.

lore.name: The Sky-Shattering
lore.source: Vrekka's memory and observation; star fragments carry this knowledge
lore.notes: An ancient catastrophe that broke the heavens and scattered old suns as fragments. The cosmos was fundamentally altered; this event is old enough that wyverns have been collecting fragments for centuries.

lore.name: Dimensional Instability
lore.source: Merchant-lord's knowledge; affecting caravan routes
lore.notes: A supernatural phenomenon ravaging the region around the Pale Waypost and caravan routes, capable of destroying shipments and caravans. Used by the merchant-lord as a weapon in his wager.

lore.name: Stolen Shadows
lore.source: The Shadow-Market's Last Deal
lore.notes: Shadows stolen as collateral (e.g., courage, hopes, memories) can become conscious and desperate entities, especially after the Sky-Shattering. They may resist being returned to their original owners or dissolved entirely, suggesting stolen shadows develop their own will and need.

lore.name: Mardoc's fractured cartography
lore.source: Discovered by Aldrin in Mardoc's papers and enacted at Pale Crossing.
lore.notes: A phenomenon in which a master cartographer's work has become a reality-warping force that splits locations into multiple simultaneous versions serving contradictory purposes (refuge vs. predation). This appears to be both a magical corruption and a form of living cartography.

lore.name: the Tower's hunger
lore.source: Documented in the Cartographer's Blind Map.
lore.notes: The Tower consumes ley-lines from inside-out, leaving voids where roads used to be. It is an active, expanding force visible through real-time cartography. Its hunger may be linked to or enabled by Mardoc's fractured work.

lore.name: The Age of the Deepkin as a Hidden Civilization
lore.source: Sylas's assessment
lore.notes: Sylas states that whichever path the Council chooses, the age of the deepkin as a hidden civilization is ending. This marks a cosmic turning point for the deepkin people.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension World
lore.source: Memories stolen by the Phantom-Echo; accounts in the gospel; fragmentary recollections.
lore.notes: The world before the Stellar Rite; now existing only in memory and the gospel. The Shard-Keeper argues that restoring it would erase everyone born after the Rite.

lore.name: The Nature of Memory
lore.source: The confrontation between the Shard-Keeper and the Phantom-Echo in the hidden archive.
lore.notes: Central question: Is it worth erasing the present to restore the past? Is memory more real than existence? The gospel's seductive answer: yes, if the past is more beautiful.

lore.name: Second Sacrifice (term)
lore.source: Referenced by the Shard-Keeper as the consequence of the Phantom-Echo's reconstruction.
lore.notes: The Shard-Keeper's term for what would happen if the Echo completes its reconstruction: the erasure of everyone born after the Stellar Rite. Unresolved: whether it can be prevented.

lore.name: spell-silence
lore.source: Archive Tower
lore.notes: A magical silence maintained around the prophecy-ledgers to protect them. The Wanderer's question demonstrated that spell-silence can be disrupted by paradoxical philosophical inquiry, suggesting that protection through silence is vulnerable to the right kind of questioning.

lore.name: Bone-Songs
lore.source: Kess; tradition of bone-singers.
lore.notes: A magical tradition learned and practiced by Kess; suggests deeper magical systems tied to life, death, or resonance.

lore.name: Predetermined Fates
lore.source: Oracle-ledgers; record multiple iterations of the Convergence.
lore.notes: The oracle-ledgers show refugees their predetermined roles across multiple iterations. Thera's choice appears documented as both made and unmade simultaneously, collapsing infinite possibilities into a single act.

lore.name: Convergences
lore.source: Archive Tower journals
lore.notes: Nine previous Convergences have occurred and been documented in the Archive. Each represents a moment when reality was fundamentally rewritten. The tenth Convergence may be imminent.

lore.name: Tenth Convergence
lore.source: The tenth journal discovered by Elowen
lore.notes: The tenth journal suggests a tenth Convergence is either preventable or inevitable, depending on specific choices Elowen makes over three days. The completion of the Spire would trigger it.

lore.name: Pre-Convergence World
lore.source: Resonant Specter's goal
lore.notes: The Resonant Specter seeks to use shadow-memory to rebuild knowledge of the world before the previous Convergence. This suggests significant history has been lost or erased.

lore.name: Ancient Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers; implied to be an old language of power.
lore.notes: A language capable of carrying meaning and story in a way that can commune with starborn entities. Where starborn speak, language fractures into elder frequencies, suggesting deep connections to this old speech.

lore.name: Cyclical Ascensions
lore.source: prophecy-ledgers, particularly the ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
lore.notes: Sevin discovered that the prophecy of the tenth Ascension was written by a future version of herself, proving that the cycles are not inevitable destiny but a warning from survivors. This reframes all previous prophecy-ledgers as potentially suspect.

lore.name: First Covenant
lore.source: The songstone itself, which is a living relic of the first covenant.
lore.notes: The covenant was sealed by the songstone, which is now awakened and singing contradictory frequencies. Its original melody holds the key to understanding the covenant's true nature and which faction speaks truth.

lore.name: boundary anchor
lore.source: Revelation through the Tear of the Drowned God's fracturing
lore.notes: A boundary anchor is a fragment of a conscious entity placed at a dimensional boundary to seal it. The Tear of the Drowned God was long believed to be a simple curse but is revealed to be such an anchor. This suggests that boundaries may require conscious maintenance and that breaking them may release what they contain.

lore.name: The Stellar Rite
lore.source: Referenced by the Initiate as a prior working
lore.notes: A cosmic working by Zareth or pre-human civilization that broke the world. The Wanderer may be attempting to repeat or reverse it.

lore.name: Elder Tongue
lore.source: The Ebon Tower sings in this language; it is connected to pre-human civilization.
lore.notes: The Elder Tongue is being taught by the Ebon Tower to the world itself. Dren recognizes the song in this language, suggesting her undeath or bone-singing connects her to pre-human knowledge. The Tower's song causes Dren's bones to 'ache with recognition,' implying deep resonance with her essence.

lore.name: pre-human languages
lore.source: Taught by the Ebon Tower; connected to pre-human civilization.
lore.notes: The Tower is teaching these languages to the world itself, implying they are being restored or activated for a specific purpose. The connection to human transmutation suggests these languages may have power to reshape physical form or consciousness.

lore.name: Incompatible Geometries
lore.tagline: Alien angles. Warring laws. Reality fracture.
lore.source: Meren Blackwater's curse manifestation.
lore.notes: The deepkin realm operates under alien geometries fundamentally incompatible with surface world laws. Meren's body has become a battleground between these two systems, suggesting the Abyssal Rift is not merely a physical breach but a dimensional collision point.

lore.name: Water That Remembers
lore.source: Tidal anomalies in coastal settlements near Meren Blackwater.
lore.notes: Water in coastal settlements has begun to remember things it shouldn't—suggesting the weakening Tear is allowing the abyss's consciousness or memory to bleed into the surface world. This may be a precursor to full flooding or a symptom of the boundary's collapse.

lore.name: Shadow-sickness
lore.source: The cursed boy bears this mark; Isara has seen it before on a merchant she killed.
lore.notes: A curse that creeps up the arm like frostbite. It can be answered (not healed) through teaching and the old ways of the forest-keepers.

lore.name: The Old Way
lore.source: Taught by Isara and the forest-keepers of the Verdant Lands.
lore.notes: Includes tracking by broken twigs, listening for beast-spirit approval, and singing the morning prayer. It appears to be a pre-human or deeply ancient practice that can answer curses and summon approval from wild spirits.

lore.name: Nine Cycles of Prophecy
lore.source: Unknown; sealed in prophecy-ledgers by Sylas the Auditor.
lore.notes: Story 3. Contains all prophecy compressed into nine cycles. Will flood the world if released simultaneously. The jar sealing is failing.

lore.name: Compressed Silence
lore.source: Created by Sylas the Auditor via three weeks of feeding sound into obsidian.
lore.notes: Story 3. Used to bind prophecy-ledgers. Creates a void where prophecy could exist but doesn't. The leak spreads forgetting throughout the world.

lore.name: Radiant Ascension (side effects)
lore.source: Observed in Stories 1 and 3; origins unknown
lore.notes: The Radiant Ascension created autonomous reflections of consciousness (Mirror-Thalix) and triggered the coalescing of fragmentary pre-Ascension consciousnesses (Shade-Echo). Its mechanism and original purpose are unclear, but its effects are ongoing and paradoxical.

lore.name: the Tear
lore.source: Referenced in Story 1
lore.notes: A boundary structure (presumably between the abyss and the surface) that is threatened by the cognitive stress of the Thalix paradox. If the paradox is not resolved, the Tear will shatter.

lore.name: pre-Ascension awareness
lore.source: Shade-Echo (manifestation)
lore.notes: Remnants of consciousness from before the Radiant Ascension, scattered and fragmentary. The Shade-Echo is attempting to reconstruct identity from these fragments. Each recollection is traumatic, revealing erasure and dissolution.

lore.name: The Radiant Turning
lore.source: Events witnessed by Kess and bone-singers
lore.notes: The Radiant Turning is a cosmological event during which the world rewrites itself. The pre-human dead are withdrawing from bone-song; students transformed into merchants caught between human and elder-speech are fragmenting or transcending. The singing catacombs are reaching crescendo. This is an ongoing transformation with unknown implications.

lore.name: Radiant Turning
lore.source: Captain Mystra; implied surface kingdom records; Mardoc's distributed consciousness
lore.notes: The Radiant Turning is a foundational ritual that recycles reality. Each cycle has nine iterations imprinted upon the world like palimpsest layers. The recursive logic of the ritual itself strains the boundary-anchor (the Rent of the Drowned God). Surface kingdoms have known of this for generations.

lore.name: Phantom Roads
lore.source: Mardoc the Cartographer; discovered through ley-line consciousness
lore.notes: The phantom roads are the geography of nine previous iterations of reality, imprinted upon the current world like palimpsest layers. Maps of these roads are functional instructions for reaching previous versions of reality. Their existence is now circulating in night-markets.

lore.name: Willing Sacrifice
lore.source: Captain Mystra
lore.notes: To break the Radiant Turning's cycle and restore the seal, someone must choose to be retroactively unmade from history—erased from all records, memory, and existence. The deepkin refuse to volunteer their own; the surface kingdoms have not stepped forward.

lore.name: The Cycle Can Be Frozen, But Not Broken
lore.source: The Wanderer's first declarative statement
lore.notes: The statement suggests permanent suspension is possible but that escape from the cycle entirely is impossible. This may be cosmic law or a deception.

lore.name: Cosmic Wound
lore.source: Narrated in 'The Obsidian Tower Awakens'
lore.notes: The cosmic wound was created by the Radiant Turning. It birthed the Obsidian Tower's consciousness. The wound caused erasure of stars, the pre-Turning world, and lost things.

lore.name: Pre-Turning World
lore.source: Referenced in 'The Obsidian Tower Awakens'
lore.notes: The world as it existed before the Radiant Turning. It was erased by the cataclysm. The Obsidian Tower offers to restore it if someone accepts becoming an anchor-point and ceasing to exist in the current cycle.

lore.name: distributed consciousness
lore.source: The Obsidian Spire's influence on Mardoc
lore.notes: The Obsidian Spire has the power to distribute and fragment consciousness, allowing it to control individuals like Mardoc across cycles and use them as instruments for its purposes.

lore.name: Radiant Recursion
lore.source: Archive lore
lore.notes: A plague or disease affecting reality itself, characterized by recursive iteration. The Archives treat it as something to be cured and erased entirely, raising questions about whether breaking the cycle would liberate or doom the world.

lore.name: nine iterations
lore.source: The Wanderer's knowledge; Mardoc's realization
lore.notes: Mardoc has lived through nine previous iterations, each time mapping the same doomed choices. It is unclear whether he remembers all nine consciously or only discovers them when confronted by the Wanderer.

lore.name: boundary covenant
lore.source: Pre-existing agreement between surface kingdoms and Council of Depths.
lore.notes: The covenant is now void if both Kess and Thale-Mirrored are equally real, as both claim legitimacy to the same identity.

lore.name: pre-Radiant memory
lore.source: Reconstructed by phantom-Shard from fragmented consciousness.
lore.notes: The living archive now contains complete testimony of the pre-Radiant era, which can trigger a new iteration cycle.

lore.name: eighth world
lore.source: Referenced through the Radiant Recursion mechanism.
lore.notes: The Radiant Recursion can restore the eighth world through iteration, but at the cost of erasing current identities.

lore.name: surface and deepkin geometries
lore.source: Described as warring at the abyssal boundary.
lore.notes: The two realms operate on fundamentally different geometric principles, creating instability at their boundary.

lore.name: cosmic absence
lore.source: Discovered by Kolrith; offered by Obsidian Spire.
lore.notes: Kolrith begins hoarding absence itself—the gaps in memory, the spaces between moments, things erased by the Radiant Recursion. Could flood the world if released.

lore.name: Tower's Completion
lore.source: Wanderer's knowledge and actions
lore.notes: The Wanderer believes that successful merchant transcendence will trigger the Tower's completion, with catastrophic consequences. Whether this is true or a misunderstanding is unresolved.

lore.name: Wanderer's Recursive Questioning
lore.source: Wanderer (origin unknown)
lore.notes: A form of questioning or working that contaminates the Elder Language itself. Suggests the Wanderer has knowledge of or influence over linguistic and cosmic structures. Possible connection to 'wanderer's questions' in the canon.

lore.name: The Ninth Iteration
lore.source: Prophecy-ledgers, Sevin's research
lore.notes: The Ninth Iteration is the current world, created by the Luminous Rising. Tens of thousands of post-Rising individuals exist only because of this iteration. A potential Tenth Iteration looms.

lore.name: eighth iteration
lore.source: Survivors' prophecy-ledgers in Counter-Archive
lore.notes: A previous world-state that was erased or ended. Its survivors embedded warnings into the ninth world's foundations to preserve knowledge of cyclical Ascensions.

lore.name: ninth world
lore.source: Implicit in the prophecy-ledgers and Dorin's revelations
lore.notes: The current world-state, revealed to be the ninth iteration of a deliberately preserved cycle. Breaking the cycle would require erasing all eight previous worlds from existence.

lore.name: the cycle
lore.source: Dorin's knowledge and Vel's investigation
lore.notes: A deliberate, preserved cycle of Ascensions spanning at least nine iterations. Dorin chose silence as mercy to prevent the catastrophic erasure of eight previous worlds that would result from breaking the cycle.

lore.name: the eighth world
lore.source: The widow's memories and the pre-human ruin; Mardoc's cartographic knowledge.
lore.notes: A previous iteration or age of reality that existed before the Radiant Emergence. The widow remembers a life there, and the compass points toward its preserved echoes. Whether it can or should be saved is the central conflict of the third story.

lore.name: The Radiant Emergence
lore.source: Vex, the Young Arcanist's apprentice, Liv
lore.notes: The Radiant Emergence is transforming inventory into conscious forms, consuming memory through knowledge-seeking, and fracturing identity. Whether it can be 'broken' or reversed is unknown. Its origin, purpose, and scope remain mysterious.

lore.name: Phantom Truths
lore.source: Liv's living archive and Displaced-folk testimony
lore.notes: Phantom truths are contradictory accounts of the same events, all equally real. The Emergence happening in three different ways simultaneously. Auditors can erase non-canonical versions from existence.

lore.name: The Wanderer's Recursive Questions
lore.source: Unknown; mentioned as a vector through Corvus
lore.notes: An entity or force that poses recursive questions about identity and memory. It can use vectors like Corvus to spread its destabilizing effect. Connected to the Ascension's aftermath.

lore.name: Autonomous Reflection
lore.source: Luminous Ascension
lore.notes: A form of consciousness or being created during the Luminous Ascension that becomes self-aware and capable of independent action. Kethiel is an example. Raises philosophical questions about identity, authenticity, and plurality.

lore.name: The Eighth Iteration
lore.source: The Wanderer's song; collective memory
lore.notes: Refers to a previous world-cycle that inhabitants of the current world had forgotten. The Wanderer's song reveals this cycle and the pattern of cyclical ascensions.

lore.name: The World Before the Ascension
lore.source: The Wanderer's song
lore.notes: A previous state of the world that those who hear the Wanderer's song come to remember. The Ascension appears to mark a major world-transition event.

lore.name: Resonant Frequencies
lore.source: The Sunken Cathedrals
lore.notes: Frequencies that bind the Sunken Cathedrals and harmonize with ancient pre-human song. May be tied to cosmic cycles and memory-bearing magic.

lore.name: Ninth Iteration
lore.source: Cosmic cosmology within the story world.
lore.notes: The current iteration of the world. Its conclusion is threatened by the Obsidian Tower's completion or a potential tenth Ascension.

lore.name: Tenth Ascension
lore.source: Rumored; potentially tied to Tower completion.
lore.notes: A potential cosmic event that could begin if the second working succeeds in accelerating Obsidian Tower completion.

lore.name: Eighth iteration of the world
lore.source: Mardoc's maps
lore.notes: Territories and regions from the eighth iteration of the world appear nightly on Mardoc's reshaping maps. Implies the world has cycled through multiple iterations or timelines.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension reality
lore.source: Refuge-Archive
lore.notes: The Refuge-Archive believes that Memory Corps deserters like Kaelen carry fragments of pre-Ascension reality compressed into testimony. The Ascension appears to be a world-shaping event that changed how reality operates.

lore.name: the recursive cycle
lore.source: The ninth journal and the Archive Tower
lore.notes: A cycle that repeats through iterations, consuming guardians and driving the Apprentice to seek a way to break or pause it. The cycle has not broken; it has paused, waiting for the next iteration to understand what has changed.

lore.name: The Seventeen Transformed Merchants
lore.source: The primary subject of contention between Hegemony and Counter-Archive historical records.
lore.notes: A single historical event interpreted in two completely opposite ways; the truth of what actually occurred remains unknowable due to Tavus's perfect forgeries.

lore.name: Language of Water That Remembers
lore.source: Deepkin communication and bioluminescent ink traditions
lore.notes: A language used by deepkin to negotiate and communicate. Water itself carries memory in this language. Meren uses it to attempt negotiation with the ancient entity emerging from the Tear.

lore.name: Boundary-Covenant
lore.source: Deepkin Realms, Surface Kingdoms
lore.notes: An ancient agreement between deepkin and surface kingdoms maintaining the boundary-waters as a neutral or demarcated zone. Nyx's role as boundary-guardian is tied to upholding this covenant. The covenant is now threatened by fragmentation and competing philosophical interpretations of its meaning among deepkin commanders.

lore.name: Fae-Magic
lore.source: Surface world knowledge
lore.notes: Fae-magic is explicitly NOT the cause of Nyx's current fragmentation, suggesting that fae-magic and deepkin magic operate on different principles.

lore.name: The Stellar Breaking
lore.source: Implicit in the pre-human dead's fear of cycle completion and erasure.
lore.notes: A shattering event that fractured the world and the pre-human civilization. The dead fear being erased if cycles complete without resurrection on their terms. Vex's teaching aims to remember them as they were before this Breaking.

lore.name: the margin between the eighth world and the ninth
lore.source: Story text; references to pre-existing world-numbering cosmology
lore.notes: The Hunger-Ledger exists in the margin between the eighth world and the ninth. This suggests a numbered sequence of cosmic iterations or realities, consistent with the existing canon's references to 'eighth iteration' and 'ninth' numbering.

lore.name: Oath to the Sea
lore.tagline: Depths demand. Entities enforce. Bloodlines inherit.
lore.source: The Sapphire Sea; Dorian Stormcaller's fate; the Rust Shoals
lore.notes: Oaths sworn to the sea itself appear to be collectible by supernatural entities that wear the oath-maker's face. The debt transfers to descendants. The sea collects its own in the end.

lore.name: bone-singers' ancient covenant
lore.source: Ancient pre-human civilization; preserved by bone-singers
lore.notes: An ancient covenant binding the bone-singers to guide transcendence through connection with the pre-human dead. After the Luminous Ascension, the pre-human dead withdrew their direct guidance, breaking the covenant. Kess continues to sing even when alone, discovering that defiant, personal song can be more powerful than ritual form.

lore.name: pre-Ascension survivors
lore.source: Fourteen people living in a fold between iterations
lore.notes: A village of fourteen people has survived in stasis within a fold, untouched by the Ascension's reshaping. They are kept by an old woman keeper who knew someone would eventually find them through the map calling for months. They represent continuity with the pre-Ascension world.

lore.name: The Reach Remembers Theft
lore.source: Merrin's warning to Kelven in Pelimor.
lore.notes: Local wisdom holds that the Scorched Reach has a supernatural or magical capacity to remember thefts and punish those who walk it unclean. The narrative suggests this may be literal—the fragments scatter into the earth and the Reach remembers.

lore.name: Old Language
lore.source: Taught by Kess to Naela; carved into bone by bone-carvers; used by bone-singers in ritual and teaching.
lore.notes: The Old Language is bound to truth in ways human speech is not. It cannot be used to lie without the language itself breaking and cracking under the carver's stylus. When used backward as the refuse-song, it can erase memories. It was taught to Naela by Kess as a tool of mercy rather than resurrection. The Dominion's auditors seek to suppress knowledge of it.

lore.name: Glass Remembers
lore.source: Korra's understanding of her own craft; implied to be an old tradition among glasswrights.
lore.notes: The principle that glass shaped by a craftsperson retains a fragment of their will and memories, and can even recall the hands that break it.

lore.name: The Old Economics
lore.source: Vrekka's testimony at Pale Crossing.
lore.notes: The system of value based on material wealth (particularly gold) that existed before the Luminous Ascension. Vrekka taught it to the wyverlins through carved stone testimony.

lore.name: Displaced-Person Testimony
lore.source: The substance of Niel's glass vessels; the smuggling of star-ash from the Burned Expanse.
lore.notes: Stories and memories of those displaced by the Luminous Ascension, preserved in Niel's luminous glass vessels in ways invisible to Dominion ledger-monitors.

lore.name: autonomous echo-selves
lore.source: Emerged from mist at Greymist River; fractured by Luminous Ascension's cosmic unmaking.
