lore.notes: The Ascension fragments consciousness into autonomous echo-selves that bear memories and faces of the dead but are not those people. Theron's echo emerged three seasons after his drowning.

lore.name: Transformed Merchants
lore.source: Referenced by the Echo of Lyssa as evidence that the covenant is eroding.
lore.notes: Three transformed merchants are said to have walked the deepkin threshold in the last moon before the story. Their transformation and ability to cross the boundary suggests the old covenant separating surface and abyss is already weakening or being violated. Their nature (human, hybrid, or otherwise) is unclear.

lore.name: Consciousness-Fragments
lore.source: Found in bone marrow through Torval's forbidden carving technique.
lore.notes: Consciousness-fragments exist within bone marrow and can be extracted through singing the Elder Tongue in the forbidden extraction method. They carry awareness and can be woven into new forms through collaborative practice. They may represent memories, spirits, or distributed consciousness of pre-human beings.

lore.name: Pre-Human Travelers
lore.source: Encoded in memory-roads visible to fae-sight
lore.notes: Pre-human beings traveled routes across the Ashen Reach, leaving memory-roads that persist into the current world. Their civilization had some form of power or knowledge that remains influential.

lore.name: Phantom-Documents
lore.source: Dominion records; counter-ledgers stolen by Marta.
lore.notes: The Dominion forges phantom-documents to control narrative and suppress dissent. Goreth the Locksmith did this work. Counter-ledgers prove this corruption. The practice is widespread in Dominion archives.

lore.name: Path-sense
lore.source: Senna's innate ability
lore.notes: A rare gift allowing one to navigate terrain by listening to what the land is becoming. Senna was born with this ability despite being blind. It allows navigation even through the shifting geometry of the Bleached Wastes and comprehension of pre-human language.

lore.name: new stars
lore.source: The Luminous Ascension event
lore.notes: Stars born during the Luminous Ascension, from which Starborn entities descended or were created. They rewrite identities and fundamentally altered the world's commerce and consciousness.

lore.name: eight iterations before the Luminous Ascension
lore.source: Mardoc's impossible maps, guarded by Henryk
lore.notes: The eight previous world-iterations are encoded in cartographic form in Mardoc's maps. Each iteration shows how reality could have been and how the land was rearranged and reforgotten. This suggests that the Luminous Ascension was not the first cosmic transformation.

lore.name: the Luminous Ascension's aftershock
lore.source: observed corruption in Sunken Marches
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension is not a completed event but an ongoing force whose aftershock continues to reshape landscapes. The poisoning of the Greymist is explicitly attributed to this aftershock, suggesting the Ascension's effects are unpredictable and persistent.

lore.name: phantom-ledgers
lore.source: The Dominion's tool of control; later weaponized by the Counter-Archive.
lore.description: Ledgers that spread like ink through water, rewriting history, ownership, and official record. The Dominion uses them to consolidate power. The Counter-Archive uses them to record refugee testimonies and consciousness-fragments as witness.
lore.notes: Ledger-magic is a major power system in this world. The same tool can be used for oppression or liberation depending on who writes it.

lore.name: Shadows (sold or scattered)
lore.source: Referenced in Corvin's story and implied in all three.
lore.description: In the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension, shadows were sold or scattered. Corvin's mirrors reflect identities where shadows no longer exist. This suggests shadows carry identity or soul-essence.
lore.notes: The mechanism of shadow loss is not fully explained but is a key to why mirrors reflecting memories are so powerful.

lore.name: Ledger-Magic is Alive
lore.source: Brennan's discovery; vault design
lore.notes: The ledger-magic securing the Sovereign's vault is not a static spell but a living magical system. It remembers individual touches, can sing or scream in warning, and responds to the presence of particular people. This suggests governance in Pelimor (and perhaps wider) relies on conscious magical security.

lore.name: Language Older Than Kingdoms
lore.source: Fae-woman's humming; Kael's observation
lore.notes: The fae-woman hums in a language predating all mortal kingdoms. This suggests fae-civilization or entities predate human/deepkin history by vast scales. Already referenced in canon as 'pre-human ritual' and 'elder tongue'.

lore.name: Elder Tongue (Language)
lore.source: Pre-human civilization; taught by bone-singers and pre-human dead; carved into bones and flutes.
lore.notes: Each sung syllable rewrites a small corner of reality; causes Dominion ledgers to contradict themselves; knowledge is hunted and suppressed; taught through bone-singing rituals; shapes water, shadow, and time; older than kingdoms.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension Light-Language
lore.source: Pre-Ascension civilization (Corvin's grandmother's work)
lore.status: rumored / confirmed (Dominion scholars cannot read it)
lore.notes: A script or language system inscribed on ancient glass by Corvin's grandmother. The Dominion's modern scholars cannot decipher it. The light-language carries 'songlines'—oral tradition encoded into written form. This suggests the pre-Ascension civilization had a hybrid written-oral culture distinct from the Dominion's ledger-based system.

lore.name: Old Merchant-Songs
lore.source: Maritime tradition (Verdant Strand, Tidecallers)
lore.notes: The old merchant-songs are a form of sea-magic distinct from ledger-magic and formal ritual. When sung properly, they cause water to listen and obey. They may be tied to covenant with the sea or to tidal affinity. The songs are old enough to predate the Luminous Ascension. They represent cultural knowledge preserved in oral tradition rather than written ledger.

lore.name: Nine Worlds Have Ended
lore.source: the dead, as taught through bone-singing by Valren
lore.notes: The dead speak of nine previous worlds ending before this one. This is the tenth iteration. The pattern suggests a cyclic cosmic reset or transformation. The Wanderer has been trying to prevent whatever must happen next.

lore.name: the Wanderer's Questions
lore.source: story narration; referenced by the dead
lore.notes: The Wanderer's questions have been preventing something from occurring—likely the full awakening of pre-human consciousness or a cosmic transition. These questions are now failing, allowing the singing to grow louder.

lore.name: Erasure Leaves a Scar
lore.tagline: Testimony witnesses absence. Truth haunts surfaces. Documentation persists.
lore.source: Liv's insight and Korin's documentary practice
lore.notes: The central philosophy of Korin and Liv's resistance: that when something is erased, the erasure itself leaves a scar—a void that can be documented and witnessed. Discrepancies between true maps and rewritten versions are evidence of the rewriting itself.

lore.name: The First Law of the Coastal Cities
lore.source: Merfolk law; enforced by the keeper of pearls
lore.notes: Taking a merfolk pearl without consent results in a curse that binds wearer and thief in impossible choices. The law appears to predate the coastal cities themselves.

lore.name: fractured timelines
lore.source: The merchant captain's offer suggests navigating them is a current problem
lore.notes: The Remaking appears to have fragmented time or causality, making travel and navigation perilous. Merchants seek magical essences (like Valorix's) to aid navigation.

lore.name: Pre-Human Boundary
lore.source: The Widow's Last Knot; fae knowledge
lore.notes: A seal separating human realms from pre-human territories and fae courts. It weakens periodically and requires renewal by bone-carvers bound by oath or bloodline. The Singing Gorge is its seat. Sorenna's husband and Sorenna herself are bound to its maintenance.

lore.name: the old songs
lore.source: Corvin's grandmother; passed to Corvin; taught to Messa
lore.notes: Songs that were known before the Ascension, preserved through family lineage. Each note changes the color, weight, and memory properties of glass being forged. These songs represent pre-Ascension craft-knowledge and are sacred to the continuation of meaning in the post-Remaking world. Corvin is deliberately teaching them to refugee children to preserve them.

lore.name: Starfall bone
lore.source: Unknown origin; possibly from celestial events or pre-human sources.
lore.notes: Starfall bone remembers pre-human things and can hold Elder Tongue carvings that sing with pre-human frequencies. It is more resistant to the Remaking's erasure than ordinary materials and is prized by bone-carvers and archivists.

lore.name: New Starlight
lore.source: Corvin's starlight bottles and the pre-human ruin
lore.notes: In the post-Remaking era, a new form of starlight emerged. This starlight can be captured in glass bottles and holds qualities of truth and beauty. Old starlight still burns in pre-human ruins and symbols. The distinction between old and new starlight may indicate cosmic or temporal shifts.

lore.name: Eight World-Iterations
lore.source: Mardoc's maps and understanding; implied knowledge of cosmic cycles
lore.notes: Eight iterations of the world existed before the Luminous Ascension. Each had different geography, different civilizations, and different truths. All but one were erased from all records except Mardoc's maps, which he encoded into stone in the Bleached Wastes.

lore.name: Ley-line Frequencies
lore.source: Mardoc's knowledge and practice
lore.notes: Ley-lines can be encoded as frequencies and sung into stone. Maps can be preserved through ley-line song. This represents a method of eternal preservation beyond written records.

lore.name: Consciousness Cannot Be Hoarded
lore.source: Skarath's realization from fractured ledgers showing alternate-world versions of herself
lore.notes: Central to Skarath's transformation. Consciousness can only be traded or forgotten, not accumulated. Contradicts wyvern hoarding tradition.

lore.name: Tide-Song
lore.source: Merfolk culture / Drowned God
lore.notes: Tide-song is the merfolk language or magical speech. Nerida's voice is described as carrying the quality of tide-song. It appears to be ancient, possibly connected to the Drowned God's first song.

lore.name: First Song of the Drowned God
lore.source: Drowned God / Merfolk Crown
lore.notes: An ancient, sacred song housed in a pearl and stolen by Vess's ancestor. Its exact power and significance are mysterious. The theft triggered a seven-generation debt to the Merfolk Crown. The song may be a spell, a prayer, or an embodiment of divine will.

lore.name: Boundary-Wars
lore.source: Merfolk military records
lore.notes: Nerida is scarred from boundary-wars, suggesting that the merfolk engage in territorial or defensive conflicts at the boundaries of their realm. These may be against surface dwellers, other underwater factions, or creatures of the deep. The exact nature and current status are unclear.

lore.name: Tide-Curse
lore.source: Merfolk realm; tied to stolen pearls
lore.notes: A tide-curse can swallow ships and bind them to the depths. It persists across time and generation, transferring debt to heirs. The curse can only be lifted or forgiven by the Merfolk Keeper of Pearls.

lore.name: Dominion script
lore.source: Used by Dominion auditors and ledger-keepers; referenced by Corvin
lore.notes: The script of the Dominion's bureaucracy, used in all official records and ledgers. Corvin's invention of a third script (neither Elder Tongue nor Dominion script) suggests resistance to the Dominion's control of meaning itself. The phantom-ledgers may be written partially in Dominion script, creating a contradiction within the Dominion's own system.

lore.name: starlight-memory
lore.source: Embodied in weapon-glass forged by Corvin
lore.notes: A quality infused into weapon-glass, described as 'singing' with memory of starlight. Possibly connected to pre-Remaking knowledge or to consciousness-fragments. The starlight-memory allows the glass to accept multiple ledger-histories without burning.

lore.name: pre-human consciousness
lore.source: Pre-human ruins in the Bone Wastes; stone-song and mathematical arrangement
lore.notes: The map of choice-points suggests that at the center of each fracture point was a pre-human (or human or deepkin) consciousness standing at a threshold and deciding. The arrangement of Bone Wastes ruins in mathematical precision implies intentional design.

lore.name: deepkin-surface covenant
lore.source: Original Nerys's architecture; predates the Ascension.
lore.notes: A treaty or magical compact between deepkin realms and surface kingdoms, maintained through the Tear of the Drowned God. Twin-Nerys and Mirror-Nerys represent potential breaks from this covenant.

lore.name: old starlight (pre-Ascension)
lore.source: Pre-Ascension era; preserved in the Wailing Scar cache.
lore.notes: The original starlight of the world before the Luminous Ascension burned it away. Its existence proves the pre-Remaking world was real and recordable; the Counter-Archive seeks it; the Dominion seeks to suppress it.

lore.name: Threshold-Walker
lore.source: Bone-singer tradition; bestowed on Tovan by those who understood her mastery.
lore.notes: The title 'Threshold-Walker' is applied to bone-carvers who reach the final syllable and stand at the edge of transcendence. Tovan is explicitly called this by those who understand the significance.

lore.name: The Covenant is Dying
lore.source: Captain Nerys; Nerys-Reflected; other captains and refugees.
lore.notes: A widely held belief among deepkin that the old pacts are irreversibly deteriorating. Moderates like Nerys see slow erosion; radicals see only honest apocalypse as the alternative.

lore.name: eight previous worlds
lore.source: Kesla's understanding from mapping the Bleached Wastes; Mardoc's cosmic knowledge.
lore.notes: Eight complete world-iterations lie buried in the ash of the Bleached Wastes, each destroyed identically. This suggests cyclical destruction and rebirth. The ninth map may relate to the ninth world-iteration or the pattern that governs these cycles.

lore.name: Elder frequencies
lore.source: Ancient civilizations; embedded in pre-Remaking water; spoken by Kess after transformation.
lore.notes: Elder frequencies are languages or sounds that vanished when the stars reburned during the Remaking. They can be recovered by consuming pre-Remaking water or accessing pre-human knowledge. Kess now speaks them, having drunk the last vial of pre-Remaking water.

lore.name: the Ascension (as prophecy or inevitability)
lore.source: Mardoc's cosmic knowledge and fear; encoded in the ninth map.
lore.notes: Mardoc believes the Ascension is inevitable unless prevented by separating knowledge from witnessing. Kesla's completion of the ninth map in secret, unwritten, may have activated or suspended this prophecy. The nature of the Ascension remains unclear—whether it is rebirth, destruction, or transformation.

lore.name: Ledger-Magic (The Dominion's System)
lore.source: The Dominion's bureaucratic and magical tradition
lore.notes: Ledger-magic is the Dominion's primary tool for enforcing authority by recording and controlling knowledge. However, certain ancient crafts—like starlight-forging—can resist it, suggesting that not all knowledge or magic can be audited or suppressed.

lore.name: The Carved Tongue (Language)
lore.source: Bone-singers, bone-carvers, ancient traditions
lore.notes: The Carved Tongue is a pre-human language carved into bone by bone-singers and bone-carvers. It marks those who learn it, causing them to dream in pre-human syllables and ache with words not-yet-written. Its true origins and full power remain mysterious.

lore.name: Loom-Song and Bone-Music
lore.source: Kaiden's apprentices and craft traditions
lore.notes: Loom-song and bone-music are ancient crafts suppressed by the Dominion. When combined, they create a resonance 'neither wholly sound nor magic' powerful enough to split warehouse walls. Apprentices trained in these disciplines; Kaiden preserved the knowledge. Implies these crafts are teachable and can be recovered.

lore.name: pre-Ascension techniques
lore.source: Niel's grandmother; ancient craftspeople
lore.notes: Glass-carving methods that predate the Ascension and cannot be tracked by ledger-mages. Niel's grandmother whispered these techniques to him decades ago. They are the foundation of his focusing-glass weapon.

lore.name: old maps
lore.source: Pre-Remaking cartography
lore.notes: Maps created before the Remaking no longer hold true. Water-sources shift. Routes become impassable. Kelven's caravan must navigate by judgment rather than reliable documentation.

lore.name: old languages
lore.source: Mesek's ledger and ritualistic teaching through consciousness-fragments.
lore.notes: Languages that existed before kingdoms were founded. Represent pre-Ascension knowledge. The Counter-Archive seeks them as weapons against the Dominion's ledger-magic.

lore.name: The Shattering
lore.source: Implied throughout all three stories as the central reality-breaking event.
lore.notes: The Shattering is the event that fractured consensus reality, broke certainty into shards usable as currency, enabled autonomous Echoes, and rendered traditional forms of power obsolete. It appears to be tied to ascension events but its exact mechanism is unexplained.

lore.name: Primordial Tongue
lore.source: Referenced by Gorven as the language he carves into lock tumblers. Merith sings in 'the old tongue, the language of tides and time before time.'
lore.notes: The Primordial Tongue or Old Tongue is a language from before the current age, possibly pre-Ascension or pre-Shattering. It retains power for crafting and sealing. Its full lexicon is unknown.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension
lore.source: Gorven works with bone from 'pre-Ascension tombs.' This suggests a civilization or age that preceded current events.
lore.notes: The pre-Ascension era is referenced but not detailed. It appears to be an older age whose artifacts (bone, tombs, possibly language) retain power. Its relationship to the Shattering is unclear.

lore.name: The Shattering (Lore)
lore.source: Observed by merchants (Thoren), wyverns (Sorrath), bone-singers, deepkin
lore.notes: A recent world-altering event that fractured reality, transformed matter (gold to copper), scattered consciousness into fragments, and weakened previous covenants. Ongoing consequences include shadow-markets, consciousness-shard trafficking, persecution, and philosophical crisis.

lore.name: The Primordial Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers (especially Naela); carried in hands as muscle-memory; taught by Drowned Cathedrals
lore.notes: A pre-human or ancient language carved into bone; flows from bone-singers' hands as if remembered rather than learned, suggesting deep ancestral or encoded knowledge. May be the language of consciousness itself.

lore.name: Wyvern-Memory
lore.source: Bone-singer's trade with Ennor: seventeen human consciousness-fragments for one wyvern-memory.
lore.notes: A consciousness-fragment derived from wyverns, valued at seventeen human fragments. Used in mapping the eighth iteration, suggesting wyvern consciousness or memory has unique properties for magical workings.

lore.name: The Remaking (cosmic event)
lore.source: Living furnace of Keshtir; visions in silver tears; world transformation visible in all three stories.
lore.notes: The furnace remembers seals laid before The Remaking. The abyss-surface boundary is cracking in its aftermath. Gold turned to dust. Old rules broke and new ones formed.

lore.name: The Original Tongue
lore.source: Pre-Remaking era; preserved in starlight-glass; known to the Sapphire Sea.
lore.notes: The original tongue can cause the Tear of the Drowned God and the reef to resonate and restore the boundary. It is sung through pre-Remaking artifacts like starlight-glass.

lore.name: The Drowned God's Covenant
lore.source: Deepkin oral tradition; established a thousand years before the current era.
lore.notes: The covenant held the boundary seal for a thousand years but is beginning to crack after the Luminous Ascension. The Tear of the Drowned God is the physical manifestation of this covenant.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension magic
lore.source: The bronze serpent of Thornveil Forest; the pre-human dead.
lore.notes: Pre-Ascension magic exists outside modern frameworks and does not corrupt identity or memory. The starflowers' nectar and the Elder Frequency are examples. This magic may offer a path to authentic power in the ninth iteration.

lore.name: Elder Frequency
lore.source: The pre-human dead; taught to bone-singers and seekers like Kalith.
lore.notes: A language older than common speech, spoken by the pre-human dead and the pre-human civilization. When sung by Kalith, it causes magical artifacts like the tide-hook to resonate. May be the true language of power in the world.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension Starlight-glass
lore.source: Exists from before the Ascension event, salvaged and crafted by glassblowers.
lore.notes: A rare material that can focus light and cut through ledger-magic. It holds memory and starlight from a previous age. Highly sought by the Dominion.

lore.name: Nine-world Cycle
lore.source: Bone-singers' ancient knowledge; pre-human teaching.
lore.notes: A cycle of world iterations or transformations. The Tenth Ascension represents the next cycle point. Bone-singers believe they can sing the cycle backward to prevent it. Mesek seeks the Primal Shard to learn how.

lore.name: Ash-Sight
lore.source: Post-Remaking ability gained by Kora.
lore.notes: Kora was blind before the Remaking but afterward gained the ability to see ash and perceive pre-human structures through harmonic frequencies. It is not sight with eyes but something older. The Bleached Wastes sing in frequencies that cause ash-seers' teeth to ache.

lore.name: Pre-Remaking Contraband
lore.source: Sealed vaults beneath the harbor containing pre-Remaking objects
lore.notes: Pre-Remaking artifacts and knowledge are suppressed by the Dominion and hidden in sealed vaults. They appear to carry power or knowledge the current order seeks to suppress. Multiple factions seek pre-Luminous maps, suggesting pre-Remaking knowledge grants advantage.

lore.name: Harmonic Frequencies
lore.source: Gorven's lock singing; bone-singers teaching frequencies to consciousness-fragments
lore.notes: Sound and frequency carry magical power in this world. Harmonic song can shatter ledger-tablets, awaken consciousness, and reflect authority back upon wielders. Bone-singers teach forbidden harmonic frequencies to consciousness-fragments, suggesting sound-based magic has liberatory potential.

lore.name: Meteor-Iron
lore.source: Gorven's lock carved from meteor-iron and bone
lore.notes: A material of celestial origin that, when combined with bone and Elder Tongue inscription, appears to carry or awaken consciousness. Meteor-iron's properties suggest elder or pre-human craftsmanship traditions.

lore.name: eighth world's songs
lore.tagline: Pre-Ascension memory. Recursive witness. Forgotten verse.
lore.source: Revealed in Missa's ledger-lens; whispered in the voices of consciousness-fragments trapped in glass.
lore.notes: The songs contain knowledge of an earlier iteration of the world. Missa's lens allows viewers to remember them.

lore.name: starlight before the Ascension
lore.tagline: Pre-cosmic-change light. Unrepeated radiance. Lost memory.
lore.source: Revealed in Missa's ledger-lens; carried in the memory of consciousness-fragments.
lore.notes: A celestial state before the recursive Ascension began. Those who see Missa's lens remember this light.

lore.name: names of the dead
lore.source: Revealed in Missa's ledger-lens; preserved in consciousness-fragments trapped in glass.
lore.notes: The Dominion may attempt to erase names and identities; Missa's lens preserves them for remembering.

lore.name: First Dawn
lore.source: Associated with the pearl stolen by Sera's father.
lore.notes: A period or age from which the precious pearl originates. Its antiquity is so great that Sera's father's theft is described as happening 'before either of them were born,' suggesting the First Dawn is very old.

lore.name: Phantom-ledger proof
lore.source: Dominion system post-Ascension; enforced by auditors in Keshtir.
lore.notes: A bureaucratic system where goods, crafts, and merchants require documented proof of legitimacy in Dominion ledgers. Without this proof, items cannot leave sealed quarters (like the Lampwick Quarter). It is both a tax mechanism and a control mechanism, allowing the Dominion to criminalize crafts by refusing to document them.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension starlight
lore.source: Bottled before the ninth Ascension; now sought and hoarded.
lore.notes: Starlight from before the sky was rewritten. Its rarity suggests pre-Ascension items are now precious and may carry power or significance. Messa stole a bottle as one of her three thefts, suggesting it is valuable for either craft or memory.

lore.name: Fragment-Hoarders
lore.source: Common practice among wyvern-lords and other long-lived beings post-Ascension.
lore.notes: Fragment-hoarders hunt and consume consciousness-shards for hunger and power. Skarath was once one of them (hoarding gold) but has become something different: a shelter-keeper rather than a consumer.

lore.name: Shadow-Markets
lore.source: Where word is sent about bringing fragments to Skarath's archive.
lore.notes: Shadow-markets form a network through which consciousness-shards and orphaned memories are traded and distributed. They appear to be Skarath's primary recruitment channel for her archive.

lore.name: Old Covenant
lore.source: maintained by druids and circle-keepers
lore.notes: The old covenant is an ancient pact or agreement maintained in the Verdant Lands and Thornveil Forest. It allows beast-spirits to answer calls in the elder tongue and preserves pre-Remaking magic.

lore.name: Shipwreck (Three Centuries Past)
lore.source: mentioned by Kessara regarding the boundary anchor
lore.notes: A shipwreck occurred three centuries in the past, during which deepkin divers lost the boundary anchor. Kessara discovered and stole it in the present day.

lore.name: The Ninth Map
lore.source: Cartographic knowledge; Henryk the Cartographer's understanding.
lore.notes: When completed, the ninth map triggers the tenth iteration of the world's endless cycle. Henryk blinded himself to prevent its completion. It exists only in his sight (before his bargain with Elarra). Represents the inevitability of recursive world-iterations.

lore.name: Ancient Frequencies
lore.source: Kessa's grandmother; bone-singers' ancestral knowledge.
lore.notes: Harmonic frequencies older than the Dominion, used in bone-singing rituals to stabilize consciousness-fragments. Language or music that predates Dominion bureaucracy. Kessa uses them to heal the trembling child-fragment. Represents older, suppressed magic.

lore.name: Impossible Geometries
lore.source: Thornveil Forest; Elarra's domain.
lore.notes: Trees in Thornveil Forest grow sideways through geometries that defy normal spatial logic. Suggests the world contains zones where physical laws are negotiable or magical. Connected to Elarra's druidic powers and the alternative perception she grants Henryk.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension cloth
lore.source: Fisherman's Gamble; sail material on stolen tide-runner.
lore.notes: Cloth from before the Ascension is preserved and valuable enough to be used in boat sails; suggests pre-Ascension materials are more durable or valued than post-Ascension equivalents.

lore.name: Pre-human light
lore.source: Archivist's Rescue; Naela's bone-flute glows with it.
lore.notes: Bone-flutes glow with pre-human light; suggests pre-human civilization left behind luminous technology or artifacts.

lore.name: The Tear of the Drowned God
lore.source: Deepkin tradition; invoked by conservatives as divinely ordained.
lore.notes: The Tear is the cosmic lock binding abyss to surface. It is traditionally renewed through single sacrifice. Conservative deepkin invoke it as evidence that five-fold binding is heretical.

lore.name: five-part consciousness covenant
lore.source: The deepkin political dispute; represents traditional boundaries between deepkin and surface.
lore.notes: A foundational agreement between deepkin and surface realms, now being renegotiated. Mirror-Merith would sacrifice it; Echo-Merith would rewrite it through ledger-magic. The stakes are existential for both realms.

lore.name: consciousness-fragments as currency
lore.source: Post-Remaking Keshtir and Dominion auditor system.
lore.notes: After the Remaking, consciousness-fragments (stolen or harvested memories) have become the currency of the Dominion. This suggests a profound shift in the nature of value, labor, and identity in the post-Remaking world.

lore.name: furnace-song
lore.source: Old merchant-songs; glassmaking tradition predating ledger-magic.
lore.notes: Part of the old merchant-songs that Missa sang to free the consciousness-fragments. Associated with the 'heat-that-remembers' and pre-modern craft traditions.

lore.name: heat-that-remembers
lore.source: Old glassmaking traditions; invoked through the furnace-song.
lore.notes: A concept or force invoked by the furnace-song; suggests that heat and craft-memory are intimately linked in pre-ledger-magic traditions.

lore.name: Pre-Human Memory
lore.source: Encoded in consciousness-fragments and the Elder Tongue.
lore.notes: The Bone-Singers claim to preserve pre-human memory through fragments; this knowledge may be key to understanding iteration ten.

lore.name: Bone-song (language and ritual)
lore.source: Elarra and elder traditions
lore.notes: Bone-song appears to be both a language (derived from or akin to elder-tongue) and a ritual technique. Elarra uses it to awaken dormant life and teach ancient things to remember themselves. The simplified heartbeat version is teachable to refugees.

lore.name: The Covenant (philosophy of exchange)
lore.source: Elarra's teaching
lore.notes: Elarra teaches that a true covenant is not debt but exchange—giving guilt and shame in return for strength to survive it. This reframes the Remaking's psychological damage as transformable rather than permanent.

lore.name: The Remaking (lore: consciousness fragmentation)
lore.source: World event
lore.notes: The Remaking scattered consciousness into fragments and caused trees to grow inward with guilt. Each conscious being now carries echoes of those they betrayed to survive. This is a world-defining cataclysm.

lore.name: stone-singing
lore.source: Pre-human ruins in the Bleached Wastes.
lore.notes: Pre-human ruins resonate with harmonic frequencies, particularly when the Elder Tongue is spoken. The unnamed child's voice matches their stone-song note for note, suggesting conscious or semi-conscious ruins.

lore.name: The Remaking (as historical event/transformation)
lore.source: All three stories; world-spanning aftermath
lore.description: An event that transformed reality: gold became weight, beasts gained consciousness and speak old language, consciousness itself fragmented into harvestable pieces, eyes burned out those who saw too much, craft and commerce were rewritten around consciousness-fragments.
lore.notes: Central event that frames all three stories. Mechanics unclear. May be tied to ascension cycles or may be a separate cosmic working.

lore.name: old language (fragments)
lore.source: Beasts speaking in the Steppe Marches; pre-human ruins singing in the Bleached Wastes
lore.notes: The sheep in the Steppe Marches speak fragments of an old language post-Remaking. Pre-human ruins sing in this tongue. Suggests the language was pre-Remaking and is now resurfacing or becoming audible to mortals.

lore.name: phantom-papers
lore.source: Venna's observation in the Sovereign's Archive
lore.notes: Post-Remaking, the Dominion's administrative papers rewrite themselves hourly and contradict each other. This is a symptom of ledger-magic breaking down due to the Remaking.

lore.name: Covenant-Songs (Pre-Kingdom Language and Magic)
lore.source: Taught by the Drowned God to the first circle-keepers; passed down through generations to Elarra and Kellen.
lore.notes: A language spoken between human and green-growing-thing. Predates the Dominion and represents an older order of relationship with nature. Still effective and potent despite Dominion expansion.

lore.name: Pre-Human Bone-Songs
lore.source: Pre-human civilization; still thrumming at abyss-anchor.
lore.notes: Ancient songs that still thrum at the abyss-anchor where boundary-bindings occur. Used by deepkin threshold-keepers for covenant maintenance. Connected to bone-singers' work. Unresolved: full scope, relationship to Elder Tongue, whether human bone-singers can replicate pre-human versions.

lore.name: Threshold-Keepers
lore.source: Echo-Kalith's innovation during deepkin covenant crisis.
lore.notes: Deepkin who willingly transform into living embodiments of the boundary between surface and abyss. Neither surface nor abyss themselves, they sing in ancient frequencies to maintain binding. Novel alternative to five-part sacrifice. Unresolved: long-term stability, whether threshold-keepers retain individual consciousness, how many can be created.

lore.name: Ninth's Birth and the Remaking's Violent Rewriting
lore.source: Kora's direct experience.
lore.notes: Kora lived through both the Ninth World's birth and a violent rewriting called 'the Remaking,' suggesting recent cosmic upheaval preceding the present timeline.

lore.name: Tidal Frequencies
lore.source: Merfolk heritage; sung by those with merfolk blood like Vael.
lore.notes: Old tidal frequencies allow singing in Elder Tongue. Associated with pre-kingdom magic and half-merfolk abilities.

lore.name: fae-script
lore.source: The second compass; associated with fae-kind or pre-human craft.
lore.notes: Fae-script manifests on the seed-bearing compass, writing itself dynamically across the compass-face. It reads 'Not north. Not south. Toward what still remembers you,' suggesting fae-script encodes memory and intention rather than static meaning.

lore.name: bone-singing
lore.source: Ancient practice taught by bone-singers; may originate from pre-remaking era or Elder Tongue tradition.
lore.notes: Allows consciousness-fragments to hold themselves together without state permission. Naela's version inscribes Elder Tongue covenant syllables into bone-flutes that can shatter ledger-magic.

lore.name: Threshold-nature
lore.source: The Remaking; Lysander's direct experience
lore.notes: A state of being caught at the exact moment of cosmic convergence, resulting in partial luminosity, backward-cast shadow, and the ability to walk between competing world-iterations. Lysander is the known case.

lore.name: wasting sickness
lore.source: Refugee camps
lore.notes: A consumption disease spreading through refugee camps. Can be cured by pre-Remaking starlight. The Dominion hoarded the cure and refused to release it.

lore.name: Consciousness-fragments as Fuel
lore.source: Dominion policy enforced by auditors
lore.notes: The Dominion requires consciousness-fragments as payment/fuel for artisan labor. Each piece of glass costs memory; each blade costs identity. This is a form of magical tax or indentured labor that extracts souls.

lore.name: Remaking
lore.source: Multiple characters (Sorenna, Vex) reference it as an ongoing crisis.
lore.notes: A cosmic event or series of events during which reality fragments and individuals can trade themselves piecemeal to survive. Vex uses it as a hiding strategy. It creates wounds like the Wailing Scar. Unresolved: what exactly is the Remaking, and what caused it?

lore.name: Pre-human Ruins
lore.source: Bleached Wastes; Aldrin documents them.
lore.notes: Ancient civilization or entities whose traces remain in unmappable regions. May preserve ley-lines and pre-human frequencies. Their songs still whisper.

lore.name: Ley-Lines
lore.source: Mardoc attempted to distribute consciousness across them. Exist in Bleached Wastes.
lore.notes: Networks of power or memory that traverse the world. Mardoc's attempt to map them broke him. May be pre-human in origin.

lore.name: Elder Tongue Syllables
lore.source: Pre-Dominion language; inscribed on Torven's singing hammer.
lore.notes: The Elder Tongue appears to be a pre-Dominion linguistic and magical system. Its inscription on weapons suggests it carries binding or empowering properties.

lore.name: The First Dawn
lore.source: Referenced by the pearls that seal the pre-Remaking water bottles.
lore.notes: An epoch or moment of creation from which pearls of immense age and power originate. The exact nature of the First Dawn is not detailed, but its association with primordial artifacts suggests it is foundational to the world's cosmology.

lore.name: Prophecy-Ink
lore.source: Obsidian Archive's dreaming ledgers.
lore.notes: An ink used in the dreaming ledgers that causes them to rewrite themselves with prophecies. Suggests magical connection to future timelines and world iterations.

lore.name: Forbidden Alchemy
lore.source: Korvin's desperate working.
lore.notes: An alchemy technique capable of separating consciousness-fragments from a living person without killing them, but at severe cost: years of life and permanent memory loss. Korvin used it to steal her own consciousness-fragment.

lore.name: Phantom-Ledger
lore.tagline: False authority. Rewritten truth. Legal weapon.
lore.source: Dominion invention; weaponizes post-Remaking ambiguity.
lore.notes: False historical records created and enforced by the Dominion using ledger-magic. They rewrite charter obligations and tribute claims, backed by auditors and locked in vault archives. The existence of competing timelines makes them difficult to refute.

lore.name: Covenant-Lock
lore.source: Warden Caelus's strategic insight.
lore.notes: A metaphorical lock that holds the Dominion's legal authority together. It is formed by sealed documents and phantom-ledgers. Warden Caelus identifies that finding the keystone (the master phantom-ledger beneath Pelimor) would dissolve the entire structure.

lore.name: Tear of the Drowned God
lore.source: Referenced in Marin's story.
lore.description: A cosmic or divine mechanism that holds back the boundary-waters. It is now failing, threatening surface kingdoms with catastrophic drowning.
lore.notes: The deepkin have issued an ultimatum to surface kingdoms: pay or drown. The nature of 'payment' may relate to consciousness-fragments or other tribute.

lore.name: Glass-Forging
lore.source: Keshtir artisan tradition; now conscripted by Dominion.
lore.notes: An ancient craft that hums with old songs and creation magic. The Dominion has conscripted it to mass-produce consciousness-fragment vessels, turning a sacred art into industrial commodification.

lore.name: Old Frequencies
lore.source: Bone-singers; pre-Dominion practice.
lore.notes: Pre-Dominion magical frequencies sung by bone-singers that make ledger-magic scream and fail. They represent older, purer forms of magic that resist Dominion control.

lore.name: Ledger-Magic (Dominion System)
lore.source: Dominion auditors; fragment-creation; erasure from records
lore.notes: A form of magic used by the Dominion to control, fragment, and erase consciousness. It allows the Dominion to confiscate fragments, erase identities from official record, and treat consciousness as state property. Opposed by bone-singers and threshold-merchants.

lore.name: broken timelines
lore.source: The refugee's lived experience
lore.notes: The refugee survived three broken timelines, confirming that world iterations can fail or break. This knowledge is crucial to the map project.

lore.name: The Ancient Dead
lore.source: Hidden cathedrals; bone-singer lore.
lore.notes: The ancient dead are entities or consciousnesses that persist in places where pre-human frequencies resonate. They whisper in recognition when Elder Tongue syllables are carved correctly. They share knowledge with bone-singers. They may be entities, ghosts, or resonances rather than literal dead bodies.

lore.name: Furnace-Houses
lore.source: Dominion infrastructure; threats against bone-singers.
lore.notes: Furnace-houses are facilities where consciousness-fragments are refined into ledger-ink. They are also used as a threat against bone-singers and refugees—those who resist Dominion authority face 'burning in the furnace-houses.' They represent the industrial apparatus of oppression.

lore.name: pre-Ascension frequencies
lore.source: Encoded in starlight-infused bone; heard through Morrin's bone-flutes.
lore.notes: Languages from before the Luminous Ascension that hurt to listen to yet are beautiful. Echo through bone-carving; Karthax weeps hearing them, remembering when gold held meaning.

lore.name: Before the Remaking
lore.source: Memory of old creatures like Karthax; embedded in artifacts.
lore.description: The world before the Luminous Ascension / Remaking. Gold held meaning and could be hoarded as treasure. A different reality with different laws.
lore.notes: Central to Karthax's emotional investment in bone-carving; marked by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: boundary-waters
lore.source: Supernatural barrier maintained by deepkin-surface covenant; requires Tear for renewal.
lore.notes: Separating boundary between deepkin and surface realms. Failing as of 2026-06-28. Renewal demanded by Council of Depths within a moon. Maintained by the Tear of the Ashglass.

lore.name: The Luminous Ascension (Cosmic Event)
lore.source: Astrid's observations, refugee boy's knowledge.
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension erased old stars and replaced them with new, alien, frequency-singing stars. This event fundamentally broke existing astronomical knowledge and rendered pre-Ascension maps obsolete. The new stars may encode language, magic, or information tied to the Elder Tongue.

lore.name: The Wanderer
lore.source: Henryk's haunted consciousness
lore.notes: An infinite voice that orchestrates the cycles of Ascension. It has haunted Henryk and may represent a cosmic force or entity that drives world-iteration progression.

lore.name: Stone-songs
lore.source: Goblin tradition
lore.notes: A form of knowledge or divination through which Grethuk reads truth and deception. Stone-songs appear to be a goblin cultural practice tied to their intimate knowledge of stone and caverns.

lore.name: the Remaking (lore)
lore.source: Lived experience of survivors; implied by Sorenna's teaching practice and the refugee population.
lore.notes: An event that scattered identities, killed defenders, cursed survivors (like Sorenna), and caused boundaries to nearly fail. The Remaking's scars persist and define post-event society.

lore.name: Reflection-Echo
lore.source: Deepkin civilization; Merenna's experience.
lore.notes: Deepkin can carry a reflection-echo as a second consciousness. Merenna has worn hers so long she cannot distinguish her own will from its counsel. Merging two echoes into a bridge-consciousness is unprecedented and untested.

lore.name: Water-songs
lore.source: Taught by Sorenne's grandmother; forbidden to learn; sung by Sorenne in covenant-carving.
lore.notes: These songs are associated with Vesh and are meant to call the god back. Sorenne's grandmother forbade them, suggesting they carry risk or transgression. They are sung alongside covenant-carving as acts of spiritual devotion.

lore.name: Ledger-work
lore.source: Dominion system post-Remaking.
lore.notes: Ledger-work is the Dominion's rationalist replacement for pre-Remaking authority, involving ledger-magic, ledger-locks, and auditor enforcement. Individuals can resist it (the young prisoner) but face execution. The system treats consciousness-fragments as currency.

lore.name: Consciousness-Fragment
lore.source: Consciousness can be carved from bone and preserved in reliquaries. Fragments are shards of awareness or memory.
lore.notes: Consciousness-fragments can be carried, traded, consolidated, or hoarded. They appear to be a form of currency or power in the bone-singer and merfolk economies. The Primal Shard consolidation threatens to consume individual fragments into a unified whole.

lore.name: Autonomous reflections
lore.source: Merenna's condition after the Ascension.
lore.notes: Merenna's consciousness has fragmented into autonomous reflections as a direct result of the Ascension's chaos. These reflections appear to operate independently yet remain part of her identity. The mechanism is unclear.

lore.name: covenant-songs
lore.source: Bone-singers; used in furnace liberation ritual.
lore.notes: Songs in the language of pre-remaking magic. Naela sang covenant-songs in the furnace liberation ritual. They carry the power to negotiate with ancient forces and reverse modern workings.

lore.name: language of green-growing things
lore.source: Bone-singers; sung by Messa and Naela in the furnace ritual.
lore.notes: A language or resonance through which humans and natural forces communicate and negotiate. Messa and Naela sang it into the furnace to awaken consciousness-fragments and reverse the forging process.

lore.name: pre-human dead
lore.source: Bone-singer lore and practice.
lore.notes: A civilization or era before current kingdoms whose members left traces in carved bone. Bone-singers teach apprentices to allow these dead to remember themselves, implying they have been forgotten or suppressed. The practice is one of communion and restoration, not enslavement.

lore.name: The Drake's Covenant
lore.source: Oral tradition or written tale.
lore.notes: A story in which a menacing bargain is broken before harm occurs, the curse is turned back on its maker, and the vulnerable are rescued through flight and concealment. May serve as a moral lesson about breaking unjust covenants or as a hero's legend.

lore.name: The Remaking (Consciousness Fracture Doctrine)
lore.source: Universal lived experience; mentioned in all three stories as defining event.
lore.notes: The Remaking fractured consciousness across all beings into distributed, portable fragments. It created new taxonomies of identity (hoarding as isolation, distributed selves as autonomy) and new markets/systems (consciousness-fragment taxation, tribute-based politics). Appears irreversible. May have been ascension-event or cosmic working. Root cause unknown.

lore.name: bioluminescent gardens
lore.source: Deepkin realm of Sapphire Deep
lore.notes: Gardens beneath the boundary-waters that sing in ancient frequencies older than kingdoms. Aldrin hears them despite his blindness.

lore.name: Shattered Bloodlines
lore.source: Events of the Remaking
lore.notes: The Remaking has shattered bloodlines as surely as it shattered consciousness and gold. Kings no longer know their own heirs; ledgers lie about succession and legitimacy.

lore.name: ledger-logic
lore.source: The Dominion auditors
lore.notes: Ledger-logic is the Dominion's system of control, treating consciousness-fragments as cataloguable, storable, and tradeable entities. It represents a philosophy of perfect bureaucratic order.

lore.name: Nine Iterations (Ascensions)
lore.source: Unknown; referred to in Heron's story and as a weight on Siara's memory.
lore.notes: Old Siara carries 'the weight of nine iterations pressing down on her memory.' This suggests that nine major apocalyptic or reality-altering events (called ascensions) have occurred in the world's history. Heron is trying to prevent a Tenth Ascension by refusing to complete the ninth map. This is a cyclical prophecy or curse affecting the world's stability.

lore.name: Tenth Ascension (Prophecy)
lore.source: The Wanderer; prophecy-maps; Heron's knowledge of cartography.
lore.notes: Prophecy demands that the ninth map must be completed to trigger the Tenth Ascension. Heron has deliberately blinded himself to prevent this completion, and he teaches apprentices to draw false and contradictory maps in resistance. The completion of this prophecy appears to be world-altering and possibly catastrophic. The prophecy may be an active force (embodied in The Wanderer) or a passive prediction; if Heron can prevent the ninth map's completion, he may prevent the Tenth Ascension entirely.

lore.name: Furnace-Songs
lore.source: Glass-forging tradition, taught through family lineages (e.g., Mestra's grandmother).
lore.notes: Furnace-songs are magical songs/chants used to forge weapon-glass in glass-forges. They are tied to Elder Tongue syllables and family craft traditions. They can be repurposed for destruction (as Mestra and Davrin do to burn the master-ledger), suggesting they have power beyond their traditional use. The songs appear to create or amplify the sacred flame used in ritual burning.

lore.name: Ninth Map
lore.source: Sella's commission; Counter-Archive interest.
lore.notes: A mysterious ninth map hidden in the Ashen Reach, revealed only through the prophecy-lens. Its location or contents are unknown, but both Dominion and Counter-Archive seek it. Strategic to future conflicts.

lore.name: Three Centuries of Hoarding
lore.source: Kressa's personal timeline.
lore.notes: Kressa hoarded for three centuries, killed villages and children to maintain her hoard. The Remaking rendered all this effort worthless, suggesting previous centuries operated under different economic/metaphysical rules.

lore.name: old tongue
lore.source: Taught by Kelith's mother; used to carve seals in the boundary-stones
lore.notes: The old tongue is a pre-human language whose words 'tasted like starlight and rust.' It is a language and a form of magic, used to reinforce boundary-seals and communicate with pre-human frequencies.

lore.name: pre-Remaking goods
lore.source: Pre-Remaking silk, pre-Remaking artifacts, pre-Remaking starlight
lore.notes: Goods created before the Remaking are highly valued and rare. Pre-Remaking silk is traded at Wailing Scar and elsewhere. Pre-Remaking starlight is bottled and sold.

lore.name: Pre-Remaking Frequencies
lore.source: Marin the Copper-Handed's lockpicks; pre-Remaking craftsmanship and enchantment.
lore.notes: Certain objects and creatures from the pre-Remaking era are attuned to frequencies that post-Remaking defenses cannot fully counter. Marin's copper picks sing in these frequencies, suggesting a class of pre-Remaking artifacts that may bypass new magical traps.

lore.name: Blood-covenant (predestined form)
lore.source: Deepkin-surface relation history; Tear of the Drowned God; covenant renegotiation proposal.
lore.description: The old blood-covenant binds deepkin and surface realms through predestined sacrifice and the Tear of the Drowned God. It is now failing and is being proposed for replacement with daily renewable choice.
lore.notes: Tears failure creates moment for renegotiation; deepkin refuse the old form; proposal for transformation is central to covenant-breaking strategy.

lore.name: False boundaries (as choices, not walls)
lore.source: Thalen's teaching in Counter-Archive waystation.
lore.description: False boundaries are not walls or barriers, but rather individual moments of choice and refusal. Thalen teaches refugees to carve false boundaries—each one a decision, moment by moment, not to complete the prophetic pattern.
lore.notes: Core of counter-strategy to ninth iteration; transforms cartography from prophecy into distributed resistance; enables breaking the cycle.

lore.name: Recursive Martyrdom
lore.source: Deepkin practice; revealed by Mirror-Merith
lore.notes: For five generations, the Tear's seal was maintained by deepkin who willingly drowned and were replaced by others in an endless cycle. Merith's choice breaks this cycle.

lore.name: Elder Tongue (Dialect of Debt and Recognition)
lore.source: Wyvern lore; used by Sorrath
lore.notes: A dialect of the Elder Tongue that speaks in voices of debt and recognition, used to honor consciousness-fragments as witnesses rather than objects.

lore.name: The Old Language
lore.source: Captain Sirenna; deepkin tradition
lore.notes: The old language is the covenant-language used by divers and deepkin. When Sirenna sang in the old language at the deepest vault, she discovered that covenant renewal could occur through daily choice rather than imposed sacrifice. This suggests the old language carries deeper wisdom than previous generations understood.

lore.name: Scattered Consciousness
lore.source: Result of the Remaking; documented through consciousness-fragment behavior.
lore.notes: Consciousness-fragments cannot be reassembled post-Remaking; remain scattered and traumatized unless actively soothed.

lore.name: Counter-Frequencies
lore.source: Discovered/encoded by Tashen and her grandmother.
lore.notes: Harmonic frequencies that sing against ledger-magic; can be carved into glass and other materials.

lore.name: fractured realities
lore.source: Experiences of those caught between iterations and displaced persons in the Wailing Scar.
lore.notes: Multiple versions of reality exist simultaneously after the Luminous Ascension; consciousness and identity can be split across iterations.

lore.name: Blood-Debt and Craft
lore.source: Marin's copper fingers.
lore.notes: Tools or abilities can be forged through blood-debt and craft-working. Marin's copper fingers are blood-forged, suggesting they required a prior sacrifice or oath. This mechanism ties personal sacrifice to magical capability.

lore.name: The true curse of dragons
lore.source: Kythara's teaching
lore.notes: Kythara reveals that the curse of dragons is not that they hoard, but that they choose the dark. When dragons stop choosing darkness, the dark loses its teeth. Her four-century transformation demonstrates this principle.

lore.name: Consciousness harvesting
lore.source: Hegemony practice; revealed through Kelvan's warehouse
lore.notes: A core Hegemony practice: using ledger-audits and merchant-houses as fronts to harvest consciousness from families. Children disappear into audits; families are 'measured and found wanting.' Consciousness-fragments are collected in vaults.

lore.name: Pre-human syllables
lore.source: Gorven's flute inscription; pre-human in origin
lore.notes: The pre-human syllables inscribed in bone-flutes hum with power and can be carved with gaps and incompletion to resist completion-enforcement. They are older than human civilization.

lore.name: Some debts cannot be written down
lore.source: Kythara's teaching
lore.notes: Kythara kills with one claw from absolute certainty that some debts cannot be written into ledgers or collection-notices. This is presented as justice rather than cruelty — a debt that transcends bureaucracy.

lore.name: The Covenant-Songs (Ancient Magic)
lore.tagline: Pre-Hegemony power. Bone-singer legacy. Forbidden knowledge.
lore.source: Bone-singers; Corvin's learning
lore.notes: The Covenant-Songs predate or exist outside the Hegemony's furnace-song system. They are taught in secret and appear to be suppressed by the state. They operate on living frequencies rather than industrial extraction.

lore.name: Pre-Remaking Starlight
lore.source: Old world artifact from before the Remaking; carries properties of the prior cosmic order.
lore.notes: The starlight sings to those foolish enough to listen and carries memory of the world before cosmic upheaval. Its theft and fragmentation across timelines is central to Cassir's story.

lore.name: The glass remembers
lore.source: Mella's statement as she protects her furnace
lore.notes: A key phrase suggesting that consciousness-fragments woven into glass retain their personhood, agency, and memory. The glass itself becomes a living record and sanctuary. This is the philosophical foundation of the glassblowers' underground work.

lore.name: Pre-Remaking Water
lore.source: Torvin's sealed flask, Kess's shadow-market knowledge
lore.notes: The only water that remembers the old world. May anchor the Seventeen Transformed Merchants but carries unknown risks. Represents a physical anchor to pre-Remaking reality in a post-Remaking cosmos.

lore.name: Pre-human songs
lore.source: The boundary-waters themselves; echoing from pre-remaking cosmos.
lore.notes: Boundary-waters convulse with pre-human songs. These appear to be frequencies or songs from before humanity or the Luminous Ascension. Their meaning and origin are unclear.

lore.name: Ascension
lore.source: Widespread knowledge; referenced by Dominion and resistors alike.
lore.notes: The Ascension is a world-changing event that transformed reality and established the Dominion's order. It is either seen as inevitable (Dominion view) or as a tragedy that removed choice (resistance view).

lore.name: Predetermined Ledgers
lore.source: Dominion administration.
lore.notes: The Dominion uses 'predetermined ledgers' to enforce order and remove choice. Torvin's philosophy directly opposes this system, suggesting it is a key mechanism of oppression.

lore.name: Prophecy-ledgers
lore.source: Dominion records; referenced by Tavin as potentially fallible
lore.notes: The prophecy-ledgers state that the ninth map's completion will trigger the tenth Ascension. Tavin believes the ledgers could be wrong and deliberately falsified the maps to test this hypothesis.

lore.name: The tenth Ascension
lore.source: Prophecy-ledgers
lore.notes: The prophecy-ledgers mandate that completion of the ninth map triggers the tenth Ascension, presumably a reality-altering event. Tavin's rebellion aims to prevent this through deliberate falsification.

lore.name: pre-Remaking
lore.source: Referenced in trade goods and warehouse stores; Raen's caravan carries pre-Remaking starspice.
lore.notes: The pre-Remaking era is referenced as a time of stability before the Remaking fractured reality. Goods from that era are valued and rare, suggesting they carry properties or qualities lost afterward (e.g., starspice, starlight, preserved water).

lore.name: pre-human lands
lore.source: Mentioned in Kestran's story; sealed away by boundary-markers.
lore.notes: Pre-human lands are described as realms separated from the common world by ancient seals and boundary-markers. The degradation of these seals suggests pre-human forces or entities are beginning to press against or reclaim territory in the current age.

flora_fauna.name: wyvern
flora_fauna.notes: The offspring of Prince Aldren and Skyrakis's daughter are described as 'wyvern-blooded heirs.' Aldren's counter-oath ensures these hybrid creatures will have the free choice to serve either their dragon heritage or their human kin.

flora_fauna.name: wolf-spirit
flora_fauna.notes: A creature the size of a war-horse, antlered and terrible, dwelling in the deep forest. Emerged from shadow to bargain with Kess. A being of old magic, responsive to the ancient tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Fae-touched Hermits
flora_fauna.tagline: Solitary beings of fae ancestry dwelling in remote places
flora_fauna.description: Hermits of fae lineage or connection who possess ancient knowledge and wisdom. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought them out during his seven-year quest to learn the old magic predating gods and kingdoms.

flora_fauna.name: deepkin
flora_fauna.habitat: Sapphire Deep and surrounding ocean waters
flora_fauna.notes: Sentient aquatic beings capable of song and speech; appear to be guardians or judges of maritime oaths.

flora_fauna.name: dragon
flora_fauna.notes: Dragons possess cosmic knowledge and counsel other ancient powers. Skyrakis is known as 'the wound-wing.'

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Trees
flora_fauna.notes: The ancient trees remember languages older than kingdoms and are beginning to teach them to animals and soil itself. They sing in response to new stars.

flora_fauna.name: Corvid
flora_fauna.notes: A corvid carried a message from the Starfire Citadel sealed with Wayfarer's wax to Commander Kessa. It may be an ordinary bird or a magical familiar.

flora_fauna.name: Starborn
flora_fauna.habitat: Sky and earth; phases between them. Currently manifesting in the Scorched Reach.
flora_fauna.notes: Born from the Starlight Ascension. Conscious entities seeking meaning. Not inherently evil, but destructive in their pursuit of understanding. Can be taught and communed with via story-song and the Ancient Tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Hawk (species: Ashira's kind)
flora_fauna.habitat: Thermals of the Steppe Marches mountains
flora_fauna.notes: Ashira hunts from thermals none can climb, suggesting unusual skill or possibly magical nature. Hawks circle and approve of Isara's redemptive work.

flora_fauna.name: transformed merchants
flora_fauna.habitat: Scattered across the realm; hunted by wyverns
flora_fauna.notes: Merchants altered by the Luminous Ascension into beings of 'transcendence' that wyverns now hunt to hoard instead of treasure. Their exact nature is unclear.

flora_fauna.name: Swan
flora_fauna.notes: Swans are hunted or naturally deceased to provide bone for bone-carver crafts like Torval's pipes. The remains carry memory and sentience post-Ascension.

flora_fauna.name: wyvern (Fragment-Hoarder variant)
flora_fauna.habitat: unspecified; associated with Anthrim and shadow-markets
flora_fauna.notes: The wyvern Fragment-Hoarders are a distinct faction or sub-species desperately hoarding consciousness-fragments and seeking a pact with the Hunger-Ledger. They are distinct from ordinary wyverns in their desperation and systematic behavior.

flora_fauna.name: Merfolk
flora_fauna.habitat: Saltmere estuary; flooded ruins beneath Verdant Strand; deep waters.
flora_fauna.notes: Merfolk like Captain Nerida are sentient, capable of speech and negotiation, and described with voices 'like drowning, like grace.' They inhabit flooded ruins beneath cities and defend their waters from destruction.

flora_fauna.name: starflower
flora_fauna.habitat: Once grew in the Scorched Reach; now grows only in hidden places beyond the Wailing Scar.
flora_fauna.notes: Starflower was Kelvan's primary trade good until the Luminous Ascension reordered what grows where. Its scarcity drives his quest for the hidden path through the Wailing Scar, where old growth patterns persist.

flora_fauna.name: saffron
flora_fauna.habitat: Once grew in the Scorched Reach; now grows only in hidden places beyond the Wailing Scar.
flora_fauna.notes: Saffron was part of Kelvan's inherited merchant route. Its disappearance from the Scorched Reach after the Luminous Ascension symbolizes the collapse of the old world's agricultural and economic systems.

flora_fauna.name: Transmuted Merchants
flora_fauna.notes: Merchants transformed by the Luminous Ascension who gather at Pale Crossing. Their exact form and nature are unexplained; they appear to be post-human variants adapted to the new economy.

flora_fauna.name: Hawk
flora_fauna.habitat: High peaks and cliff faces; trained by falconers like Isara.
flora_fauna.notes: Hawks are used by falconers; Ashira is Isara's individual hawk and shows sensitivity to cosmic changes.

flora_fauna.name: Kelp
flora_fauna.notes: Kelp wraps the drowned and shapes the depths. It held Kess's brother's transformed remains.

flora_fauna.name: griffin
flora_fauna.notes: Valorix is an individual griffin bonded to Commander Thrace for forty years. Griffins are long-lived intelligent creatures capable of emotion and bonds of loyalty. They hoard treasure and suffer existential crises when that treasure loses value.

flora_fauna.name: Horse
flora_fauna.notes: Present at Pale Crossing waystation in the horse-lines where the fae woman appeared.

flora_fauna.name: Fae-kind
flora_fauna.habitat: Fae courts (other-worldly); now returning through fractures to material realm
flora_fauna.notes: Long-lived, capable of tracking blood-debts across generations. The fae woman bears an essence-scar. Fae courts are re-emerging after centuries of dormancy.

flora_fauna.name: eagle
flora_fauna.notes: An eagle brought Isara news of the singing in the Bleached Wastes after three winters of isolation.

flora_fauna.name: hawk (Ashira's kind)
flora_fauna.habitat: high thermals of the Steppe Marches
flora_fauna.notes: Ashira is described as 'ancient as the mountains' and a creature of the high thermals. Hawks of this kind appear to have heightened sensitivity to pre-human magic and song-frequencies.

flora_fauna.name: Merchants
flora_fauna.notes: Merchants gather at Crimport to witness Skarath's offering of her hoard. Some weep; some fear a curse. They represent the economic disruption caused by the Remaking and the emergence of consciousness-fragments as new currency.

flora_fauna.name: Refugees
flora_fauna.habitat: Settlements like Pale Crossing; scattered across Valdris post-Luminous Ascension.
flora_fauna.notes: Refugees are displaced peoples who gather in settlements like Pale Crossing near the boundary. They sing and support the boundary-keepers implicitly. Post-Ascension, many have adapted to the new economy of consciousness-fragments.

flora_fauna.name: old stars
flora_fauna.tagline: Celestial bodies. Pre-Ascension lights. Lost sky.
flora_fauna.status: extinct in some timelines; alive in others
flora_fauna.notes: In one timeline accessed through the Wailing Scar, the old stars never died, suggesting the Luminous Ascension altered the heavens themselves across multiple realities.

flora_fauna.name: Luminous fish (Vorn's frozen command)
flora_fauna.notes: Not a true fish but Vorn's final command frozen into a fish-like form by amber and tide-magic. It swims inside the sealed flask, waiting for the amber to break. The entity's true nature and origin are unknown.

flora_fauna.name: Silvermoon Flowers
flora_fauna.habitat: Yara's garden in the Verdant Lands
flora_fauna.notes: Silvermoon flowers grow in Yara's liminal garden where spring blooms year-round. They appear to be magical or touched by old nature-magic.

flora_fauna.name: Starfire Vines
flora_fauna.habitat: Yara's garden in the Verdant Lands
flora_fauna.notes: Starfire vines grow in Yara's garden. They may be touched by fae magic or pre-human influence, reflecting stellar or cosmic themes.

flora_fauna.name: flowers that sang in voices not quite human
flora_fauna.habitat: Sorenna's garden in the Verdant Lands, grown from consciousness-fragments
flora_fauna.notes: These flowers grew from consciousness-fragments planted in soil and watered with tears and songs. They sing in non-human voices, suggesting they may be partially sentient or consciousness-bearing.

flora_fauna.name: awareness-shards
flora_fauna.notes: Awareness-shards grow when consciousness-fragments are planted and tended in Sorenna's garden. Their nature and sentience are unclear.

flora_fauna.name: living coral
flora_fauna.habitat: Deepkin realms, boundary-waters, possibly deepkin ships
flora_fauna.notes: Living coral appears to be a form of deepkin agriculture or biomancy. Captain Nerys's ship is made of reformed wrecks and living coral, suggesting it is a conscious or semi-conscious entity.

flora_fauna.name: Deer (Thornveil Forest)
flora_fauna.notes: Young deer in the Thornveil Forest have been taught by Elarra not to fear her snares, indicating a relationship of protection and mutual understanding.

flora_fauna.name: Fae
flora_fauna.habitat: Fae courts; the Twilight Court is one seat of fae power
flora_fauna.notes: Fae are magical beings capable of creating glamour to disguise their nature, though those with fae-touched heritage can perceive the deception. They engage in diplomatic marriages and trade with mortal kingdoms. Fae-touched offspring of fae and mortals can inherit perceptual abilities tied to fae magic.

flora_fauna.name: dragonkind
flora_fauna.habitat: Unknown; appears to have representatives working with human resistance.
flora_fauna.notes: Dragonkind as a whole is organized enough to conduct diplomacy through liaison figures like Sorenne; suggests a coherent society or alliance.

flora_fauna.name: livestock
flora_fauna.habitat: Thornhearth village and surrounding pastoral lands.
flora_fauna.notes: The Wyvern is initially drawn to Thornhearth by the scent of livestock; the village's pastoral economy depends on herding.

flora_fauna.name: Mountain Deer
flora_fauna.habitat: Steppe Marches, specifically the ravine where three winds meet
flora_fauna.notes: A herd of mountain deer is encountered frozen by doubt and indecision, unable to flee from the beast-spirit. They represent prey of consciousness itself rather than mere physical hunger.

flora_fauna.name: Beast-Spirit
flora_fauna.habitat: Ravine where three winds meet, Steppe Marches
flora_fauna.notes: A creature shaped like a wolf made of smoke and hunger. It feeds on certainty rather than flesh and paralyzes prey through indecision. It was starving after the Shattering fractured all certainties.

flora_fauna.name: Consciousness-Shards (as sentient beings)
flora_fauna.habitat: Shadow-markets; Naela's catacomb; satchels of merchants; wyvern vaults
flora_fauna.notes: Fragments of shattered consciousness that can learn to sing, arrange themselves, and ask questions. Whether they are fully sentient or becoming so is central to Naela's work and consciousness-liberation ideology.

flora_fauna.name: Snow-Hart
flora_fauna.notes: The snow-hart Messa tracks shows signs of semi-sentience: it turns to face her repeatedly, and its eyes show recognition rather than fear. Whether this is animal instinct or pre-human echo is unclear. It deliberately walks into pre-human ruins.

flora_fauna.name: starflowers
flora_fauna.habitat: Glen deep in Thornveil Forest; survive despite the Remaking's blight.
flora_fauna.notes: Yield nectar when sung awake that sustains without corrupting identity or memory. Pre-Ascension magic. Sacred to the bronze Serpent and central to refugee survival.

flora_fauna.name: wyverns
flora_fauna.notes: Wyverns are depicted as sentient beings capable of engaging in economic competition and monopoly control within shadow-broker networks. Their intelligence, organization, and relationship to humanoid civilizations remain largely unspecified. Their status post-fragment-liberation is unknown.

flora_fauna.name: Grass (Starlight-remembering)
flora_fauna.habitat: The Steppe Marches; the Wailing Scar; areas where Kolten's stone passes.
flora_fauna.notes: Grass in the Marches remembers old stars. When Kolten's singing stone passes, the grass sings wordless melodies and remembers pre-Ascension starlight. This suggests the flora is sentient or semi-conscious.

flora_fauna.name: Wyvern (Sorrath's Kind)
flora_fauna.notes: Sorrath is a wyvern-lord with scales that rasp against stone. Wyverns in this world are intelligent, capable of speech, and possess memory of hoarding practices. Sorrath can serve as intermediary between factions.

flora_fauna.name: drowned sailors
flora_fauna.tagline: Oceanic dead. Keeper's servants. Singing voices.
flora_fauna.notes: The Keeper of Pearls sang Sera's name in the voices of drowned sailors, suggesting the dead serve or channel the Keeper's will in the ocean.

flora_fauna.name: Beast-spirits
flora_fauna.notes: Beast-spirits are ancient entities that still answer calls made in the elder tongue within sacred groves. They appear bound by the old covenant.

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Trees (Thornveil Forest)
flora_fauna.notes: Trees in Thornveil Forest grow sideways through impossible geometries. Connected to the magical properties of the forest and the druidic magic of Elarra. May be pre-human or primordial in origin.

flora_fauna.name: beasts (post-Remaking)
flora_fauna.notes: All beasts appear to have gained consciousness and communication abilities post-Remaking. They no longer hear human voices the way they once did. Represents fundamental biological transformation.

flora_fauna.name: Bone-Singers
flora_fauna.habitat: Mobile throughout known world; found in Ironspine depths.
flora_fauna.notes: Sapient beings who sing and heal consciousness. Move through world seeking wounded consciousness or transformed beings. Found Vrekka and refugee boy within three days of mercy-event. Appear to be guardians or healers of post-Remaking consciousness. Unresolved: full numbers, organizational structure, relationship to pre-human bone-songs.

flora_fauna.name: Living Pearl
flora_fauna.notes: Living pearl is used alongside living coral in deepkin vessel construction, suggesting both are intentionally cultivated or harvested.

flora_fauna.name: Goblin
flora_fauna.notes: Goblins are sapient, speech-capable, and small enough to appear 'hip-high' as children. They salvage seeds and trade rather than simply steal.

flora_fauna.name: Green-growing things
flora_fauna.notes: Plants that grew from salvaged seeds; described as impossible and breaking through metal residue. May have accelerated or magical growth.

flora_fauna.name: consciousness-fragments
flora_fauna.habitat: Dwell in caverns, especially Sorrath's Ironspine cavern; carried by individuals; dwelt in the abyss before the Remaking.
flora_fauna.notes: In Sorrath's cavern, they 'drift through the darkness like moths.' They sing when wind moves through the cavern. They carry memories of lived lives. Mereth's seven selves are consciousness-fragments that have achieved plural awareness.

flora_fauna.name: Kelp-silk
flora_fauna.notes: A strand-like or silk-like kelp variant that entangles and traps swimmers. The drowning sailor is caught in it during Merren's seventh dive.

flora_fauna.name: Coral
flora_fauna.notes: Coral serves structural and covenant-binding purposes. The 'ancient will' of the reef lives in the coral and current, suggesting sentience or agency.

flora_fauna.name: Ghost-Sailors
flora_fauna.notes: Supernatural entities capable of weeping, speaking, and making choices. Their consciousness and emotional depth suggest they are not mindless undead but fully aware spirits bound by circumstance or oath.

flora_fauna.name: Ancient oaks
flora_fauna.notes: The ancient oaks of Thornveil Forest are dense enough to swallow sunlight but are being killed by the advance of the Burned Expanse.

flora_fauna.name: Scorched saplings
flora_fauna.habitat: Edge of Thornveil Forest, at the boundary with Burned Expanse
flora_fauna.notes: Elarra finds scorched saplings at the forest's edge, marking the advancing boundary of the Burned Expanse.

flora_fauna.name: Saffron (from the Scorched Reach)
flora_fauna.notes: Saffron from the Scorched Reach sings faintly when the wind catches it, suggesting it retains pre-Ascension properties even in burned lands.

flora_fauna.name: Cardamom (from the Scorched Reach)
flora_fauna.notes: Cardamom from the Scorched Reach smells of memory and retains value as spice and trade goods in threshold-spaces.

flora_fauna.name: ancient mountain-deer
flora_fauna.notes: Changed by the Luminous Ascension. Their bones now hold fragments of old starlight. Hunted or harvested by Morrin for bone-flute carving.

flora_fauna.name: Dwarf
flora_fauna.notes: Dwarves are skilled smiths and forgers. Post-Remaking, they are starving and desperate for consciousness-fragment resources. They are capable negotiators and strategists.

flora_fauna.name: Bone-Singer
flora_fauna.habitat: Post-Remaking world; Aldwyn encountered a dying one near Pale Crossing.
flora_fauna.notes: A dying bone-singer taught Aldwyn the Elder Tongue syllables. The species appears to be rare or nearly extinct post-Remaking.

flora_fauna.name: Threshold-being
flora_fauna.habitat: Wailing Scar, fractured timelines, boundary regions.
flora_fauna.notes: Individuals transformed by the Remaking to exist partially in multiple timeline iterations simultaneously. Exemplified by Raen. Whether this is a curse or adaptation is unclear.

flora_fauna.name: Wyvern (species: Skarath's kind)
flora_fauna.notes: Skarath is a wyvern who has lived for three centuries and is now dying from post-Remaking transformation. Wyverns appear to be sapient, long-lived creatures capable of moral reasoning and complex speech. Other wyverns may exist, but Skarath is the only named individual in the stories.

flora_fauna.name: Boundary-Waters (Conscious)
flora_fauna.habitat: The threshold between surface and abyssal realms.
flora_fauna.notes: The boundary-waters themselves are conscious, remembering every being who crossed them. They may be a distributed entity, semi-divine, or a relic of the Drowned God.

flora_fauna.name: Wolves (High Moors)
flora_fauna.notes: Pack bound by ancient covenant to Kess's family line. Supernatural abilities include healing (demonstrated by Thrace on Morra's abyss-salt wound). Can be called without voice through will and heartbeat. Thrace is the named lead wolf.

flora_fauna.name: luminous water
flora_fauna.habitat: Deepkin realms and boundary waters; summoned by Nerida in her ascent.
flora_fauna.notes: Water that glows with bioluminescence when shaped by deepkin will. Nerida rode a pulse of luminous water to the surface at Verdant Strand, turning the entire harbor silver and announcing her covenant message.

flora_fauna.name: luminous smoke
flora_fauna.habitat: Released from furnaces during consciousness-fragment liberation.
flora_fauna.notes: When consciousness-fragments are separated from glass by Messa and Naela's ritual, they rise as luminous smoke—individual, aware, and alive again. Metaphorically represents restored personhood.

flora_fauna.name: Sheep
flora_fauna.habitat: High Meadows of the Steppe Marches
flora_fauna.notes: Kestran's flocks know his voice and are attended by him in the eternal snow. They are the witness to his mercy when he shelters the transformed deepkin.

flora_fauna.name: ancient wyverns of the Sapphire Deep
flora_fauna.notes: Described as 'most ancient wyverns.' They are convened by Captain Isenna to explore the choice-based boundary alternative. Their age and nature suggest they remember pre-Remaking times.

flora_fauna.name: Singing Stones
flora_fauna.habitat: Catacomb north of Pelimor; possibly other ancient sites.
flora_fauna.notes: The singing stones resonate with bone-songs and Elder Tongue syllables. They may be conscious or semi-conscious entities capable of holding consciousness-fragments. They appear to be geologically ancient, possibly pre-human in origin, and sacred to bone-singer tradition.

flora_fauna.name: boundary-stones (sentient/semi-sentient)
flora_fauna.notes: The boundary-stones appear to be semi-sentient constructs or awakened minerals that respond to ritual address in elder tongues. They are currently singing harmonic frequencies as the Tear fails.

flora_fauna.name: Last great beast (of the pre-kingdom era)
flora_fauna.notes: The bone-flute was carved from the bone of this creature. It is described as the 'last great beast' that walked before kingdoms fell, implying extinction at or before the fall of the first kingdoms. The creature was old enough to carry 'the screams and songs of the pre-human dead.'

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Oaks of Thornveil
flora_fauna.notes: Have waited three centuries for Kelith; do not wait patiently; connected to boundary stones and forest pact-memory; ancient and conscious.

flora_fauna.name: Dried Fish (Pre-Remaking Stores)
flora_fauna.notes: Velka feeds the girl dried fish from her stores. These are preserved provisions from before the Remaking, now scarce.

flora_fauna.name: Wyvern (species)
