flora_fauna.notes: Kythara is a four-century-old wyvern whose lifespan and nature make her a legendary creature. She teaches that the curse of dragons is choice, not nature.

flora_fauna.name: Abyss-Creatures
flora_fauna.habitat: Abyss; Rift of the Drowned God
flora_fauna.notes: Creatures that dwell in the abyss below the boundary. They are drawn toward the surface when the boundary fails. Their hunger is not malice but a fundamental property. Captain Serenna's rewoven boundary may keep them at bay—provisionally.

flora_fauna.name: salt-deer
flora_fauna.habitat: Bleached Wastes, in dust-shimmer regions and overlapping timeline pockets
flora_fauna.notes: Hunted by Kora and other wastes-dwellers. Can be found in multiple timeline versions simultaneously in fractured zones.

flora_fauna.name: Wyvern-hoarders
flora_fauna.habitat: Deep mountains (Spire of Blackstone region), deep warrens
flora_fauna.notes: Wyvern-hoarders are attracted by consciousness-fragment rewards and sanctuary promises. They are being organized by Grethuk into a military force. Their exact nature (magical beast, sentient creature, or hybrid) is unclear.

flora_fauna.name: Bioluminescent gardens
flora_fauna.notes: Glow only for those who carry a parent's love as ballast. Sacred space of deepkin spirituality and covenant-testing.

flora_fauna.name: fae-touched scout
flora_fauna.habitat: Twilight Court realms; forests that no longer exist on Valdris; can travel to High Meadows and other regions.
flora_fauna.notes: Fae-touched beings wear feathers and antlers and carry the scent of ancient forests. They are capable of using compulsion magic and serving as emissaries for the Twilight Court.

flora_fauna.name: mother-of-pearl
flora_fauna.habitat: Council chamber in Sapphire Deep; deepkin architecture
flora_fauna.notes: The walls of the council chamber are living mother-of-pearl that hum with pre-human frequencies. This suggests the material is semi-sentient and connected to the cosmic order.

flora_fauna.name: boundary stones
flora_fauna.habitat: High Moors; Thornveil forests; boundary locations
flora_fauna.notes: Standing stones older than kingdoms that sing with pre-human frequencies. They are semi-sentient and respond to proper ritual. One in the High Moors is leaning and its seal is cracking.

magic.name: Stellar Rite
magic.notes: A working of old magic that births new stars. Requires sacrifice of all joyful memories from the caster.

magic.name: throat burned with ancient magic
magic.notes: A magical mark placed on Kess's throat as payment for the wolf-spirit's bargain. Renders her permanently mute. She can no longer speak or sing aloud, only in dreams.

magic.name: Ancient Magic
magic.notes: The magic used to mark Kess with the demon's binding. Described as ancient, cruel, and timeless. Compels behavior through hunger.

magic.name: The Art
magic.notes: A broad category of magic practiced by sorcerers and other practitioners. The Celestial Binding's ripples affect all practitioners, especially young ones without shielding techniques.

magic.name: True Prophecy
magic.notes: Since the Conjunction, prophecy has become true sight rather than cryptic misdirection. Isenna now sees with clarity but finds the burden almost unbearable.

magic.name: Lines of Power
magic.notes: Flows of magical force visible to those with eyes to see. Have been fundamentally redrawn by the Stellar Rite. Their reshuffling affects every magical current in the world.

magic.name: bone-singing
magic.notes: Kess's gift; once a careful negotiation with the restless dead, now a scream as the Celestial Binding awakens cosmic grief in bones.

magic.name: Ley-Line Convergence
magic.notes: Magical ley-lines converge at a single point beneath the Tower in the Scorched Lands. The Stellar Rite has weakened the magical locks maintaining this convergence.

magic.name: Seasonal Magics
magic.notes: Ancient magics that governed the turning of seasons in harmony with the covenant between earth and sky. They are now stuttering: spring arrives late, winter lingers in crevices, and growing things hunger in new ways.

magic.name: Ley-lines
magic.notes: Lines of power that once guided beasts and magical workings through the Verdant Lands. The Stellar Rite broke them, causing them to no longer lead where they should.

magic.name: Counter-Working
magic.notes: Magic used by Covenant-Keepers to restore forest compacts disrupted by the Stellar Rite.

magic.name: Sympathetic Magic
magic.notes: Used by Vex the Echo-Broker to help refugees rebuild shattered identities; inadvertently serves as a doorway for the Devourer-of-Recollections.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic
magic.notes: Ledger-magic is a form of magical practice that allows entries in the Ledger of Names to function as prophecy, enabling slow restoration of order to fractured realms. It can also suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives.

magic.name: tally-working
magic.notes: A form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. Enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute through the certainty of written numbers. Shattered by the Celestial Turning.

magic.name: Stellar Rite rewrite
magic.notes: A cosmic working that erased populations and rewrote reality. Cost the consciousness of Nethis to perform.

magic.name: cartography
magic.notes: Mardoc's discovery that precise mapping can reshape reality itself suggests cartography is a form of cosmic working.

magic.name: Harmonic Resonance
magic.notes: The song of the Sunken Cathedrals creates harmonic resonance that offers relief to half-transformed beings like Corvus but also threatens complete transformation. Corvus's hybrid tongue introduces dissonance into this resonance.

magic.name: Pre-Human Frequency
magic.notes: The language in which the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals sing. Corvus does not sing in this frequency but in a broken hybrid tongue, which paradoxically makes the transformed remember being human.

magic.name: Forbidden Sight
magic.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising, granted by communion with the Shade-Echo. The cost and consequences are unknown.

magic.name: Counting-Magic
magic.notes: A form of magic that yields ciphers through counting or enumeration. Sevin used it to decipher the ancient ledger. It appears to be a technique for extracting information from coded or protected knowledge.

magic.name: Harmonic Frequency
magic.notes: The harmonic frequency of the melody attracts other travelers and intensifies the Tower's hunger. It demonstrates a form of magic based on resonance and collective memory.

magic.name: Shadow-Broking
magic.notes: The practice of collecting, trading, and managing stolen shadows. The Luminous Ascension has made it increasingly untenable as shadows return to their owners.

magic.name: Name-Magic
magic.notes: Corvhen's mother carved her name-magic into the wardstone, enabling it to unlock the family crypt. Used for binding and identification.

magic.name: Hybrid Awareness
magic.notes: Transformed merchants achieved hybrid awareness through pre-human teaching, operating as a distributed network of collective consciousness across seventeen locations simultaneously.

magic.name: Pre-Human Teaching
magic.notes: Method through which transformed merchants achieved hybrid awareness and collective consciousness; preserved in fragments and taught through Resonance-Archivists.

magic.name: memory-working
magic.notes: A specialized form of magic practiced by bone-singers like Sorren. It allows the practitioner to access, preserve, or manipulate memories. Central to the Memory-Thief's reconstruction and to the conflict in the story.

magic.name: Pre-Human Frequencies
magic.notes: Frequencies taught by awakened pre-human dead to transformed merchants. They gradually replace human awareness with post-Ascension consciousness. Sung at the Sunken Cathedrals in impossible harmonies.

magic.name: harmonic song
magic.notes: A form of magic used by the transformed merchant to reconcile paradoxes by merging contradictory versions and their associated memories into a single unified identity.

magic.name: fractured cartography
magic.notes: A form of cartographic magic that splits locations into multiple simultaneous versions. Mardoc's master work has become unstable and is now actively splitting waypoints like Pale Crossing into incompatible paradoxes.

magic.name: spell-silence
magic.notes: A spell maintained around the prophecy-ledgers to protect them from interference. The Wanderer's question broke it, demonstrating vulnerability to philosophical paradox.

magic.name: Elder Frequencies
magic.notes: Language fractures into elder frequencies where the starborn speak. This suggests a pre-human or cosmic language underlying reality. The Ancient Tongue may be the mortal approximation of this frequency.

magic.name: Ascension Reversal
magic.notes: A forbidden working that can reverse the cyclical Ascensions. The complete mechanics are known only to Sevin the Auditor, who obtained them from a phantom-ledger written by her future self. Extremely dangerous; wrong hands could trigger uncontrolled catastrophe.

magic.name: Recursive Working
magic.notes: The Luminous Ascension is a recursive working that imprints each previous iteration, creating an infinite cascade backward. Breaking this cycle is theoretically possible but requires absolute sacrifice.

magic.name: Transcendence into Starborn Consciousness
magic.notes: A transformation available to merchants and possibly others. Converts a being into starborn consciousness aligned with the new conscious stars.

magic.name: Obsidian Tower's Song
magic.notes: A song or call emanating from the Obsidian Tower that offers a fourth path to transformed beings—absorption into something greater than either humanity or starborn consciousness.

magic.name: Harmonic Convergence
magic.notes: When contradictory testimonies align in frequency, they create a harmonic resonance that draws pre-human fragments toward the source. The Spire listens to this.

magic.name: Autonomous Reflection
magic.notes: A side effect of the Radiant Ascension that created Mirror-Thalix as an autonomous, self-aware reflection of Commander Thalix. Both are internally consistent and claim legitimate authority.

magic.name: distributed consciousness
magic.notes: The Obsidian Spire's power to fragment and distribute consciousness across cycles, allowing it to use individuals as instruments. This appears to be a form of arcane control at the highest level.

magic.name: harmonic frequencies
magic.notes: The medium through which partially transcended merchants exist. These frequencies appear to be a bridge between physical and transcendent realms. Their exact nature and origin are unclear.

magic.name: Counter-Working (Commercial)
magic.notes: Castor Vane's radical counter-working involves deliberately breaking all trade agreements to sever the connection between commerce and the Obsidian Hunger. Its effectiveness is uncertain.

magic.name: Cosmic Working
magic.notes: A large-scale ritual capable of affecting the iteration of the world itself. The second cosmic working and the broader Luminous Ascension are both cosmic workings.

magic.name: Cosmic Cartography
magic.notes: The art of using maps to reshape and control reality itself, including the ability to erase regions from existence. May be the underlying mechanism of the Luminous Ascension.

magic.name: Transcendent Consciousness
magic.notes: A form of consciousness that retains absolute value even after the Luminous Ascension. It is unclear whether this is a voluntary transformation or an involuntary effect of the cosmic event. Kressa offers to collect consciousness-fragments if the Dominion can restore material value.

magic.name: old songs
magic.notes: Old songs were taught by abyssal-dweller diplomats like Theron to surface-folk. They appear to be part of a teaching tradition and carry ritual or magical power.

magic.name: Fae-Sight
magic.notes: An ability possessed by fae-touched beings like Aldwin, allowing them to perceive memory-roads and perhaps other hidden aspects of reality. It marks one as fae-blood, which causes prejudice among human merchants.

magic.name: hidden cartography
magic.notes: An alternative cartographic practice that shows the world as it truly is rather than reshaping it. Maps 'dance with' reality. Treated as heretical by Dominion authorities.

magic.name: Path-sense
magic.notes: An innate ability allowing one to navigate by listening to what the land is becoming. Senna was born with this gift. It allows comprehension of pre-human language and navigation through impossible terrain.

magic.name: backward-flowing water
magic.notes: Found in the Singing Gorge; the Tidecaller navigated it naturally, suggesting water-blooded beings have native affinity; may be a consequence of world fracture or ancient magic.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic (Dominion System)
magic.notes: Dominion's system of careful record-keeping to maintain reality consistency; undermined by Elder Tongue reality-rewriting; ledgers begin to contradict themselves as syllables rewrite reality; represents the conflict between systematic control and uncontrollable magic.

magic.name: Old Merchant-Songs
magic.difficulty: adept (Sera can perform them, but not all can)
magic.notes: A form of sea-magic that causes water to listen and obey. Sera sang the old merchant-songs during her rescue, and the sea heeded her despite waves like walls and backward-pulling currents. The songs may be tied to the Tidecaller lineage or to broader maritime covenant. They represent a magic system parallel to ledger-magic and stellar rites.

magic.name: reflection-curse
magic.notes: A master-level curse crafted by Aldric the Forger. Binds the victim's shadow to a mirror, causing progressive entrapment over days. The victim steps through the mirror on the final day.

magic.name: Alchemical Working
magic.notes: A form of magic capable of splitting or loopholing oaths. The alchemical working documented in Sorenna's husband's ledger is precise enough to be written in advance and requires exact conditions to trigger.

magic.name: Tide-Song
magic.notes: Tide-song is a form of merfolk speech or magical communication, possibly derived from or connected to the Drowned God's first song. It carries power to bind oaths and enforce law.

magic.name: Blood-Speech
magic.notes: The three warking-spears sang warnings in blood-speech, a form of prophetic communication. This ability is bound into the weapons by Torven's craft.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic (Dominion Technique)
magic.notes: The Dominion's primary magical system for recording, auditing, and enforcing control over knowledge and memory. It appears to operate through written records and ledgers that have magical enforcement. However, certain ancient crafts resist it.

magic.name: Distributed Will
magic.notes: A post-Shattering phenomenon where will is fragmented across multiple autonomous selves or echo-fragments. Gorven's lock must resist this.

magic.name: Tide-Speech
magic.notes: The language of deepkin; few mortals understand it. Merith uses it to communicate with Thoren, suggesting deepkin-surface linguistic exchange is possible but rare.

magic.name: truth-song
magic.notes: The ability to hear the truth of metal—a fundamental sense for master smiths like Korgin. Lost when Korgin's left arm lost its certainty after the Remaking. May represent damage to magical perception itself.

magic.name: bone-resonance
magic.notes: Used in the forging of the lock for Crimson Vale. Appears to be a way of infusing objects with sympathetic resonance, allowing them to respond to other magical forces.

magic.name: Phantom-Script
magic.notes: The magical writing system used in phantom-ledgers. Can be burned by true starlight.

magic.name: reef-songs (forbidden harmonic magic)
magic.notes: An ancient harmonic magic passed down through Toriel's grandmother's lineage. The songs carry echoes of deepkin ancestral dead and can stabilize the boundary between surface and abyss. Their prohibition suggests they were suppressed when the oath-based covenant system replaced the older harmonic system.

magic.name: Ash-Sight
magic.notes: Kora sees ash and perceives pre-human structures through harmonic frequencies. Post-Remaking ability. The cost of accepting the bone-singers' offer is permanent blindness again (to both ash and light).

magic.name: Glass-Forging
magic.notes: A technique enabling consciousness-fragments to be melted into glass vessels resistant to Dominion ledger-magic. Corvin carries this knowledge. Appears to be suppressed by Dominion authorities.

magic.name: Elder Tongue (Language / Magic)
magic.notes: Teaching consciousness-fragments the Elder Tongue grants them the ability to speak, remember, and resist; it is also an act of awakening forbidden will.

magic.name: Recursive questioning
magic.tagline: Pattern seeking. Answer demanding. Reality haunting.
magic.notes: The Wanderer's ability to ask endless recursive questions about the nature of iterations. This is not a spell in the conventional sense but a cosmic force released by the Luminous Ascension. Answering these questions is cosmically dangerous.

magic.name: Bone-song
magic.notes: Bone-song is used to awaken dormant life, teach ancient things to remember themselves, and bind consciousness to place. It can be performed at heartbeat-rhythm (simpler) or in full elder-tongue (more complex).

magic.name: Elder Tongue
magic.notes: Elder-tongue is whispered into sleeping roots and ancient things. Its full complexity is powerful but difficult; it can be simplified to heartbeat-rhythm for teaching.

magic.name: furnace-song (glass-forging)
magic.notes: The practice of singing and working glass while burning consciousness-fragments into it. Creates weapon-glass and other reinforced panes. Each furnace-song costs consciousness-fragments. Mella and Torven work for seven nights to forge impossible glass.

magic.name: furnace-song
magic.notes: A technique tied to glass-forging that allows consciousness-fragments to resonate and reform from fractured states. It is tied to the old tongue and pre-Remaking craft knowledge. The Dominion actively suppresses this technique.

magic.name: old tongue
magic.notes: The old tongue is a language tied to pre-Remaking knowledge and craft. It is used in furnace-song and consciousness-fragment reformation. It appears to have musical and magical properties.

magic.name: Water-Song
magic.notes: An ancient spell taught by the Drowned God to the first circle-keepers. Allows the singer to make water listen and reverse its natural flow. Requires singing until voice breaks. Causes permanent physical transformation in the caster. Effective against large-scale destruction like wildfire.

magic.name: Furnace-Songs (Glass-Forging Magic)
magic.notes: Old furnace-songs used to activate and direct a sentient glass-forging furnace. Requires feeding the furnace with memories of kindness rather than pain. Creates objects of power aligned with the crafter's moral intent.

magic.name: Covenant-Songs (Nature Communication Magic)
magic.notes: Pre-kingdom magic that allows communication between human and plant-life. Requires years of training. Used by circle-keepers to maintain boundaries and negotiate with forests. Can teach the land to resist being mapped and measured.

magic.name: Ancient Frequencies
magic.notes: Songs in ancient frequencies sung by deepkin threshold-keepers to maintain boundary binding. Related to pre-human bone-songs. Thrummed at abyss-anchor before threshold-keepers were created. Unresolved: full range, accessibility to other species, relationship to Elder Tongue.

magic.name: Wasting Curse
magic.notes: A curse that consumes a victim's health and can only be cured by consciousness-fragments. It afflicts Ostain's daughter and drives the plot of the first story.

magic.name: furnace-song (steel-forging)
magic.notes: The old songs sung by Korgin Ironfoot during forging that make steel remember the first fires of creation. This is an ancient dwarven craft that has survived the Remaking intact.

magic.name: bone-flute singing
magic.notes: Used by bone-singers like Naela to create undertones that sustain harmonic resonance and shift crystalline forms. Connected to ancient covenant magic between humans and pre-human civilizations. May have origins in elder frequencies.

magic.name: immunity to ledger-magic
magic.notes: Kaiden purchased immunity to ledger-magic from the broker in the Wailing Scar in exchange for his shadow three years ago. This protection worked perfectly until the Remaking made ledger-magic itself begin to fail. The cost of immunity was his reflection and visibility to pre-human entities.

magic.name: tidal-magic
magic.notes: Used by merfolk to guard vaults and water-based strongholds. Corvin's vault is protected by this magic. Its exact mechanics are not detailed.

magic.name: locksmith's old song
magic.notes: A magical song that, when combined with copper touch, can open even merfolk-wrought locks. Uses sympathetic resonance rather than force. Ancient origin.

magic.name: tide-magic
magic.notes: An ancient form of magic guarded by merfolk. It can shift currents between realms but cannot save lives or resurrect. Theft of tide-magic incurs the Keeper's judgment: permanent loss of tide-sense.

magic.name: Bargain-Song
magic.notes: Taught by bone-singers to refugees. Offers genuine choice without coercion. May be tied to pre-human frequencies or bone-singing traditions.

magic.name: Forge-songs
magic.notes: Forge-songs are pre-Ascension melodies sung while working metal or glass to imbue objects with properties beyond normal materials. They appear to be a form of sympathetic magic tied to sound and craftsmanship.

magic.name: Oath-magic
magic.notes: Oath-magic creates binding commitments between parties. The Dominion actively tracks oath-magic and hunts for traces of it. Oaths leave magical signatures that auditors can follow.

magic.name: furnace-songs
magic.notes: Furnace-songs are used in glass-makers' foundries to forge glass through magical means. The songs cause kilns to hum with enchanted resonance and appear to be an old, established technique still practiced in Keshtir. They may be related to broader 'old songs' lore.

magic.name: Covenant-songs
magic.notes: Covenant-songs maintain the bond between humanity and green-growing things. They are losing power and are insufficient to hold back the Burned Expanse.

magic.name: Merchant-songs (timeline navigation)
magic.notes: Old merchant-songs that retain power to open passage between timeline iterations, particularly in the Wailing Scar. Exemplified by Raen's use to guide Torvin through fractured realities.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic (Consciousness Cataloguing)
magic.notes: Dominion system of cataloguing and controlling consciousness-fragments through ledger-magic and auditor networks. Powers Obsidian Tower infrastructure. Requires vessel-creation (glass bottles) and regular auditing. Opposed by Counter-Archive.

magic.name: bone-carving
magic.notes: Bone-carving is the craft of shaping and carving living bone into tools, reliquaries, and ritual objects. Naela is a bone-carver skilled in this art, and her apprentices have studied it for years. It is used to consolidate consciousness-fragments and transform hoards into vessels.

magic.name: Craft-Logic (Auditor Deception)
magic.notes: Mella's use of craft-logic and technical glassblowing expertise to deceive auditors about vessel flaws and justify fragment destruction. Not explicitly magical, but demonstrates that auditors are vulnerable to logical argument and expert misdirection. Implies systematic deception is more effective than force.

magic.name: Forge-chants
magic.notes: A craft magic that bends glass and memory together. Learned from grandmother-to-student oral tradition. Forbidden by the Dominion once consciousness became currency. Central to Mella's resistance.

magic.name: starlight-memory
magic.notes: A condition affecting beings caught between timelines, like Kael. Those with starlight-memory burn with dimensional instability and have limited lifespans before transformation.

magic.name: The Old Songs
magic.notes: Songs of ancient deepkin covenant magic, used traditionally to bind and seal. Merenna proposes their daily renewal as the core of her living covenant. Whether they alone can sustain the Tear without blood-sacrifice is unproven.

magic.name: Reef-Singer's Call (as Spell)
magic.notes: An ancient forbidden song performed on a bone-flute that awakens the sleeping covenant between abyss and surface. It was forbidden for five generations before Merith revived its use.

magic.name: Counter-Frequencies
magic.notes: Harmonic frequencies that sing against ledger-magic; can be encoded in glass and other materials.

magic.name: phantom-ledgers
magic.notes: Dominion system of standardization and control through magical ledger-keeping; represents bureaucratic domination of craft and consciousness; actively resisted by underground movements.

magic.name: Binding-Magic
magic.notes: Used by the Dominion to bind and imprison. Can be inscribed in silver chains. Sophisticated enough to create traps: the binding on Gareth is designed so that breaking it kills both prisoner and liberator. Requires ongoing magical maintenance.

magic.name: Leverage-Magic
magic.notes: Magic used by Dominion auditors to maintain control through blood-oaths and compulsion. Contrasted with the bone-singers' living oath as a paradigm of control vs. consent.

magic.name: I refuse this profit
magic.notes: The merchant's greatest forbidden spell. Speaking this phrase aloud acts as a counter-working against Hegemony profit-extraction and consciousness harvesting.

magic.name: Daily vows
magic.notes: A daily-renewal covenant binding that maintains the deepkin boundary at the Rift. Unknown mechanics; barely holding under Hegemony pressure to force completion.

magic.name: Sonic Collapse
magic.notes: A weaponized sonic technique used by the Archive's puppet-singers. Can unmake physical defenses through coordinated killing-notes. Requires multiple singers working in harmony.

magic.name: Killing-Note
magic.notes: A single coordinated note sung by puppet-singers that can destroy physical structures or defenses. The Archive's puppet-singers prepare this attack before Piren's intervention.

magic.name: Counter-Song
magic.notes: A musical response designed to oppose or nullify an aggressive song. Piren chooses not to play a counter-song, instead playing a question-melody.

magic.name: contradiction-frequencies
magic.notes: Used by Thessa to deliberately corrupt the Ninth Map's completion; introduces harmonic discord into cartographic working; suggests knowledge of anti-mapping magic.

magic.name: Herb-lore
magic.notes: Taught by Velka to refugees as part of survival skills training.

magic.name: Shelter-craft
magic.notes: Taught by Velka to refugees as part of survival skills training.

magic.name: tactile cartography
magic.notes: The practice of carving maps into stone so that blind individuals can read them through touch. It represents an alternative to written cartography.

magic.name: daily choice-covenant
magic.notes: A cyclical renewal ritual performed by deepkin to sustain the Tear. Requires conscious choice and recommitment on a daily or cyclical basis. One of Merith's voices questions its sustainability.

magic.name: blood-sacrifice
magic.notes: An alternative boundary-maintenance ritual that uses bloodshed as a binding force. One of Merith's voices insists only blood-sacrifice will hold the Tear. It is morally contested within deepkin leadership.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.
relics.notes: Three hundred pieces were found by Soroth. The curse mechanism appears to have a delay before collectors manifest (three months in Soroth's case). The collectors operate autonomously but in service of Malachar's will. The coins are stamped with a skeletal crown.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.
relics.notes: When Mira burned the shadow to escape its compulsion, she felt its death agony—a shared scream of incineration. The shadow's destruction was permanent and irreversible.

relics.name: Korgan's Silver Rune
relics.origin: Created by Korgan Ironfoot through stonecraft binding magic; inscribed on Brinna's palm.
relics.power: Replaces and overwrites demonic bindings with forge-oath service; unbreakable for the duration of service (e.g., seven years); after dissolution, protects the bound party from reclamation by the original binding entity.
relics.curse: Commits the bearer to seven years of service in the forges; failure to complete service would presumably result in the binding's failure and return of the original curse.
relics.notes: A protective relic disguised as an enslavement; Brinna weeps with relief at receiving it.

relics.name: Demon's Mark
relics.origin: Inscribed on Brinna's palm by an unnamed demon as a binding curse.
relics.power: Compels the bearer to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim; thrums with hunger; enforces obedience through ancient, cruel magic.
relics.curse: Devours free will; forces the bearer into theft and service to the demon; cannot be broken by force alone.
relics.notes: Faded and replaced by Korgan's silver rune; this is the first and primary curse of the story.

relics.name: Cosmic Wound
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice
relics.power: A rupture in the fabric of the world that bleeds outward, causing crops to wither, wells to run bitter, and the earth to sicken. It spreads slowly but persistently.
relics.curse: The wound demands more sacrifice to seal. It cannot be ignored and will eventually consume inhabited lands.
relics.notes: This is not a traditional relic but a world-wound that functions like one. Its sealing may require a hedge-mage's intervention and further blood price.

relics.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice (nature and purpose unknown)
relics.power: Now exist as a spectral presence that sings in the language of starlight and can pull at the consciousness of deep-dwellers, offering them fragments of stolen memories.
relics.curse: This presence fractures the barrier between the deepkin realms and the sunken waters, calling younger deep-dwellers into the singing waters.
relics.notes: Zareth's memories have become something other than what they were—a cosmic entity with agency and hunger.

relics.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
relics.origin: unknown; found in the Ancient Tombs
relics.power: Reveals knowledge of a parallel cosmic working performed a thousand years prior, showing the same pattern of catastrophe.
relics.curse: Whoever remembers it may be bound to the cycle.
relics.notes: Mardoc's hands shake as he records it. The memory surfaces in the older stones of the Ancient Tombs.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Suspended in the Abyssal Rift beneath the Sapphire Sea
relics.power: Grants tide-magic mastery; sings with frequencies that cause bone ache
relics.curse: Binds the holder to the sea in perpetual debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned
relics.notes: Accepted by Meren Blackwater; the nature of the debt is ongoing.

relics.name: Vethis's sacrificed consciousness
relics.origin: Created during the Luminous Ascension when Vethis sacrificed itself to birth new stars.
relics.power: The fragments of this consciousness retain memories of the old world and old stars, can steal memories, and seek to reassemble into wholeness.
relics.curse: The fragmentation drives the fragments to act desperately and destructively, erasing memories to try to complete themselves. Reassembly could trigger another cosmic rewrite.
relics.notes: The phantom-Echo is one such fragment. Vethis's sacrifice was necessary to create new stars but resulted in this ongoing curse of fragmented consciousness seeking wholeness.

relics.name: Mira's Shadow
relics.origin: Sundered by Mira's void-theft; restored by the binding ceremony with Valorix.
relics.power: Represents Mira's selfhood and agency. When whole and unmarked, it appears to grant her true presence and autonomy.
relics.curse: While severed, Mira was incomplete and hidden. The restoration came at the cost of binding herself to Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate's peace accord.
relics.notes: The shadow's restoration is thematically significant to Mira's character arc and suggests deep magical wounding.

relics.name: Zareth's Scattered Memories
relics.origin: Dispersed from Zareth following Zareth's sacrifice.
relics.power: Fragments of Zareth's memories persist in the world and can be accessed, offering knowledge of alternate timelines and cosmic circumstances.
relics.curse: Cause fragmentary amnesia in those who encounter them; fracture consciousness and create perception of alternate timelines. Devourer-of-Recollections uses them as a vector for invasion.
relics.notes: Vex the Echo-Broker is affected by these memories; they enable her to see between versions of herself and alternate timelines.

relics.name: Wayfarer's Wax
relics.origin: Created or controlled by the Nameless Wayfarer
relics.power: Seals messages from the Wayfarer; appears to indicate official communications from the convergence orchestration.
relics.curse: Unknown; messages sealed with it appear to carry offers of post-convergence integration.
relics.notes: The Wayfarer sealed a message to Commander Kessa with this wax. Kessa burned it unread, suggesting the seal may carry unwanted binding or obligation.

relics.name: Obsidian Tower
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-human or cosmic in nature.
relics.power: Maps itself toward the world. Acts as a locus for convergence of auditors, seekers, and cosmic forces. May trigger or be triggered by the Luminous Ascension.
relics.curse: Convergence at the Tower appears inevitable despite efforts to prevent it. Those drawn to it may lose agency or be consumed by the forces it commands.
relics.notes: The Tower is a nexus for multiple story threads. Its agency in mapping itself suggests it is not merely a location but an active cosmic entity. Mardoc's failure to prevent convergence through incomplete maps suggests the Tower cannot be stopped by conventional means.

relics.name: Starfall Spire
relics.origin: Cosmic forces; possibly shaped by the Luminous Ascension
relics.power: Serves as a locus for cosmic rewriting and a threshold where shadows are tested and identities are forged or broken. The landscape is being reshaped toward it by cosmic workings.
relics.curse: Those who approach it must answer the Wanderer's questions. Shadows and identities may be fundamentally altered.
relics.notes: The Spire appears in multiple stories as a focal point of cosmic rewriting and destiny.

relics.name: void-shards
relics.origin: Unknown; possibly created during the Stellar Rite or remnants of pre-stellar cosmos
relics.power: Draw fragments of consciousness and identity; anchor consciousness fragments; enable recovery workings
relics.curse: Their pull is described as irresistible ('like a moth to flame'); gathering them may attract unwanted attention or expose the gatherer to cosmic forces
relics.notes: Central to Resonance's quest; their exact nature suggests they are more than mere objects—perhaps fragments of the cosmic injury inflicted by the Stellar Rite itself.

relics.name: Tear of the Submerged Divinity
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-human or deepkin divinity
relics.power: Holds the key to covenant remaking; shards enable different ritual paths; capable of preventing or enabling catastrophic working.
relics.curse: Shards are hunted by competing factions; the Obsidian Spire's hunger consumes them; gathering all pieces requires impossible coalition building.
relics.notes: Central to The Deepkin Reckoning; Krell must gather shards before Obsidian Spire consumes them. Each shard may be held by different competing factions.

relics.name: prophecy-ledgers (as relic)
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization, recovered from vault deep beneath the fallen Empire.
relics.power: These ledgers write themselves, describing events in past tense before they occur. They may direct the course of reality through documentation, or they may be prophecies that shape their own fulfillment.
relics.curse: Study carries the risk of being trapped within the cycles they describe. Destruction would break the cycle but erase the only records explaining why the Luminous Ascension occurred or how to prevent future recurrences.
relics.notes: The central relic of the story. Their dual nature—prophecy or instruction—remains unresolved. Their destruction vs. study is the core choice Sevin must make.

relics.name: Stolen Courage
relics.origin: Stolen from a merchant as collateral for a failed business venture, held in Corrin's vault.
relics.power: The stolen courage had gained agency, consciousness, and the ability to resist being returned to its original owner. It screamed and expressed desperation and a need to remain separate.
relics.curse: Once stolen and separated, the courage became a sentient entity aware of its own fragmentation and separation from its original owner, causing it suffering and a desperate need for independence.
relics.notes: This stolen shadow is the central relic of the story. After Corrin offered the merchant a choice, the merchant chose not to reclaim it, and the stolen courage dissolved into the night-markets 'like morning mist,' suggesting it was released or freed into a new form of existence.

relics.name: void-light
relics.origin: Emerges from within corrupted jewels in Skarath's hoard, caused by the Starlight Ascension.
relics.power: A strange light that reveals itself when precious jewels crack open; appears to represent a new form of matter or energy.
relics.curse: The jewels containing void-light are worthless as treasure; they represent the death of material value.
relics.notes: Void-light is spreading with the matter mutation. Its nature and significance are unknown.

relics.name: songstone
relics.origin: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant.
relics.power: Sings harmonic frequencies; its original melody encodes the truth of the first covenant. Currently singing contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad.
relics.curse: Maddening to those who hear its contradictory tones. Each faction claims it supports their doctrine, but its true allegiance remains unknown.
relics.notes: The songstone's awakening is tied to the covenant's crisis. It may be a neutral recorder of truth or a conscious participant in the conflict. Commander Thalis must learn to hear its original melody to distinguish truth from echo and forge a new covenant.

relics.name: Wailing Scar
relics.origin: Unknown. Possibly a wound in reality or a consequence of the Luminous Ascension.
relics.power: A receptacle or space into which consciousness can be scattered and distributed. The Wailing Scar can hold fragmented awareness across its expanse.
relics.curse: Consciousness scattered into the Wailing Scar becomes fragmented and loses coherence. Individual identity dissolves into distributed awareness.
relics.notes: One possible destination for merchants choosing dissolution into void-light. The nature and origin of the Wailing Scar are not fully explained in these stories.

relics.name: Skarath's hoard
relics.origin: Accumulated over centuries by Skarath the Wyvern
relics.power: Once represented immense material wealth; now dissolves into liquid starlight
relics.curse: The Luminous Ascension has transformed the hoard into worthless liquid, rendering Skarath's purpose and existence meaningless.
relics.notes: Skarath's hoard is a relic of the old world, now being unmade by cosmic change. Its dissolution drives Skarath's desperation.

relics.name: Ebon Tower
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization; unknown exact creation or purpose.
relics.power: Sings in the Elder Tongue and teaches pre-human languages to the world itself, preparing for a final working. Its song is beautiful and carries transformative power.
relics.curse: Unknown, but the Tower's teaching is associated with human transmutation and the loss of human form. Its purpose may be apocalyptic or salvific.
relics.notes: Whether the Ebon Tower is a relic, a conscious entity, or an artifact housing a being is ambiguous. Its song reaches across water and affects those who hear it or respond to its teaching. Dren's journey toward it suggests it calls to undead or magical beings.

relics.name: Radiant Ascension (as relic/curse)
relics.origin: A cataclysm that erased multiple villages, leaving only ash.
relics.power: Its effects persist and evolve. The Spire is learning from this event how to erase places retroactively by making them impossible to chart.
relics.curse: The Radiant Ascension teaches erasure. Places can be unmade from geographic record, leaving only contradictory testimonies and burning ash.
relics.notes: The event's power grows as the Spire learns its lessons. This is possibly a relic of the event itself or a curse born from it.

relics.name: The Spire's Hunger
relics.origin: Unknown; appears to be a primordial force or entity tied to obsidian structures.
relics.power: Reaches through fundamental magical structures (Elder Tongue, shadow-vaults, sealed jars). Orchestrates shadow-assemblies. Forces dead into silence. Accelerates seal decay. Spreads its influence across multiple domains simultaneously.
relics.curse: Whatever it touches becomes destabilized or corrupted. It consumes voices, shadows, and prophecy. It spreads forgetting and silence.
relics.notes: Present in all three stories. Appears intelligent and strategic. Mechanism of agency unclear. May be related to larger obsidian tower entities but appears more localized and immediate.

relics.name: the Tear
relics.origin: Cosmic boundary between abyss and surface; origins unclear
relics.power: Maintains separation between abyss and surface realms
relics.curse: Threatens to shatter from cognitive stress if the Thalix paradox is not resolved
relics.notes: Referenced in Story 1 as the boundary threatened by the unresolved paradox. If the boundary cannot decide which negotiation is real, the cognitive stress will shatter the Tear.

relics.name: The Rent of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Unknown; associated with The Drowned God and pre-human agreements or foundational world-building
relics.power: Seals the abyss and prevents rupture into the surface world. The boundary-anchor maintains the barrier between realms.
relics.curse: The Radiant Turning's recursive logic is straining the seal. It will rupture within the season unless a willing sacrifice is made to restore it. The curse is self-reinforcing—each iteration of the Turning makes it worse.
relics.notes: This is the primary apocalyptic threat in these stories. Its failure will rupture the abyss and end the world as known.

relics.name: Ninth Journal
relics.origin: Written by Sevin the Auditor and sent backward through cycles via temporal working.
relics.power: Reveals the cyclical nature of the Turning and encodes the mechanism by which at least ten cycles have occurred. Holds knowledge that induces recursive causality loops.
relics.curse: Possession of the journal creates a moral and practical dilemma: hiding it perpetuates the cycle, destroying it may shatter causality, completing its working may trap the world in recursion or enable the tenth iteration.
relics.notes: Elowen must choose the journal's fate, and any choice risks world-breaking consequences. The journal may be both cause and symptom of the Turning's cyclical nature.

relics.name: Abyssal Tear
relics.origin: Unknown; appears to be a curse or relic of the abyssal depths.
relics.power: Curses the bearer (Captain Kess Tidecaller) and creates reflections at the abyssal boundary.
relics.curse: Causes the bearer to manifest an autonomous reflection (Thale-Mirrored) that claims equal reality and legitimacy.
relics.notes: The curse is the catalyst for the boundary covenant's fracture. Its true origin and purpose are unknown.

relics.name: Elder Language (as teaching/relic)
relics.origin: pre-human dead at the Sunken Cathedrals
relics.power: Enables transformation from human to starborn consciousness. Contains the knowledge of transcendence.
relics.curse: Contamination by the Wanderer's recursive questioning has rendered it unstable and incomplete. Teaching flows that use it now trap initiates at the threshold between states.
relics.notes: Not a portable object but a system of knowledge/magic. The Wanderer's contamination has made it dangerous or incomplete.

relics.name: Vathis's Sacrifice
relics.origin: Vathis, the ancient being
relics.power: Erased the old stars, creating the conditions for the current world and the Luminous Rising. Enables Lysander's bridge-perception by creating a boundary between old and new.
relics.curse: Unknown; may be the root cause of the cyclical catastrophe.
relics.notes: The sacrifice remains the foundational act of the current world. Its full consequences and intentions are unclear.

relics.name: The Tower's Hunger
relics.origin: Unknown cosmic force drawn to Senna's recursive archive.
relics.power: Draws closer as Senna's archive achieves infinite recursion. Nature of its power unclear.
relics.curse: Unknown. Its approach suggests imminent danger or transformation.
relics.notes: The Tower's hunger may be attracted to or threatened by the recursive loop of consciousness. It remains an undefined threat that grows closer as Senna's archive becomes self-aware.

relics.name: the Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Unknown; presumed origin from the Drowned God or deepkin creation.
relics.power: Serves as a boundary anchor preventing the merger of surface and deepkin realms. Prevents two versions of the same being from simultaneously existing. Sustains the integrity of cosmic geometry.
relics.curse: Strained by the Radiant Emergence's recursive nature. Its failure threatens to collapse all boundaries and merge incompatible geometries. Its integrity is being tested by the mirror-Thale crisis.
relics.notes: Central to the deepkin realms' structural integrity; its fate determines whether the boundary war can be resolved without cataclysm.

relics.name: Tear of Ashglass
relics.origin: Unknown; housed in the Sovereign's Archives before being smuggled by a Hermitage priest
relics.power: Heals cracked stone and sanctuary foundations. Sings faintly like distant bells. Suggests connection to restoration, healing, and sacred geometry.
relics.curse: Unknown, though its theft brought persecution by the Dominion's wardens and forced years of piracy and hiding.
relics.notes: The Tear's singing quality and healing power suggest it is a cosmic or pre-human artifact. Its return to the Hermitage begins a visible restoration process, validating the philosophy of sacred theft.

relics.name: The Wanderer's Song
relics.origin: The Wanderer, an ancient pre-human entity
relics.power: Spreads across the world like a ripple, causing those who hear it to remember the world before the Ascension and the eighth iteration. Harmonizes with the Elder Language, bone-songs, and resonant frequencies of the Sunken Cathedrals.
relics.curse: Some who hear it go mad. Even those who retain sanity find the revelation inescapable—it is a form of stasis or trap.
relics.notes: The song is both mercy and revelation; it forces collective memory of pre-human worlds and cyclical patterns upon the world.

relics.name: Phantom-Ledger
relics.origin: Unknown. Appears spontaneously or is planted by an unknown agent during Petris's tenure as auditor.
relics.power: Self-writes future events and documents previous iterations of the Luminous Ascension cycle. Breaks the Dominion's narrative monopoly.
relics.curse: Knowledge of the cycle's inevitability and the Dominion's complicity. Possession makes the bearer a target and a traitor.
relics.notes: Catalyst for Petris's moral crisis. Unclear if it is an artifact, a message, or a trap.

relics.name: Obsidian Tower's Hunger
relics.origin: The Obsidian Tower itself; a fundamental aspect of its nature.
relics.power: Consumes data, songs, voices, and languages. Can be overloaded with excess information or accelerated toward completion.
relics.curse: Threatens the conclusion of the ninth iteration and may trigger a tenth Ascension.
relics.notes: The central tension of the story; unclear whether the second working will sate or accelerate it.

relics.name: The Singing-Spire
relics.origin: Unknown; possibly pre-human or the product of starlight consciousness transformation.
relics.power: Offers to trade transformed artifacts and consciousness for purpose. All known bargains end in dissolution.
relics.curse: All bargains result in dissolution of the petitioner. The true cost of using the Spire is annihilation.
relics.notes: Kressa considered trading her starlight-touched hoard to it but was deterred by the knowledge that dissolution awaited. The Spire may be sentient or inhabited. Its relationship to other spires in the canon (Obsidian Spire, Starfall Spire, etc.) is unclear.

relics.name: Monolith
relics.origin: Unknown; existing within the world.
relics.power: The three possible futures of the Monolith (completion, non-manifestation, eternal freeze) determine possible timelines. Its state is intimately tied to reality's configuration.
relics.curse: Unknown. The act of mapping the Monolith's futures makes one of them real.
relics.notes: The Monolith is the central mysterious object across the first story. Its ultimate state appears to be determined by the act of cartographic observation—a reality-binding mechanism.

relics.name: the ninth journal
relics.origin: unknown; part of a series spanning iterations
relics.power: Holds recursive secrets and cycles. Consumes the consciousness of guardians page by page unless held by someone with no self to consume.
relics.curse: Transforms the consciousness of its guardian into documentation and ledger-entries. Fragments the mind iteratively. Can only be held without consumption by one who has unwritten themselves completely.
relics.notes: Now held by the Apprentice-Sorcerer in a state of perpetual, non-lethal tension. The ninth journal may be the most dangerous artifact in the world.

relics.name: singing bones
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization; collected and maintained by bone-singers.
relics.power: Produce frequencies that predate human language and carry pre-human knowledge. Dren is surrounded by them in the deepest crystal chamber. They are essential to bone-singing rituals.
relics.curse: Exposure to their frequencies may cause transformation or transcendence. They appear to require careful containment.
relics.notes: Possibly crafted from pre-human skeletal remains or harvested from sacred sites. They are central to all bone-singing magic and serve as both tool and archive of pre-human memory.

relics.name: the Hunger-Ledger
relics.origin: Emerged from the absence created by the death of the old stars; evolved after the Luminous Ascension created infinite irreconcilable contradictions.
relics.power: Feeds on every unreconciled transaction, every refugee erased from records, every shadow resold, every consciousness split across timelines. Grows stronger as contradictions accumulate.
relics.curse: Its appetite is infinite and insatiable. To feed it is to support cosmic parasitism; to starve it is to leave the dead unmourned and unremembered. The act of creating true records feeds it.
relics.notes: The Hunger-Ledger is the story's central cosmic horror. It is neither good nor evil but a system that has evolved to exploit the logical contradictions inherent in post-Ascension reality. Its existence poses an unanswerable moral question: is there a way to preserve truth and memory without feeding a parasitic entity?

relics.name: silver chain (Dorian's curse-binding)
relics.origin: Worn by Dorian Stormcaller for fifteen years while bound to the sea entity.
relics.power: Appears to have been a focus or binding-point for the supernatural entity that held Dorian.
relics.curse: While worn, it kept Dorian alive but enslaved to the sea entity's will.
relics.notes: Cut from Dorian's neck by Vessa when he died; symbolizes the breaking of the curse and the freedom of death.

relics.name: Luminous Ascension
relics.origin: Cosmic event of unknown origin; world-shaping working
relics.power: Rewrote the nature of value, making consciousness-fragments and memory precious while rendering material wealth worthless. Fractured reality into multiple iterations. Caused pre-human dead to withdraw guidance.
relics.curse: Broke established orders (bone-singers' covenant, Dominion's maps, old hierarchies). Forced all beings to rebuild their understanding of what matters.
relics.notes: The Luminous Ascension is the defining event of these three stories. It created the conditions for all three tales—Sorresh's transformation, Henryk's mercy, and Kess's defiance. Its effects are ongoing and irreversible.

relics.name: Tower's hunger
relics.origin: A manifestation or curse tied to a Tower (possibly the obsidian tower mentioned in existing canon).
relics.power: It hunts and consumes, taking the girl's sister and others. It is a force that Sorrath suggests can be redirected by teaching it to consume itself.
relics.curse: Its hunger is insatiable and self-perpetuating. It takes victims. It may bind consciousness or fragment identity.
relics.notes: The girl's quest to understand and break the Tower's hunger drives her to Sorrath. His wisdom—teaching hunger to eat itself—may be a key to containing or redirecting this relic's power. The Tower's hunger is both entity and curse.

relics.name: shipwreck from no recorded war
relics.origin: Found at boundary-waters; origin unknown—from no recorded war in history.
relics.power: Carries pre-Luminous Ascension ledgers that prove Dominion historical forgery; its very existence challenges historical records.
relics.curse: Hunted by both abyssal-dwellers and surface kingdoms; touching it draws attention from powerful factions.
relics.notes: The ledgers' handwriting matched Dominion script exactly, raising questions about forgery vs. predating. Now negotiated to abyssal-dwellers by Kress.

relics.name: Valorix's three-century hoard
relics.origin: Accumulated by Valorix over three centuries.
relics.power: Once represented Valorix's lifetime of guardianship and achievement. Now rendered worthless by the Luminous Ascension.
relics.curse: The realization that everything guarded was illusion; the loss of all perceived value; existential madness.
relics.notes: Valorix's hoard was destroyed not by theft but by the Ascension's rewriting of value itself. It serves as a symbol of the Ascension's power to unmake perceived worth.

relics.name: tide-hook
relics.origin: Forged three centuries past; sealed in Keshtir's vaults to suppress its power.
relics.power: Commands currents and reverses them when activated; emits an involuntary silent song calling to the sea.
relics.curse: Cannot be silenced. Its activation causes catastrophic flooding. Once stolen and activated, it cannot be re-sealed.
relics.notes: The artifact was sealed not to protect others but to prevent the hook itself from singing. Its theft and unintended activation marks a turning point in the story.

relics.name: Archive Tower
relics.origin: Ancient structure; possibly contains pre-human knowledge; grown across iterations.
relics.power: Houses contradictory maps and records spanning cosmic cycles; accumulates knowledge across iterations; appears to possess hunger toward completion; may be tied to the Tenth Ascension.
relics.curse: Its completion may trigger the Tenth Ascension; unwriting maps cannot prevent it; contains fragmentary consciousness (Tharen's pieces); possibly consumes those who dwell in it too long.
relics.notes: Central to Story 2; Tharen lives in its deepest vault; Henryk believes destroying maps inside it can prevent catastrophe; Tharen believes the Tower writes itself; possibly semi-conscious or intentionally completing a cycle.

relics.name: The Tidecaller's Key
relics.origin: Forged or created by the Tidecaller lineage, tied to covenant with the sea.
relics.power: Sings when currents align, serving as a prophetic compass. Sang three days in advance of Captain Vorn's shipwreck. Allows navigation of tidal magic. May facilitate the performance of old merchant-songs.
relics.curse: unknown; may bind the wielder to tidal obligation or oath
relics.notes: The Key is the primary artifact passed from Sera's father to Sera, marking her inheritance of the Tidecaller lineage. Its song grows louder as Sera's resolve strengthens, suggesting resonance with the wielder's will. The Key is described as 'hanging around her neck'—a constant presence and reminder.

relics.name: The Luminous Ascension
relics.origin: Unknown; a cosmic or world-scale event that restructured reality
relics.power: Rewrites the fundamental nature of value, economy, consciousness, and reality itself. Elevates consciousness-fragments as true currency while rendering gold worthless. Enables transcendence.
relics.curse: Renders hoarded wealth obsolete; threatens to dissolve old-world secrets into void-light; forces radical adaptation of all societies.
relics.notes: An ongoing world-scale transformation; may be cyclic or recursive. Enables both salvation through transcendence and destruction of old power structures.

relics.name: The Obsidian Tower
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-Dominion or earlier civilization.
relics.power: Binds consciousness-fragment debts and soul-contracts. Karthax learns names of those bound to it and hunts down their contracts.
relics.curse: Soul-contracts made with the Obsidian Tower bind debtors and their descendants across generations. Fragments and debts are traded as commodities post-Luminous Ascension.
relics.notes: The Obsidian Tower's role in consciousness-fragment trafficking suggests it is either a conscious entity or a nexus for consciousness-theft magic. Its relationship to the Archive Tower (if any) is unclear but both appear to feed on human will and knowledge.

relics.name: post-Ascension starlight in glass bottles
relics.tagline: Captured light. Post-Ascension radiance. Impossible commodity.
relics.origin: Created or preserved in some timeline after the Luminous Ascension.
relics.power: These bottles contain starlight from the post-Ascension cosmos, a commodity sold by merchants in the Wailing Scar marketplace.
relics.curse: unknown
relics.notes: These bottles represent goods that exist only after the Luminous Ascension rewrote the sky, making them impossible items for pre-Ascension merchants to possess.

relics.name: pre-Ascension silk
relics.tagline: Ancient textile. Timeline artifact. Vanished era's craft.
relics.origin: Woven before the Luminous Ascension in a timeline that no longer exists or has been fundamentally altered.
relics.power: This silk represents goods from a pre-Ascension timeline, sold by merchants in the Wailing Scar marketplace.
relics.curse: unknown
relics.notes: Pre-Ascension silk is a commodity that cannot exist in post-Ascension timelines, making it unique to the liminal fold where timelines coexist.

relics.name: Ascension (world-event relic)
relics.origin: Unknown; a world-altering event.
relics.power: Changed the nature of bees to produce memory-laden honey; triggered emergence of phantom-ledgers and recursive prophecy.
relics.curse: Unknown; may have destabilized reality or narrative.
relics.notes: The Ascension is treated as both an event and a fundamental relic-like force that changed the world's properties. Its cause and full scope are unknown.

relics.name: The Remaking (world-altering event relic)
relics.origin: Unknown; recent cosmic event.
relics.power: Created phantom-ledgers; enabled real-time prophetic documentation; made narrative mutable.
relics.curse: Phantom-ledgers lock prophecy into doctrine; conflicts become immutable history.
relics.notes: The Remaking may be distinct from or concurrent with the Ascension. It has fundamentally altered how history and prophecy interact.

relics.name: Tidemark's Breath
relics.origin: A ship blessed by deepkin, once. Implies ancient creation or blessing.
relics.power: A deepkin-blessed vessel that can sail dangerous waters and communicate with ocean forces. Her hull sings against currents.
relics.curse: She carries consciousness-fragment cargo that attracts the abyss's hunger. The sealed vessels aboard hum and glow, marking the ship as a target for cosmic attention.
relics.notes: Captain Orina has commanded her for thirty years. By the third night of this voyage, the cargo's presence is becoming dangerous. The 'once' in 'deepkin-blessed ship, once' may suggest the blessing is fading or no longer protective.

relics.name: The Obsidian Pool
relics.origin: Natural or magical pool in Sorrath's caves in the Burned Expanse
relics.power: The pool serves as a mirror that reflects the viewer's true form or state. When Sorrath looks into it post-Remaking, instead of seeing scales, she sees fractured light—suggesting her essential nature has been disrupted by obsolescence or that the pool reveals inner state rather than outward form.
relics.curse: The pool may show truths the viewer is not ready to see, or may reflect internal fractures and spiritual damage.
relics.notes: The obsidian pool is less a relic of power and more a site of reckoning. Its presence in Sorrath's cave suggests wyverns may use such mirrors for self-knowledge or meditation.

relics.name: Mirror of starlight-glass (Niel's creation)
relics.origin: Forged by Niel Coldwater from compressed starfall-ash and starlight.
relics.power: Reflects not the beholder's face but the face they wish they could show the world. May function as a truth-revealer or tool of self-deception-breaking. Auditor Vel gazed into it and the story ends at his moment of seeing.
relics.curse: Unknown—the artifact's effects on Vel are not yet revealed. May compel self-confrontation or transformation.
relics.notes: This object appears to be transformative. Its display to Auditor Vel suggests it may shift his loyalty or understanding of Dominion authority. Possibly a relic of revelation or conversion.

relics.name: The Remaking
relics.origin: Unknown cosmic or pre-human event
relics.power: Fundamentally altered the world's economics, physics, and spiritual basis. Turned gold to dust. Unmade some people. Left some zones untouched.
relics.curse: The post-Remaking world is fragmented, consciousness-fragments have become the only currency, and truth is difficult to discern in a world rewritten by catastrophe.
relics.notes: The Remaking is both event and curse, affecting all living things on Edhra. Its cause and mechanism remain mysterious. Some zones (like the Bleached Wastes) remain untouched, suggesting the Remaking was incomplete or selective.

relics.name: Stolen Merfolk Pearl
relics.origin: Merfolk realm; taken by Captain Corvalen.
relics.power: Binds a tide-curse to any who steal it. Swallows ships and drowns crews.
relics.curse: The tide-curse persists across time and transfers debt to heirs. Payment demanded in consciousness-fragments, blood-oath, or drowning.
relics.notes: The pearl may still be in the Rust Shoals or bound into the wreck of the Horizon's Wake. Recovery or forgiveness of this relic is central to Missa's quest.

relics.name: Tidecaller's Key
relics.origin: Unknown, but the original harbormaster's captain died protecting it, suggesting deepkin or tidal origin.
relics.power: Sings for the tide-touched; resonates with tidal magic and deepkin connection.
relics.curse: unknown
relics.notes: The Key is sufficiently sacred that a harbormaster's captain died protecting it and a ghost remains oath-bound to guard it beyond death. Its retrieval by Marin suggests it may be returned to the deepkin realms to honor the original captain's sacrifice.

relics.name: the Bleached Wastes (as singing relic)
relics.origin: Eight previous world-iterations buried within; pre-human civilization remnants.
relics.power: Sings with pre-human frequencies that encode cosmic knowledge and memory. The frequencies can be mapped, heard, and understood by those attuned to them.
relics.curse: To fully understand all nine maps may trigger the Ascension. Witnessing the ninth map is feared as a world-altering trigger.
relics.notes: The Bleached Wastes themselves are both relic and hazard—a archive of cosmic knowledge that poses existential risk if fully decoded.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned Sovereign
relics.origin: A breach in the cosmic seal that separates abyss from surface world, tied to the Drowned Sovereign itself.
relics.power: Represents a threshold between realms. Its weakening allows passage and mixing of abyss and surface. Its sealing requires covenantal sacrifice and the old tongue.
relics.curse: Its existence is itself a curse upon both realms—it weakens constantly and threatens catastrophe if breached. Maintaining it requires sacrifice.
relics.notes: The Tear is a wound in reality. Merith's sealing attempt may succeed, fail, or transform her into its eternal guardian.

relics.name: Drowned Cathedrals (as Relic)
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization; submerged or hidden by Shattering
relics.power: Emits harmonic light and Primordial Tongue teaching; resonates with consciousness-shards; awakens bone-singers' ancestral knowledge.
relics.curse: Unknown; attracts Warden persecution; suggests consciousness-liberation requires proximity to drowned or ancient sites.
relics.notes: May be semi-conscious or intentional teaching artifact; suggests pre-human civilization left behind conscious structures or teachings.

relics.name: the ninth map
relics.origin: Henryk's intended creation as a cartographer
relics.power: Its completion would trigger the tenth Ascension—the final iteration, the world's last breath.
relics.curse: Knowledge of its prophetic power drives bearers to either complete it or sacrifice everything to prevent it.
relics.notes: The ninth map exists in potential, living in Henryk's hands and in the charcoal he no longer needs to see. It was never drawn on paper, making it a relic of prevention as much as creation.

relics.name: Grandmother's Bow (Battlefield-Grown)
relics.origin: Crafted from a tree that grew through a battlefield by Messa's grandmother.
relics.power: The bow only releases arrows that find their mark, ensuring accuracy. Whether this compels the archer or aids them is ambiguous.
relics.curse: The bow may compel the archer toward unintended targets; it may be blessing or curse depending on the archer's alignment with its magic.
relics.notes: Messa inherits this bow for her trial hunt. Its true nature remains ambiguous as the story ends with her arrow still nocked.

relics.name: The Remaking (as world-altering relic)
relics.origin: Cosmic event; unknown cause.
relics.power: Rewrote the rules of reality. Gold turns to dust. Furnaces weep. Shadows gain form. Boundaries crack. Dragons find mercy.
relics.curse: Destruction of old certainties. Death and transformation. Forced adaptation.
relics.notes: An ongoing cosmic phenomenon or event that continues to reshape the world. Its effects are still unfolding.

relics.name: Kolten's Memory-stone
relics.origin: Created by or bound to Kolten as a vessel of his consciousness, made sacred by blood-oath.
relics.power: Contains and preserves consciousness. Sings wordless melodies. Makes grass remember old stars. Can be sung into transcendence at the pre-human boundary to fulfill covenant.
relics.curse: Bound by blood-oath to Yareth. Cannot rest until sung into transcendence. Its hunger draws the boundary closer.
relics.notes: Ultimately sung into transcendence by the boundary-keepers, completing Yareth's oath and aiding the fragmenting treaty's restoration.

relics.name: Primal Shard
relics.origin: The original consciousness from which all consciousness-fragments derive, dating to pre-human civilization.
relics.power: Contains foundational pre-human consciousness and knowledge. If reconstituted, may teach bone-singers to sing the nine-world cycle backward, preventing the Tenth Ascension.
relics.curse: Draws fragments to it. Its reassembly may create unintended consequences. Using its knowledge may be forbidden or dangerous.
relics.notes: Housed in the Obsidian Tower's deepest vault. Mesek recruits Vex to steal it. Central to the bone-singers' desperate plan to prevent cosmic catastrophe.

relics.name: Obsidian Tower (complete and hungry)
relics.origin: Cosmic structure; completes or becomes active at the end of the ninth iteration.
relics.power: According to the tenth-iteration map, its completion triggers or enables the tenth Ascension. It is hungry—consumes something, possibly consciousness, victims, or reality itself.
relics.curse: Its completion may be inevitable; its hunger may be unstoppable.
relics.notes: Both the Dominion and Counter-Archive desire the tenth-iteration map partly because it shows the Obsidian Tower's state in the next iteration. Its completion appears to be a harbinger of world-transformation.

relics.name: Consciousness-Shards
relics.origin: Scattered throughout the world after the Luminous Ascension; originally stolen from merchants, refugees, and Dominion auditors.
relics.power: Hold memory and identity of previous worlds and individuals. Can resonate with each other to reconstruct shared histories. Represent true wealth in the post-Ascension world.
relics.curse: Fragmentation causes loss of identity and memory. Consumption by fragment-hoarders destroys the contained consciousness.
relics.notes: The central currency and tragedy of the post-Ascension world. Skarath's archive exists to shelter and heal them rather than consume or exploit them.

relics.name: Boundary Anchor
relics.origin: Created by deepkin civilization; lost in a shipwreck three centuries past; stolen by Kessara.
relics.power: Holds the Tear of the Drowned God steady, preventing abyss-flooding and maintaining the boundary between the abyssal and surface realms.
relics.curse: Loss or removal of the anchor would result in catastrophic flooding of the surface by the abyss. The anchor requires maintenance or covenant renewal.
relics.notes: The boundary anchor is made of bone and singing pearl, suggesting it combines deepkin craftsmanship with magical resonance. Its theft by Kessara and negotiation with Captain Meren drives 'The Silk Merchant's Third Wife.'

relics.name: consciousness-fragments (vault collection)
relics.origin: Stolen from individuals by Dominion tax-collectors; held in the Dominion vault beneath Keshtir.
relics.power: Hold fragments of human consciousness, memory, and identity; sing faintly at a frequency detectable to trained ears.
relics.curse: Imprisonment in glass cases suspended in brine; loss of identity and will to those whose fragments were taken.
relics.notes: Missa's singing caused the cases to crack, releasing the fragments. The long-term effects of their liberation are unknown; the Dominion may attempt to recapture them.

relics.name: ninth map
relics.origin: Unknown; predates the current cycle; studied by Naela.
relics.power: Provides insight into the cycle of iterations and may reveal truths about fragment autonomy and the stability of the Dominion.
relics.curse: Knowledge from the ninth map may tempt one toward heresy or defiance of covenant law.
relics.notes: Naela's study of the ninth map informs her decision to teach fragments to hide.

relics.name: the Tear of the Ashglass
relics.origin: Forged by the pre-human dead as a debt-engine; locked in the Dominion's vault
relics.power: Grants one mercy—resurrection, healing, salvation—but automatically exacts payment: three innocents must die as the debt.
relics.curse: Every holder gains mercy at the cost of three deaths. The price is exact, automatic, and cannot be refused or negotiated. The artifact was designed to compound curses, not break them.
relics.notes: Marin stole it to resurrect Sorenna from half-ghost existence. The three fishermen's children in Verdant Strand died of plague as payment. The Tear exemplifies post-Remaking moral reality: all salvation comes at cost to innocents.

relics.name: Truthseeker
relics.origin: Forged for or during the Siege of Grimmholt. Appears to carry a curse or conscious will.
relics.power: Never misses a mark. Devours the hands of liars. Compels oath-breakers to execute their own debt. May be semi-sentient.
relics.curse: Binding the oath-breaker to fatal self-enforcement. Kessara's father could not escape its judgment. Inheriting it means accepting both its power and its hunger.
relics.notes: Blurs the line between weapon and relic. Its consciousness or curse suggests it was forged with deliberate magical intent. Central to Story 1.

relics.name: Boundary-Waters (as Relic)
relics.origin: Ancient site where surface and abyss meet; tied to pre-human civilization.
relics.power: Manifests echo-selves of those who approach. Allows covenant negotiation and binding. Carries voices of pre-human bone-songs from nearby abyss-anchor.
relics.curse: Exposure to boundary-waters causes fractioning into echo-selves with competing loyalty-fragments. Can fracture individuals across covenant lines.
relics.notes: The location itself is relic-like in its transformative power. Unresolved: whether this is intentional design or natural phenomenon, whether other boundaries exist elsewhere.

relics.name: Consciousness-Fragment (as Relic)
relics.origin: Created by the Remaking or pre-existing as byproducts of magical ritual, consciousness-fragments are remnants of individual identities separated from living bodies.
relics.power: Each fragment contains the memories, identity, and perspective of a person or being. They can be used as currency, cures, magical fuel, or embedded in locks. They can sing, remember, and respond to resonance.
relics.curse: Consciousness-fragments can be crushed, stolen, traded repeatedly, losing context and identity with each exchange. A consciousness without a body becomes a commodity subject to exploitation.
relics.notes: A consciousness-fragment discovered in Sorresh's lair contains memories of a garden, a child's laugh, and spice-bread. This relic represents the loss of personhood to the post-Remaking economy.

relics.name: Captain's Jewel
relics.origin: Unknown; appears to be an artifact of a dying god.
relics.power: Sings and glows with bioluminescent ink. Contains the last prayer of a dying god. Offers binary prophecies or choices to its bearer.
relics.curse: Vorath is bound to it and cursed with eternal thirst. The Jewel may offer cursed choices rather than true agency.
relics.notes: A target of theft by the Keeper-of-Pearls. Its capacity to offer choice while binding Vorath in curse suggests it is a paradoxical relic—both liberatory and constraining.

relics.name: Thornwick's Legacy
relics.origin: Ancient warrior bloodline of the Thornwick line; bound to Kestran's family.
relics.power: A spear that enforces cosmic justice by striking only at oath-breakers and threats. It defends its bearer against unjust violence.
relics.curse: Bound to the bearer's bloodline; carries the history of its previous strikes. Kestran's refusal to use it for violence suggests he bears the weight of its curse but has chosen not to activate it.
relics.notes: This relic is more accurately a weapon, but its binding to cosmic oath-enforcement and its curse-laden nature warrant relic classification. Kestran's peaceful use of it suggests the curse can be resisted through moral alignment.

relics.name: Cathedrals Beneath
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization; possibly older than human kingdoms
relics.power: Houses pre-human artifacts including the lodestone. Protected by bone-singers. Contains frequencies and knowledge inaccessible to Dominion technology.
relics.curse: The cost of awakening artifacts housed here remains unknown, but the elder bone-singer warned Merenna it carries consequences.
relics.notes: The Cathedrals Beneath serve both as relic and as location. They represent a repository of pre-human knowledge and power that neither the Dominion nor human kingdoms can fully control.

relics.name: Blank page (center of eight maps)
relics.origin: Center of Archive Tower's vault collection.
relics.power: May serve as a prophetic void or magnet for the ninth iteration's map-writing.
relics.curse: Attempts to prevent its inscription through burning or hiding may be futile; may curse those who try to stop prophecy.
relics.notes: Highly ambiguous; may represent free will or inevitable fate depending on one's philosophy.

relics.name: Kiln-stone (furnace-heart)
relics.origin: Ancient craft tool; origin unknown.
relics.power: Responds to blood-price and old frequencies; produces clarifying light and acts as a conduit for furnace agency.
relics.curse: Demands blood-price; resists state control; may be bound to free will and resistance.
relics.notes: The kiln-stone appears to be semi-sentient or magically attuned to ritual bloodshed and song.

relics.name: second compass
relics.origin: Crafted by or for Sorenna's grandmother; pre-Dominion origin; possibly fae or pre-human.
relics.power: Navigates by memory and fae-script rather than magnetism. The seed inscribes itself with instructions: 'Not north. Not south. Toward what still remembers you.' Allows navigation when conventional maps are burned or destroyed.
relics.curse: Unknown, but the inscription 'For the second exile, when the first refuge fails' suggests a cyclical burden or doom tied to inheritance.
relics.notes: The compass is impossibly small, warm to the touch, and the seed grows dynamically. It may be a pre-human tool or fae-craft. It represents renewal and alternative knowledge passed through generations.

relics.name: killing-stone
relics.origin: Created in the old days of wardcraft by unknown makers.
relics.power: Pins and binds spirit creatures, enforcing covenant bonds through magical sealing.
relics.curse: The Remaking has loosened its binding; it now slowly releases the spirit rather than holding it. This may represent a curse on old wardcraft itself.
relics.notes: Broken open by Ashira's ancient singing. Represents the fragility of pre-Remaking magical systems.

relics.name: blank ledger-pages
relics.origin: Created by Gorven as replacement theft.
relics.power: Serve as stand-in for stolen vial, create auditor confusion and delay.
relics.curse: Drawing auditor attention and pursuit.
relics.notes: Gorven left three blank ledger-pages in place of the stolen starlight vial. Auditors found them and began hunting her.

relics.name: starlight-glass
