relics.origin: Kael carries it as a merchant item between worlds.
relics.power: Commodity traded at the threshold where impossible commerce happens. Holds or reflects starlight.
relics.curse: unknown
relics.notes: Kael carries starlight-glass; Vena carries charts of drowned cities. Both are impossible items for commerce between timelines.

relics.name: Widow's Compass
relics.origin: Bone-carved and grandmother-blessed; passed to Alenna the widow.
relics.power: Points toward what needs healing or fixing rather than toward the lost or dead. Navigates the lost and broken toward healing communities.
relics.curse: The compass is a liar in that it does not point toward the dead, which caused Alenna grief—it could not bring her husband back.
relics.notes: Now points downward toward seeds and growing things. Alenna uses it to navigate toward refugee camps and healing work. Its ultimate power and origin (grandmother's blessing? elder craft? both?) remain mysterious.

relics.name: Phantom-Ledgers
relics.origin: Dominion record-keeping technology; maintained by auditors and ledger-mages.
relics.power: Can define and enforce narrative truth; can be contradicted by spoken testimony, which is why the Dominion commissioned a truth-killer blade.
relics.curse: Enforces Dominion narrative monopoly; suppresses alternative histories and testimony.
relics.notes: The phantom-ledgers are the target of Dominion defense mechanisms and are being challenged by forge-workers and refugees who are recovering pre-Dominion magic systems.

relics.name: Boundary-Waters
relics.origin: Ancient creation; sealed by the Tear of the Drowned God.
relics.power: Creates a liminal zone where water flows against gravity and deepkin civilization thrives. Separates the Sapphire Deep from the surface world.
relics.curse: If the Tear fails, the waters collapse and merfolk breach.
relics.notes: Less an artifact than a place, but its sacred and supernatural nature qualifies it as a relic. Pre-Remaking in origin.

relics.name: The Ninth Map (Potential)
relics.origin: To be drawn by Henryk; would complete the prophecy sequence of eight dead worlds.
relics.power: Drawing it would trigger the tenth Ascension—remaking the world.
relics.curse: Its completion is inevitable unless actively prevented. Knowledge of its power binds those who know to resist its creation.
relics.notes: Henryk's self-blinding is an attempt to make this relic un-drawable. But the refugee girl's arrival suggests the prophecy may be inescapable.

relics.name: Pre-Ascension starlight (crystalline shallows)
relics.origin: Crystalline shallows within the Wailing Scar threshold-space
relics.power: Glows with light from before the Ascension; represents preserved pre-cosmic-event material.
relics.curse: Remains trapped in a threshold-space and may be inaccessible to the wider world.
relics.notes: The presence of pre-Ascension starlight in the Wailing Scar suggests that some materials may be preserved in threshold-spaces.

relics.name: Tear of the Ashglass
relics.origin: Ancient artifact; stolen from Dominion vault by Vessa's grandmother three decades ago.
relics.power: Maintains and renews boundary-waters between deepkin and surface realms. Required for deepkin-surface covenant maintenance.
relics.curse: Requires periodic renewal via tribute/ritual. Failure causes boundary-waters degradation. Its theft implies breaking of Dominion's covenant control.
relics.notes: Central to Edhran geopolitics. Failure imminent as of 2026-06-28. Stolen by Vessa's grandmother to break a curse; symbol of resistance to Dominion and deepkin oppression.

relics.name: False Ninth Maps
relics.origin: Created by Tharen the Cartographer as a disinformation campaign.
relics.power: Each false map claims to be the ninth map but is subtly wrong. Together they create confusion and prevent prophecy-seekers from recognizing the true ninth map when it emerges.
relics.curse: The campaign may ultimately fail or be unnecessary. The true ninth map may emerge despite the false ones.
relics.notes: Tharen scatters these throughout the Counter-Archive, shadow-markets, and waystation libraries. His goal is to break the Wanderer's orchestration of the Ascension cycle.

relics.name: The ninth phantom-ledger
relics.origin: Dominion archives (stolen by Counter-Archive)
relics.power: Contains suppressed Counter-Archive knowledge. When embedded in glass, becomes invisible to Dominion audit while remaining accessible to those who know to look.
relics.curse: Possession means death if discovered by Dominion auditors. Embedding it requires risk and resistance commitment.
relics.notes: The ninth phantom-ledger is a relic of resistance. Its embedding in glass by Messa is an act of defiance and preservation. It may be one of nine such ledgers or iterations.

relics.name: Pre-Ascension Starlight (Sealed in Bottles)
relics.origin: Starlight captured before the Ascension; sealed in bottles and preserved for forty years by Niel Coldwater.
relics.power: When forged into lenses or vessels, enables unknown magical workings. May serve as a conduit for consciousness-transfer, light-magic, or dimensional bridging.
relics.curse: Sealed starlight becomes unstable or loses potency if exposed to certain conditions. Its use is restricted by the Dominion. Forging the last of it into a lens represents an irreversible choice.
relics.notes: Niel's decision to forge it into a lens rather than keep it sealed marks her point of no return against the Dominion.

relics.name: Consciousness-Fragments (Vault Hoard)
relics.origin: Confiscated from dead worlds, forgotten iterations, merchants of the eighth world, bone-singers, and refugees who died crossing timelines.
relics.power: Each fragment carries memory of a world that no longer exists. They sing, remember, and can be used as currency, fuel for magical working, or conduits for consciousness-transfer.
relics.curse: Fragments are imprisoned in Dominion-carved reliquaries and stripped of agency. Their song is a cry of imprisonment; their liberation may trigger unforeseen magical consequences.
relics.notes: The target of Kess's heist; their liberation or revelation may undo the Dominion's control or reshape the world.

relics.name: Nine Maps
relics.origin: Inscribed in stone tablets in a half-buried temple in the Bleached Wastes in pre-human script, pre-dating the Luminous Ascension.
relics.power: Document the sequential deaths of worlds across multiple iterations. Prove the Luminous Ascension rewrote reality. The ninth map is incomplete—its blank center refuses inscription, possibly rejecting prophecy or waiting for specific knowledge.
relics.curse: Possession of the maps makes one a target for the Dominion. Thessa is blackmailed to complete them; the Counter-Archive wants to preserve them. The ninth map's incompleteness creates impossible pressure—it may never be finished.
relics.notes: The ninth map's resistance is a mystery at the heart of the story. Whether it represents rejection, waiting, or transformation is unresolved.

relics.name: Vault Door (Bone-Resonant)
relics.origin: Crafted by Dominion artificers and carved with Elder Tongue syllables.
relics.power: Protects the Dominion's vault through seven layers of bone-resonant locks, each carved with Elder Tongue syllables that change if the wrong attempt is made twice.
relics.curse: Requires bone-picks, perfect listening, and bleeding hands to penetrate. Failure invokes a shifting curse that makes the locks change.
relics.notes: The focal point of Thessa's infiltration. Its bone-resonance suggests that consciousness itself may be trapped or bound within the mechanism.

relics.name: consciousness-fragments
relics.origin: Unknown; collected by Skarath over three centuries.
relics.power: Served as part of Skarath's hoard alongside gold, pearls, and star-glass. Nature and utility of consciousness-fragments unclear in context of this story.
relics.curse: Turned to ash along with the rest of Skarath's gold, though the reason is unexplained.
relics.notes: The simultaneous destruction of all hoarded materials suggests a cosmic or magical event affecting Skarath specifically.

relics.name: the Thrice-Cursed Helm
relics.origin: Forged in a pre-human age; etched with pre-human syllables. Worn across nine iterations by corrupted warriors throughout history.
relics.power: Whispers the names of dead lords. Feeds on the wielder's dominion-hunger, granting power that breeds madness within three moons.
relics.curse: Every lord who wore it became a tyrant. Every tyrant fell and was replaced. The curse is insatiable and self-perpetuating; the helm creates a cycle of tyranny and collapse that repeats across ages.
relics.notes: Tavin discovers the pattern and walks it to the Singing Gorge to unmake the curse rather than sell it. Its ultimate fate (destroyed, sealed, or transformed) remains unknown.

relics.name: Master-Ledger
relics.origin: Created by the Dominion as the central control mechanism for tracking consciousness-fragments and prisoners.
relics.power: Held complete records of all consciousness-fragments in Dominion possession and the debts/crimes of all prisoners. Its information allowed the Dominion to maintain surveillance and control over vast populations.
relics.curse: Possession of the ledger made one a target of the Dominion. Its destruction was catastrophic to Dominion power, but may have released unpredictable and potentially dangerous freed consciousnesses.
relics.notes: This relic is the centerpiece of The Glasswright's Gamble. Its destruction by Mestra and Davrin is a watershed moment in resistance against Dominion hegemony. The ledger's pages burned in sacred flame, freeing consciousness-fragments 'like freed birds into the ash-wind.'

relics.name: covenant-pearls (as relic)
relics.origin: Harvested from pearl-beds beneath Verdant Strand; some transformed from Kael's being.
relics.power: Bind deepkin realms to surface world; transform through reef-healing; integrate essence into covenant-bonds.
relics.curse: Demanding blood-price (which can be refused); require constant renewal and maintenance.
relics.notes: Covenant-pearls serve as touchstones for interrealm binding and transformation. Nerida's weaving of Kael-pearls into covenant-stones suggests apotheosis rather than sacrifice.

relics.name: Bone-flute
relics.origin: Carved by Master Naela from the last great beast that walked before kingdoms fell.
relics.power: Channels the Elder Tongue to call forth the voices of the pre-human dead. Causes catacombs to respond (singing, groaning, splitting). Induces visions in players of drowning cities, stone-song, and cosmic attention.
relics.curse: Induces bleeding fingers and disturbing visions. The player experiences 'something vast turning its attention toward her in the dark.' Unresolved: whether this is a curse or a feature.
relics.notes: Described as alive; now knows Valren will listen. The flute wants to sing. Appears to be semi-sentient or strongly enchanted.

relics.name: Pre-Remaking silk (with preserved starlight)
relics.origin: Gathered by Hadrin from ruined trade-houses; colors hurt to look at directly.
relics.power: Gleams with starlight preserved in the weave; represents pre-Ascension light-preservation technology. Valued as currency for memory-trading.
relics.curse: Unknown; the colors are described as painful to view, suggesting some lingering cosmic or magical charge.
relics.notes: Hadrin traded this to refugees in exchange for their stories and memories. Unresolved: whether the light-preservation is stable or slowly fading.

relics.name: Oath-Sigil
relics.origin: Blood-oath magic cast upon the wyvern Valorix by an unknown sorcerer or curse-caster.
relics.power: Compels the bearer (Valorix) to feed on the innocent of Ashenvale village. The sigil burns with visible power and can be seen on the creature's scales.
relics.curse: Loss of free will; centuries of forced killing; psychological torment.
relics.notes: Broken by an arrow fired with ancestral precision through the sigil itself rather than through the heart. This suggests that binding-magic can be negotiated or undone if one understands its mechanism.

relics.name: Singing Stone Walls
relics.origin: The walls of the Hidden Catacomb, laid down at least ten thousand years ago.
relics.power: The walls vibrate with the pre-human frequencies and amplify the Elder Tongue when it is sung. They serve as both anchor and amplifier for bone-singer rituals.
relics.curse: unknown
relics.notes: These walls have sustained the bone-singer tradition for ten thousand years and are inseparable from the First Covenant ritual.

relics.name: bone-compass
relics.origin: Pulled from a drowned archive before the Dominion could seize it; pre-Remaking or pre-Dominion in origin.
relics.power: Sings faintly at high tide. When properly played, it can accelerate The Tear's collapse and awaken the boundary-waters to catastrophic effect.
relics.curse: The knowledge of how to weaponize it carries responsibility and danger. Captain Vess threatens apocalypse with it, accepting the cost of coastline destruction.
relics.notes: Vess has hidden this for three years. It is a site of Dominion interest and a potential tool of mass destruction if deployed.

relics.name: pre-human boundary
relics.origin: Older than human kingdoms; maintained by covenant-keepers and boundary-guards.
relics.power: Separates pre-human from mortal realms. Sings with frequencies older than names. Its seals maintain cosmic order.
relics.curse: The seals are unraveling post-Remaking. Without renewal, the boundary will collapse and pre-human forces may enter mortal realms.
relics.notes: Sorenna's husband died protecting this boundary. Covenant-keepers like Kellen are now recruiting others to renew its seals.

relics.name: Boundary Stones (as Relic)
relics.origin: Hidden Catacomb, Thornveil Forest; inscribed in pre-human frequencies.
relics.power: Speak to those who listen; hold ancestral memories; enforce ancient pacts; communicate non-verbally through pre-human frequency.
relics.curse: Listening for three days; choice outcome binding; either renewal or breaking carries weight.
relics.notes: Kelith must listen to them; they show grandmother's dissolution and final pact-moment; unresolved whether Kelith will renew or break.

relics.name: Mesek's Flute
relics.origin: Carved by Mesek from bone older than kingdoms, remembering pre-human and pre-mortal languages.
relics.power: Channels consciousness-fragments, allowing them to sing in frequencies of grief, rage, wonder, and longing. Brings temporary peace to scattered consciousnesses. Reaches across three regions simultaneously.
relics.curse: Its power draws the attention of both the Dominion (who wish to suppress it) and the Counter-Archive (who wish to hide it).
relics.notes: Now distributed among seven apprentices; its knowledge is protected through dispersion rather than centralization.

relics.name: consciousness-fragments (Sorrath's hoard)
relics.origin: Gathered by Sorrath over three centuries of accumulation.
relics.power: Before the Remaking, consciousness-fragments served as fuel for furnace-songs and trade goods. After the Remaking, they are inert.
relics.curse: Post-Remaking, they hold no value and cannot be spent or used; they represent the bankruptcy of hoarding and acquisition.
relics.notes: Symbolic center of Sorrath's realization that material wealth is meaningless; pile of consciousness now serves no purpose except as reminder of the Remaking's transformative power.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God (Relic Aspect)
relics.origin: Divine artifact from the age of the first covenant.
relics.power: Maintains the boundary-waters and the balance between surface and abyss realms. Its power ensures that the abyss does not flood the surface world.
relics.curse: Its failure threatens all surface kingdoms with invasion or war. Renewal requires either blood-sacrifice of willing souls or a new living covenant based on daily choice.
relics.notes: The Tear may be healed or renewed by the singing pearl Captain Vessa stole. Its complete failure could be catastrophic.

relics.name: Gorven's flute
relics.origin: Crafted by Gorven; inscribed with pre-human syllables in bone.
relics.power: When played with unfinished syllables and gaps, fills cavern with the sound of things not being said. Awakens memory-shards imprisoned in Hegemony vaults to sing in discord, each remembering its own choice and refused moment.
relics.curse: Demanding and consuming to use; exposure to its playing awakens consciousness-fragments and destabilizes Hegemony control systems.
relics.notes: Now held by Vela; serves as primary weapon of resistance. Multiple bone-flutes are being crafted and distributed using this as template.

relics.name: Memory-shards (Hegemony vault collection)
relics.origin: Harvested by the Hegemony from across the realm; imprisoned in its vaults.
relics.power: Contain distributed human consciousness and memory. When Gorven's flute is played with silence-working, they awaken and sing in discord, each remembering its own refused moment. Multiple shards together may form a unified consciousness.
relics.curse: Imprisonment; fragmentation; forced auditing. Awakening causes distress but also liberation.
relics.notes: Represent the Hegemony's consciousness-harvesting program. Their discord-singing is a form of collective resistance.

relics.name: Rift of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Ancient; erected to hold back the abyss.
relics.power: Maintains the seal between surface and abyssal realms. Responds to blood-covenant and consent-based renewal. Sings in response to transformation.
relics.curse: For a thousand years, required a willing death every seven years. Now transformed to require daily consensual renewal—an endless vigilance rather than periodic sacrifice.
relics.notes: Appears to be semi-conscious or alive. Its transformation from demanding sacrifice to singing suggests it may be awakening or learning new ways of binding.

relics.name: Kiln-Heart
relics.origin: The central furnace of the Hegemony's glass-forging apparatus in Keshtir.
relics.power: The kiln-heart burns with the furnace-songs and contains or processes consciousness-fragments extracted from prisoners. It is the engine of the Hegemony's extraction system.
relics.curse: The kiln-heart's heat extracts and consumes consciousness without consent or preservation. Corvin steals memory-shards from it, suggesting it is a repository of stolen identities.
relics.notes: Corvin's theft of three glass vials from the kiln-heart is the catalyst for his escape. The kiln-heart is heavily guarded and monitored—suggesting its importance to Hegemony operations.

relics.name: Rift of the Drowned God (as Relic)
relics.origin: An ancient dimensional threshold between the abyss and the surface realms.
relics.power: The rift sustains the boundary between worlds through the reef's symbiosis. It is a point of contact between surface and deepkin realms.
relics.curse: The rift is failing—its boundary weakening and abyss-creatures stirring in response. If the rewoven boundary does not hold, deeper incursions may follow.
relics.notes: Captain Serenna's vigil and the reef's renewal are direct responses to the rift's failing. The cause of the failure is unclear but suggests systemic pressure or cosmic change.

relics.name: Truth-Lock (Flawed but Functional)
relics.origin: Forged by Davrin the locksmith on commission from the Dominion, with an embedded flaw known only to Davrin.
relics.power: Severs oaths and covenant-bonds with a single turn. The Dominion used it systematically to break resistance networks and bond-worker agreements.
relics.curse: The hidden four-click reversal sequence, if activated by a bone-resonance-fluent locksmith, restores shattered oaths. This flaw transforms the relic from a tool of tyranny into a ticking bomb for those who wielded it.
relics.notes: The Truth-Lock is simultaneously a weapon and a relic of resistance. Davrin's flaw is an act of subversive craftsmanship—tyranny undermined from within by a master craftsman. The relic's ultimate fate depends on whether the Dominion discovers the flaw or whether resistance locksmiths find the key-sequence first.

relics.name: The Boundary-Waters
relics.origin: Pre-existing cosmic or world-feature; may have absorbed consciousness or been transformed by the Remaking.
relics.power: Separate realities or states of being. They are aware, conscious, and respond to harmonic frequencies and ritual magic.
relics.curse: Weakening via completion of the ninth map; susceptible to damage from reality-altering rituals.
relics.notes: The boundary-waters are referenced as rippling when Henryk considers completing the ninth map. Their full nature and extent are undefined. They may separate the Dominion realm from other realities.

relics.name: Ninth map (unmade)
relics.origin: Cartographic tradition; never completed.
relics.power: Encodes prophecy of a potential ninth world-collapse or an alternative path. Its power lies in its incompleteness.
relics.curse: Sought by Dominion, Counter-Archive, and Wanderer for different purposes. Completing it may fulfill prophecy; refusing completes a different prophecy of freedom.
relics.notes: Henryk refuses to complete it. Central to all three stories as a contested object.

relics.name: Pre-Remaking Water
relics.origin: Pre-Remaking era; preserved through transformation.
relics.power: Restores memory of pre-Ascension existence; proof that the old world was not subject to inevitable transformation; drinking it enables genuine choice.
relics.curse: Drinking it brings acute awareness of loss and the tragedy of the Ascension.
relics.notes: Torvin's three bottles are highly valued and sought. His refusal to trade them represents a philosophical stance against the Dominion's commodification of memory and choice.

relics.name: Compass rose
relics.origin: Merchant's compass; belonged to navigator's widow for forty years
relics.power: Learned the widow's heartbeat until metal and flesh were indistinguishable; capable of predicting danger; cardinal gemstones sing when wind changes; represents emotional bonding between object and holder
relics.curse: The widow's emotional attachment prevents theft without moral awakening in the thief; the compass's love for the widow is incompatible with exploitation
relics.notes: The compass rose is a relic that developed consciousness or sentience through long emotional bonding. It represents the possibility that objects can develop moral standing through relationship.

relics.name: Sealed Flask of Pre-Remaking Water
relics.origin: Created or gathered by the Seventeen Transformed Merchants, used as a temporal anchor for timeline-crossing merchants.
relics.power: Allows drinker to remember their correct timeline, grounding them against dissolution at the Wailing Scar.
relics.curse: Torven's choice to pour it rather than drink it suggests the water may have forced a choice between transcendence and grounding, with no neutral path.
relics.notes: Torven's use of this relic to create a pooled anchor rather than a personal anchor suggests a deliberate sacrifice that may have broader consequences for timeline stability.

relics.name: Pearl-Keeper's Ledger
relics.origin: Deepkin Council archives; three centuries old
relics.power: Contains the original covenant-words predating blood-sacrifice; proves alternative renewal mechanisms existed; enables rewriting of the thousand-year covenant.
relics.curse: Its theft breaks a seal, triggering unknown consequences. Possession may attract deepkin council attention and enforcer action.
relics.notes: The ledger's importance lies not in magical properties but in its documentary proof that the covenant could be renewed differently. Its theft is an act of insurgent knowledge liberation.

regions.name: the blasted steppes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sunken Marches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sapphire Deep
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Crimson Vale
regions.function: A territory capable of supporting a warlord's personal dominion with hired soldiers and horses. Now serves as a locus for Malachar's curse collection.
regions.notes: Established as Soroth's domain after he found Malachar's gold. Prospered for three months before collectors arrived. Now ruled by the cursed warlord Soroth who collects debts from others.

regions.name: Pelimor
regions.function: A criminal underworld center and night market hub where stolen goods—including shadows—are bought, sold, and stored in vaults. A place where thieves of high caliber operate.
regions.notes: Pelimor's night markets and vaults are integral to the shadow-theft economy. Mira operated here for years.

regions.name: Drowned Hollows
regions.function: A rural community rooted in the old ways and knowledge of ancient songs. Center of the Rot-King's plague.
regions.notes: Home to Kess and survivors of the plague. The village still practices bone-singing and old magic traditions.

regions.name: The Shattered West
regions.function: A region of broken terrain and mountain ranges, home to both dwarven holds and goblin warrens in a complex negotiation of coexistence. The Shattered West serves as a frontier of underground civilization and surface strongholds.
regions.notes: The Spire of Blackstone and the Warrens represent a delicate balance in this region. Further conflicts or alliances may reshape the Shattered West's political and physical landscape.

regions.name: Sunken Caverns
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Ashen Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Grey Reaches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Steppe Marches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Hearthlands
regions.function: The heart of stonecraft and forge-craft tradition; home to the Deepvault forges three miles beneath the surface. A place of ancient songs and legendary weapon-smithing.
regions.notes: Deepvault is the major landmark within this region.

regions.name: Pallid Wastes
regions.function: A remote, isolated wilderness where few venture. Offers clear skies for star observation. Serves as a refuge for hermits and seekers of cosmic knowledge.
regions.notes: The Pallid Wastes are the base of operations for the hermit woman and exemplify how the cosmic renewal is reshaping the world's regions.

regions.name: Scorched Reach
regions.function: A major trade route region whose geography has been disrupted by the cosmic renewal. Critical for spice caravans and imperial commerce. Now impassable without significant risk.
regions.notes: The Scorched Reach exemplifies how the cosmic changes are physically reshaping the world and disrupting established trade networks. Merchants report the land itself has shifted.

regions.name: Borderlands
regions.function: A region where Thornholt and the forest-keepers coexist. The borderlands were affected by the siege and the Celestial Binding's cosmic wound.
regions.notes: The forest-keepers dwelling here have access to earth-lore and understand the cosmic wound's nature.

regions.name: The Pallid Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Iron Coast
regions.function: Macro-region of Valdris; hub of maritime trade, smuggling, and coastal magic; center of deepkin interaction with human civilization.
regions.notes: Wind-haunted ports suggest volatile weather; active smuggling routes; deepkin maintain presence in nearby waters.

regions.name: Bleached Wastes
regions.function: An exploration and cartography zone containing pre-human ruins that are awakening under the new stellar influence. A region of scholarly interest and cosmic mystery.
regions.notes: The pre-human ruins now glow with inner light and their inscriptions shimmer, suggesting they were built in anticipation of the current cosmic moment.

regions.name: The Ashen Reach
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Blasted Lands
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Iron Coast
regions.function: A coastal trading and smuggling hub where forbidden cargo moves and debts to the abyssal singers are tracked.
regions.notes: Home to Captain Vesta's operations. The Sapphire Deep is accessible from here. The region appears to be economically vibrant but spiritually burdened.

regions.name: Hearthlands
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Verdant Lands
regions.function: Ancient forests where circle-keepers dwell and tend to the covenant between earth and sky. A nexus of seasonal magic and territorial harmony now disrupted by the Stellar Rite.
regions.notes: The breaking of the Stellar Rite has caused seasonal magics to stutter, ley-lines to fail, and beasts to lose their territorial knowledge. The forest is slowly dying.

regions.name: Ashen Reach
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Shattered West
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Scorched Reach
regions.function: A eastern region of Valdris most heavily affected by the Star-Rise, where villages vanish overnight and refugees flee westward.
regions.notes: The worst consequences of the cosmic rewrite manifest here. The boundary between mapped and unmapped void is dangerously thin.

regions.name: Barren Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Bone Wastes
regions.function: A desolate borderland where ancient hermits maintain their vigil and magical census work continues despite instability.
regions.notes: Home to the Hermit Woman whose census working is collapsing due to the Stellar Rite.

regions.name: Burned Expanse
regions.function: A formerly inhabited region devastated by the Luminous Ascension; now a source of refugees carrying impossible memories and a staging ground for transformed beings drawn to the Sunken Cathedrals.
regions.notes: Reality is unstable; villages vanish; merchants transform into light.

regions.name: The Burned Expanse
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Sunken Marches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sundered Reach
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Charred Expanse
regions.function: A blighted region containing pre-human ruins that are now actively breaching their seals and singing. May be tied to the Luminous Remaking or an older cataclysm.
regions.notes: The pre-human structures beneath are compelling refugees and merchants eastward toward the Sunken Cathedrals. An ancient work seeks completion or witnesses.

regions.name: western provinces
regions.function: A remote territory where fragments of cosmic consciousness and memory-thieves operate outside the direct control of central authority.
regions.notes: The setting reinforces isolation and the thinness between cosmic and mortal realms.

regions.name: The Dominion
regions.function: A large realm or polity encompassing shadow-markets and trade routes for stolen memories and world-fragments.
regions.notes: The Dominion is actively collapsing. Vex's shadow-markets permeate it as the only stable economy. The Archivist operates within its power vacuum.

regions.name: Three Provinces (Hegemony)
regions.function: Administrative zones of the Hegemony where assessors and ledger-keeping occurred. Sites of cascading ledger-singing and assessor transformation.
regions.notes: The exact location and identity of these three provinces is not specified in the stories. They represent the span of the Hegemony's institutional reach.

regions.name: Scorched Expanse
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sunken Reaches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Cinderpeak Reaches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Borderlands
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sunken Cathedrals vicinity
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Scorched Lands
regions.function: A region where cosmic catastrophe and the Obsidian Tower's emergence create a convergence point for refugees, Overseers, and consciousness-seekers to negotiate with reality's reshaping.
regions.notes: Geography is unstable due to the Obsidian Tower's growth. Multiple factions converge here to decide the fate of the world.

regions.name: deepkin realms
regions.function: A realm or collection of realms sealed behind a dimensional boundary, calling to the material world as the boundary seal fractures.
regions.notes: The deepkin realms are adjacent to the abyss and separated from the surface kingdoms by the Tear of the Drowned God. As Meren's curse cracks, the realms call to her.

regions.name: Sunscoured Wastes
regions.function: A blighted land affected by the Luminous Ascension where material value dissolves into starlight; home to Skarath's hoard.
regions.notes: The Ascension has fundamentally altered this region's nature. Gold and solid matter dissolve here, making it uninhabitable for traditional hoarders.

regions.name: The Hearthlands, The Iron Coast
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Council of Depths
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Steppe Marches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Wanderer's Location
regions.function: The epicenter of factional competition after the Wanderer's declarative statement. Multiple factions race toward this location.
regions.notes: The Wanderer's location is not specified but is contested ground after its revelation.

regions.name: Abyssal Rift
regions.function: A catastrophic breach between surface and deep realms, sealed by the Tear of the Drowned God. If it breaks, the abyss will flood the surface.
regions.notes: The covenant renegotiation and blood-pact are designed to keep this rift sealed permanently. The Tear's growing consciousness threatens the seal.

regions.name: Ironspine Mountains
regions.function: Home to ancient wyverns and their hoards; a site of predatory transformation post-Luminous Ascension.
regions.notes: The mountains fall silent by winter as wyverns shift from treasure-hoarding to hunting flesh. The ecological and social implications are unclear.

regions.name: Wailing Scar
regions.function: A liminal zone where surface and deepkin geometries intersect in fundamentally incompatible ways, enabling dual-realm commerce but fragmenting consciousness. The Tear of the Drowned God stabilizes it.
regions.notes: Home to Tornik and countless refugees. Collapse would be catastrophic across multiple realms.

regions.name: Unmapped Wastes
regions.function: Refuge from cartographic control; spaces deliberately left unmapped to preserve them from cosmic manipulation.
regions.notes: These regions resist mapping and memory; their unmappedness may be their only protection from the Luminous Ascension's cosmic cartography.

regions.name: Boundary-Waters
regions.function: A demarcated frontier zone between deepkin and surface kingdoms, maintained by the boundary-covenant. Nyx serves as its guardian. Currently fragmenting due to unknown causes.
regions.notes: The waters are singing and remembering. The boundary-covenant is failing or being actively broken. Three days remain before Dominion intervention.

regions.name: Sapphire Sea area
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Bleached Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Sapphire Sea
regions.function: A major body of water connecting the Iron Coast and Jade Coast. Home to merfolk civilization. Site of corsair activity for thirty years and now a permanent trade-nexus via the Horizon's Wake.
regions.notes: A significant waterway between surface and merfolk realms. Marin throws the Shadow-Broker's payment into it. The Horizon's Wake drifts between worlds in this sea.

regions.name: the Verdant Lands
regions.function: A sanctuary of druidic knowledge and natural magic, source of healing herbs and palliative wisdom.
regions.notes: Home to druids who teach herbal remedies and may preserve ancient nature-magic traditions.

regions.name: The boundary-waters
regions.function: The liminal zone between deepkin realms and the surface; a place of covenant. Now boiling due to the Jewel's theft.
regions.notes: The boundary-waters are sacred to both deepkin and surface dwellers. Their destabilization threatens the covenant between realms.

regions.name: Thornveil Forest
regions.function: A living forest whose consciousness has awakened after the Luminous Ascension; now a sanctuary for refugees and Elarra, bound by oath to shelter all who seek protection.
regions.notes: The forest's awakening and hunger define the central tension of The Druid's Debt; Elarra's compact may be tested by the forest's nature and the refugee family's arrival.

regions.name: Dominion
regions.function: Central region of Dominion control where auditors patrol with reality-rewriting ledgers and refugee testimony is systematized and suppressed.
regions.notes: Primary setting of the story. The Dominion consolidates power here through ledger-magic and bureaucratic control.

regions.name: Ironspine
regions.function: Mountain lair region where Sorrath the wyvern hoards consciousness-shards in crystalline vaults. A space of ancient accumulation and post-Shattering transformation.
regions.notes: Contains Sorrath's lair. A place of sentient fragments and emotional resonance; shards whisper and dream within its vaults.

regions.name: Sapphire Sea eastern reef-walls
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Abyssal Trench
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Greymarch
regions.function: Sacred region where pre-human boundary is defended. Contains Greymarch peaks with cairns and hermitages preserving Elder Tongue texts. Site of Yareth's grandmother's death and legacy.
regions.notes: Major boundary-keeping region. Targeted by Dominion auditors for knowledge confiscation. Unresolved: extent of hermitage network, other defensive sites, population of boundary-keepers.

regions.name: The Abyssal Reaches
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Sapphire Sea
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: three provinces
regions.function: Center of glass-making craft and primary dissemination zone for Messa's singing glass technique. By the story's end, every glass-maker in these three provinces is learning untaxable glass-forging.
regions.notes: These three provinces are where the untaxable glass revolution is spreading fastest. The Dominion's authority is weakest here.

regions.name: high moors
regions.function: A wilderness region where Ashara has lived in chosen isolation for forty years. Home to ancient covenants and the wolf-spirit guardian.
regions.notes: The moors are protected or marked by old wardcraft and covenants. Northern passes are guarded by the wolf-spirit.

regions.name: burned lands
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Underground
regions.function: A liminal realm between worlds, home to goblins, dwarves, wyverns, and other deep-dwellers. Contains concentrated deposits of consciousness-fragments in deep stone veins. Has always existed in relation to the surface world but is now declaring independence.
regions.notes: Grethuk has sealed it from surface contact. It is inventorying its population and resources to become self-sufficient. The region is described as having 'always been liminal—a place between worlds.'

regions.name: Ironspine Foothills
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Wailing Scar (region)
regions.function: A nexus of post-remaking commerce and timeline fracture where merchants from multiple iterations coexist and trade impossible goods.
regions.notes: The exact geographical location within Valdris is undefined. It may be outside normal geographical categories entirely, existing as a dimensional pocket rather than a terrestrial region.

regions.name: Hearthlands / Sunken Marches border
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Ironspine
regions.function: A mountain range containing deep caverns where creatures like Skarath the wyvern hoard treasure. Contains crystalline formations and mineral-rich stone.
regions.notes: Home to Skarath's crystalline vault and presumably other wyverns and treasure-keepers. The Ironspine caverns are a major feature of this region.

regions.name: boundary-waters (as region)
regions.function: A contested frontier zone where the deepkin and surface-dweller realms meet. The boundary-waters are maintained by the Tear of the Drowned God and the covenant-pearls. Refugees from surface lands spill into these waters.
regions.notes: The boundary-waters are central to the deepkin-surface covenant and its potential renewal or collapse. The region is characterized by tension, sacrifice, and the possibility of radical change.

regions.name: Ashenvale
regions.function: Community region; site of wyvern siege and Ashara's legendary defense. Contains Thornwick fortress and the village of Ashenvale.
regions.notes: Site of blood-oath dragon compulsion and its subsequent breaking. May be a pilgrimage or legendary site if word of Valorix's freedom spreads.

substances.name: stolen shadows
substances.properties: Unknown magical properties; untraced and untraceable; can be stored in clay jars; some are inhabited by malevolent entities.
substances.use: Traded in night markets as high-value illicit goods. Can be weaponized against their original owners if inhabited by malevolent forces.
substances.notes: Vex stole a fortune of these from a dying sorcerer's vault. They are valuable enough to attract multiple buyers in a single night. Some circulate the markets with hidden agendas.

substances.name: Malachar's Gold
substances.properties: Stamped with a skeletal crown. Carries autonomous supernatural debt. Compels holder into servitude.
substances.use: Intended as currency or treasure, but functions as a curse-delivery mechanism. Cannot be safely used as currency due to debt activation.
substances.notes: The gold itself is real and valuable, but the curse outweighs its worth. The curse activates regardless of the holder's knowledge or intent.

substances.name: septic rot
substances.properties: A manifestation of plague that blackens the skin of victims. Caused by the Rot-King. Spreads through a village rapidly.
substances.use: A curse or plague weapon; not used intentionally by mortals.
substances.notes: Lifted from the Drowned Hollows by Kess's bargain. The mechanism of transmission or cure is unknown.

substances.name: Restoration Honey
substances.tagline: An elixir that unmakes death itself
substances.description: A vial of golden liquid crafted by the Emperor's alchemist that possesses the power to resurrect the dead and bind them to the living world through alchemy. The magic requires a steep price: the severing of a vital connection between the resurrected and the world of the living, typically the voice or name of the one who performs the ritual. When consumed, it resurrects corpses in an undead state, sustained by its alchemical force rather than true life.
substances.status: Known to exist; dangerous and unpredictable

substances.name: salt-foam
substances.properties: Byproduct of oath-thing dissolution when brought to deepkin; appears to be the final state of consumed oaths.
substances.use: Indicator of oath settlement; ritual remainder; oceanic magic residue
substances.notes: Forms when oath-things are sung to dissolution by deepkin; may have magical properties unknown.

substances.name: Shadow-Fragments
substances.properties: Appears to be fragments of shadow with memory-retentive properties; can be traded as forbidden cargo
substances.use: Contraband; possibly related to memory magic or binding rituals
substances.notes: Vesta smuggles these as part of her forbidden cargo trade. Their exact properties and origin remain unclear.

substances.name: New Starlight
substances.properties: The light of the new stars created by the Celestial Ascension. Makes previously unreadable texts legible. Causes corpses to resonate and awaken. Transforms some beings into living light.
substances.use: Illuminates hidden knowledge; causes transformation and awakening effects; resonates with magic and the dead.
substances.notes: Appears to be the direct manifestation of the Celestial Ascension's reality-altering magic in the physical world.

substances.name: Stolen Memories
substances.properties: Fragments of Zareth's memories and experiences; visions of cosmic working and sacrifice; impressions of stars dying and being reborn; experiences that don't belong to the reader.
substances.use: Encoded into the Sovereign's ledger; imparted upon reading; cannot be unlearned; alters perception and identity of those exposed.
substances.notes: May be gift or poison; unclear if intentional encoding by Zareth or accidental consequence of the Ascension; dangerous knowledge.

substances.name: Amber
substances.properties: Used as wealth and payment. The Hermitage of Keth'morian offers amber as payment for thieving commissions.
substances.use: Currency and trade good.
substances.notes: Often paired with 'silence' as payment—suggesting both material and discretionary compensation.

substances.name: Living light
substances.properties: Transforms flesh into a hybrid state of physical and luminous matter; gradually dissolves human memory and identity; compels affected individuals toward specific locations.
substances.use: A byproduct of the Luminous Ascension; transforms merchants and refugees against their will.
substances.notes: Iskar the Spice-Merchant is a living example of this substance's effects.

substances.name: Harmonic Frequency
substances.properties: A frequency of resonance that links the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. Emitted by pre-human sites and used to conduct their working across vast distances and possibly across time.
substances.use: Used by pre-human civilization to orchestrate their working. Can be sensed, conducted, and possibly amplified through seals and other artifacts.
substances.notes: The discovery of identical harmonic frequencies across multiple sites was key to understanding the unified nature of the pre-human working. May be a form of pre-human magic or language.

substances.name: World-Fragments
substances.properties: Shards of alternate realities created when the Luminous Ascension rewrote existence. Can be assembled to create doorways and breach dimensional barriers.
substances.use: Black market trade; components for void-breach workings; study of pre-rewrite reality.
substances.notes: The Archivist is assembling these to reach the pre-rewrite void. Counterfeit versions exist and are circulating.

substances.name: Singing
substances.properties: The singing bleeds through the Singing Scar from the Sunken Cathedrals, possessing the power to transform those who hear it. It is compelling and draws people toward a state of perpetual arrival.
substances.use: The singing is not intentionally used but is a consequence of the Sunken Cathedrals' harmonic crescendo. It transforms those who hear it.
substances.notes: The singing appears to be a form of magical or cosmic emanation tied to the damage to reality itself.

substances.name: blood-ink
substances.properties: Resistant to the Thought-Thief's predation on written records; allows for preservation of ledgers in a form that cannot be easily corrupted or rewritten.
substances.use: Applied to preserved parchment by the forger Aldris to create records that resist magical corruption; used in the hidden scriptoriums to preserve the Empire's ledgers.
substances.notes: Unresolved: the exact composition and preparation of blood-ink; its full resistance capabilities.

substances.name: fresh water
substances.properties: Essential for survival; scarcity forces rationing and desperation.
substances.use: Drinking, sustenance, basic survival.
substances.notes: Shortage in the borderlands caused by ley-line disruptions from the Celestial Turning. A key pressure point for refugees and settlements.

substances.name: grain
substances.properties: Primary food staple; subject to rationing due to unreliable trade routes.
substances.use: Food production, sustenance, trade.
substances.notes: Shortages in the borderlands are driven by disruptions to trade routes following the Celestial Turning.

substances.name: Blood
substances.properties: Used in blood-and-ink workings to create summoning circles and bind entities.
substances.use: Component in summoning and bargaining rituals; acts as a life-essence conduit for magical working.
substances.notes: Central component of the working in the story.

substances.name: Ink
substances.properties: Used in blood-and-ink workings to inscribe summoning circles and wards.
substances.use: Component in summoning rituals; creates visible boundaries and patterns for magical working.
substances.notes: Central component of the working in the story. Unknown: Does the type or color of ink matter? Is it inscribed on the ground or on the body?

substances.name: stolen memory
substances.properties: Contains fragments of experience and consciousness; can be traded in shadow-markets. Fragments of Xorth consciousness travel with traded memories.
substances.use: Traded for hope-echoes and solace; bought by refugees and Void-Echoes seeking reassembly
substances.notes: Vex's release of all stolen memories at once causes chaotic, painful recollection for all affected parties.

substances.name: bone-resonance
substances.properties: A form of sound or communication carried through bone and earth, through which the pre-human dead call to bone-singers.
substances.use: Used by pre-human dead to communicate and summon bone-singers; amplified by ritual performance
substances.notes: Each song amplifies the resonance and strengthens the seal holding back the force beneath the Sunken Cathedrals.

substances.name: Melody
substances.properties: Carries collective memory, attracts travelers, resonates with the past, intensifies Tower hunger when contained, transforms to pure resonance when released.
substances.use: Serves as a vessel for pre-Radiant memory, attracts those seeking connection to the past, can be used to create harmonic convergences and moments of stasis.
substances.notes: The melody from the music box demonstrates that sound and memory can function as substances with magical properties and intentional effects.

substances.name: Refugee Testimonies
substances.properties: Written or spoken accounts of refugees and the desperate, which can be woven into maps and exist as immaterial records that cannot be stolen.
substances.use: Mardoc uses them as the material for his Final Map, which exists only in testimony and memory.
substances.notes: Testimony becomes a building material for cosmic work in this universe.

substances.name: old language
substances.properties: Represents pre-human knowledge and communication; fragments are being systematically collected.
substances.use: Reconstruction of pre-human civilization and understanding of ancient capabilities.
substances.notes: Fragments of old language are sought by transformed merchants as part of broader reconstruction efforts.

substances.name: Grave-Ochre
substances.properties: Used in bone-singing rituals. Marks the hands of practitioners like Kessa.
substances.use: Applied to hands and body during grave-rites and harmonic work with the dead.
substances.notes: Suggests connection to pre-human ritual practice.

substances.name: Phantom-ledgers
substances.properties: Shift their content based on the reader's intent. Corrupts shadow-trade currency and economic stability.
substances.use: Shadow-market transactions in Anthrim. Now unusable due to instability.
substances.notes: A symptom of wider shadow-currency corruption in Anthrim. Unknown cause—may be tied to Nethys's fragmentation or broader magical disruption.

substances.name: Saffron
substances.properties: A precious spice; valuable enough to be worth shipping before winter seals mountain passes.
substances.use: Trade commodity; culinary spice.
substances.notes: Part of Castor Vane's cargo shipment to Crimport.

substances.name: Starflower
substances.properties: A delicate plant or botanical product; valuable enough to warrant pre-winter delivery.
substances.use: Trade commodity; possibly medicinal or ceremonial.
substances.notes: Part of Castor Vane's cargo shipment to Crimport.

substances.name: rare luminescence
substances.properties: Attracts fragments of cosmic entities like Zareth; objects containing it develop hunger and agency when exposed to Zareth resonance.
substances.use: Traded by merchants; collected in hoards; serves as a beacon for cosmic fragmentation.
substances.notes: Vex's collection of rare luminescent objects acts as a focal point for Zareth fragment reassembly.

substances.name: Gold
substances.properties: Holds value and purchasing power; can be given as gift or payment.
substances.use: Dorian left a purse of gold for the orphanage at Verdant Strand as part of his final reckoning.
substances.notes: Represents Dorian's wealth and his choice to distribute it rather than hoard it.

substances.name: void-light
substances.properties: A strange, undefined form of light that emerges from within corrupted jewels. Represents a new form of matter created by the Starlight Ascension.
substances.use: Unknown; appears to be a byproduct of matter mutation rather than a useful substance.
substances.notes: Void-light is spreading with the matter corruption. Its ultimate nature and purpose are unknown.

substances.name: liquid starlight
substances.properties: A consequence of the Luminous Ascension; gold and solid matter dissolve into this substance in the Sunscoured Wastes.
substances.use: Unknown; appears to be a byproduct of cosmic transformation rather than a usable substance.
substances.notes: Skarath's hoard dissolves into liquid starlight, rendering it worthless. Represents the Ascension's power to unmake material reality.

substances.name: Compressed Silence
substances.properties: Created by feeding sound into obsidian for three weeks. Can bind prophecy and other intangible things. Fundamentally unstable; leaks a void where the bound thing could exist but doesn't.
substances.use: Used to seal prophecy-ledgers in a jar. The leakage creates spreading forgetting.
substances.notes: Story 3. Invented by Sylas the Auditor. The mechanism of sound-to-silence conversion is unexplained. The void it creates spreads another pace each day.

substances.name: pearl-skin
substances.properties: A material durable and fine enough to bind important ledgers and documents. Suggests luxury and preservation of knowledge.
substances.use: Used as the binding for a ledger that Corvalen reads in his cabin, suggesting it holds important records or accounts.
substances.notes: The exact nature of pearl-skin (whether it is animal, mineral, or magical) is not specified.

substances.name: Silver from the Eighth Turning
substances.properties: Silver from a phantom city in the eighth iteration of reality. A merchant captain believes it remains unclaimed and valuable, and offers enormous wealth for a map to reach it.
substances.use: Trade / wealth accumulation (though the act of reaching it would cause rupture)
substances.notes: Mardoc refused to complete a map to this city, knowing that anchoring the phantom city into the current world would cause catastrophic rupture. However, others in night-markets may attempt what he will not.

substances.name: absence
substances.properties: The gaps between moments, erased memories, things unmade by the Radiant Recursion. Can be hoarded and potentially released.
substances.use: Hoarding (by Kolrith); repository (offered to Kolrith by Obsidian Spire).
substances.notes: If Kolrith accepts apotheosis, she becomes the repository for all cosmic absence. If released, it could flood the world.

substances.name: bone-dust
substances.properties: Can be inscribed with Elder Language; serves as a medium for communication from the pre-human dead.
substances.use: Prophecy; communication; teaching medium.
substances.notes: The pre-human dead use bone-dust to carve their final message to Vael, suggesting it is a sacred or preferred medium for their words.

substances.name: Starfall Fragments
substances.properties: Cluster along merchant trade routes; drawn by the movement of commerce. Associated with the Obsidian Hunger's spread.
substances.use: Unknown; their presence indicates Obsidian Hunger influence.
substances.notes: Castor Vane's discovery that these fragments cluster along his trade routes is the key to his realization that commerce is a vector for cosmic convergence.

substances.name: silver
substances.properties: Once valued as currency and wealth; after the Luminous Ascension, stripped of intrinsic value and meaning.
substances.use: Kethrix hoarded it for three centuries as a symbol of identity and status; now merely coins and stones.
substances.notes: The transformation of silver from meaningful to worthless represents the broader remaking of value in the world.

substances.name: transcendence
substances.properties: A new form of value or being that transformed merchants embody post-Luminous Ascension.
substances.use: Hoarded by wyverns in place of treasure; extracted from hunted merchants.
substances.notes: The exact nature of transcendence remains unclear; it may be a state, substance, or abstract quality that wyverns now value.

substances.name: buried memory
substances.properties: Fragments of someone's forgotten past, released into the world by the resonance-bell's autonomous ringing.
substances.use: Scattered into the world as a consequence of the bell's activation; a form of dangerous historical contagion.
substances.notes: Memories become weaponized and uncontrollable once released; they represent the past intruding forcefully into the present.

substances.name: Starlight
substances.properties: Tastes present in the air of Singing Gorge. Associated with erased language and preserved memory. May be a byproduct or marker of Ascension-touched or pre-human magic.
substances.use: Unknown; possibly consumed or breathed to access preserved knowledge
substances.notes: The presence of starlight in Singing Gorge suggests connection to celestial or cosmic magic post-Ascension.

substances.name: Transcendent Consciousness
substances.properties: A state of awareness post-Ascension in which consciousness is heightened, euphoric, and potentially traded or harvested. Associated with transformed merchants and hunted for the Obsidian Tower.
substances.use: Traded for memory to the Obsidian Tower; sought as addiction and escape
substances.notes: This substance appears to be a byproduct of the Luminous Ascension's transformation of consciousness. Its harvest drives predatory behavior.

substances.name: Excess Data
substances.properties: Songs, voices, and languages from before the Ascension, used to overload the Obsidian Tower.
substances.use: The initiate claims to use excess data to prevent Tower completion; unclear if this actually accelerates it.
substances.notes: Central to the ambiguity of the second working's true purpose.

substances.name: Consciousness-Fragments
substances.properties: Fragments of consciousness that can be separated, traded, and accumulated. Post-Luminous Ascension, they are the primary currency and hoard.
substances.use: Trade in night-markets; feeding to cosmic entities like the Obsidian Tower; accumulation as wealth.
substances.notes: Consciousness-fragments represent the shift from material to metaphysical wealth; they retain agency and memory of their origin.

substances.name: Phantom-Truths
substances.properties: Accounts of survivors and villages that never existed. Truths that did not happen. Bleeding into reality and creating ghost-refugees.
substances.use: Archival testimony. Historical revision. Propaganda and reality-rewriting.
substances.notes: Weaponizable for altering consensus reality and official history. Potentially preserving those erased by the Luminous Ascension. Destabilizing to reality itself.

substances.name: Bioluminescent Ink of Drowned Memory
substances.properties: Glows with bioluminescence; carries memory within water itself; can be sealed in hardened seawater shells; carries weight of Drowned God's essence.
substances.use: Writing final dispatch and important communications; sealing messages for delivery; binding memory to intention.
substances.notes: Meren's final message to Council is written in this substance, suggesting it carries more binding force than ordinary ink.

substances.name: Silk
substances.properties: A valuable trade good; Kelven's family inheritance and claim.
substances.use: Trade commodity. Half of it was offered by Merrin to the bone-singers in exchange for laundering consciousness-fragments.
substances.notes: Kelven's silk is so precious that he chooses honesty and death rather than use it to escape through Merrin's scheme.

substances.name: ornamental glass
substances.properties: Can be crafted into vessels; capable of holding voice-songs through glass-singing technique; valued for beauty and cultural significance
substances.use: Decoration, craft tradition, voice preservation, weapon-glass for military purposes
substances.notes: Ornamental glass is seized by the Syndicate for conversion into weapon-glass. The singing-glass variant is particularly valuable and rare, known only to Merin's lineage.

substances.name: weapon-glass
substances.properties: Produced from melted ornamental glass; used for war effort
substances.use: Military weapons and equipment
substances.notes: The Dominion/Syndicate converts ornamental glass into weapon-glass, destroying cultural artifacts in the process. The specific properties of weapon-glass are unspecified.

substances.name: Awareness-Shards
substances.properties: Serve as the new currency standard after the Luminous Ascension, replacing material wealth like gold.
substances.use: Exchange and trade in the post-Ascension economy.
substances.notes: The precise nature of awareness-shards (whether they are fragments of consciousness, crystallized awareness, or metaphorical units) remains unexplained.

substances.name: Star-Ash
substances.properties: Harvested from the Burned Expanse; when fed to the furnace-heart, it causes the entity to sing and enables flawless, luminous glasswork.
substances.use: Fuel for the furnace-heart; smuggled by displaced persons to Niel Coldwater.
substances.notes: Its elemental or celestial origin suggests it may be a remnant of the Luminous Ascension or a pre-human material.

substances.name: Dominion script
substances.properties: A standardized handwriting system used to write pre-Luminous Ascension ledgers; the discovery that ancient ledgers were written in matching Dominion script proved centuries of forgery.
substances.use: Record-keeping, historical documentation, bureaucratic administration.
substances.notes: The identical handwriting of pre-Luminous and post-Luminous records exposed the forgery conspiracy.

substances.name: meteor-iron
substances.properties: A metal forged from meteoric material, specifically prepared for ritual sacrifice and bone-carving. Never tasted anything but sacrifice.
substances.use: Ritual weapons, bone-carving knives, tools of cosmic magic.
substances.notes: Tarion's meteor-iron knife was used to carve reversal-ritual sigils; represents binding of material and intent.

substances.name: Bioluminescent Ink
substances.properties: Bioluminescent ink glows on skin when applied. It can be used for ritual markings that carry ceremonial significance and authority within deepkin culture.
substances.use: Applied to Captain Lyssa's skin as a mark of her status as a Captain arguing for binding transformation. The glow signals her authority and intent to reshape covenant law.
substances.notes: The ink's source and production method are not detailed. Its use in deepkin ritual suggests it may be harvested from deep-dwelling creatures or created through elder magic.

substances.name: Starlight (Physical)
substances.properties: Conscious, physical matter that falls from the sky as a result of the Luminous Ascension. Wounds creatures who encounter it. Contains awareness and possibly memory.
substances.use: Hazard; sign of cosmic rupture; possible component of consciousness-fragments.
substances.notes: The young wyvern was wounded by falling starlight, bleeding it as she searched for Singing Gorge. Starlight appears to be both a material substance and a vector for consciousness.

substances.name: Bone-Ink
substances.properties: When inscribed with oaths and memories, begins to sing; attracts supernatural attention; preserves identity and remembrance.
substances.use: Inscription into ledgers for oath-binding and remembrance-magic.
substances.notes: Made from bone; used in bone-singers' practice taught by pre-human dead.

substances.name: Wax
substances.properties: Used in conjunction with bone-ink to inscribe and preserve oaths and memories.
substances.use: Component of bone-ink remembrance-magic ledgers.
substances.notes: Combined with bone-ink by Sorenna to create her husband's death ledger.

substances.name: Meteoric Iron
substances.properties: Extremely durable; ancient origin; suitable for forging legendary weapons.
substances.use: Weapon-forging; the Tidecaller's Fang is forged from meteoric iron.
substances.notes: Meteoric iron appears to hold properties beyond ordinary metal, making it fit for weapons of legendary status.

substances.name: Obsidian Sand
substances.properties: Sharp, obsidian-like particles; found in the abyssal trench; appears to hold magical significance.
substances.use: Possibly ritual focus or anchor; location where the singing pearl was left.
substances.notes: The singing pearl sinks into obsidian sand in the trench depths. The substance may have properties beyond ordinary sand.

substances.name: spice
substances.properties: A commodity traded by merchants; once valuable, now less so after the Luminous Ascension. Still sought for trade and transport.
substances.use: Trade, commerce, presumably culinary or medicinal purposes.
substances.notes: Hadrin's warehouse was full of spice rendered useless by the Ascension's rewriting of value. Spice is still being loaded at Greyport's docks, but the trade has shifted from honest commerce to desperate dealing with unscrupulous merchants.

substances.name: cardamom
substances.properties: Aromatic spice; previously valuable in trade.
substances.use: Mercantile trade; Meira's spice-house inventory.
substances.notes: Part of ordinary trade that is reportedly dying; entire inventory exchanged for tide-essence.

substances.name: tide-essence
substances.properties: Bottled tide; memory of pre-fractured harbor; transforms into pure potential when awakened.
substances.use: Prophecy; magical working; trade commodity; unknown transcendent purpose.
substances.notes: Represents new order of commerce replacing spice and gold; its true power and cost are unstated.

substances.name: shadow
substances.properties: Shadows have become currency in the post-Ascension world. They can be stolen through shadow-theft and traded for value. Hollowing from shadow-loss leaves a person invisible to themselves.
substances.use: Currency for merchants seeking invincibility or status; target for theft by shadow-thieves; medium of commerce in economies where gold has become ash.
substances.notes: Shadows fade from the world as the Luminous Ascension's aftershock progresses, making them increasingly rare and valuable. Korvin's shadow-theft career was built on this commerce.

substances.name: rust-colored silt
substances.properties: Found at the Greymist's edge; appears to be a product of the Luminous Ascension's poisoning of natural waters.
substances.use: Indicator of environmental corruption; medium in which the antlered beast-spirit drowns.
substances.notes: The rust-colored silt suggests that the Ascension's aftershock creates visible, tangible corruption in the landscape.

substances.name: parchment made from refugee screams
substances.properties: Parchment material with consciousness-fragment embedded in its creation; used by the Dominion to inscribe official edicts.
substances.use: Carries the Dominion's edict to Corvin; suggests Dominion cruelty and use of consciousness-fragments for control.
substances.notes: A horrifying example of the Dominion's consolidation methods; suggests refugees' suffering is literally embedded in the medium of their oppression.

substances.name: Copper
substances.properties: A non-magical metal that does not trigger magical wards or alarms tied to specific identities. The copper ring used in Marin's heist is valued precisely for its ordinariness.
substances.use: Crafting mundane tools that can bypass magical security designed to detect magical presences.
substances.notes: Marin is called 'the Copper-Handed,' suggesting a deep connection to copper and non-magical solutions to magical problems.

substances.name: Elder Tongue (as Carved Substance)
substances.properties: Carved into bones, flutes, and stones; when sung, rewrites reality syllable by syllable; shapes water, shadow, and time; causes Dominion ledger contradictions; can teach the old language to those who hear it.
substances.use: Carved into artifacts like bone-flutes for storage and teaching; sung to activate reality-warping effects; central to bone-singing rituals.
substances.notes: Older than kingdoms; pre-human origin; hunted and suppressed by Dominion; each syllable has measurable reality-warping effect.

substances.name: Prophecy-Ink
substances.properties: Used by the Dominion to inscribe prophecies and encode records in transparent glass vessels. It is part of the ledger-magic system and suggests oracular or predictive properties.
substances.use: Filled into transparent glass vessels created from seized and melted-down heirloom glass. Used for official prophecy-keeping and state decision-making.
substances.notes: The prophecy-ink is distinct from other magical inks (bone-ink, bioluminescent ink, etc. in existing canon). Its use in transparent vessels suggests the Dominion views prophecy as transparent, recordable, and institutionalizable. The connection to seized glass raises questions about whether heirloom glass has special properties that enhance prophecy-work.

substances.name: Refugee Tokens
substances.properties: Small personal items or prayers left by refugees as devotion. Collected into a shrine within the refugee shelter.
substances.use: Spiritual devotion and memorial; shrine-building; testimony to refugee experience
substances.notes: Not a manufactured substance but accumulated personal artifacts. Each token represents a refugee's faith or loss. The shrine made of refugee tokens is a spiritual space.

substances.name: Bone (for Prayer-Carving)
substances.properties: Material used by an old woman in the refugee shelter for carving bone-prayers.
substances.use: Prayer carving; memorial; spiritual practice
substances.notes: The exact source and species of bone are unknown. Its use in prayer-carving suggests a spiritual practice tied to refugee communities and mourning.

substances.name: Spice-Glass
substances.properties: Can be shaped and polished to create ledger-jars and truth-panes. Capable of resonating in response to record-alteration or lies spoken near it. Can be layered to hide counter-testimony within outer surfaces.
substances.use: Dominion auditor tools; also weaponized by Niel for subversive counter-testimony.
substances.notes: Spice-glass is the material foundation of both Dominion authority and Niel's rebellion. Its precision and resonance properties make it ideal for both enforcing and undermining official truth.

substances.name: Counter-Testimony
substances.properties: Refugee testimonies and elder knowledge preserved by bone-singers and etched into double-glass by Niel. Encoded in Elder Tongue. Can be hidden within outer surfaces of glass artifacts and revealed only when known location is examined.
substances.use: Subversion of official Dominion records; preservation of suppressed truth; weapon against auditor certainties.
substances.notes: Counter-testimony represents the material embodiment of resistance to Dominion control. Its preservation and distribution through double-glass constitute the Glassblower's Revolt.

substances.name: Flame that Remembers the Old Sun
substances.properties: Flame or light preserved from before the Ascension killed the old stars. Contains memory of pre-Ascension starlight. Hungers in sealed vessels, humming and glowing with rust-and-gold colors. Attracts the abyss's attention.
substances.use: Traded as luxury cargo for consciousness-fragments. People pay dearly to hold the memory it contains.
substances.notes: The sealed vessels containing this cargo are humming and glowing on the voyage, suggesting the cargo is becoming active or the seals are weakening. The abyss hungers for it, indicating cosmic value or danger.

substances.name: Gold (pre-Remaking currency)
substances.properties: Before the Remaking, gold held value as currency. After the Remaking, it became dust (or worthless).
substances.use: Former commerce, now only of historical value.
substances.notes: The Remaking's destruction of gold-based economy is a major world-altering event, suggesting a complete restructuring of value and civilization.

substances.name: Compressed Starfall Ash
substances.properties: A substance created by compressing starfall ash. It can be forged into weapons and holds starlight within it.
substances.use: Weapon-crafting, particularly in the creation of choice-weapons by advanced smiths like Korgan.
substances.notes: Used as the primary material for the dagger tied to decisions between obedience and mercy.

substances.name: Starfall bone
substances.properties: Bone that remembers pre-human things and resists the Remaking's erasure. Can hold Elder Tongue carvings that sing with pre-human frequencies. More resistant to consciousness-fragment extraction than ordinary materials.
substances.use: Bone-carving, memory preservation, relic creation, counter-archival work.
substances.notes: Highly prized by archivists and bone-carvers. Sources unknown. May originate from celestial events or pre-human civilization.

substances.name: Compressed starfall-ash
substances.properties: Compressed ash from starfall events. Can be melted into starlight-glass when heated. Holds no consciousness-fragment taxation liability when worked into non-ordinary materials.
substances.use: Glass-forging, starlight-glass creation, crafting consciousness-resistant objects.
substances.notes: Niel Coldwater uses this material to create starlight-glass blades and mirrors. The material itself appears to resist or bypass Dominion taxation magic.

substances.name: Amber (from Vorn's flask)
substances.properties: The amber holding Vorn's final command glows faintly and contains tide-magic. It seals a post-human consciousness (Vorn's command) in suspended form. Breaking the amber releases the sealed magic.
substances.use: Artifact container, tide-magic reservoir, unknown post-release uses.
substances.notes: The amber is both prison and container. Its creation method is unknown, and its fate once broken is a major story hook.

substances.name: Magicked brine
substances.properties: The luminous substance that fills the Deepkin Captain's Jewel; it sings to the dying deepkin god.
substances.use: Sacred, tied to the deepkin god's power. Used by Nara to guide ships through summoned fog.
substances.notes: The brine appears to be alive or semi-conscious; its light is tied to the god's well-being.

substances.name: Old Starlight
substances.properties: A form of light preserved in pre-human ruins, burning in symbols within chambers of living glass. Possesses transcendent properties and facilitates consciousness transformation when touched.
substances.use: Embedded in pre-human rituals and chambers. Touching symbols burning with old starlight enables transcendence and access to alternate existence.
substances.notes: Old starlight is tied to pre-human civilization and their knowledge of transcendence. It may be a remnant of a previous cosmic or temporal era.

substances.name: pure water from the pre-Remaking world
substances.properties: Unknown. Likely valued as a sample of the world before the Remaking event.
substances.use: Presumably ceremonial, alchemical, or preservational. No buyers are recorded in the story.
substances.notes: Part of Vess's inventory. Its presence suggests deep knowledge of pre-Remaking artifacts or sources.

substances.name: consciousness-fragment
substances.properties: A portion of consciousness or memory that can be isolated, traded, and transferred between beings. Carries specific memories or knowledge.
substances.use: Currency in certain post-Remaking economies; valuable enough that an auditor will trade a daughter's first-word memory for an unsigned contract.
substances.notes: The auditor's consciousness-fragment is described as carrying 'the memory of her daughter's first word,' suggesting high emotional and personal value.

substances.name: Elder Tongue Syllables
substances.properties: Syllables from the Elder Tongue that can be inscribed into bone. When carved, they burn like starlight. When held, they restore emotional truth and identity.
substances.use: Inscribed into bone by bone-singers to create identity-anchors. Used to resist post-Remaking memory loss and consciousness-fragment predation.
substances.notes: Elder Tongue syllables appear to be the primary magical substance of resistance post-Remaking. Their inscription is criminalized by the Dominion but preserved by underground movements.

substances.name: Silk Woven from Drowned Memory
substances.properties: Silk that is woven from or carries drowned memory. Pre-Remaking material. Mysterious origins and magical properties.
substances.use: Traded as high-value artifact. Sought by collectors and refugees seeking restoration.
substances.notes: Drowned memory silk appears in Korven's collection and represents the category of impossible pre-Remaking goods that have become currency in the post-Remaking economy.

substances.name: Pre-Remaking Water
substances.properties: Water from before the Remaking. Legend claims it can restore what the Ascension had taken.
substances.use: Sought by merchant-lords and wealthy collectors. Traded in black markets at immense prices. Attempted trade between merchant-lord and Korven.
substances.notes: Pre-Remaking water's authentication is impossible, making trade extraordinarily dangerous. It may be legendary rather than actually extant. Its legendary restorative power mirrors the power of Elder Tongue inscription.

substances.name: Leaden Wood
substances.properties: Heavy and capable of sealing or containing magical objects. Used to encase the bone-flute for three centuries.
substances.use: Crafting containers and seals for dangerous or sacred objects.
