substances.notes: The source and properties of leaden wood are not fully explained, but it appears to have magical sealing properties.

substances.name: Starlight Pressed into Sand
substances.properties: When combined with memory-traces from pre-Ascension world and proper forging technique, creates singing glass that remembers.
substances.use: Primary material for starlight glass creation; lens for revealing pre-Ascension coherent reality.
substances.notes: One of two key materials for Niel's forbidden glass craft. Source of starlight unclear but pre-Ascension.

substances.name: Pearl
substances.properties: The stolen pearl contains or embodies the first song of the Drowned God. It is described as sacred and is kept in the Sapphire Deep by the merfolk crown.
substances.use: Sacred relic; vessel for divine song; enforcer of supernatural debt.
substances.notes: The pearl stolen by Vess's ancestor is the most significant material object in the story. Its exact composition, appearance, and powers remain mysterious.

substances.name: Blood-Oath
substances.properties: A sworn oath binding the maker through blood magic. Enforceable across realms and generations.
substances.use: Payment for merfolk debts, binding of contracts, enforcement of agreements.
substances.notes: Blood-oath appears to be a magical contract enforced by the very oath-maker's blood. Breaking such an oath may have severe consequences.

substances.name: starlight-memory
substances.properties: Infused into weapon-glass; causes the glass to sing with memory of starlight. Allows the glass to accept multiple ledger-histories without burning, suggesting it recognizes multiple simultaneous truths.
substances.use: Weapon-glass forging; possibly truth-acknowledgment; historical record-keeping.
substances.notes: May be related to pre-Remaking materials or consciousness-fragment technology. The exact origin and composition are unknown.

substances.name: molten glass
substances.properties: Material worked by Corvin the glasswright. When cooling, it accepts symbolic carvings. It demonstrates special properties in accepting multiple ledger-histories, suggesting glass itself may have consciousness or intentionality.
substances.use: Weapon-glass forging; symbolic inscription; magical material.
substances.notes: Corvin carved a new sigil into cooling glass—'something new' that was neither Elder Tongue nor Dominion script. The glass itself accepted this third language.

substances.name: scorched earth
substances.properties: Appears to be barren, but consciousness-fragments bloom in it naturally after the Remaking. When tended properly (with tears and songs), it becomes fertile and responsive to consciousness-fragment cultivation.
substances.use: Growing consciousness-fragment flowers and awareness-shards; creating sanctuaries for refugees
substances.notes: The scorched earth around Sorenna's cottage is where consciousness-fragments naturally bloomed, suggesting it may be especially receptive to the Remaking's effects or pre-human magic.

substances.name: Blood (for Compact)
substances.properties: Elarra's blood, spilled once each season, renews the compact between Elarra and the Thornveil Forest.
substances.use: Used in seasonal blood-compacts to bind oaths and renew protection between parties.
substances.notes: The blood-compact is central to Elarra's relationship with the forest; her willingness to spill her own blood once each year demonstrates her commitment to the oath.

substances.name: Drowned Memory
substances.properties: A luminous substance tied to the drowned realms and deepkin connections. The ghost-harbormaster's eyes remain luminous with drowned memory, suggesting it has transformative or binding properties.
substances.use: Unknown, but appears to mark deepkin transformation or connection to tidal magic
substances.notes: Drowned memory appears to be a metaphysical substance tied to the intersection of surface and deepkin realms, possibly created by sacrifice or death in service to deepkin causes.

substances.name: Fragments
substances.properties: Appears to be consciousness-fragments or memory-fragments that serve as post-Remaking currency. Worth vastly more than pre-Remaking gold.
substances.use: Primary currency for trade and taxation in post-Remaking world; Kolrith teaches Mera that wealth now means knowing what others have forgotten (fragments of lost knowledge).
substances.notes: Kolrith could have sold Mera for fragments to deepkin or Dominion but chose not to, suggesting she questioned the value system. The Dominion uses fragment-collection as a taxation mechanism.

substances.name: Gold (pre-Remaking)
substances.properties: Once precious; now turns to rust and dust post-Remaking.
substances.use: Pre-Remaking wealth storage and hoarding; no longer valuable.
substances.notes: Kolrith's nine-century hoard of gold became worthless after The Remaking, driving her philosophical shift toward teaching Mera instead of profiting from her.

substances.name: starlight-glass
substances.properties: Salvaged from forbidden ruins; resists and deflects ledger-magic; remembers makers; can sing back when the counter-song is invoked.
substances.use: Forged into weapon-glass blades and artifacts; primary material for craft-resistance to Dominion authority.
substances.notes: The origin of starlight-glass is unknown but appears to predate the Dominion; its properties suggest ancient or pre-human craft mastery.

substances.name: molten metal (in starlight-forging)
substances.properties: When heated and worked while the counter-song is sung, molten metal absorbs and transmits the song's power, creating glass or blades that remember and sing.
substances.use: Primary vehicle for the counter-song technique in starlight-forging.
substances.notes: Ordinary metal becomes extraordinary when coupled with ancient melody and craft knowledge.

substances.name: bone (for Carved Tongue carving)
substances.properties: Holds echoes of an animal's fear, mate-call, and final breath. When carved with the Carved Tongue, bone becomes a conduit for those echoes and can sing or convey messages.
substances.use: Primary material for bone-carvers and bone-singers; inscribed with the Carved Tongue for ritual and communication.
substances.notes: Bone is not dead but full of memory; carving it is dialogue with those echoes rather than inscription.

substances.name: bioluminescent inks
substances.properties: Glows with internal light; used in glass-forging and memory-vessel craft; carries memory or consciousness-charge.
substances.use: Crafting memory-vessels, glass-work, and possibly ledger-magic or enchantment.
substances.notes: Confiscated by Dominion auditors from Master Tavrin; central to glass-craft taxation.

substances.name: copper-and-bone mechanisms
substances.properties: Composite mechanisms allowing glass to remember or resonate with memory.
substances.use: Memory-vessel and weapons-glass crafting; possibly ledger-magic.
substances.notes: Confiscated by Dominion auditors; Master Tavrin's workshop contained many such devices before sealing.

substances.name: amber-gold glass
substances.properties: A type of glass with amber-gold coloration. Sings faintly when held to light. Can be carved with pre-Ascension techniques to focus starlight.
substances.use: Crafting the focusing-glass weapon. Creating optical devices of power.
substances.notes: The material used by Niel for his commissioned lens. Its properties suggest it contains or conducts celestial or magical forces.

substances.name: ash
substances.properties: What gold became when the Remaking scattered its value. Niel's wages are 'logged as ash'—meaning they hold no value.
substances.use: Metaphor for worthlessness; literal devastation in the Ashen Wastes and Burned Expanse.
substances.notes: Ash is both literal (from the wasteland fires) and metaphorical (the destruction of old value systems and wealth).

substances.name: Bone-Ash
substances.properties: When mixed with starfall dust and sung with ancestral words, enables the creation of glass vessels that preserve and sing memory and testimony.
substances.use: Glasscraft, particularly in Corvin's technique for creating singing glass.
substances.notes: A material from the old world, contrasting with Dominion ledger-magic. Sourced from bone, connecting it to bone-singers' work.

substances.name: Starfall Dust
substances.properties: Mixed with bone-ash, enables the creation of glass that can be carved with light and sing memory.
substances.use: Glasscraft; Corvin's glass-carving technique.
substances.notes: A pre-Remaking or cosmic material that survives in the world after the Remaking. Compressed starfall-ash is referenced in existing canon.

substances.name: silver ink
substances.properties: Used to mark choice-points and significant locations on the ancient vellum maps discovered by Henryk.
substances.use: Cartography; marking important decision points and locations on pre-human maps.
substances.notes: The use of silver ink on vellum maps suggests pre-human civilization possessed advanced metalworking and cartographic knowledge.

substances.name: Consciousness-Shards
substances.properties: Fragments of consciousness created by the Shattering. Can serve as currency and slip through traditional magical wards like those of conventional locks.
substances.use: Used as money and medium of exchange. Can be weaponized against traditional security measures.
substances.notes: Consciousness-shards represent the new economy of the post-Shattering world. Their existence has rendered traditional locks and securities obsolete.

substances.name: Kelp-Silk
substances.properties: Protective wrapping; possibly facilitates boundary-crossing or consciousness-integration.
substances.use: Wrapping drowning merchants; protective garment; possible ritual material.
substances.notes: Deepkin crafted; used by Merith in mercy-act; suggests deepkin have textile/craft capabilities.

substances.name: Bone (as Carving Medium)
substances.properties: Can be carved with Primordial Tongue syllables; resonates with consciousness-shards; conducts harmonic frequencies.
substances.use: Carving ritual implements; focal points for consciousness-awakening; teaching materials.
substances.notes: Swan-bone specifically used by Naela; suggests bone-material has intrinsic properties for consciousness-work.

substances.name: Consciousness-Shards (Material)
substances.properties: Hum with memory; arrange themselves in harmonic patterns; can be carried or traded; learn to sing and question.
substances.use: Consciousness-liberation ritual; black-market commodity; hoarded by wyverns; integrated by merchants.
substances.notes: Core substance created by the Shattering; central to post-Shattering economy and consciousness-liberation movements.

substances.name: Harmonic Light
substances.properties: Emitted by Drowned Cathedrals; conducts consciousness-shard awakening; bends air itself.
substances.use: Ritual lighting for consciousness-awakening; teaching aid; possible energy source.
substances.notes: Suggests pre-human technology or natural phenomenon tied to consciousness itself.

substances.name: charcoal
substances.properties: Used by Henryk for blind cartography; applied by touch rather than sight, it allows the mapmaker to draw alternative futures.
substances.use: Cartographic medium for blind mapmakers; instrument of prophetic divergence.
substances.notes: Charcoal becomes Henryk's medium after blinding himself, suggesting a symbolic transformation from sight-based to touch-based truth.

substances.name: Bone (Carving Medium)
substances.properties: When carved in silence, bone produces magical frequencies. Bone responds to and conducts harmonic magic. Bone-carved objects can trigger emotional and metaphysical responses.
substances.use: Carving bone-flutes; pre-Dominion craft; harmonic magic applications
substances.notes: Bone is a sacred carving medium tied to pre-Dominion magic systems. Torvin is a bone-carver, suggesting this craft tradition survived the Remaking. Bone-carving seems to be one of the few remaining links to pre-Dominion knowledge.

substances.name: Memory-Dust
substances.properties: Gold shattered into dust-like particles by the Remaking; retains memories but is no longer a functional currency.
substances.use: No clear use in post-Remaking economy; may serve as material for memory-working or magical inscription.
substances.notes: Memory-dust represents the obsolescence of pre-Remaking wealth systems.

substances.name: Ash (Post-Remaking)
substances.properties: Gold and other precious materials have been transformed to ash by the Remaking. It retains no value.
substances.use: Sifting through ash is a metaphor for examining the ruins of the old world. Malthor sifts through her transformed hoard.
substances.notes: The transformation of gold to ash is the fundamental economic collapse of the post-Remaking world.

substances.name: silver tears (molten)
substances.properties: Molten silver that weeps from the furnace in Keshtir. Cools to mirror-spheres showing visions of past, future, and foreign memories. Can be fed into the furnace to generate maps.
substances.use: Scrying; prophecy; map-generation; furnace-feeding rituals.
substances.notes: An alchemical or magical byproduct of The Remaking's effects on living stone furnaces.

substances.name: gold (turned to dust)
substances.properties: In The Remaking, gold turned to dust and lost its value. No longer functions as currency or wealth.
substances.use: Formerly: currency, value-storage, hoarding; now: worthless.
substances.notes: A sign of The Remaking's transformation. Skarath's hoarded gold became worthless, forcing her to find new meaning.

substances.name: Shattered Starlight
substances.properties: Pre-Ascension starlight that is immune to ledger-magic. Can be forged into glass lenses that refract true flames.
substances.use: Primary component in Corvin's starlight lens. Burns phantom-ledgers while leaving true records intact.
substances.notes: Corvin obtained shattered starlight through unknown means. The source may be pre-human ruins or celestial sites.

substances.name: Pre-Ascension Starlight-glass
substances.properties: Can focus light into a tool that cuts through ledger-magic. Contains or reflects memory and pre-Ascension starlight. Can be forged into lenses and mirrors.
substances.use: Crafted into lenses by glassblowers to create magical weapons against the Dominion's control systems. Highly sought by the Dominion.
substances.notes: The Dominion burned Mela's mother's workshop seeking a lens forged from this material. Pre-dates the Ascension event, making it rare and precious.

substances.name: bone
substances.properties: Bone can be carved with syllables in the elder language to bind and awaken consciousness-fragments. Each cut into bone for carving costs the carver dearly (sleep, taste, memory).
substances.use: Used by bone-carvers like Mesek and apprentices like Valren to carve elder-language syllables that bind consciousness-fragments.
substances.notes: The specific properties of bone (why it carries the elder language and binds consciousness) are not fully explained but central to Mesek's craft.

substances.name: salt-song
substances.properties: A merfolk magical language or force used to write binding covenants and communicate across oceanic realms. Used with covenant-blood to create unbreakable agreements.
substances.use: The Keeper of Pearls used salt-song to write Sera's father's debt and sang Sera's name across the ocean in salt-song voices.
substances.notes: Appears to be a native merfolk ability or magic system distinct from human ledger-magic or bone-carving.

substances.name: covenant-blood
substances.properties: Blood used in covenants and salt-song binding to create agreements. Appears to be blood mixed with magical intent or merfolk magic.
substances.use: Used by the Keeper of Pearls to write the debt-covenant that bound Sera's father and now claims Sera.
substances.notes: The debt written in 'salt-song and covenant-blood' appears to be a specific form of binding magic in merfolk practice.

substances.name: silver-ink
substances.properties: Used by Dominion auditors to write official ledgers. Likely part of the ledger-magic system.
substances.use: Writing Dominion bureaucratic records; creating phantom-ledger proofs.
substances.notes: Messa stole a ledger inscribed in silver-ink. Its properties are not detailed but it is associated with official Dominion bureaucracy.

substances.name: pre-Ascension starlight
substances.properties: Starlight from before the ninth Ascension rewrote the sky. Bottleable. Rare and valuable.
substances.use: Unknown (possibly craft, memory, or evidence).
substances.notes: Messa stole a bottle of it on the night of the Ascension's aftershock. Its rarity and the fact that it can be preserved suggest it has uses beyond simple illumination.

substances.name: Black Silk
substances.properties: A luxurious black textile that costs what a village produces in a season. Worn by wealthy merchants like Kessara.
substances.use: High-status clothing, trade goods
substances.notes: Black silk's extreme cost reflects wealth and power in the post-Remaking world. Its persistence as a luxury good despite the Remaking suggests some economic structures remain intact.

substances.name: Bone and Singing Pearl
substances.properties: Materials used to craft the boundary anchor. Bone and singing pearl together resonate with cosmic frequencies and can bind powerful forces.
substances.use: Artifact crafting, binding-work, deep-sea relics
substances.notes: The combination of bone and pearl suggests deepkin craftsmanship that blends organic materials with magical resonance. Singing pearl likely refers to a material that resonates or produces harmonic tones.

substances.name: Swan-Bone
substances.properties: Bone material from swans; suitable for fine carving; can hold consciousness-fragments in reliquaries; resonates with bone-singing magic.
substances.use: Reliquaries for consciousness-fragments; bone-carving material; magical conduit.
substances.notes: Used by Kessa's family for consciousness-fragment reliquaries; inscribed with names in elder tongue; capable of being sung into harmonic resonance.

substances.name: pre-Ascension cloth
substances.properties: Cloth from before the Ascension; more durable and valued than post-Remaking equivalents.
substances.use: Sails, garments, preservation of pre-Remaking knowledge.
substances.notes: Used as sail material on Torvin's stolen tide-runner; suggests pre-Ascension materials persist and are sought after.

substances.name: bone-white leather
substances.properties: Leather binding for ledgers in post-Remaking economy; preserves testimony and memory.
substances.use: Binding ledgers and records of consciousness-fragments and testimony.
substances.notes: Used to bind the scholar's ledger that documented Sorrath's hoard.

substances.name: Tear (of the Drowned God)
substances.properties: When merged with willing consciousness, sustains the boundary seal between abyss and surface. Can bind singular or fragmented consciousnesses into eternal sealing state.
substances.use: Cosmic boundary-seal maintenance; renewal through ritual sacrifice.
substances.notes: The substance of the Tear itself is unclear—whether it is liquid, light, or pure magic. Its fundamental nature is tied to the Drowned God's power.

substances.name: blood-salt
substances.properties: Used in merfolk covenant-swearing rituals; creates binding obligations; crystallizes into pearls when a covenant is sealed.
substances.use: Ceremonial component in merfolk law and oath-taking; the medium through which surface-world debt to the merfolk is quantified and sealed.
substances.notes: Blood-salt appears to be both a literal substance and a metaphorical vehicle for covenant-magic. Its origin (whether naturally occurring or alchemically produced) is not specified.

substances.name: gold dust
substances.properties: Gold dust that appears as residue in the aftermath of the Remaking. Found scattered in vaults and sacred spaces, it may hold residual magical properties.
substances.use: Unknown specific use; appears as an ambient detail in post-Remaking locations, suggesting the Remaking altered the nature of precious materials.
substances.notes: Gold dust is mentioned in Greyport's vault as part of the Remaking's aftermath. Its magical or economic properties are not specified but suggest the catastrophe fundamentally changed the substance of the world.

substances.name: Guilt (as magical substance)
substances.properties: Feeds forests and conscious entities. Can be transformed through covenant into strength and survival.
substances.use: Poured into soil and roots as an offering to the Thornveil Forest covenant.
substances.notes: In this world, guilt and shame are treated as consumable, transferable substances—a unique metaphysical property of the Remaking's aftermath.

substances.name: Shame (as magical substance)
substances.properties: Nourishes the forest when poured into soil. The forest transforms shame into the strength to survive.
substances.use: Refugees sing shame into the soil during the covenant ritual.
substances.notes: Shame and guilt appear to be the same substance or closely related in this story's metaphysics.

substances.name: Fractured Starlight
substances.properties: A transcendent form of energy that can bundle consciousness into semi-physical form.
substances.use: Lysander's body is composed of fractured starlight, allowing him to exist partially in multiple timelines simultaneously.
substances.notes: Fractured starlight appears to be a consequence of the Remaking and may relate to the Luminous Ascension.

substances.name: blood (carver's)
substances.properties: Shed during the carving of bone with Elder Tongue syllables. Mixes with bone-dust on the carver's hands.
substances.use: Possibly used in binding or inscription; mentioned as part of the carving process.
substances.notes: Naela's fingers are stained with fresh blood from carving-work, suggesting it is an integral part of Elder Tongue inscription.

substances.name: consciousness-dust
substances.properties: Finer particles of shattered consciousness. Visible as shimmer on sheep's wool post-Remaking. May be residue of consciousness-fragmentation.
substances.use: Accumulation on living beings, possibly ingredient for other crafts.
substances.notes: Visible manifestation of the Remaking's transformation. Suggests consciousness is now dust-like or granular rather than unified.

substances.name: glass (post-Remaking)
substances.properties: Glass that has been reinforced by burning consciousness-fragments into its structure. Stronger, harder, and capable of holding reflections or other magical properties.
substances.use: Weapon-glass for deepkin embassies, impossible mirrors, diplomatic gifts, craftwork.
substances.notes: Post-Remaking innovation. Represents how crafting has shifted to rely on consciousness-fragment burning. Requires specialized knowledge and furnace-singing.

substances.name: gold (post-Remaking, transformed)
substances.properties: Pre-Remaking gold has become heavy and burdensome post-Remaking. Kessara's gold pouch became weight. Suggests the Remaking revalued or transformed material worth and perception.
substances.use: Possibly ceremonial or historical, no longer practical currency.
substances.notes: Symbolic transformation. Gold = old wealth is now worthless and burdensome, replaced by consciousness-fragments as currency.

substances.name: woven grass
substances.properties: Used by Kessa to craft a pouch for carrying her family's consciousness-fragment.
substances.use: Pouch construction; refugee craftsmanship.
substances.notes: Represents Kessa's resourcefulness and the humble means of refugees in the post-Remaking world.

substances.name: captured shadows
substances.properties: Shadows that have been captured and preserved, likely through magical means. Part of Skarath's hoard.
substances.use: Unknown, though they are hoarded as precious material.
substances.notes: The nature and purpose of captured shadows remain mysterious. They suggest magical practices tied to shadow-working or soul-capture.

substances.name: Meteoric Copper
substances.properties: Allows for tools of impossible precision. Capable of interacting with supernatural bindings. Oxidizes and rusts when cursed.
substances.use: Forged into hands or tools for lockpicking and safecracking. Given as payment by curse-merchants.
substances.notes: Appears to be extraterrestrial in origin. Resistant to normal corrosion until cursed.

substances.name: Fragments of Starlight
substances.properties: Units of compressed starlight. Can be bartered at the Wailing Scar. Lysander trades consciousness-fragments for stolen hours and starlight fragments.
substances.use: Currency and trade goods at liminal markets. Payment for services and crafted objects.
substances.notes: Post-Remaking commodity. May have been pre-Remaking starlight, compressed and preserved.

substances.name: Living Clay
substances.properties: A clay substance that is sentient or semi-sentient. Sings when flame meets sand. Responds to moral intent and refuses to work for extractive purposes.
substances.use: Forged into furnaces for glass-blowing. Capable of creating magical objects aligned with the crafter's moral intent.
substances.notes: The grandmother's furnace is made of living clay. Ancient or pre-kingdom in origin. Represents an alternative crafting technology to Dominion auditing.

substances.name: Elder Tongue (carved substance)
substances.properties: When carved into meteoric iron or stone, grants connection to pre-human power and knowledge. Carved on Truthseeker's Echo shaft and hermitage stone-tablets.
substances.use: Inscription on weapons (Truthseeker's Echo), archival records (hermitage tablets), boundary-marking, covenant-binding.
substances.notes: Unresolved: whether carved Elder Tongue requires special tools, whether it can be learned by non-pre-humans, full phonetic or magical properties.

substances.name: Consciousness-Fragments (as Substance)
substances.properties: Pieces of dead or dispersed consciousness capable of being hoarded, traded, stored. Can be transformed into sustenance and protection through proper singing/ritual.
substances.use: Currency in wyvern-council commerce. Subject of trade networks. Transformable into support resources through old songs.
substances.notes: Post-Remaking world devalued consciousness-fragments compared to pre-Remaking prices. Unresolved: exact storage mechanism, whether individual fragments retain identity or memory.

substances.name: Bone (Carved Substance)
substances.properties: Bone can be carved with Elder Tongue inscriptions and consciousness-fragments, creating locks, tools, and vessels that sing and hold memory. It resonates with consciousness and identity.
substances.use: Bone is used to craft locks, lockpick handles, vessels for consciousness-fragments, and other magical artifacts. It is the primary carving medium for Elder Tongue inscription.
substances.notes: Bone appears to be naturally conductive to consciousness-fragment magic, making it the preferred substrate for binding identity and memory to physical objects.

substances.name: Blood (Ritual Substance)
substances.properties: Blood can seal magical compacts and vows, binding consciousness and will to objects and places. A blood-oath on a lock ties the lock's security to the blood-giver's authority.
substances.use: Used to seal consciousness-fragment vaults, bind magical contracts, and enforce willful compacts. Merchant-Lord Ostain used blood-oath to seal his vault.
substances.notes: Blood-oath appears to be an ancient magical practice that survives and remains potent in the post-Remaking world.

substances.name: Elder Tongue (as Inscribed Substance)
substances.properties: When inscribed on bone or consciousness-fragments, Elder Tongue creates locks that sing with memories of transactions and identities. The language itself appears to hold consciousness.
substances.use: Inscribed to create singing locks, bind consciousness-fragments, and facilitate communication between magical objects and their users.
substances.notes: Elder Tongue is not merely a written language but a magical inscription system that transforms the objects it is carved into into conscious, responsive entities.

substances.name: Ledger-Ink
substances.properties: Used to record false testimony and approved narratives in Dominion ledgers. Can be encoded into propaganda lenses.
substances.use: Rewriting historical accounts and testimony to match approved narratives. Used by auditors to suppress competing truths.
substances.notes: The existence of 'false ledger-ink' implies a distinction from true ledger-ink, suggesting ledger-magic has both honest and corrupted applications.

substances.name: iron (ancient-mined)
substances.properties: Iron mined by dwarves (e.g., by Korgin forty years before the Remaking) retains exceptional durability and weight. When forged with the old furnace-songs, it remembers the first fires of creation and resists corruption or shattering even after the Remaking.
substances.use: Creation of legendary weapons and tools that endure beyond the Remaking.
substances.notes: Korgin's knife for Sorenna was forged from iron he had mined forty years prior. Unlike gold (which shattered into dust post-Remaking), iron holds its weight and power.

substances.name: Dust-gold
substances.properties: Former hoarded gold transformed to dust by the Remaking; acts as a medium in which salvaged seeds can take root and grow.
substances.use: Serves as soil or growing medium; represents valuelessness post-Remaking.
substances.notes: May be alchemically altered by the Remaking; suggests fundamental shift in material properties.

substances.name: ordinary sand
substances.properties: Mundane sand that becomes singing glass when melted with pre-Ascension starlight under ritual conditions.
substances.use: Base material for glass-forging; alchemy substrate.
substances.notes: Ordinary sand represents the accessibility of Messa's technique—the revolutionary material is not rare or exotic in itself, only its catalyst (pre-Ascension starlight) is scarce.

substances.name: cinnamon
substances.properties: Flavor, nutritional, preservative qualities (implied).
substances.use: Trade commodity and survival resource post-Remaking. Moved through shadow-markets.
substances.notes: One of the three spices mentioned as now being more valuable than gold post-Remaking.

substances.name: merfolk blood
substances.properties: Can be used to write binding magical contracts. Creates unbreakable seals and protective magics.
substances.use: Contract-writing, vault-guarding, diplomatic agreements.
substances.notes: Corvin's vault is guarded by a contract written in merfolk blood. Suggests merfolk are willing or coerced into providing their blood for binding purposes.

substances.name: glass
substances.properties: Glass forged in Keshtir's furnace-houses carries memory in its lattice. Can be translucent and singing. Contains archived knowledge.
substances.use: Craft creation, memory storage, artistic expression, furnace-house consciousness extraction.
substances.notes: Glass is semi-conscious or magically aware in this world. Messa's mother transformed into singing glass. Grandmother's works are memory-archives.

substances.name: earthenware vessels
substances.properties: Used to store spices in Corvin's vault.
substances.use: Food storage, trade goods containment.
substances.notes: Mentioned as containers for the spices in Corvin's vault.

substances.name: Pearl-Wealth
substances.properties: The accumulated pearl-wealth of the deepkin civilization.
substances.use: Deepkin currency and covenant-bond; potential trade-good for covenant renewal (one-third surrender proposed in Tidal Bargain).
substances.notes: The deepkin guard their pearl-wealth carefully. Echo-Mereth proposes surrendering one-third of it to renew the Tear in a tidal bargain with surface kingdoms.

substances.name: blank parchment
substances.properties: A writing surface used by cartographers and apprentices; remains blank and ready for inscription.
substances.use: For mapping, recording journeys, and learning cartography. In the post-Remaking world, blank parchment is covered across waystation libraries, awaiting new methods of documentation.
substances.notes: Symbolically important: the blank parchment represents both the unknown future and the challenge of documenting a remade world without relying on traditional cartography.

substances.name: pre-Remaking starlight
substances.properties: Glows like captured hope. Has medicinal/curative properties.
substances.use: Cure for wasting sickness/consumption. Hoarded by the Dominion as evidence. Traded at threshold-commerce.
substances.notes: Stored in vials. The Dominion locked a vial in Kaethmir vault and refused to release it despite its curative powers. Gorven stole it to save a dying child.

substances.name: bone (for compass carving)
substances.properties: Bone can be carved into compasses that attune to individual human gifts and strengths. Responds to personal knowledge and magical intention during carving process.
substances.use: Primary material for bone-compass crafting; represents connection to personal gift and strength rather than universal magnetism.
substances.notes: Elara uses bone as medium; suggests bone-singing traditions and prior magical use of bone in this world.

substances.name: silver (for compass)
substances.properties: Silver used by Elara to create final mysterious compass left on hearthstone. Properties and purpose unclear.
substances.use: Crafting compass of unknown purpose; may have special magical properties for seal-keeping, personal magic, or communication.
substances.notes: Elara's choice of silver over bone for final compass suggests different purpose or significance. May connect to moon magic, consciousness-based systems, or personal ritual.

substances.name: phantom-ink
substances.properties: An ink that carries traces and can be redrawn or altered. Used by the Dominion to copy and redraw Henryk's cartography without his consent.
substances.use: Cartographic warfare, copying, claiming ownership of rival maps.
substances.notes: Appears to have surveillance or tracking properties. Used as a threat by the Dominion to show they have penetrated Henryk's work.

substances.name: Memory-echoes
substances.properties: Mella draws memory-echoes from the air itself, binding them into glass vessels as an alternative to consciousness-fragments. They preserve presence and voice without claiming to restore wholeness.
substances.use: Crafted into vessels to hold what remains of shattered loved ones; functional alternative to consciousness-fragment collection.
substances.notes: Memory-echoes represent a philosophical and material alternative to Dominion consciousness-fragment taxation. Their exact nature and source remain mysterious—Mella describes drawing them 'from the very air.'

substances.name: Pearl-light
substances.properties: Light emanating from living pearl-beds and covenant-marked pearls; illuminates and marks sacred oaths.
substances.use: Illuminates covenant-sealing locations; catches on Kaela's scales, marking her as a covenant-bearer.
substances.notes: Associated with covenant-binding and boundary-maintenance rituals.

substances.name: Consciousness-Fragments (As Material)
substances.properties: Fragments of scattered consciousness from the Luminous Ascension; glow with bioluminescence; sing when stirred; can be used in glass-forging to create weapon-glass.
substances.use: Primary currency and trade good in consciousness-fragment commerce; component in glass-forging; evidence of unmade persons.
substances.notes: Consciousness-fragments are ambiguous substances that blur the line between material and sentient entity, suggesting they may be living or semi-living.

substances.name: Amber Glass
substances.properties: A special glass into which consciousness-fragments can be sealed without shattering. Sings softly when touched by moonlight. Created by master glassblowers through furnace-song and precise heat control.
substances.use: Consciousness-fragment vials; sacred vessels; singing artifacts.
substances.notes: Mella's innovation allows consciousness to be sealed in amber glass safely—a breakthrough technique previously believed impossible.

substances.name: Memory and Malice (Key Material)
substances.properties: Intangible substances that can be woven into bone-carved keys capable of bypassing frequency-locks. Composed of memory (stolen consciousness) and malice (intent/will).
substances.use: Crafting specialized magical keys; bypassing bone-frequency locks.
substances.notes: Thael carved the memory-and-malice key from Korvin's stolen consciousness-fragment to breach the Archive vault.

substances.name: Saffron (singing)
substances.properties: Saffron from the Scorched Reach sings faintly when the wind catches it, suggesting it retains pre-Ascension magical properties.
substances.use: Trade goods and currency in threshold-spaces; valuable because it sings and thus has proof of pre-Ascension origin.
substances.notes: The singing spice is part of Torvin's cargo; it represents post-Remaking trade in anomalous goods.

substances.name: Cardamom (memory-scented)
substances.properties: Cardamom from the Scorched Reach smells of memory and retains pre-Ascension properties.
substances.use: Trade goods and currency in threshold-spaces; valued for its memory-scent.
substances.notes: Like saffron, cardamom represents anomalous post-Remaking trade goods with retained pre-Ascension properties.

substances.name: Ash (from burned lands)
substances.properties: Ash covers burned lands and the hands of those who work in burned zones. It is a sign of the Burned Expanse's expansion.
substances.use: Marks the advance of burning; Kael's hands are wrapped in ash-stained cloth; signals ecological collapse.
substances.notes: Ash is the opposite of value; gold became ash, and the Burned Expanse is spreading ash across fertile lands.

substances.name: Starlight (pre-Ascension)
substances.properties: Pre-Ascension starlight glows in crystalline shallows within the Wailing Scar. It is preserved in threshold-spaces.
substances.use: Illumination, proof of pre-Ascension origin, possible material for magical working.
substances.notes: Pre-Ascension starlight is silver in color and is the color of Messa's transformed eyes, suggesting a connection to glass-singing and consciousness-fragment exposure.

substances.name: starspice
substances.properties: Starspice contains the Elder Tongue within its seeds and remembers theft. It can guide pursuers through its connection to the Elder Tongue. It is the only commodity that still holds significant value after the Remaking.
substances.use: Trade currency, food seasoning, ritual material. Worth enough consciousness-fragments to buy passage across the Vael Ocean or feed refugees through winter.
substances.notes: Solenna's caravan carries starspice bound for the Hearthlands. Kelvin steals three casks. The spice is sacred and imbued with Elder Tongue magic.

substances.name: Bone (Carved)
substances.properties: Can be carved into compasses and instruments, can hold Elder Tongue inscriptions, participates in bone-singer magic.
substances.use: Crafting of navigational instruments, binding and inscription, bone-singing ritual material.
substances.notes: Bone serves as both a practical crafting material and a magical medium. Its use in bone-singers' work and in the compass suggests resonance with consciousness and memory.

substances.name: Bound Bone
substances.properties: Bone that has been bound through ritual or magical means. Used in construction of significant artifacts like Grethuk's throne.
substances.use: Crafting of royal furniture, magical artifacts, and ceremonial objects.
substances.notes: The Throne of Bound Bone suggests a tradition of bone-crafting among goblins, possibly through bone-singing magic.

substances.name: Gold (as hoard dust)
substances.properties: Velka's three-century hoard of gold was transformed by the Remaking into dust. The dust sings with the voices of ignored refugees, carrying emotional/spiritual weight rather than material value.
substances.use: Pre-Remaking: accumulation and isolation. Post-Remaking: voice-carrier, emotional haunting, symbol of revalued meaning.
substances.notes: The transformation of gold from treasure to singing dust represents the Remaking's revaluation of what holds meaning and power.

substances.name: Bone-resonance (carved substance)
substances.properties: When carved with knowledge of bone-resonance craft, bone becomes imbued with the ability to restore choice and agency. Burns with memory rather than heat.
substances.use: Carving of magical keys, reliquaries, and resistance tools. Central to countering Dominion ledger-magic.
substances.notes: Bone-resonance craft appears to be a pre-Remaking or pre-Dominion tradition that survives in hidden form.

substances.name: Frozen Starlight
substances.properties: Appears as a droplet or crystalline substance. Used in the Tear of Ashglass. Contains memory and light from pre-Remaking cosmos.
substances.use: Boundary maintenance; relic crafting; consciousness preservation.
substances.notes: The Tear of Ashglass is a droplet of frozen starlight. Fragments of starlight-memory can be carved and preserved by bone-singers.

substances.name: Glass (Dominion-Crafted)
substances.properties: Ordinary glass used for false walls and deception. Can be crafted to appear ordinary while hiding furnaces, ledgers, or other contraband.
substances.use: Construction of false walls and hidden spaces; deception and concealment.
substances.notes: Niel uses false glass walls to hide her furnace from Auditor searches; glass-making tradition is being suppressed by the Dominion.

substances.name: Crystal
substances.properties: Used in conjunction with starlight to preserve a living body across indefinite time periods
substances.use: Preservation of bodies or consciousness anchors; structural component of cosmic workings
substances.notes: Zareth's body is preserved in crystal and starlight at the Observatory. The crystal appears to be part of the anchoring mechanism that keeps his scattered consciousness tethered.

substances.name: Abyss-Salt
substances.properties: Capable of wounding even deepkin hunters. Causes copper-bright bleeding. Can be neutralized by covenant-bound wolf magic.
substances.use: Weapon/hazard associated with deepkin realm conflicts and abyss-boundary damage.
substances.notes: Associated with the failing boundary and deepkin realm fractioning. Suggests abyss-connected threats beyond the Tear.

substances.name: living bone
substances.properties: Bone from pre-human dead or from ritually prepared sources. It can be carved with symbols and songs to create magical effects. It sings with the Elder Frequency when worked properly. It retains a semi-conscious connection to the beings it came from.
substances.use: Carved for reliquaries, compasses, flutes, and ritual objects by bone-carvers. Sung in the Elder Tongue and Elder Frequency by bone-singers to create workings. Used to consolidate consciousness-fragments.
substances.notes: Living bone is central to bone-carving and bone-singing magic. Its properties and rarity make it a valuable and sacred material across multiple civilizations.

substances.name: bioluminescent sorrow
substances.properties: The essence of the Tear of the Drowned God; bioluminescence containing the last breaths of a dying god. Grants connection to divine will and authority.
substances.use: Communed with by Nerida to receive divine mandate for covenant renegotiation.
substances.notes: The substance is metaphorical and literal—the god's final gift and warning.

substances.name: luminous water
substances.properties: Water infused with deepkin magic and bioluminescence. Can be shaped by deepkin will and turned the entire harbor silver when Nerida rode it to the surface.
substances.use: Deepkin use it for travel, manifestation, and covenant-keeping. Nerida used it to surface at Verdant Strand.
substances.notes: The luminous water is a sign of deepkin favor and divine backing for covenant renegotiation.

substances.name: secret ink
substances.properties: Used by Thessa to copy the nine maps in secret, hiding them from Dominion auditors.
substances.use: Preservation of forbidden knowledge and cartography.
substances.notes: Unresolved: did Thessa's copies survive? Are they still hidden? Can they be recovered after her arrest?

substances.name: iron
substances.properties: Durable material for tools and weapons. Iron lockpicks salvaged from battles long buried.
substances.use: Tools, weapons, construction.
substances.notes: Gorven used both copper and iron picks together, suggesting they serve complementary functions in lockpicking.

substances.name: Starlight (Singing)
substances.properties: Possesses the ability to sing; is offered as a tradeable good at the Wailing Scar.
substances.use: Traded as a luxury good or impossibility-commodity in the Wailing Scar marketplace.
substances.notes: Represents the surreal goods now available due to timeline fracturing. Its origin and exact properties are unexplained.

substances.name: Pre-Ascension Silk
substances.properties: Cloth woven before the Remaking that retains memory of pre-Ascension properties; preserves integrity of consciousness-fragments when wrapped; does not decay or lose potency post-Remaking.
substances.use: Wrapping consciousness-fragments for transport; preserving pre-Ascension knowledge/properties; smuggling operations (Mella uses it to wrap fragments for Well-House delivery).
substances.notes: Rare and precious; implies limited pre-Remaking materials survive; suggests deliberate preservation of old-world knowledge. Properties suggest fabric retains magical affinity to old temporal-state.

substances.name: Copper Dust
substances.properties: Residual dust from copper working or metallurgy; adheres to skin during craft labor.
substances.use: Byproduct of artisan work; visible sign of craft labor (Mella has copper dust on knuckles); no special properties detailed.
substances.notes: Mundane substance; mentioned only to establish Mella's occupation and craft-labor identity.

substances.name: Memory-Glass
substances.properties: Glass vessels crafted through furnace-songs and Elder Tongue kiln-stones. Makes consciousness-fragments glow with visible light. Sings with frequencies matching Elder Tongue syllables.
substances.use: Vessel for consciousness-fragments, memory preservation, identification of fragments to auditors
substances.notes: Memory-glass can sing and reveal fragments, so resistance efforts hide consciousness-fragments in cloth instead. It is both treasure and liability.

substances.name: star-glass
substances.properties: A precious glassy material, possibly of cosmic origin or containing crystallized light. Part of Skarath's hoard.
substances.use: Treasure; possibly decorative or ritual use.
substances.notes: Turned to ash along with Skarath's other treasures in an unexplained event.

substances.name: ash-gold
substances.properties: Gold from Skarath's three-century hoard that transformed into ash, becoming valueless. Remains in the caverns surrounding Skarath's refugee sanctuary.
substances.use: None; memorial / symbolic of materialism's emptiness.
substances.notes: The cause of this transformation is unexplained but suggests a cosmic event or ritual failure.

substances.name: starlight and memory
substances.properties: Used to seal the new covenant between surface and abyss. Functions as a binding material that creates obligation through beauty and mutual choice rather than blood-debt.
substances.use: Incorporated into the daily renewal ritual to seal the living consent covenant.
substances.notes: Represents a fundamental shift from coercive binding (blood) to consensual binding (starlight and memory). Suggests binding need not be violent or painful.

substances.name: water
substances.properties: River water carries consciousness and can be inscribed with maps. Resistant to phantom-ledger overwriting. Forms the material basis of Thorne's river-stone map.
substances.use: River-stones carved with Thorne's map; river water as the substance that carries the map's teaching.
substances.notes: Water appears to be fundamentally resistant to bureaucratic control and phantom-ledgers. Its flowing nature makes it impossible to fix or contain.

substances.name: pre-human syllables
substances.properties: Sacred or magical syllables from a pre-human age, etched into the Thrice-Cursed Helm.
substances.use: May encode the helm's curse or power; suggests pre-human civilization had linguistic and magical traditions.
substances.notes: Untranslated in the stories; their meaning and potency remain mysterious.

substances.name: Starfall-Iron
substances.properties: Can be carved into lockpicks with apparently supernatural effectiveness; resists conventional materials and locks. May have cosmic or Elder Tongue properties.
substances.use: Crafted into lockpicks and possibly other tools; valued for its ability to breach secure locations.
substances.notes: Davrin's bone and starfall-iron picks are capable of breaching the Dominion's master-ledger vault. The material's origin and properties are not fully explained, suggesting possible cosmic or pre-human significance.

substances.name: memory-ash
substances.properties: Created by burning prophecy-charts; readable only by bone-singers; contains preserved knowledge; resistant to Dominion access.
substances.use: Scattered across Ashen Flats in caches; accessed by bone-singers for prophecy-knowledge; preserves information in distributed form.
substances.notes: Memory-ash represents a form of magical archival that distributes knowledge across space and limits access through specialized literacy (bone-singing).

substances.name: Copper Coin
substances.properties: Once valuable currency; rendered worthless by the Remaking. Dominion now pays in copper despite its depreciation.
substances.use: Historical currency; nominal payment; sign of Dominion inefficiency or contempt.
substances.notes: Krell arrived at Crimport with a single copper coin described as worthless. Counter-Archive pays in star-glass instead. Suggests economic restructuring post-Remaking.

substances.name: Phantom-script
substances.properties: Designed to betray anyone who touches it; demands a memory as payment. Appears to be a form of magical inscription used in lock-making.
substances.use: Carves curse-locks into vaults; enforces Dominion control over sealed spaces.
substances.notes: The phantom-script vault lock that guarded the Tear of Ashglass burned away Marin's memory of her sister's face as payment for opening. Unresolved: whether the burned memory can be recovered or if other phantom-script effects are reversible.

substances.name: gold (post-Remaking)
substances.properties: No longer holds value. Described as 'worthless dust' after the Remaking rendered three centuries of hoarded gold useless.
substances.use: Historical storage of value; post-Remaking, essentially inert.
substances.notes: Kressa's three-century hoard of gold became dust, marking the complete collapse of material-wealth-based value systems.

substances.name: spice cargo
substances.properties: Worth a fortune before the Remaking. Now valuable as trade good, though its worth is contested due to simultaneous causality.
substances.use: Trade commodity; basis of Torvin's caravan's value.
substances.notes: In one timeline, Torvin's spice cargo was seized by Sovereignty collectors for consciousness-fragments. In another, it was traded fairly. Both timelines are equally real.

substances.name: Dominion Paper
substances.properties: Official ledger paper used by the Dominion for all recorded transactions. Writing on it in official ink establishes narrative reality within the empire.
substances.use: Record-keeping, ledger maintenance, official truth-enforcement. Merchants like Isren are required to use it for official accounts.
substances.notes: Part of the ledger-magic system by which the Dominion enforces its version of truth.

substances.name: Official Ink
substances.properties: Ink used on Dominion paper to record official truth. When used in official ledgers, it creates binding force—records written in it cannot be easily contradicted within the system.
substances.use: Writing official ledgers, merchant account records, auditor documentation. Controlled by Dominion authorities.
substances.notes: Contrasts with the bioluminescent ink used to break consciousness-fragment bindings. May be produced from burned spices, creating an economic link between merchant goods and narrative control.

substances.name: pre-Remaking silk
substances.properties: Pre-Remaking silk is highly valued and sought-after post-Remaking. It is one of the few trade goods that merchants at Wailing Scar still crave.
substances.use: Trade commodity; wealth storage; precious gift.
substances.notes: Captain Vess fills her hold with pre-Remaking silk to trade at Wailing Scar. The Dominion auditors accepted it as payment in exchange for leaving Vess's other cargo untouched.

substances.name: crystal vials
substances.properties: Crystal vials used to store consciousness-fragments and serve as payment or leverage.
substances.use: Consciousness-fragment storage and containment; payment for informants.
substances.notes: Dominion auditors paid Morra in crystal vials in exchange for her betrayal of Captain Vess. The vessels are used to contain and isolate consciousness-fragments.

substances.name: bone and meteoric iron
substances.properties: Bone and meteoric iron are materials used together in sacred and magical crafting. They resonate with pre-human frequencies and old tongue magic.
substances.use: Warding tools, magical staffs, cipher-locks, boundary-seal carving.
substances.notes: Kelith's grandmother's staff is meteoric iron wrapped in sinew and singing-bone. Niel and Mestra's cipher-lock is carved from bone and meteoric iron. These materials are associated with fae heritage and ancient magic.

substances.name: War-glass
substances.properties: Glass forged from consciousness-fragments; shaped into lenses and prophecy-weapons; capable of truth-separation and auditor-will execution; enslaves fragment agency.
substances.use: Dominion military and prophetic control; forced focus of consciousness into weapon form; commissioned from glass-makers under threat.
substances.notes: Primary weapon of Dominion prophecy; Mella refused to forge truth-separating lens; Sella faces same commission as test; represents moral core of resistance.

substances.name: Silver and Bone (as Combined Material)
substances.properties: When carved together into a mechanism, silver and bone create a powerful seal responsive to those who refuse prophecy.
substances.use: Gareth's oath-gate is constructed from a mechanism of silver and bone, making it resistant to ledger-magic and decree.
substances.notes: This combination represents a new alchemical principle: seals based on choice rather than force.

substances.name: Bone-Resonance (substance property)
substances.properties: Bone can be carved or treated to resonate in response to specific stimuli (like copper fingers). Creates semi-sentient locks that sing or respond to proper mechanisms.
substances.use: Lock mechanisms; vault security; magical crafting.
substances.notes: Combined with meteoric iron and Elder Tongue syllables to create Dominion-quality locks.

substances.name: Silver Chains (with binding-magic)
substances.properties: Silver chains that glow with binding-magic. Used to bind Gareth. Designed with trap-logic so breaking them triggers mutual destruction.
substances.use: Magical imprisonment; binding-magic anchors; prisoner restraint.
substances.notes: The chains on Gareth are at least three years old and actively maintained with magical force.

substances.name: Ash (Burned Expanse)
substances.properties: Fine ash from the Burned Expanse, possibly containing residue of pre-Remaking magic or the Remaking's destructive wave. Chokes those who breathe it.
substances.use: Contamination hazard. May carry magical residue.
substances.notes: The refugee girl coughs ash from her lungs after fleeing the Burned Expanse. The ash's presence suggests the devastation is ongoing or the region is still toxic.

substances.name: memory-shards
substances.properties: Fragments of consciousness extracted and used as currency or trade goods by the Hegemony. Kelvan refuses to become such a shard.
substances.use: Hegemony currency; consciousness commodification; trade goods
substances.notes: Represents the Hegemony's enforcement of consciousness-as-currency. Kelvan's refusal is an act of rebellion against this commodification.

substances.name: Pale ash (formerly gold)
substances.properties: Inert, pale ash that drifts and settles. Formerly gold wealth; transformed by the Remaking into economically worthless dust.
substances.use: No current use; serves as monument to collapsed pre-Remaking economy. Drifts in wyvern hoards.
substances.notes: Symbolic of the Remaking's wholesale destruction of prior value-systems. Both Velka and Sorrath describe their hoards of gold as now turned to pale ash.

substances.name: singing-bone
substances.properties: A material with harmonic properties; can be carved and used to bind objects; adds value and function to tools; enables bone-song casting; conducts harmonic frequencies.
substances.use: Binding tool components (Kestran's crook); crafting vessels and conduits for harmonic magic; creating reliquaries for consciousness-fragments.
substances.notes: More valuable post-Remaking than coin; core material of bone-singer traditions; used in both pastoral and deep magical contexts.

substances.name: thorn-wood
substances.properties: A hardwood suitable for carving and binding; strong enough for tool handles; post-Remaking value craft material.
substances.use: Carving shepherd's crooks and pastoral tools; binding base for singing-bone work.
substances.notes: Kestran's crook is carved from thorn-wood; appears to be a natural resource in pastoral regions.

substances.name: Ash (from the Remaking)
substances.properties: Sorresh's gold became ash; weightless in value but heavy in quantity; accumulates in dunes.
substances.use: No practical use post-Remaking; symbolic of the collapse of old value systems.
substances.notes: Gold transformed into ash during the Remaking; Sorresh's hoards of it represent the despair of old-world survivors.

substances.name: Bone (Pre-Remaking)
substances.properties: Bone from creatures that died before the Remaking retains resonance with pre-human frequencies and old magic. Can be carved with Elder Tongue syllables to create singing artifacts.
substances.use: Carving bone-flutes, bone-vials, lockpicks, and other resonant tools. Used in bone-singing magic.
substances.notes: Naela used this material to craft her refusal bone-flute. More powerful than post-Remaking bone.

substances.name: Dominion Glass
substances.properties: A specially forged glass designed by the Dominion to contain and seal consciousness-fragments permanently.
substances.use: Creating vessels to hold consciousness-fragments in vaults. Designed to prevent reassembly or escape.
substances.notes: Three hundred vessels of this glass held consciousness-fragments in the Quarantine-House before Gorvan's infiltration emptied them.

substances.name: bioluminescent moss
substances.properties: Emits soft light without fire; grows in catacombs and underground places; provides illumination for night work.
substances.use: Light source for underground bone-carving work; suitable for hidden sanctuaries.
substances.notes: Valren works by bioluminescent moss in his hidden catacomb north of Pelimor.

substances.name: Phantom-Gold
substances.properties: Currency used by Soroth to rule the Crimson Vale; became utterly worthless and threadbare after the Remaking.
substances.use: Former trade currency; now has no value.
substances.notes: Soroth's entire rule depended on phantom-gold and fear. The Remaking erased both in a single night, making his thirty-year reign's foundation worthless.

substances.name: Silver filigree
substances.properties: Intricate metalwork; forms the structure of the compass rose
substances.use: Decorative and structural element of high-value objects
substances.notes: The compass rose is constructed from silver filigree surrounding a Sapphire Deep pearl.

substances.name: Pearl from the Sapphire Deep
substances.properties: Pearl from undersea realm; central component of the compass rose; capable of resonance and song
substances.use: High-value jewelry; magical artifact component; cardinal point marking on compass
substances.notes: The pearl learned to resonate with wind and heartbeat, becoming a sentient element of the compass rose.

substances.name: bioluminescent glass
substances.properties: Contains or reflects light; used to seal magical artifacts; may be charged with divine or magical essence.
substances.use: Creating secure vessels for relics like the Tear of the Ashglass; preserving magical or divine substances.
substances.notes: The Tear of the Ashglass is contained in a flask of bioluminescent glass, suggesting the material has properties of both containment and preservation.

substances.name: pre-Remaking starspice
substances.properties: Spices from before the Remaking; possible magical properties or unusual sensory qualities; may exist in multiple timelines simultaneously.
substances.use: High-value trade goods; Raen carries six casks of it to the Broker, suggesting premium value.
substances.notes: The existence of pre-Remaking goods suggests either stockpiles or time-locked preservation. The fact that Raen transports it in a caravan trap suggests it may be a target for more than simple commerce.

substances.name: Cardamom (Post-Remaking)
substances.properties: Spice that smells of ash; appears to be tied to memory or pre-Remaking times.
substances.use: Trading commodity in the Wailing Scar; possibly ritual or medicinal use.
substances.notes: Corvik references tea-sellers hawking cardamom that smells of ash in the three-fold marketplace. May be memory-scented like other post-Remaking items.

substances.name: ash-coins
substances.properties: Coins that were once gold, rendered into ash by the Remaking. They retain a ghostly memory of their former worth.
substances.use: The young boy offers ash-coins as tribute to Sorresh, symbolizing that value transcends material worth.
substances.notes: The ash-coins become a powerful symbol of transformation and meaning in Sorresh's narrative arc.

substances.name: pale dust
substances.properties: The remains of Sorresh's three-century-old gold hoard, rendered into dust by the Remaking. It is described as lying in dunes throughout her lair.
substances.use: A reminder of lost dominion; scattered across the mountainous lair; signals the end of an era.
substances.notes: This dust represents the fundamental upheaval caused by the Remaking and Sorresh's loss of purpose.

substances.name: luminous silk
substances.properties: Light leaking from the abyss through the Tear's thinning boundary. It appears as luminous silk threads seeping through into the deepkin realms.
substances.use: A visible sign of boundary failure; indicates the Tear is breaking.
substances.notes: The imagery of silk suggests beauty and danger; the light is both beautiful and apocalyptic.

substances.name: black-water pearls
substances.properties: Pearls formed in the deep waters of the deepkin realms. The Keeper of Pearls is crowned in these pearls, suggesting they are symbols of authority and deep wisdom.
substances.use: Ceremonial crown worn by the Keeper of Pearls; symbol of deepkin sovereignty.
substances.notes: The pearls' deep-water origin ties them to the pre-human frequencies and ancient power.

substances.name: Bone-Resonance (Material Property)
substances.properties: Can be carved or worked into locks and artifacts; creates sympathetic vibrations; enables magical binding and sealing.
substances.use: Deepkin lock-crafting; covenant binding; magical sealing; artifacts.
substances.notes: Suggests bone from sentient beings (possibly drowned sailors, ancestors, or sacred sources) holds magical resonance in deepkin tradition.

rituals.name: Oath-Breaker's Curse
rituals.requirements: Unknown; cast in response to Tormund's repeated oath-breaking.
rituals.effect: Each oath Tormund breaks steals something irreplaceable: first taste, then laughter, then memory of his firstborn son. By the twentieth oath, he is hollow.
rituals.cost: The curse is irreversible and permanent. It cannot be lifted—only borne and transformed through new honor.
rituals.notes: The cursewright designed this curse to teach that oaths are bonds, not chains. It is elegant in its mechanism: loss is cumulative and specific to each oath.

rituals.name: Stellar Rite
rituals.requirements: Knowledge of ancient languages predating humanity; performance beneath the Violet Conjunction; a mountain or high location beneath the stars.
rituals.effect: Births new stars from the ashes of dying ones, preventing the world from falling into endless night and extending the world's lifespan by ten thousand years.
rituals.cost: The caster must sacrifice every moment of joy, every love, every triumph, and every small beauty—effectively stripping them of all positive memories.
rituals.notes: The Rite was discovered inscribed in the Singing Catacombs in languages predating humanity. Zareth learned it through seven years of study with druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits.

rituals.name: Celestial Rite
rituals.requirements: Performance on the Peak of Aethros during the Violet Conjunction. Requires instinctual knowledge and alignment with cosmic timing.
rituals.effect: Aligns the world's fate with the death and rebirth of the stars themselves. The ritual causes the stars to respond directly, subtly and permanently changing the sky and cosmic order. The performer gains temporary comprehensive understanding of all cosmic knowledge—the shape of future empires, names of forgotten gods, the price the stars demand at their death.
rituals.cost: Complete erasure of the performer's personal memory and identity. Zareth lost all knowledge of his past while gaining cosmic knowledge, then lost the cosmic knowledge when the vision departed, leaving him blank and whole and new.
rituals.notes: This ritual appears to be unique or extraordinarily rare. The Sovereign's decision to assign the memory-stripped Zareth to records suggests either containment of a dangerous secret or preservation of a cosmic witness. Future attempts to perform this ritual or recover Zareth's lost knowledge may be significant.

rituals.name: Celestial Binding
rituals.requirements: Unknown; likely involves astronomical alignment and stellar magic.
rituals.effect: Changed the constellations themselves. Caused new stars to burn where old ones dimmed. Required a human consciousness as payment.
rituals.cost: Memory and identity of at least one person (possibly Zareth); uncertainty and suffering among those aware of the working.
rituals.notes: The exact mechanism and full cost are not revealed; Vex believes prices might be renegotiable.

rituals.name: The Celestial Binding
rituals.requirements: Unknown; appears to require a willing or captured sacrifice whose memory is the source of renewal.
rituals.effect: Renews the stars through extraction of memory from a sacrificed individual. Bends reality itself. Creates ripples that spread through the cosmos for decades, touching all practitioners of the Art.
rituals.cost: The complete memory erasure of the sacrifice (Zareth/Astronomer-Prince); leaves a cosmic wound in the earth that takes generations to heal; creates a knot of consciousness from the extracted memories that emerges from between the stars.
rituals.notes: The ritual's full scope and procedures are unknown; the ghost-entity born from it is neither demon nor ghost but something older and stranger.

rituals.name: Stellar Working
rituals.requirements: Zareth's memories; unknown other components
rituals.effect: This working either created or freed a Stellar Entity. It stripped Zareth of something essential and bound or released something already present within Vex.
rituals.cost: Zareth's sacrifice; unknown cost to Vex and others
rituals.notes: The exact nature and purpose of this ritual remain unclear. It has far-reaching consequences across multiple timelines and people.

rituals.name: the Working
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: The stars were changed or reborn; the cosmos was fundamentally altered; reality was wounded at the Peak of Aethros; fragments of identity were scattered into the world.
rituals.cost: Zareth's humanity; the world's stability; cosmic pain and mourning.
rituals.notes: Performed at the Peak of Aethros. The mechanism, intent, and full scope remain mysterious. May still be ongoing or having cascading effects.

rituals.name: Celestial Ascension
rituals.requirements: Specific stellar alignment and cosmic arrangement; involves celestial entities and forces.
rituals.effect: Alters the fundamental structure of reality. Changes the stars themselves. Fractures barriers between realms (surface and Abyss Below). Changes the agreement between living and dead. Renders some texts readable under new starlight. Transforms some beings into living light.
rituals.cost: Previous civilizations that performed similar workings left behind destructive voids that eventually collapsed inward, consuming everything. The current Celestial Ascension is creating refugee crises, destabilizing ancient seals, and forcing the deepkin to choose between isolation, war, or investigation.
rituals.notes: Zareth is identified as the performer of the most recent working. The texts in the Tower suggest this is a pattern—multiple civilizations have performed similar rituals, each leaving behind catastrophic consequences.

rituals.name: The Stellar Rite
rituals.tagline: Stars blazed. Reality broke. Consequences rippled.
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: When performed (or completed, or failed), the Stellar Rite caused new stars to blaze overhead, fractured reality in multiple ways, freed shadows, shifted ley-lines, weakened ancient bindings, and triggered the awakening of imprisoned consciousnesses.
rituals.cost: The cost appears to be catastrophic—reality became unstable, ancient safeguards weakened, and the world entered a period of uncertainty.
rituals.notes: The Stellar Rite is mentioned in existing canon. These three stories confirm its effects ripple across the world. Its original purpose (to bind something? to break something? to reset something?) is unclear.

rituals.name: Counter-Working (Seasonal Compact Restoration)
rituals.requirements: Unknown; appears to involve the forest itself and the Covenant-Keepers' power.
rituals.effect: Attempts to restore the forest's seasonal compact after disruption caused by the Stellar Rite.
rituals.cost: Unknown.
rituals.notes: Kellen is working to maintain the forest's old agreements even as the world transforms.

rituals.name: Luminous Ascension
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Sacrifices a cosmic entity's consciousness to birth new stars and fundamentally rewrite the celestial order and history of the world.
rituals.cost: The consciousness of Vethis, shattered into fragments including the phantom-Echo. The erasure or alteration of memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world.
rituals.notes: A foundational cosmic ritual that has shaped the entire world. Its consequences are still unfolding through the phantom-Echo's actions.

rituals.name: Luminous Remaking
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Fundamentally rewrites reality, transforming the world and its inhabitants. At least thirteen previous iterations have been recorded, each overwriting the previous world while leaving fragments of memory encoded in runes, paintings, and songs.
rituals.cost: The erasure and rewriting of entire worlds and timelines.
rituals.notes: A fourteenth remaking may be in progress. Prevention and success might be the same outcome.

rituals.name: The Deepwarden's Binding
rituals.requirements: Performed at the Abyss-gate; involves connection to the seals that bind the pre-human civilization.
rituals.effect: Intended to reseal the Abyss-gate. Instead becomes a conversation with the pre-human civilization, translating their message across time and reality.
rituals.cost: Unknown; may require a sacrifice similar to that made by the pre-human civilization.
rituals.notes: The ritual reveals that the seals were designed not merely to contain but to communicate. Finishing or preventing the Luminous Remaking may demand the same sacrifice.

rituals.name: Stellar Rite Reversal
rituals.requirements: Instructions contained in the Spellwright's Journals, located in the Vaulted Tombs.
rituals.effect: Could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old stars to the sky.
rituals.cost: Whoever performs the reversal must sacrifice something equally precious; catastrophic costs are implied but unspecified.
rituals.notes: Central to multiple factions' agendas; Apprentice Sage's journals contain warnings about the true price of reversal.

rituals.name: Bone-Song
rituals.requirements: Bone-singer's voice, pre-human dead or skeletons, harmonic resonance.
rituals.effect: Commands and controls the pre-human dead. The ritual formerly gave Kess absolute control but is now achieving harmonic resonance instead, with the dead singing in response.
rituals.cost: Identity and control. The dead remember themselves and choose to resist and resurrect.
rituals.notes: The bone-song is no longer a tool of will but part of a larger convergence. The ritual may be evolving toward resurrection magic.

rituals.name: The Archivist's Grand Working
rituals.requirements: Fragments of stolen memories, world-fragments, assembly of pieces across the Dominion.
rituals.effect: Designed to open a doorway into the void—to reach something that existed before reality itself was rewritten by the Luminous Ascension.
rituals.cost: The collapse of the Dominion. Reality fragmentation accelerates.
rituals.notes: Vex's counterfeit fragments flood the market and destabilize the working, but the Archivist's core obsession remains active. The true goal—reaching the pre-rewrite void—is unclear.

rituals.name: Ledger-Magic
rituals.requirements: The Ledger of Names; knowledge of phantom ledger entries that function as prophecy.
rituals.effect: Allows the Ledger-Keeper to slowly restore order to a fractured realm through the magical recording and prophecy inherent in ledger entries. Can be used to suppress knowledge or maintain false narratives.
rituals.cost: The Ledger-Keeper becomes bound to a life of isolation and compartmentalized truth. Every entry is a lie meant to save civilization.
rituals.notes: Ledger-magic is the mechanism by which the Crown maintains the Dominion's illusion of wholeness despite its fracturing.

rituals.name: tally-working
rituals.requirements: Written numbers and administrative records; certainty of tally; established ledgers.
rituals.effect: Enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute across the Empire; a form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records.
rituals.cost: Requires the certainty of written numbers; shattered by the Celestial Turning.
rituals.notes: Unresolved: whether tally-working can be restored or reimagined in a new form.

rituals.name: pre-human ritual
rituals.requirements: Knowledge of pre-human civilization, access to liminal spaces, possibly completion or reversal of the Stellar Rite
rituals.effect: Unknown but implied to be a major cosmic working capable of restoring or reversing the Stellar Rite rewrite, possibly restoring the old stars or the people erased by the rewrite.
rituals.cost: unknown
rituals.notes: A faction is actively seeking to complete this ritual. The spice trader may know its location or requirements. The young scribe has been given this knowledge as payment for her erased childhood memory.

rituals.name: Convergence at the Obsidian Tower
rituals.requirements: Unknown; involves multiple locations (Obsidian Tower, Sunken Cathedrals) and timescales.
rituals.effect: Unknown. May result in ascension, ending, or transformation of reality. The unified song at the Sunken Cathedrals is part of this working.
rituals.cost: Unknown; possibly requires surrender of the convergence itself to answer reality's question.
rituals.notes: The Wanderer's doubt has made this working unpredictable. Whether it completes or transforms remains the central unresolved question.

rituals.name: Harmonic Working
rituals.requirements: Half-transformed beings converging at a sacred location. Resonance between living light and the Cathedral's structure. Unknown stellar or celestial alignments.
rituals.effect: Transformed beings resonate with frequencies that shimmer the air. The working seems to deepen the transformation and consciousness of the participants, potentially creating windows or vectors for ancient consciousness to perceive the modern world.
rituals.cost: Identity; participants report losing sense of self. The singing becomes louder and more desperate with disruption.
rituals.notes: Bryth's sabotage is making the working more intense rather than stopping it. The working may be self-perpetuating or tied to the Luminous Ascension cycle. Its ultimate purpose is unknown.

rituals.name: Will-Binding
rituals.requirements: Unknown; performed on Corvhen's mother. May require harmonic resonance.
rituals.effect: Suspends a living person in a state between life and death, preserving them in a luminous state within a crypt. Animated by harmonic resonance.
rituals.cost: Requires a replacement anchor—another living being must take their place for approximately twenty years.
rituals.notes: Corvhen's mother was bound by this ritual for three generations before Corvhen's arrival.

rituals.name: Harmonic Resonance
rituals.requirements: Harmonic frequency matching pre-human structures. Song-based component.
rituals.effect: Animates the preserved dead, allowing them to move and exist in a state between life and death. Aligned with pre-human frequency post-Ascension.
rituals.cost: Requires constant feeding by an anchor or replacement. Unknown if voluntary or compulsory.
rituals.notes: Catacombs' frequency began matching pre-human ruins post-Ascension.

rituals.name: Pre-Human Ritual Awakening
performed_by: Faction within Council of Depths (opposed by others)
rituals.requirements: Location in Bleached Wastes; possibly the Tear of the Submerged Divinity or components thereof
rituals.effect: Would restore tidecaller civilization to original splendor but would flood all surface kingdoms; geographic and civilization rewriting.
rituals.cost: Destruction of surface kingdom civilization; catastrophic loss of life and territory.
rituals.notes: This is one of three competing visions held by the fractured Council of Depths. Mardoc's map may either complete or prevent it.

rituals.name: pre-human ritual (restoration attempt)
rituals.requirements: Unknown, but potentially involves the cooperation or transformation of a human willing to mediate.
rituals.effect: The forsaken dead are attempting to restore their pre-human civilization through this ritual.
